Summary: Performance issues for Simulink models with many instances of models referenced in normal mode
Description: Simulating, generating code, or updating the diagram might have poor performance for model hierarchies that have many Model blocks that simulate in normal mode and reference the same model. The behavior depends on the size of the referenced models and the number of Model blocks that reference them. When a model hierarchy has hundreds or thousands of instances of the same referenced model, and the instances simulate in normal mode, MATLAB might hang or crash.
Workaround: For most of the Model blocks, set the?Simulation mode?block parameter to Accelerator. Alternatively, in the Simulink Toolstrip, change the simulation mode of the top model from Normal to Accelerator. When a model simulates in accelerator mode, its referenced models also simulate in accelerator mode.In R2021a Update 8, you can enable an optimization manually that improves the performance of models with many instances of referenced models that simulate in normal mode. To turn this optimization on, enter this command in the MATLAB Command Window.Simulink.ModelReference.enableMultiInstanceModelRefOptimization?To turn the optimization off, enter this command in the MATLAB Command Window.Simulink.ModelReference.disableMultiInstanceModelRefOptimizationThis optimization happens automatically in subsequent releases, starting with R2021b.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Moore charts might step more than once per time step during simulation
Description: During Normal or Accelerator mode simulation, Moore semantics charts with two or more levels of state hierarchy might step more than once per time step. Code generated from such models is not affected by this bug.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 6, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 5, R2023a

Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 6, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 5, R2023a

Summary: Code generation halts with error:?Unrecognized function or variable rtNaN
Description: Code generation halts with error: Unrecognized function or variable rtNaN  This error indicates that a code generation failure occurred for the model construct where the output signal from a rate transition block splits and provides input into two or more different asynchronous function call subsystems.
Workaround: To resolve this code generation error, you can apply either of these solutions:Split the signal before the rate transition block. Use a rate transition for each signal that is input to the asynchronous function call subsystems.In Configuration Parameters > Solver > Solver details, enable Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer after removing the rate transition block(s) at the split(s).
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 5, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: gradient?with two inputs might not apply a scalar spacing input correctly
Description: If?F  is not a vector and h  is a scalar, ?[dx,dy,...] = gradient(F,h)   only applies the spacing h  to the dx  output. Su bsequent outputs are computed with a spacing of 1 . MATLAB execution computes all outputs with the spacing h .
Workaround: Do any one of the following:?Apply the spacing manually:? [dx,dy,...] = gradient(F/h).Supply the scalar h?input for each dimension of F.? For example, if?F?is 3-D,??[dx,dy,dz] = gradient(F,h,h,h).
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Generated code might contain MISRA C:2012 Rule 2.2 violation
Description: The generated code might contain a MISRA C:2012 Rule 2.2 violation for a model that has Optimize global data access ?configuration parameter set to Minimize global data access .  The violation occurs because of the presence of dead code in the generated code.
Workaround: Set the configuration parameter Optimize global data access?to either None?or Use global to hold temporary results.

Summary: Internal assertion occurred during the HDL code generation when you use Selector block for selecting signal from Bus ports
Description: When your model containing a Selector block that is used to select or de-mux signal from bus ports by setting the Selector block parameter?Index Option ?to?Index vector (port)or Starting index (port) , HDL code generation fails with the following internal assertion error:? Summary: Simulink models with Assignment block inside nested iterator subsystems might trigger incorrect warnings or errors
Description: Simulink models with an Assignment block inside nested iterator subsystems might trigger a warning or error that should not occur. This issue might happen when all of these conditions are true: The Assignment block is inside nested For or While Iterator subsystems. The Index Option  parameter is Index Vector (Port)  or Starting Index (Port) .  The Initialize Output (Y)  is Specify size for each dimension in table .  The Action if any output element is not assigned  ?parameter is??Error  or  Warning .  All output signal elements of the Assignment block are assigned after the top-level subsystem completes each step.  An example of an error message that displays in the Diagnostic Viewer is: An error occurred while running the simulation and the simulation was terminated  Caused by:  At least 1 element in the output signal of <path_to_Assignment_block> has not been assigned

Summary: datetime time zones do not reflect recent changes to the IANA time zone database, including time zones in Mexico
Description: MATLAB uses the IANA time zones database for up-to-date time zone information. MATLAB is missing recent updates to this database. As of the R2022b general release, MATLAB includes database version 2022a. However, in R2022b MATLAB does not include the 2022f version of the IANA database, which also captures time zone updates from database versions 2022b, 2022c, 2022d, and 2022e. Most notably, these updates include changes to a number of time zones which no longer observe daylight saving time (DST). For example, in October 2022 the government of Mexico announced that the country no longer observes DST except near the US border.?The time zone data in MATLAB does not reflect this recent change. IANA Time Zone Database:?https://www.iana.org/time-zones  In versions of MATLAB that do not have these changes, datetime  arrays in what used to be the DST period will have incorrect UTC offsets. Specifically, they will be one hour ahead of the actual times in those time zones.
Workaround: If possible, update to a release with an IANA time zone database version of 2022f or later.To check the IANA time zone database version in MATLAB, use the second output of the timezones function. When the database is up to date, the second output is '2022f'.>> [~,vers] = timezones vers =    '2022f'https://www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/ref/timezones.htmlIf you are unable to update to a newer release, you can obtain the correct time zone information for datetime arrays on or after October 30th 2022 (where the desired time zone is in Mexico) by using?the corresponding fixed time zone offset. For example, replace the time zone 'America/Chihuahua'?with the time zone 'UTC-6' on or after?October 30th, 2022.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Enabled Subsystem block with virtual outport might output an uninitialized value in Accelerator mode
Description: When you run a model in Accelerator mode where all of the following conditions are true, Simulink might output an uninitialized value from the Enabled Subsystem block. The Enabled Subsystem block: Has a ground signal input to its?Enable ?port. Contains a Constant block connected to an Outport block with?Ensure outport is virtual ?check box selected. Is connected to a Bus Creator block.  ?The Bus Creator block: Creates a bus with one element. Has the?Output has nonvirtual bus ?check box selected. Is connected to an Outport block.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 6, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 5, R2023a

Summary: Models might simulate slowly, stop responding, or produce a wrong answer if the Minimize algebraic loop?occurrences?parameter is enabled for referenced model
Description: Simulations of a model hierarchy might run slowly, stop responding, or produce a wrong answer due to an incorrect Jacobian pattern when all of these conditions are met: The model uses an implicit solver with the Solver Jacobian method ?parameter set to Sparse perturbation .  The model contains one or more referenced models that are part of an algebraic loop. One or more referenced models in an algebraic loop has the Minimize algebraic loop occurrences ?parameter enabled.?
Workaround: To avoid or resolve this issue, use the full perturbation solver Jacobian method in the top model.In the Simulink Toolstrip, on the Modeling?tab, click Model Settings.Select the Solver?pane.?Expand Solver details.Set the Solver Jacobian method?to Full perturbation.For some models, the full perturbation solver Jacobian method might not be an appropriate choice or might slow simulation. If using the full perturbation solver Jacobian method does not work for your model, you can disable algebraic loop minimization for the referenced models instead.To disable the Minimize algebraic loop occurrences?parameter for a referenced model:Open the referenced model as a top model.In the Simulink Toolstrip, on the Modeling?tab, click Model Settings.?Select the Model Referencing?tab.Under Options for referencing this model?clear Minimize algebraic loop occurrences.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Microsoft Excel date values stored in the 1904 date system as doubles might be incorrectly imported by readcell
Description: readcell  imports datenum  values using the 1900 date system. In releases R2019a through R2021a, when UseExcel  is true  and DatetimeType  is exceldatenum , returned values originally stored using the 1904 date system are 1462 days greater than expected. For more information on the 1900 and 1904 date systems, see?Date Systems in Excel .
Workaround: In releases R2019a through R2021a, to read the correct datenum values, specify the name-value argument UseExcel as false.>> C = readcell(filename,"DatetimeType","exceldatenum","UseExcel",false)If unspecified, the default value for UseExcel is false.>> C = readcell(filename,"DatetimeType","exceldatenum")
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

That referenced model accesses a global data store defined by a Simulink.Signal  object in a data dictionary or the base workspace. The Initial value ?property of that?Simulink.Signal  object is set to a MATLAB variable or Simulink.Parameter  object. Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Changing initial value of a chart output and recompiling can result in wrong answers
Description: Stateflow might retain incorrect type information from a previous model update or simulation when all of the following conditions are true: The output data of the chart is set to "inherit" The initial value of that output data is specified to a value other than the default  For example, if the initial value for the output is a 2x2 matrix of type double, and after running the simulation you change the initial value to a 4x4 matrix of type single, the output signal might retain its original size and type on subsequent runs, leading to wrong simulation results.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Simulink model that uses?SeriesNetwork?or?DAGNetwork?object containing?featureInputLayer?might produce error during simulation or code generation
Description: Your Simulink model might produce an error during simulation or code generation if both ?these conditions are true:? The model contains a MATLAB Function block or a Predict block This block uses a?SeriesNetwork ?or?DAGNetwork ?object containing a?featureInputLayer   In such situations, model simulation (using Simulink), CPU code generation (using Simulink Coder), or GPU code generation (using GPU Coder) might produce this error: Index exceeds the number of array elements (1).
Workaround: To use?featureInputLayer?in model simulation,?use MATLAB Function block and call predict extrinsically.?For example, create a new function that you will call from the MATLAB function block by using feval:?function out = callPredictExtrinsically(matfile, in)persistent mynet;if isempty(mynet)    mynet = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork(matfile);endout = predict(mynet, in);endCall this function extrinsically from the MATLAB Function block:function predVal = fcn(in)if coder.target('sfun')    predVal = coder.nullcopy(single(1)); % preallocate the output with correct size and type based on your network    predVal = feval('callPredictExtrinsically', 'network.mat', in);endend

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code that uses deep learning libraries might produce incorrect answers if 1x1?convolution2dLayer?post-dominates?maxPooling2dLayer
Description: Generated code that uses MKL-DNN, ARM Compute or cuDNN library might produce incorrect answers if your deep learning network satisfies all  these conditions: The network contains a  maxPooling2dLayer ?followed by a  convolution2dLayer .  All paths in the layer graph that start at the  maxPooling2dLayer ?go through the  convolution2dLayer ?(that is, the? convolution2dLayer ?post-dominates ?the?maxPooling2dLayer ). The  convolution2dLayer ?has  FilterSize  equal to  [1 1], ?and ?has equal strides that are greater than  1 , and ?has no spatial padding .  The  maxPooling2dLayer ?has  FilterSize  greater than  1 , or ?has strides greater than  1 , or  has spatial padding.  The only layers between the? maxPooling2dLayer ?and  convolution2dLayer ?are  reluLayer, additionLayer, clippedReluLayer, leakyReluLayer.   This is an example network layer graph that might produce incorrect answers in code generation:? ?lg = layerGraph([imageInputLayer([5 5 3], Normalization = 'none', Name = 'input')?  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? maxPooling2dLayer([2 3], Name = 'maxpool')?  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? reluLayer(Name = 'relu')?  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? convolution2dLayer(1,3,Stride = [2 2], Name = 'conv')]);?  This issue affects code generated by using any  of these products: MATLAB Coder  Simulink Coder  GPU Coder when using  TargetLibrary='cudnn'?
Workaround: For code generation targeting Intel, ARM, or AMD based CPUs, use?TargetLibrary='none'.For code generation targeting NVIDIA GPUs, use TargetLibrary='tensorrt'.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Unexpected license checkout when using "Other uses of functionName" links in help text
Description: After calling help on a function that is overloaded in several classes, clicking on the "Other functions named functionName  " link at the bottom of the help text can cause licenses to be checked out.
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):         cd <MATLAB>        unzip <.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.        Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

Summary: CAN blocks drop floating-point digits in DBC file
Description: The CAN Pack, CAN Unpack, CAN FD Pack, and CAN FD Unpack blocks can be configured to use CANdb specified signals, in which case numerical parameters are read from a DBC file to perform the conversion from messages to signals. If the DBC file contains signal definitions whose scaling  and offset  parameter values are floating-point, these values are rounded to four decimal places. These rounded values are used in simulation and in any generated code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for the?activation?function when called on additionLayer might produce incorrect results for MKL-DNN target

In such situations, the generated code might produce incorrect results for the activation values if your network contains these layers in succession: convolution2dLayer?  batchNormalizationLayer  (optional) additionLayer  (the activation  function is called on this layer) Activation layers like?reluLayer  and leakyReluLayer   This issue affects the MKL-DNN target.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Constant-folding expressions that accept nonvirtual buses as inputs might generate incorrect results
Description: The code generator might generate incorrect results when all  of the following are true: ?You generate code for a model that contains a MATLAB Function block. The code in the MATLAB Function block has an expression that accepts a nonvirtual bus containing a matrix as an input. The code uses the matrix in the nonvirtual bus input to initialize a matrix variable. A value is assigned to an element of the initialized?matrix variable. The code uses the coder.const  directive to constant-fold the expression.   Constant-folding the expression in the generated code might produce incorrect results. For example, consider the function  loca l. The function is called by passing the input  u, ? which is a nonvirtual bus that contains a matrix of complex numbers in the field f . function y = fcn(u)  ? ? u.f = repmat(0+2i,2,1);  ? ? t=coder.const(local(u));  ? ? y=t.f;  end  function ovar = local(ivar)  ? ? ovar.f = ivar.f;  ? ? temp = ivar.f;  ? ? temp(1) = 5+5i;  end  The function local ? initializes the variable temp  using the input and assigns the value 5+5i  to a matrix element. The generated code constant-folds the expression local(u) ,?which ?produces the incorrect result?[0+2i,5+5i], ?where t he correct result is [0+2i,0+2i] .
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Simulation results might be incorrect for export-function model with State Writer block that writes to Outport block
Description: Simulating an export-function model produces incorrect results when all these conditions apply: The?Data Import/Export > Format ?configuration parameter is set to?Dataset . The model contains two root Inport blocks that output function-call signals. Each function-call signal connects to a function-call port of a function-call subsystem. The first function-call subsystem contains a State Writer block. The State Writer block writes to an Outport block in the second function-call subsystem. The corresponding output port directly drives a root Outport block.  The output of the root Outport block is logged each time either function-call subsystem executes. When the first function-call subsystem executes, the output of the root Outport block is logged incorrectly. When the second function-call subsystem executes, the output of the root Outport block is logged correctly.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: SimBiology projects lose data and become corrupted when apps are left open overnight
Description: If you leave a SimBiology project open overnight in the SimBiology Model Builder  app or the SimBiology Model Analyzer  app, the project might lose data and become corrupted. This data loss can lead to errors when saving or loading the project or when trying to load data or results from the project. This corruption happens because the operating system sometimes deletes temporary files needed by the SimBiology project.
Workaround: You can prevent the operating system from deleting SimBiology temporary files by changing the MATLAB temporary folder. Set the appropriate environment variable to a different location, such as your documents folder. On Windows, set the environment variable TMP. On macOS or Linux, set the environment variable TMPDIR.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 1, R2023a

Summary: Statefow annotations cannot be added using Quick Insert
Description: When you invoke the Simulink Editor's Quick Insert by clicking inside some object, and not over an empty space on the canvas, there will be no item in search results that allows you to add annotation.?
Workaround: Add annotations from the tool palette instead.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Connecting a root inport to an Initialize Function block might produce incorrect code
Description: The code generator will produce incorrect code when you connect a root inport to an Initialize Function block and the data type of the root inport is any ?of the following: An enumerated type whose default enumeration has a nonzero integer value A fixed-point type with nonzero bias A bus type that has an element whose data type is one of the preceding categories  The generated code will incorrectly overwrite the inport value before referencing it. For example: model_U.In1 = -5;  model_DW.A = modul_U.In1;

Summary: Generated code for interp1 with interpolation method set to?'pchip' or 'makima'?might run slower than MATLAB
Description: Suppose that: You generate code for the function interp1  with interpolation method set to 'pchip'  or 'makima'  During code generation, you disable the configuration parameter Check memory integrity ?(IntegrityChecks )   In such situations, the generated code might run slower when compared to MATLAB. The performance issue affects both MEX and standalone code generation.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Under certain conditions, lookup tables with table data defined via signal might generate code with output incorrectly set to zero
Description: Incorrect zero output might occur in code generation for models for which all of these conditions are true: The model contains an n-D Lookup Table block with: Data specification  set to Table and Breakpoints  Table data: Source ?set to Input port   The  Optimize using the specified minimum and maximum values ?configuration parameter in Model Configuration Parameters  > Code Generation  > Optimization  > Advanced parameters  is set to On

Summary: Definite integrals with parameters might be incorrect for some parameter values
Description: Definite integrals that share the same parameters in the integrands and the bounds might be incorrect for some parameter values. For example, the following result is only correct for x >= 2 : >> syms x u>> res = int(sign(u-x+2),u,[0 x])res =4 - x

Description: On a PC configured to use a Japanese environment, Simulink throws the following error when you try to change the Function  parameter of the MinMax block using the drop-down list that opens when the block is selected and you click  min? or  max  at the center of the block:  ĄŃĄéĄá©`Ąż 'Function' ¤ËŚť¤ą¤ë MinMax Block 'MinMax' ÄÚ¤ÎÔO¶¨¤¬źo„ż¤Ç¤ą
Workaround: Use the Block Parameters dialog box instead.

Summary: Data type Propagation Block may result in numerical mismatch
Description: HDL Code generation from a subsystem containing Data type Propagation block may result in numerical mismatch. This is due to incorrect reference type data from the Data type propagation block in the generated model. However, the generated HDL Code is correct. This only occurs if there are no optimizations enabled in the model.?
Workaround: consider enabling any of the optimizations like Distributed pipelining, streaming/sharing, Adaptive pipelining...
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022b Update 5, R2023a

Summary: MATLAB slows down when using echotcpip or UDP functions
Description: Due to a memory leak, MATLAB might slow down and become unresponsive when using echotcpip  or UDP functions. The issue is more prominent when you send and receive large quantities of data (e.g., data sizes of 1 GB).  This issue can occur when using the following functions and possibly other functions: echotcpip  echoudp  udpport("byte")
Workaround: Restart MATLAB to free up the leaked memory. However, the issue might reappear if you use the affected functionality again.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b

Summary: Compatibility check fails for a Simulink Test harness associated with a Subsystem Reference when block replacement is turned on
Description: When a Simulink Test harness, associated with a Subsystem Reference , has block replacement  turned on, Simulink Design Verifier fails to construct the correct block replacement model, and compatibility checking fails.  The following error is reported in the Diagnostic Viewer:  Simulink Design Verifier failed to initialize: Block Replacement failed

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation and code generation result for Truth Table block that directly connects to a conditionally executed subsystem Outport block
Description: The initial value of the Truth Table block output is overwritten by the Outport block when all these conditions apply: The model contains a conditionally executed subsystem, such as an enabled or triggered subsystem. The subsystem contains a Truth Table block. An output of the Truth Table block directly connects to an Outport block of the subsystem. The parameter Initial value ?of the Truth Table block output port has a different value than the parameter Initial output ?of the Outport block.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the Truth Table block and the Outport block.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: Full-duplex audio can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Full-duplex audio can crash MATLAB if audioDeviceReader  is set up before audioDeviceWriter , or when an overrun or underrun occurs, in particular with devices that have more than four channels. This bug affects audioPlayerRecorder , or the simultaneous use of audioDeviceWriter  and audioDeviceReader  with the same device (with Driver  set to 'ASIO'  if using Windows).
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Simulink.Parameter with expression value has a different behavior than Simulink.Parameter with numeric value in mask initialization
Description: When you use aSimulink.Parameter  object as the value of a mask parameter, if the value of the parameter object is an expression, the behavior of the initialization code is different than if the value of the parameter object is a numeric value. When the value of the parameter object is an expression the parameter is not restored as a Simulink.Parameter  object after exiting the initialization code.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: MATLAB Job Scheduler workers might get stuck in the 'busy' state after rerunning a communicating job
Description: Workers of a MATLAB Job Scheduler cluster might get stuck in the 'busy ' state when jobs created with createCommunicatingJob() or batch(...,'Pool',N)    fail and are automatically rerun. Consequently, the workers are unable to run jobs and tasks again.  The likelihood of a worker getting stuck increases with the number of workers that the job uses.
Workaround: Restart workers stuck in the 'busy' state with the stopworker and startworker scripts.To prevent workers from becoming stuck, disable the automatic rerun for communicating jobs. Use one of the following:For jobs created with createCommunicatingJob(), set the MaximumRetries property on the first task of the communicating job to 0 before submitting the job using the following syntax.? ? >> task = createTask(...)            ? ? >> task.MaximumRetries = 0Set the MaximumRetries property of your cluster profile to 0 in the Cluster Profile Manager.  This option works for all communicating jobs created with the cluster profile, including jobs created with batch(...,'Pool',N), but also disables the automatic rerun for independent jobs.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b

Summary: Generated model does not reflect optimizations for masked Library or Subsystem.
Description: When you enable optimizations for your design that contains masked Library or masked Subsystem and generate a model. The generated model is incorrect as it does not reflect optimizations for? Masked Library or Masked Subsystem. This?may result in numerical mismatch. However, thegenerated HDL code is correct.
Workaround: Consider disabling optimizations or if optimizations are required, consider disabling?Model Generation in the?Model Configuration Parameters > Global Settings > Model Generation > Generated Model.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022b Update 5, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Nonvirtual bus element at Outport block accessed by State Writer block might cause incorrect SIL mode simulation results
Description: SIL mode simulation produces incorrect results when all these conditions apply: A State Writer block?sets the state of an Outport block in a conditionally executed subsystem. The State Writer block is in an Initialize Function block. The Outport block receives a signal from a Mux block. The Mux block receives a signal selected from a nonvirtual bus.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Variant?Bus Element Out without signal names may generate incorrect code when output is driven from a subsystem
Description: A Simulink model containing conditional?Bus Element Out ? may generate incorrect code when the below conditions are true: The variant condition on the Bus Element Out  is propagated through a Variant Source  or Variant Sink . The Bus Element out  does not have a signal name assigned. The model contains Bus Creator  and Bus Selector  blocks for routing the conditional bus signals. The output is driven from an atomic subsystem or a virtual subsystem with Treat as grouped when propagating variant conditions  parameter is turned on  In the generated code, you can notice the output (e.g.:?rtb_VM_Conditional_Signal_Out1_BusCreator_0 ) is incorrectly reset to zero.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

Summary: 4-Way 3-Position Valve (IL) block pre-parameterization option might cause unexpected behavior
Description: The 4-Way 3-Position Valve (IL) block pre-parameterization option Bosch 5-.WE J19  lacks the minimum information to pre-parameterize the block. The option can cause unexpected behavior of the block.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The model parameter Allow clock-rate pipelining of DUT output ports can result in inconsistent I/O between the original model and HDL code
Description: When using the model parameter?ClockRatePipelineOutputPorts , the ordering of the outputs in the generated model and HDL code may not be the same as what was modeled originally. If these outputs have differing amounts of data rate latency, the validation model can show a mismatch.  Additionally, if input signals inside the DUT are left unconnected or drive unconnected logic that is considered dead and removed by optimizations, the signals are missing from the generated model and HDL code.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: heatMapSFGalleryItem warning thrown for Stateflow charts calling functions containing protected models
Description: If you have a Stateflow chart containing a Simulink function which in turn contains a protected Model block, Simulink throws the following warning when you update or run the model: Warning: An error has occurred during refresh of Action named heatMapSFGalleryItem.  Warning: Unable to access model name for Model block 'topmodel/ Chart/ wrapper/ Model' because it references a protected model ('refmodel. slxp'). Use parameter 'ModelFile' to get the file name of the protected model.

Summary: False enumerated data type mismatch error between C and Simulink
Description: When you simulate or generate code for a model containing a C Caller block or a C Function block, Simulink throws a false enumerated data type mismatch error between C and Simulink enum  types when both of the following conditions are true:  C enum  type has type name as prefix for all enum  names. Simulink enum  type sets addClassNameToEnumnames  to true.  You see an error message similar to the one below:  Value of enumeration elements specified for argument 'u1' for C Caller block?'C Caller1' does not match the argument's type in the custom code.?Check the enumeration definition for 'myEnum' in Configuration Parameters > Simulation Target pane.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator does not report an error when a non-inlined S-function accesses a global parameter with storage class
Description: The code generator does not report an error when a model that contains a non-inlined S-function accesses a parameter object in the base workspace or a data dictionary. This results in generated code that might behave incorrectly. This incorrect code generation can occur when any of the following conditions are true:The parameter object has a storage class other than Auto  or Model Default  The parameter object has storage class Auto  or Model default  and the default mapping for External parameters   is a value other than Default  The default mapping for Model parameters  is a value other than Default
Workaround: If a non-inlined S-function references a parameter object in the base workspace or a data dictionary, check that all of the following conditions are satisfied:The parameter object has storage class Auto or Model DefaultThe default mapping for External parameters is DefaultThe default mapping for Model parameters is Default
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 4, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated when export-function model has access functions and root level Model block with muxed input signals
Description: Incorrect code would be generated when all of following conditions are met: Inside an export-function model, data root Inport blocks are configured to have access functions. At least one of those data root Inport blocks has its output signal muxed with other signals to drive a Model block in the root level of the export-function model.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for?a model containing a?Unit Delay block inside a?For Each Subsystem block
Description: The generated code contains an incorrect initialization of a Unit Delay block state for a model that meets all of these conditions: The Unit Delay block is inside a For Each subsystem. The Initial Condition  of the Unit Delay block initialization is specified by a parameter instead of a fixed value. In the For Each Subsystem block parameters dialog box, the?Parameter Partition ?is selected .   There is a bug with the same symptoms introduced in release R2018b. See 2047809 for more information.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Using?visdiff with models containing internally saved test harnesses might error if Simulink Test license not available
Description: Using?visdiff  with models containing internally saved test harnesses might error if a Simulink Test license is not available. For example, comparing the f14 and f14_2 models using visdiff('f14','f14_2')   might produce this error:? Unable to check out a Simulink Test license needed to use this feature.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might produce incorrect results if model contains For Each subsystem that accepts a nonscalar signal as input
Description: The generated code might be incorrect if your model satisfies?all ?these conditions: The model contains a For Each subsystem. The For Each subsystem accepts an input that is a nonscalar signal In the subsystem, at least one nonscalar signal connects to multiple output ports.  This is an example of generated incorrect code: void mRepro_ss_7(const real_T rtu_input[10], real_T rty_Out3[10]){? real_T tmp;? int32_T ForEach_itr;? int32_T i;? int32_T i_0;? int32_T tmp_0;? memcpy(&forEachInput[0], &rtu_input[0], 10U * sizeof(real_T));? memcpy(&shared_output2[0], &rty_Out3[0], 10U * sizeof(real_T));? for (i = 0; i < 2; i++) {??? for (i_0 = 0; i_0 < 5; i_0++) {????? forEachInput[i + (i_0 << 1)] = rtu_input[(i_0 << 1) + i];??? }??? for (ForEach_itr = 0; ForEach_itr < 2; ForEach_itr++) {????? tmp =? mRepro_no_2_19a_ConstP.ForEachSS_rtw_collapsed_sub_expr_0[ForEach_itr];????? for (i_0 = 0; i_0 < 5; i_0++) {??????? tmp_0 = (i_0 << 1) + ForEach_itr;??????? rty_Out3[tmp_0] = forEachInput[tmp_0] * tmp;????? }??? }? } }  In this code, the?memcpy ?operation for copying data from?shared_output ?to output port is executed before the?ForEach_itr ?loop. Here the?memcpy ?operation for copying data from?shared_output ?to output port is executed after the?ForEach_itr ?loop. void mRepro_ss_7(const real_T rtu_input[10], real_T rty_Out3[10]) {  real_T tmp;  int32_T ForEach_itr;  int32_T i;  int32_T s1_iter;  int32_T tmp_0;  memcpy( & forEachInput[0], & rtu_input[0], 10 U * sizeof(real_T));  for (s1_iter = 0; s1_iter < 2; s1_iter++) {    for (i = 0; i < 5; i++) {      forEachInput[s1_iter + (i << 1)] = rtu_input[(i << 1) + s1_iter];    }    for (ForEach_itr = 0; ForEach_itr < 2; ForEach_itr++) {      tmp = mRepro_no_2_23a_ConstP.ForEachSS_rtw_collapsed_sub_expr_4847605804183166791_1[ForEach_itr];      for (i = 0; i < 5; i++) {        tmp_0 = (i << 1) + ForEach_itr;        rty_Out3[tmp_0] = forEachInput[tmp_0] * tmp;      }    }    memcpy( & shared_output2[0], & rty_Out3[0], 10 U * sizeof(real_T));  }}

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Models containing Stateflow Charts that have conditionally executed?while?or?do-while?constructs might result in incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code might occur for a model that meets the following conditions:The model contains a Stateflow Chart and a While Iterator block. The?Function packaging ?parameter of the Chart is set to Auto  or Inline.  The Chart also contains a conditionally executed while  or do-while  construct.  In the generated code, a part of the?while ?or do-while  loop is incorrectly hoisted.? For example, the correct code contains this while  loop that contains a do-while  loop while() {?  ? ? a =? inMatrix[1];  ? ? do {?  ? ? ? ? a++;?  ? ? ? ? // do something else  ? ? } while();  ? ? for(i=...) {?  ? ? ? ? outMatrix[i] += a? ? ?  ? ? }? }? In the incor rect code, the do-while  loop is incorrectly hoisted, and the outer while  loop becomes: do {  ? ? // do something else?  ?} while();?  while() {?  ? ? temp = inMatrix[1];?  ? ? for(i=...) {?  ? ? ? ? outMatrix[i] += temp ?} }?
Workaround: Set the?Function packaging of the Stateflow chart to Reusable function or Nonreusable function.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for?eig might incorrectly return NaN values
Description: The eig  function uses an iterative algorithm to solve for eigenvalues and eigenvectors, and this algorithm might fail to converge in certain situations. In such situations, the function returns NaN ?values , and targets that support warnings also produce a warning. This issue is a possibility in both  MATLAB and the generated code. The implementation of the eig  function in the generated code is based on an older version of LAPACK and?might return NaN ?values when the output in MATLAB is finite. This issue can occur if both  these conditions are true:? ?You generate a static library, a dynamic library, or an executable by using MATLAB Coder or Simulink Coder ?You do not instruct the code generator to include calls to an external LAPACK library  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b

The code signature error cited above has been resolved as of July 1, 2022. Please contact?technical support ?if you still encounter this error.??

Summary: Code generation error when code interface packaging is C++ Class and system target file is custom derived from ert.tlc
Description: C++ code generation might produce an error for a model with the configuration parameter CodeInterfacePackaging ?set to C++ Class? when the model uses a custom target file derived from ert.tlc. The error assertion might be similar to either of the following: Code generation assertion 'fpcArgSpecType != FPC_Value' failed in..  STD exception 'std::logic_error': 'basic_string::_M_construct null not valid' was caught.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect rapid accelerator mode simulation results for models using root-level bus element ports and function-call input ports
Description: Simulating a model results in incorrect answers when all ?of these conditions apply: The model simulates in rapid accelerator mode. The model has at least one In Bus Element or Out Bus Element block at the root level. The model has at least one Inport block at the root level that outputs a function-call signal and has a sample time of -1 . The ExternalInput  configuration parameter specifies time steps when the function-call input will run.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Export-function model with multiple function-call root Inport blocks driving a function-call subsystem with discontiguous output might result in incorrect code
Description: The signal memory for the root Outport block in the generated code might not be written to when all these conditions apply:? The model that contains the root Outport block is an export-function model. The model contains two root Inport blocks that output function-call signals. The root Inport blocks connect to the same Mux block. The output of the Mux block drives a function-call subsystem. The function-call subsystem drives a root Outport block. The signal that drives the corresponding Outport block in the function-call subsystem is discontiguous. A discontiguous signal can result from Mux, Selector, or Bus Selector blocks.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated when a parameter or signal is an array of buses and specifies preservation of dimensions

Description: Accelerator mode simulation generates incorrect result for an export function model if all the following conditions are met: The model contains a Simulink function at the root level. The Simulink function is called from another export function within the model. The Simulink function contains a nested function-call subsystem.

Summary: Code generation for MATLAB code containing str2double function might fail with an error
Description: Code generation from MATLAB might fail if both  these conditions are true:  Your MATLAB code passes a character array to the?str2double  function?  The code configuration property Dynamic Memory Allocation for Variable-Size Arrays ('DynamicMemoryAllocation') is disabled   For example, consider this MATLAB function:? function out = foo(inData)idx = zeros(2,2);j = 1;idx(2,2) = 2;for i = 1:2    idx(j, 1) = i - 1;    j = j + 1;endc = char(inData(idx(2,1):idx(2,2)));out = real(str2double(c));end Suppose that you attempt to generate code for foo  by running these commands:? cfg = coder.config('lib');cfg.DynamicMemoryAllocation = 'Off';codegen -config cfg foo -args {[49 48]} -report In such situations, MATLAB displays the following error message: Computed maximum size is not bounded.  Static memory allocation requires all sizes to be bounded.  The computed size is [1 x :?].  Please consider enabling dynamic memory allocation to allow unbounded sizes.  Error in ==> str2double  When using Simulink Coder, this issue can affect code generation for a Simulink model that satisfies all? these conditions: The model contains a MATLAB Function block The code in the MATLAB Function block passes a character array to the?str2double ?function  Dynamic memory allocation in MATLAB functions disabled

Summary: Analysis functions of dsp.Channelizer object return incorrect filters for non-maximally decimated cases
Description: For the dsp.Channelizer object, when DecimationFactor is less than NumFrequencyBands (non-maximally decimated channelizer), the getFilters, fvtool, and freqz object functions depict incorrect filters. To obtain the correct channelizer filter, the prototype filter is supposed to be shifted by 2¦Đ?M, where M is the number of frequency bands. In the case of non-maximally decimated channelizer, the getFilters, fvtool, and freqz object functions shift the prototype filter by 2¦Đ?D to calculate the filters, where D is the decimation factor.  Note that the Channelizer object uses the correct set of filters in the implementation in the step function. The issue occurs only when you use the getFilters, fvtool, and the freqz object functions.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink fails to report inconsistent initial values between Outport block and signal object specified inside referenced model
Description: Simulink fails to report inconsistent initial values between an Outport block and a signal object specified inside a referenced model, and instead simulates the model. When this issue occurs, the simulation and code generation results might not be the same, depending on which initial value is applied last.?? This issue might occur if the model has these conditions: The model contains a conditionally executed subsystem. The conditionally executed subsystem has an Outport block with an explicit value specified for the Initial output  parameter, that is, not an empty matrix ([] ). The Outport block is driven by an output port of a Model block. Inside the referenced model, the signal driving the root Outport block corresponding to the output port of the Model block has a Simulink.Signal  object associated with it. The InitialValue ?field? of the signal object is not an empty string and the initial value is not the same as the initial output value specified for the Outport block in the top model.
Workaround: When a Simulink.Signal object with an explicit initial value is applied to a signal, make sure that the destination, conditionally executed subsystem Outport block uses an empty matrix ([]) as the initial value. Also, apply this workaround when the signal in question crosses a model reference boundary.

Summary: Unexpected effect of rotational velocity on pressure and force in the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block
Description: Increasing rotational velocity decreases rather than increases pressure and interface force magnitude in the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block.
Workaround: This workaround is equivalent to the expected behavior of the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block. Replace the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block with these three blocks:Single-Acting Actuator (IL)Rotating Channel (IL)Rotating Cylinder Force (IL)Make the following connections. These connections match the attached picture:Rotating Channel (IL) port A to previous connections of the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port A.Rotating Channel (IL) port B, Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port A, and Rotating Cylinder Force (IL) port X.Rotating Cylinder Force (IL) port C, Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port R, and previous connections of the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port R.Rotating Cylinder Force (IL) port R, Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port C, and previous connections of the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port C.Rotating Channel (IL) port W and Rotating Cylinder Force (IL) port W to previous connections of the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port W.Make the following parameter updates:Parameters in the Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block should correspond to parameters on the Actuator, Hard Stop, and Initial Conditions tabs of your original Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block. The value of the?Piston cross-sectional area?parameter in the Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block equals the value of the annular area in-between the?Piston outer radius?and?Piston inner radius?parameters in the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block.Parameters in the Rotating Channel (IL) block should match parameters on the Fluid Channel tab of your original Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block. These parameters in the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block map to corresponding parameters in the Rotating Channel (IL)?with a different name:Fluid entry radius maps to Port B radius of rotation.Fluid channel cross-sectional area maps to Orifice area.The Rotating Cylinder Force (IL)?block parameter Mechanical orientation?should have the opposite setting of the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL)?block. All remaining parameters in the Rotating Cylinder Force (IL) block map directly from parameters with the same names in the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: MATLAB may crash if the Spatial Contact Force block is used in a simulation that is over constrained.
Description: If the?Spatial Contact Force block is used to model contact between a File Solid geometry and another geometry type, MATLAB may crash when running the simulation. The issue may arise if the simulation is over constrained or mechanically invalid.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: IP core register initial value is incorrect for fixed-point values with non-zero fraction length
Description: When mapping a Simulink input port to a register in the IP core workflow, you can optionally specify an initial value for the register. If the mapped Simulink port has a fixed-point data type with non-zero fraction length, then the register initial value specified is incorrect in the generated code.
Workaround: You can cast the Simulink port data type to be a fixed-point data type with zero fraction length at the DUT boundary (using a Data Type Conversion block in Stored Integer mode). You can then convert it back into the desired fixed-point data type inside of the DUT (using a Data Type Conversion block in Stored Integer mode). When setting the register initial value, set the value using the stored integer representation of the desired initial value.For example, if the original Simulink port had data type fixdt(0, 16, 15)?and the desired initial value is 0.5, cast the port data type to uint16 at the DUT boundary and set the initial value as?storedInteger(fi(0.5, 0, 16, 15)).
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Stateflow chart hard codes some size information of non-scalar symbolic dimension
Description: When generating code for a 2-D matrix that is defined by symbolic dimensions,  the resulting code inserts a numeric offset in the matrix assignment based on the current value of one dimension of the matrix.?This causes an issue if you specify a different dimension post code generation since the numeric offset will not match the matrix dimension.

Summary: Unable to load saved model workspace parameter value during model load/open operation
Description: Unable to load saved parameter values from model workspace during model load/open operation for?Narrowband Transmit Receive Array  or Monopulse Feed  Blocks (from Phased Array System Toolbox ). The issue is specific to values coming from the model workspace and in this case the block is loaded with the default settings coming from the library?
Workaround: Right click on the model and choose Model PropertiesClick the Callbacks tab and modify PostLoadFcn and CloseFcn as given below:In PostLoadFcn, add the model work space variables into the base work space:? ? ? ? hws = get_param(gcs,'modelworkspace')? ? ? ? a = getVariable(hws,'a')? ? ? ? e = getVariable(hws,'e')? ? ? ? s = getVariable(hws,'s')In CloseFcn, clean up the base work space.

Summary: Exporting models with mixed datatypes in bus outputs as tool-coupling FMUs might produce incorrect simulation results
Description: When a model is exported as a tool-coupling FMU, the simulation results from the FMU might be incorrect if the following conditions are true: The model has a bus or nested bus output. The bus or nested bus has elements or sub-bus with mixed datatypes (boolean, double, int...).
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Root-level In Bus Element block with top-level virtual bus and nested nonvirtual bus might produce incorrect code
Description: A Simulink model might generate incorrect code when all the following conditions are true: A root-level In Bus Element block selects a nonvirtual bus from a virtual bus. A Bus Selector block selects a multidimensional leaf signal from the nonvirtual bus. A Selector block selects an element of the multidimensional leaf signal. The selected element of the multidimensional leaf signal passes to an atomic subsystem.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block after the Bus Selector block that selects the multidimensional leaf signal. Then, double-click the Signal Conversion block and select the?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization parameter.

Summary: SQO report generation fails when report generator version is older than Polyspace Access version
Description: If you generate an SQO report from Polyspace Access results, and if the version of the Polyspace product that you use to generate the report is older than the Polyspace Access version, the report generation fails. You see an error message similar to this:? Polyspace Report GeneratorLoading resultsError: internal - unable to find check definition for: Defect_AUTOSAR_LIB_NON_COMPLIANCEError: internal - unable to find check definition for: Defect_INCORRECT_VALUE_FORWARDINGError: internal - unable to find check definition for: Defect_NOEXCEPT_FUNCTION_THROWS...?  The issue occurs when the Polyspace Access SQO definition that you use to generate the report contains defect or coding rule checkers that are not available? in the older Polyspace product version.
Workaround: To work around this issue:Log into PolyspaceAccess.Create a custom SQO definition where you select only defect andcoding rule checkers that are available in the older Polyspace product version.Apply the custom SQO definition to the project for which you want to create an SQO report.?Even if you are able generate an SQO report with the original SQO definition, use the custom SQO definition instead to avoid seeing defect and coding rule checkers that are not available in the version of the product used to generate the report.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b

Summary: Generating a coverage report for a harness model containing a truth table block might cause an error
Description: MATLAB errors if you perform all the following steps in order: You simulate a harness model that contains a truth table block with coverage enabled You apply a coverage filter that filters out the truth table block? You close the harness model You generate a coverage report  At the final step, MATLAB shows an error that looks like: Error using cvi.TopModelCov.setUpFiltering (line 174) IDENTIFIER: 'ObjectParamContainsInvalidHandle' input must be a valid MATLAB message identifier. For more information, search for "message identifier" in the MATLAB Help.    Alternatively, if you run your tests using Test Manager in Simulink Test: You open the model under test. You run a test in Test Manager which simulates the harness that contains a truth table block and has a coverage filter applied on the truth table block. You generate a coverage report.  MATLAB shows the same error. In this case, if the harness model is not open when you initiate the test, Test Manager closes the harness model automatically when the test is complete.
Workaround: Do not close the harness model until after you generate the coverage report. If you are using Test Manager in Simulink Test, open the harness model before running tests so that Simulink Test does not close the harness upon test completion.

Summary: MATLAB might crash for models with multiple callsites of the same function or system object analyzed by Simulink Design Verifier
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might cause MATLAB to crash in rare instances when running Simulink Design Verifier with  Test Generation or Design Error Detection being run with MCDC objective enabled.  Multiple calls to the same function or multiple instances of the same system object in the model.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect simulation results for compartments when unit conversion is enabled
Description: SimBiology might return incorrect simulation results for compartments when all of the following conditions are met: Unit conversion is enabled. The model contains multiple compartments with different units. The model contains nested compartments.  In such cases, SimBiology might use the wrong unit multiplier to convert a compartment capacity from internal units to user-specified units. Internally, the simulation proceeds with the correct units, and the reported simulation results are correct for species and parameters.
Workaround: Work around the problem by doing any of the following:Disable unit conversion.Ensure all compartments have the same units.Convert all nested compartments to top-level compartments.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Signal label mismatch during test harness compliation
Description: If you have a test harness for a subsystem whose output signal is explicitly named, and signal label propagation is enabled, this error might occur during compilation:Signal label mismatch: <harness_name>/Subsystem output port 1 label <signal_name>, should match <harness_name>/Output Conversion Subsystem output port 1 label <signal_name><propagated_signal_name>.
Workaround: 1) Manually set Show propagated signals?to ON?for the?signals that are outputs of the signal conversion subsystem.2)? Set Signal label mismatch?to none in the?Diagnostics?> Connectivity?category of Model Configuration Parameters.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Concatenating empty variable-sized signals might produce incorrect results
Description: Incorrect code generation and rapid accelerator simulation might occur when Vector Concatenate blocks accept empty variable-sized signals as inputs.See the attached model for an example. The model contains a Vector Concatenate block which accepts a variable-sized signal from an Enabled Subsystem as an input. The block concatenates the variable-sized signal with another signal. When the Enabled subsystem is disabled, it passes an empty output, and as a result the Concatenate block just passes through the other signal. The incorrect generated C code from the example model: ? repro_DW.concatenateBlk_DIMS1 = repro_DW.Enabled_SS._DIMS1[0] + 1;  ? loop_ub = repro_DW.Enabled_SS._DIMS1[0] * repro_DW.Enabled_SS._DIMS1[1] - 1;  ? for (i = 0; i <= loop_ub; i++) { // -------------------> When the output of Enabled Subsystem is empty, this loop is never executed  ? ? tmp_data = repro_B.Enabled_SS.u; // -----------------> This is trying to copy output of Enabled Subsystem to tmp_data  ? }  ? repro_B.concatenateBlk[0] = tmp_data; // --------------> tmp_data is still copied to output of Concatenate block  ? repro_B.concatenateBlk[1] = repro_P.Constant3_Value;  The generated code treats the empty signal as non-empty and incorrectly copies the signal to the output of the Concatenate block.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for?a model containing a Merge block and a Unit Delay block or signals specified to have a Reusable custom storage class
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets either of the following scenarios: Scenario 1: The model contains a Merge block and a Unit Delay block. The outputs of a set of conditionally executed subsystems are feeding into a Merge block. The output of the Merge block is connected to a Unit Delay block and then fed back to the input(s) of any of the conditionally executed subsystems. The input(s) of the conditionally executed subsystems is/are used in a computation generating an output different from the one connected to the Merge block. The execution of that output happens after the execution of the output connected to the Merge block.  Scenario 2: The model contains a Merge block. The outputs of a set of conditionally executed subsystem are feeding into a Merge block. The output of the Merge block is specified to have a Reusable custom storage class. The input of any of these subsystems is specified to have a Reusable custom storage class with the same variable name as the output of the Merge block. The input(s) of the conditionally executed subsystems is/are used in a computation generating an output different from the one connected to the Merge block. The execution of that output happens after the execution of the output connected to the Merge block.?
Workaround: Connect a Signal Conversion block to the output of the Merge block. In the Signal Conversion block parameters dialog box, select the Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization parameter.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier may incorrectly show?Incompatible for models with arrays of complex numbers as output or input to function calls
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may incorrectly show?Incompatible  for models that meet the following conditions:The model contains arrays of complex numbers? The complex array is either the input or the output of a function call The complex array is not a simple variable?  For example, the following pattern may cause the model to be incorrectly shown as incompatible:? obj.complexArray = functionCall(obj.complexArray);  However, the following pattern will not be shown as incompatible:? complexArray = functionCall(complexArray);
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a

Summary: Unexpected error may occur during HDL Code Generation for a large model that uses the NCO HDL Optimized block
Description: HDL code generation may error out for a model that uses the NCO HDL Optimized block with frame-based output. This error happens for bigger models like the Wireless HDL Toolbox reference application models. Before R2022a, this block was named NCO HDL Optimized, and was included in the DSP System Toolbox HDL Support library. Starting in R2022a, the block is named NCO and is in the DSP HDL Toolbox library. The error message is:? Error using hdlcoder.pirctx/addZeroProtection  Unexpected Error during HDL Code Generation. Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MinMax block might produce incorrect results in accelerator and rapid accelerator modes with NaN input and zero-crossing detection
Description: The MinMax block produces incorrect results in accelerator and rapid accelerator modes when the following conditions are true: Model solver is variable-step. MinMax block Enable zero crossing detection  is selected. MinMax block input has continuous sample time. Input to MinMax block has double data type. One of these is true: MinMax block has only one input and the first element of the input is NaN. For example, input [NaN 1] can output NaN, which is an incorrect result. MinMax block has multiple inputs and the first input has a NaN. For example, for a MinMax block with two inputs, the first input has an input of NaN and the second input has a 1, the resulting incorrect result is a NaN.

Summary: Incorrect Unsatisfiable objectives for MCDC objectives where the overall condition is always false
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might report incorrect Unsatisfiable (or Dead Logic)  objectives for MCDC objectives when the following conditions are met: The MCDC objective is part of a MATLAB System or MATLAB Function block. The overall expression for a || or && operation is always false.?  When the overall expression is?A && B , when this issue is experienced, all MCDC objectives are reported as Unsatisfiable : A && B with A true  A && B with A false  A && B with B true  A && B with B false?  However,  if A can be false, "A && B with A false " should be reported as Satisfiable?  if B can be false (when A can be true), "A && B with B false " should be reported as Satisfiable    On the other hand, when the overall expression is?A || B , when this issue is experienced, all MCDC objectives are reported as?Unsatisfiable : A || B with A true  A?||?B with A false  A?||?B with B true  A?||?B with B false?  However, "A || B with A false " should be reported as?Satisfiable?  "A || B with B false " should be reported as?Satisfiable

Summary: C++ and AUTOSAR code generation may not reflect Shared utilities identifier format setting

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The initial condition of an output port block may be ignored if the output port block is inside an inactive variant subsystem block.
Description: An output port block inside an inactive variant subsystem may be removed according to the configuration of the variant subsystem block. This may lead to the loss of initial condition and cause answer mismatch while simulating in normal or rapid accelerator modes as the behavior when the output port block is removed in these two modes could be different according to the setting.?

Summary: HDL IP core generation using Xilinx Vivado fails as of January 1, 2022
Description: Since January 1, 2022, IP core generation fails when the synthesis tool is set to Xilinx Vivado. The following error message can be observed in the HDL Workflow Advisor Task 3.2:? Failed Task "Vivado IP Packager" unsuccessful. See log for details. Generated logfile: hdl_prj\hdlsrc\<myModel> \workflow_task_VivadoIPPackager.log  The log file at \hdl_prj\hdlsrc\<myModel>\workflow_task_VivadoIPPackager.log  reports an error message similar to the following: bad lexical cast: source type value could not be interpreted as target  ? ?while executing"rdi::set_property core_revision 2201031458 {component component_1}"  ? ?invoked from within  "set_property core_revision 2201031458 [ipx::current_core]"  ? ?(file "vivado_ip_package.tcl" line 57)
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log into an account with the appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.Locate the following file under your <MATLAB> folder and rename the file to "IPEmitterVivado_backup.p":<MATLAB>\toolbox\hdlcoder\hdlcommon\+hdlturnkey\+ip\IPEmitterVivado.pWindows Systems:?You might be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when renaming this file.  Download and unzip the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report.In the unzipped folder, navigate to the subfolder that matches the release you are using. For example, if you are using the R2021b release, open the R2021b subfolder.If you do not see a subfolder that matches the release you are using,?contact MathWorks Technical Support.In this folder, there is a newer version of the?IPEmitterVivado.p file. Copy this file and paste it in the location mentioned above.Windows Systems:?You might be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when pasting this file.Restart MATLAB.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Error occurs when closing an App Designer app while execution is paused due to a call to uiwait in the startupFcn for the app
Description: The below error occurs when closing an app that has the HandleVisiblity  property of the figure set to 'callback' ?when execution is paused due to a call to uiwait  in the app's startupFcn . This is likely to be the case for multiwindow apps where the primary app launches a secondary app that calls?uiwait  in its?startupFcn .Warning: The following error was caught while executing 'onCleanup' class destructor:  Error using matlab. ui. Figure/ setInvalid or deleted object.   Error in appdesigner. internal. service. AppManagementService>@()set(fig, 'HandleVisibility', 'callback') (line 150)?c = onCleanup(@()set(fig,  'HandleVisibility',  'callback'));  Error in onCleanup/ delete (line 80) obj. task();
Workaround: There are two workarounds:In the app with the call to?uiwait?in the?startupFcn, change the HandleVisiblity property of the figure to 'on'.?If it is necessary to keep the HandleVisiblity?of the figure?set to 'callback', instead, in the app with the call to uiwait in the startupFcn do the following:Add a CloseRequestFcn callback to the figure.In the function body of the CloseRequestFcn callback, replace the call to delete(app) with uiresume(app.UIFigure).In the primary app, when calling the secondary app, get a handle to the secondary app and delete the secondary app on the next line.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When generating HDL code using the "MATLAB Datapath" architecture of a?MATLAB function block, HDL Coder can produce incorrect code for logical indexing expressions used in size identification functions
Description: When generating HDL code using the "MATLAB Datapath" architecture of a?MATLAB function block, HDL Coder can produce incorrect code for logical indexing expressions. A logical indexing expression used inside of a size identification function can result in incorrect code generation. Some examples of size identification functions are?length ,?numel , and?size .  For example, this expression produces incorrect generated code: numel(x(x>10))

Summary: Generated HDL code for RS Decoder block includes a combinational loop
Description: While running HDL synthesis for the RS Decoder block, the synthesis tool returns combinational loop warnings. This warning results in incorrect timing analysis. For reliable behavior on hardware, this condition should be avoided in HDL designs.

Summary: Simulink Real-Time simulation may produce an error if Measure function execution times is selected
Description: Consider a model that contains an S-function with methods that use an empty code block. If you select the model configuration parameter?Measure function execution times ?and run a Simulink Real-Time simulation, the simulation may produce this error: Array indices must be positive integers or logic values.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Matrix gain (K*u) operations with scalar output might produce simulation mismatch between the original model and generated model
Description: HDL code generation for a Simulink model might result in a numeric mismatch between the original and generated model when all the following conditions are met: Operations in a Simulink model involve performing the matrix gain (K*u) operation and the result type is scalar. The gain factor K is a tunable parameter.
Workaround: Convert the matrix gain value K to a product component with the first input connected to a constant. Then set the constant block value to the gain factor K.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Inconsistent simulation and code generation results when input to log or log10 is negative

Summary: LPV System Block produces incorrect answer for LTI arrays with irregular grid and unsorted data in the SamplingGrid property.
Description: The LPV System block interpolates the model matrices and the offset data according to the chosen interpolation rule. However, when the LTI array corresponds to an irregular grid, the interpolation is always performed using the nearest neighbor approach. In this case, if the SamplingGrid  data is not monotonic for each variable, the interpolation may not be performed correctly. Example:?? sys = rss(2,1,1,1,3);  sys.SamplingGrid = struct('Alpha',(1:3)','Beta',[20 30 10]')
Workaround: Sort the systems in the LTI array based on their SamplingGrid field values before using it in the LPV System block.GridInfo =ltipack.SamplingGrid.getGridStructure(sys.SamplingGrid)sys = reshape(sys(:,:,GridInfo.SamplePerm),GridInfo.GridSize);?Reorder the offsets in states, state derivatives, inputs and outputs in a similar way (if present). For example, if the InputOffset is non-empty, reorder the data as follows:?InputOffset?= reshape(InputOffset(:,:,GridInfo.SamplePerm),GridInfo.GridSize);?

Summary: Security Issue: Default configuration of matlab.io.xml.dom.Parser?with document type definitions (DTDs) is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attack
Description: The XML markup parser class matlab.io.xml.dom.Parser  parses documents with document type definitions (DTDs) by default, which makes the parser vulnerable to an XML External Entity (XXE) attack if you try to parse a malicious document. A malicious DTD might cause the parser to disclose potentially sensitive information in files local to the machine running the parser or other internal content that the parser has access to, for example, via http  requests. The following is an example of a malicious DTD that attempts to disclose a password file: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>  <!DOCTYPE foo [  ? <!ELEMENT foo ANY >  ? <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd" >]>  <foo>&xxe;</foo>?   In some cases, the DTD might instigate a denial-of-service attack by accessing a local resource and causing it to not stop returning data.
Workaround: Configure the XML Parser to not parse documents with DTDs:p = matlab.io.xml.dom.Parser;?p.Configuration.DisallowDoctype = true;?or??p.Configuration.AllowDoctype = false;?Only parse documents with DTDs from trusted sources.?

Summary: Simulation of a Simulink model with multiple MATLAB Function blocks loading similar networks might produce incorrect answers
Description: Consider a Simulink model with multiple MATLAB Function blocks that load networks differing only in weight values. Simulation of such a model might produce an incorrect answer, if the networks share the same name. For example, suppose that?net1.mat  and net2.mat  contain networks that differ by weights only and are loaded in two different MATLAB Function blocks. The two blocks contain these two MATLAB functions respectively: MATLAB Function block 1?  function scores1 = fcn(u)persistent netCif isempty(netC)    netA = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net1.mat', 'network');endscores1 = predict(netA,u);end MATLAB Function block 2 ? function scores2 = fcn(u) persistent netDif isempty(netD)    netB = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net2.mat','network'); % network name is same as in first MATLAB function blockendscores2 = predict(netB,u);end  Simulating a model that contains both these MATLAB Function blocks might produce an incorrect answer.?
Workaround: Specify unique network names for unique networks.MATLAB Function block 1?function scores1 = fcn(u)persistent netCif isempty(netC)    netA = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net1.mat', 'network1');endscores1 = predict(netA,u);endMATLAB Function block 2?function scores2 = fcn(u) persistent netDif isempty(netD)    netB = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net2.mat','network2');% network name is different from first MATLAB function blockendscores2 = predict(netB,u);end Alternatively, do not provide any network name. MATLAB and Simulink will automatically create unique network names.?MATLAB Function block 1?function scores1 = fcn(u)persistent netCif isempty(netC)    netA = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net1.mat');endscores1 = predict(netA,u);endMATLAB Function block 2?function scores2 = fcn(u) persistent netDif isempty(netD)    netB = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net2.mat');endscores2 = predict(netB,u);end
