Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: In single clock mode, the timing controller can be incorrectly generated when requesting Oversampling and not utilizing Clock-Rate Pipelining.
Description: In rare instances, the timing controller generated by HDL Coder might not be appropriately clocking the design. The issue is evident during simulation of the HDL code because all or parts of the design have the clocking gated to 0. The issue occurs when the model has a base rate which is not used inside the DUT Subsystem. The model base rate, the fastest rate in the DUT, and oversampling factor all must have a certain relation in order for the timing controller to not be generated correctly. For example, if the model base rate is 1, the fastest rate in the DUT is 4, and the oversampling factor is 2, this issue can occur. The issue does not occur in most cases where an oversampling request creates an unused clock.
Workaround: To avoid the issue, choose one of these two workarounds:Alter the sample time of elements outside the DUT so that the DUT contains the fastest rate reported by the Simulink Timing Legend.Remove the oversampling request by setting Oversampling to 1, its default.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When doing horizontal concatenation followed by vertical concatenation
Description: MATLAB Coder might generate incorrect code if your MATLAB code satisfies?all ?of these conditions: Generates code using row major layout. Completely initializes a variable-size array p . Writes to a piece of p . Concatenates p  horizontally. Concatenates p  vertically.   Incorrect generated code uses wrong indices to access p .? For example consider the following MATLAB code: function p = foo()p = [1 1];coder.varsize('p',[2 3]);p(1,1) = 2;p = [p, 1];p = [p; ones(1,3)];end In the generated code, function foo  uses out-of-bounds indices to access p : void foo(const emlrtStack *sp, real_T p_data[], int32_T p_size[2]){   (void)sp;   p_data[1] = 1.0;   p_data[0] = 2.0;   p_size[1] = 3;   p_size[0] = 2;   p_data[3] = 1.0;   p_data[6] = 1.0;   p_data[7] = 1.0;   p_data[8] = 1.0;} Output p  has size 2x3 but foo  is writes to p  using indices 6, 7, and 8 at the end.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Unable to perform assignment because the size of the left side is 720-by-960? and the size of the right side is 960-by-720.?

Description: When using createInputDataset  on incorrectly configured models, the resulting error message is generic and uninformative.

Description: After the completion of Simulink Design Verifier analysis, the results will not have the expected output field if:There is an output port in the model which is a n-dimensional vector, and It is represented using either row matrix [1 n] or column matrix [n 1].

Summary: undefined reference compilation failure when building model reference hierarchy with MinGW
Description: Consider a top model that references a middle model that in turn references a bottom model. When you build the top model, a compilation failure might occur if all of these conditions apply:? The selected compiler is MinGW.? The build process for the middle model includes custom source code. The bottom model calls a function that is implemented in the custom source code included for the middle model.  You might observe a linker error. For example: undefined reference to my_custom_code_function  .

Summary: Using mixed signal and message ports in the same reference component in an architecture model might produce incorrect error messages
Description: Compiling an architecture model that contains reference components with mixed signal and message input or output ports might produce incorrect error messages.
Workaround: Instead of mixing signal and message ports in the same reference component, place all signal input or output ports in one reference component and message input or output ports in another reference component.

Summary: MATLAB can crash when calling getadjacencymatrix or when using the Model Debugger on a very large SimBiology model
Description: MATLAB can crash when calling?getadjacencymatrix  on a SimBiology model that contains hundreds of reactions using null as reactant or product. The?Model Debugger for SimBiology  also relies on getadjacencymatrix  and can crash when used with such a model.
Workaround: Replace null reactants and products with a dummy species. You can adapt the following code, which updates a SimBiology model stored in variable modelObj by replacing null with a dummy species named NULL, while ensuring the reaction rate remains the same.reactions = modelObj.Reactions;nullPattern = regexpPattern('\<null\>');for i = 1:numel(reactions)    reactionString = reactions(i).Reaction;    if contains(reactionString, nullPattern)        allSpecies = [reactions(i).Reactants; reactions(i).Products];        oneSpecies = allSpecies(1);        oneCompartment = oneSpecies.Parent;        nullSpecies = sbioselect(oneCompartment, 'Name', 'NULL');        if isempty(nullSpecies)            nullSpecies = oneCompartment.addspecies('NULL', 'Units', oneSpecies.Units);        end        nullSpeciesQualifiedName = nullSpecies.PartiallyQualifiedName;        reactionString = replace(reactionString, nullPattern, nullSpeciesQualifiedName);        rate = reactions(i).ReactionRate;        reactions(i).Reaction = reactionString;        reactions(i).ReactionRate = rate;    endend
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Variant Subsystem with model block as choice may cause missing?initialization?in the generated code
Description: Variant Subsystem with model block as choice may cause missing initialization when referenced models drive root outports with constant sample time.? This issue can be reproduced when all below conditions are met:Variant Subsystem with code-compile  activation time. Variant Subsystem has model block as choices. Inside the referenced models of model block, we have blocks with constant sample time (such as Constant  block) directly connected to outport.  ?
Workaround: Create a wrapper atomic subsystem on the model choice within the Variant Subsystem.Add a signal conversion block at the output of the model choice which is fed from constant rate. This ensures that the output of the wrapper subsystem is fed from the output of the signal conversion block.

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0231?displays an incorrect warning for hidden Simulink blocks inside Stateflow charts
Description: Model Advisor check?Check character usage in block names  ? (id:? ?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0231)? displays an incorrect warning for hidden Simulink blocks inside Stateflow charts.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results from C Function or C Caller blocks in For Each Subsystem blocks with masked parameters
Description: Simulink can produce incorrect simulation results when a C Function or C Caller block inside a For Each Subsystem block uses masked parameters. If the masked parameter is sliced by the For Each Subsystem block, the C Caller and C Function block will retrieve an incorrect slice of data during simulation. This bug does not affect code generation behavior.?

Summary: Network Analyzer does not count learnable parameters in dlnetwork learnables of custom layers
Description: When you use the analyzeNetwork function to analyze a network, it can report an incorrect number of learnable parameters for each layer. The value is incorrect if: The network contains custom layers. Those custom layers define learnable properties which are dlnetworks. Those dlnetwork learnable properties contain other learnable parameters.  Network Analyzer ignores the learnable properties of those nested dlnetworks, so does not include their parameters in the total learnables in the network. The Network Analyzer shows a value for the "Total learnables" of each layer which treats a dlnetwork learnable as a single-element learnable parameter, and fails to count any of the learnable parameters inside the dlnetwork it contains.?This issue does not occur with networks containing custom layers whose learnable parameters are numeric arrays.

Summary: Compatibility check fails for models containing Stateflow chart with grouped states and Data Store Memory access
Description: If a model consists of a Stateflow chart  with grouped states and if the chart is accessing Data Store Memory (DSM),  then Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails with following error message:Simulink Design Verifier failed to initialize: Checking compatibility failed  Cannot change transitions inside a grouped state or a grouped graphical function
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0643?does not detect fixed point data type in Simulink data object
Description: Model Advisor check?Check usage of fixed-point data type with non-zero bias?  (ID: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0643)  does not flag warning when fixed point data type with non-zero bias is used inside a Simulink data object.

Summary: HDF-EOS2 functions gd.ij2ll, gd.getPixels, and gd.readField return transposed values
Description: The?gd.ij2ll  and gd.readField ?functions return?lat  and lon  values that are the transpose of the corresponding values returned in R2020a and previous versions. Similarly, the?gd.getPixels  function?returns values of row  and col ?that are interchanged.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: IRVs in AUTOSAR exported function model might result in incorrect code when configured for expression folding
Description: Incorrect code can be generated for inter-runnable variables (IRVs) in an AUTOSAR exported function model if both of the following conditions exist: An AUTOSAR runnable writes to a Merge block to which an AUTOSAR IRV is connected, and reads from the same Merge block. That is, the Merge block is part of a self-loop read and write pattern. The model configuration option Eliminate superfluous local variables (expression folding) ?is selected.  For example, consider a runnable named Function_Foo  in which two inports read from IRV1 and IRV2, respectively, and IRV2 is connected to a Merge block. The runnable has two outports connecting to IRV2, which are the Merge block and Out1, respectively. If Eliminate superfluous local variables (expression folding) ?is cleared, correct code is generated: void Function_Foo(void) {  float64 IRV1;? float64 IRV2_p;? IRV1 = Rte_IrvIRead_Function_Foo_IRV1();? IRV2_p = Rte_IrvIRead_Function_Foo_IRV2();? Rte_IrvIWrite_Function_Foo_IRV2(IRV1);? Rte_IWrite_Function_Foo_Out1_Out1(IRV2_p);} If Eliminate superfluous local variables (expression folding) ?is selected, thelocal variables that set the values from IRV1 and IRV2 before the Merge blockare removed and so the generated code incorrectly folds the IRV2 read functionacross the IRV2 write function: void Function_Foo(void) {? Rte_IrvIWrite_Function_Foo_IRV2(Rte_IrvIRead_Function_Foo_IRV1());? Rte_IWrite_Function_Foo_Out1_Out1(Rte_IrvIRead_Function_Foo_IRV2());}
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, Code Generation > Optimization pane, clear the option?Eliminate superfluous local variables (expression folding).

Summary: JMAAB check for db_0140?does not report Constant block that do not display?nondefault block?parameter values
Description: Model Advisor?check?Check for nondefault block attributes? (id:?mathworks.maab.db_0140)? does not report warning for? Constant  block?that use nondefault block parameter values and are not displayed in the model diagram.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong answer for model with Outports using GetSet or AccessFunction storage class and a referenced model containing a Function-Call Subsystem block
Description: Code generation of a model might result in a silent wrong answer when all of these conditions are true: The model contains a Model block that is directly connected to an Outport block. The Outport block uses a GetSet  storage class or other storage class with Data Access  set to Function  and Access Mode  set to Value . The referenced model contains a Function-Call Subsystem block that is connected to the Outport block.  Code generation might also be incorrect if the output signals of the Model block in the parent model use a GetSet  storage class.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block in the parent model between the Model block and the Outports that are mapped to?GetSet or Function?storage class.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails for models having custom code location specified using relative paths
Description: In models where custom code location is provided using relative paths, Simulink Design Verifier extraction workflow fails during compatibility check in the following scenarios:Running analysis directly on the model using sldvcompat  or sldvrun  Extracting the model first using sldvextract  and then running the analysis on the extracted model

Summary: Signal Editor user interface stops responding after signal data type is changed to enum
Description: The Signal Editor user interface stops responding if an enumeration data type is entered in the data type field and you do not click the Enter  key before clicking elsewhere in the signal tree area.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model containing a Lookup Table block having symbolic variable with negative sign
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains a Lookup table block having a port with symbolic expression. The symbolic expression contains a symbolic variable with a negative sign (example [(-A<=B)+9] )  In the generated code, unwanted unsigned cast is inserted to the symbolic variable with a negative sign which might result in integer wrapping.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 4, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: edfwrite?does not create files when full path is provided or overwrite files on the MATLAB path
Description: The edfwrite  function does not create new files when the full path is provided or overwrite files that are on the MATLAB path. You can only create or overwrite files in the current directory. When you attempt to write the file anywhere other than the current directory, the function throws an error in the command window: Error using datestr (line 131)Not enough input arguments.Error in edfwrite/getFileProperties (line 1738)        obj.FileModDate =? string(datestr(fileInfo.datenum));Error in edfwrite (line 502)? ?     obj = getFileProperties(obj, fileInfo, true);
Workaround: To avoid this issue, you can either:Move to the relevant directory before using edfwrite and return to the current directory if needed.Work only in the current directory.

Summary: Navigation link from external document to MATLAB/Simulink does not work
Description: When you create a link from lines of MATLAB code in a MATLAB function block to an external document such as an IBM DOORS Module or a Microsoft Office document, navigating from the external document to the linked lines in MATLAB code by using the backlink in the external document might not work. You might see this error: file not found  or invalid link .

Summary: Signal Analyzer and Signal Labeler?show incorrect spectrogram plots for complex-valued signals longer than 1 million points
Description: The Signal Analyzer ?and Signal Labeler ?apps display incorrect spectrograms for complex-valued input signals when the signal length exceeds 1 million points.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Description: The?Sub-ID a? of Model Advisor?check?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0630 (jc_0630_a:?Check number of inputs to Multiport Switch block )?displays the following warning for?Multiport Switch block when parameter Data port order  is set to Specify indices  .? Warning :? The following Multiport Switch blocks have less than 2 data ports.  This warning is incorrect as number of data ports is atleast two as per guideline because the setting doesn't support one data port case.

Summary: Loading a MAT-file containing a MAXP object crashes MATLAB
Description: When you load a MAT-file that contains a MAXP object, MATLAB crashes. For example, the following script crashes MATLAB: d = matlab.io.xml.dom.Document('book');e = getDocumentElement(d);save;load('matlab.mat','e');

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Logging of subsubbus is incorrect if subbus is not the first field in the main bus
Description: Simulink incorrectly logs data for certain bus signals that are in a hierarchy.? For example, create a bus with the following structure: MainBus?  ? ? int a1;  ? ? SubBus subBus;  ? ? ? ? int a2;  ? ? ? ?SubSubBus subSubBus;  ? ? ? ? ? ? int a3;  then set this bus type to output data y  and initialize it as y.a1 = 1 ,  y.subBus.a2 = 2 ,  y.subBus.subSubBus.a3 = 3 . Simulink logs the value for a3  as 2 , instead of 3  as expected.
Workaround: The workaround is either not to use subsubbuses or make sure their offset is zero. For example, the bus in the previous example can be defined as following:MainBus??? ? SubBus subBus;? ? ? ?SubSubBus subSubBus;? ? ? ? ? ? int a3;?? ? ? ? int a2;? ? int a1;In this case the offset of SubSubBus in the main bus is zero.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: Simulink compilation slows down when a top model contains a Ground block or an Inport block using large nested buses
Description: Simulink model compilation slows down when the model contains a Ground block or an Inport block at the top level using large nested buses.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a

Summary: Physical Signal blocks in a loop might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Some combinations of Simscape Physical Signal blocks in a loop might cause MATLAB to crash during model compilation.
Workaround: Add a?PS Signal Specification block to the physical signal loop to prevent the crash.

Summary: Running analysis on subsystem harness model using? Simulink Design Verifier errors out and results in model incompatibility
Description: When an external subsystem harness is created using Simulink Test and the component under test contains Combinatorial Logic block  and Stateflow chart , Simulink Design Verifier errors with an error message Invalid Object Id  and deems the model incompatible.
Workaround: Convert the subsystem harness, and all other harnesses owned by the same harness owner model to internal, ORSet the Synchronization Mode of the harness to Synchronize on Push and Rebuild Only. The synchronization mode of a harness can be altered either by using the API sltest.harness.set or with the Harness Properties dialog.

Summary: Model protection might delete some variables from data dictionaries
Description: Creating a protected model might delete some variables in the data dictionaries that are used by the model when all of the following conditions are true: In the Create Protected Model dialog box, Contents ?is set to?Protected Model (.slxp) and dependencies in a project . An open data dictionary references the data dictionaries that are used by the model being protected.  The variables that are not used by the protected model are deleted from the data dictionaries.
Workaround: Close all the open data dictionaries before model protection.?
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might be incorrect for a model with Product of Elements block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a Product of Elements block with CollapseMode  set to All dimensions .? Output data type differs from the input data type for Product of Elements block.  The code generated results in loss of precision when the elements are multiplied.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 1, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Import Tool may incorrectly interpret numeric values in comma-separated files on systems with Decimal Separator set to comma?
Description: The Import Tool incorrectly interprets numerical values in text files when the following conditions are true: The system Decimal Separator is comma The delimeter used when importing the file is set to comma?
Workaround: In the Import Tool, go to "Delimiter Options", and change the Decimal Separator to period.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Simulink Test qualification test suites tBLT and tCustCritTol produce unexpected results
Description: The following Simulink Test qualification test cases might incorrectly fail due to an issue with the test case callbacks. In addition, the baseline results do not match the expected results. SLT_AST_066  in tBLT.mldatx  SLT_AST_010_AEQ  in tCustCritTol.mldatx  SLT_AST_030_AEQ  in tCustCritTol.mldatx  SLT_AST_130_AEQ  in tCustCritTol.mldatx
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: MATLAB might crash during code generation when a model contains a parameter with symbolic dimensions that has non-ASCII characters
Description: MATLAB might crash during code generation when a model contains a parameter with symbolic dimensions that has non-ASCII characters such as for example, '\n' .
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Metrics Dashboard and model metrics API use incorrect bin edges for metric distributions when the metric results are close in value
Description: If metric results are close in value, the Metrics Dashboard and model metrics API place the results into a single distribution bin, but use a bin edge that is less than the maximum result value. For example, consider a model that has 2 blocks at the top level and a subsystem that contains 8 blocks. When you collect the metric for calculating the number of blocks in the model, the metric result values are 2 top-level and 8 subsystem-level blocks, but when you get the metric distribution, the bin edges are 2 and 7.
Products: Simulink Coverage, Simulink Check, Requirements Toolbox

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect answer may be generated with models that have blocks with long parameter names
Description: If a block in a model has a long parameter name and there are either multiple instances of that model in the root model or there are multiple different referenced model blocks with that block, then generating the code for the root model may result in incorrect answers from the executable generated from the generated code. If there is not enough room within the identifier length (default 31 characters) for each parameter name to be fully mangled, the code generator generates instances with conflicting parameter names.
Workaround: Increase the default length of the identifier from 31 to a higher value.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: JMAAB check for na_0002:?sub-checks do not correspond to correct sub-ids
Description: Model Advisor?check?mathworks.jmaab.na_0002 (Check fundamental logical and numerical operations) ?sub-check  a  incorrectly corresponds to the sub-id  b  of the guideline and vice-versa.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when an existing model containing a MATLAB Function Block or Stateflow Chart is simulated in R2021a Update 4 or R2020b Update 6
Description: MATLAB might crash when you simulate a model after installing R2021a Update 4 or R2020b Update 6, if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains at least one MATLAB Function Block or Stateflow Chart When using R2021a Update 4, a simulation cache folder and/or Simulink cache file generated using R2021a or a previous R2021a Update exists on path When using R2020b Update 6, a simulation cache folder and/or Simulink cache file generated using R2020b or a previous R2020b Update exists on path The model is unchanged since it was previously run  See also??Bug Report 2553782 ?and?Bug Report 2563472
Workaround: Before simulating the model in MATLAB R2021a Update 4 or R2020b Update 6, delete the following files and foldersModel Simulation cache folder. This folder is commonly named 'slprj'Model Simulink Cache file. This file is named <modelName>.slxcThis only needs to be done once, to remove cache files and folders generated by a previous MATLAB version.

Summary: Stubbed constructs are incorrectly treated as returning the same value each time they are called in that time step
Description: When a stubbed function is read inside of a loop or inside of a function that is called multiple times per time step, the stubbed function returns the same value each time it is called for that time step.For example, if you had a MATLAB Function block with the following code: for i = 1:10  y(i) = rand();end the rand() ?incorrectly returns the same value for each iteration of the loop.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a

Description: Model Advisor?check Check usage of transition conditions in Stateflow transitions? (id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0772)? does not report warning for transitions with symbols  //   and  /* */   used in C action language chart .

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0009?does not report?subsystems that do not have propagated signal labels
Description: The?Sub-ID a? of Model Advisor?check?jc_0009_a:?Check Signal name propagation for subsystems?(id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0009)  does not?report warning for? subsystems that do not have propagated signal labels. This issue happens when the setting to display propagated signal labels for all signals (Diagnostics ->Signals ->Propagated Signal Labels)  is turned off  after enabling it once .

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0009?does not report subsystems with RTWSystemCode parameter set as Auto
Description: The?Sub-ID a? of Model Advisor?check jc_0009_a:?Check Signal name propagation for subsystems?(id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0009)? does not report warning for? subsystems with RTWSystemCode  parameter set as Auto  . This is incorrect as subsystems where reusable functions   are set only can be excluded as per the guideline.

Summary: parquetread returns the wrong datetime values when importing TIMESTAMP columns with associated time zones
Description: parquetread ?returns the wrong datetime ?values when importing columns whose physical type is either?TIMESTAMP_MILLIS ?or?TIMESTAMP_MICROS ?and has an associated time zone. The datetime  arrays returned by parquetread  are shifted by the UTC offset associated with the time zone. See the example below for more details. Create a datetime  array whose TimeZone  is set to "America/New_York" . >> NewYorkDatetimes = datetime(2021, 11, 3, 9, 0, 0, "TimeZone", "America/New_York") + hours(0:5)'; >> t = table(NewYorkDatetimes) t =  6x1 table     NewYorkDatetimes     ____________________    03-Nov-2021 09:00:00   03-Nov-2021 10:00:00   03-Nov-2021 11:00:00   03-Nov-2021 12:00:00   03-Nov-2021 13:00:00   03-Nov-2021 14:00:00>> parquetwrite("example.parquet", t); The variable NewYorkDatetimes  is shifted up by 4 hours, which is the UTC offset for America/New_York . >> t2 = parquetread("example.parquet")  t2 =  6ˇÁ1 table     NewYorkDatetimes     ____________________   03-Nov-2021 13:00:00   03-Nov-2021 14:00:00   03-Nov-2021 15:00:00   03-Nov-2021 16:00:00   03-Nov-2021 17:00:00   03-Nov-2021 18:00:00  The corresponding values in t2  and t  are all off by 4 hours.  >> t2.NewYorkDatetimes - t.NewYorkDatetimesans =   6ˇÁ1 duration array   04:00:00   04:00:00   04:00:00   04:00:00   04:00:00   04:00:00
Workaround: To correct the issue, you can use tzoffset to compute the duration values required to undo the UTC offset applied to the datetime values.>> t = parquetread("example.parquet");  >> offset = tzoffset(t.NewYorkDatetimes);>> t.NewYorkDatetimes = t.NewYorkDatetimes + offset  t =  6ˇÁ1 table      NewYorkDatetimes    ____________________     03-Nov-2021 09:00:00    03-Nov-2021 10:00:00    03-Nov-2021 11:00:00    03-Nov-2021 12:00:00    03-Nov-2021 13:00:00    03-Nov-2021 14:00:00

Summary: Using VISA-related functionality on Intel-based macOS Big Sur 11.4 or 11.5.2?might crash MATLAB
Description: MATLAB might crash on Intel-based macOS Big Sur 11.4 or 11.5.2 if the Instrument Control Toolbox Support Package for National Instruments VISA and ICP Interfaces is installed. Impacted MATLAB releases install NI-VISA 20.0 with this support package. The crash can occur when using any of the following VISA-related functions, apps, or Simulink blocks: visa  visadev  visadevlist  instrhwinfo  Test and Measurement Tool Query Instrument?block To Instrument?block  For example, the following line of code crashes MATLAB: instrhwinfo("visa","ni")
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the following steps to install the NI-VISA 21.0 driver on your Intel-based macOS Big Sur 11.4 or 11.5.2 machine:Download and install NI-VISA 21.0 from NI-VISA version 21.0 for macOS.Restart your machine, as directed by the NI installer.Allow NI-VISA to load system extensions by following the instructions for "System software blocked" from the?macOS User Guide.Apply the workaround for NI bug number 1555478 mentioned in the NI documentation. Upgrading to NI-VISA 21.0 alone will not solve the issue. See https://www.ni.com/en-us/support/documentation/bugs/21/ni-visa-21-0-known-issues.html.Restart your machine, as directed by the NI installer.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model with Iterator subsystem with Assignment block might produce incorrect simulation and code generation results
Description: Simulating your model in accelerator or rapid accelerator mode or generating code from your model might produce incorrect results when the model meets all of the following conditions: The model contains a reusable subsystem whose output is an input to a For Iterator or While Iterator subsystem. The For or While Iterator subsystem contains an Assignment block. The immediate data source of the input initialization signal (Y0) ?port?of the Assignment block is located outside the For or While Iterator subsystem but inside the reusable subsystem. The aforementioned immediate data source is either (1) a Mux block or (2) a Bus to Vector block whose input is a virtual bus signal.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Copy block with the?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization?parameter selected before the input initialization signal (Y0)?port?of the Assignment block.

Summary: 1D - Direct Lookup Table(n-D) block that has Diagnostic for out-of-range input parameter set to 'Warning' or 'None' generates incorrect HDL code.
Description: The HDL code generated for your Simulink model might be incorrect when your model has these parameter settings for?Direct Lookup Table(n-D) ?block: Number of table dimensions  ? is set to '1'. Diagnostic for out-of-range input?  is selected as 'Warning ' or 'None '.  The incorrect HDL code may cause simulation failures in ModelSim when verifying the generated code.

Summary: Loading an App Designer app with a grid layout might result in an inactive breakpoint

Inactive breakpoints aredisplayed in gray.This issue might occur in apps that contain a grid layout,and occurs most often in apps with many components in the grid layout.
Workaround: Tomake an inactive breakpoint active, modify and save the app, and thenset the breakpoint again.

Summary: Extra node when comparing Simulink models containing blocks or annotations that have the same name as a system, block, annotation, or line parameter
Description: When comparing Simulink model files, an extra node may appear in the comparison report when all  these conditions are true:? You are using the comparison tools on the MATLAB desktop or publishing the comparison report as HTML, PDF, or Word files. You added or deleted a block or annotation. The name of the added or deleted block or annotation matches the name of a system, block, annotation, or line parameter.  In the comparison report, the extra node appears on the opposite side to the block or annotation that has the same name as a parameter.
Workaround: Rename the added or deleted block or annotation. Use a name that does not match the name of any Simulink system, block, annotation, or line parameters.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Permute function is scalarized incorrectly
Description: In rare cases, incorrect code generation for the permute function may occur when all of the following conditions are met: The input matrix to the permute function has at least 3 dimensions. Exactly one dimension of the input matrix is variable-sized with an upper bound. At least one dimension?of the input matrix is fixed with the same size as the upper bound. Additional dimensions of the input matrix are fixed and are either size 1 or are the same size as the upper bound. The permute  function rearranges the fixed and variable-sized dimensions. MATLAB Coder is configured with InlineBetweenUserAndMathWorksFunctions  set to 'Always '.  Consider the following MATLAB code:     function out = foo(in,upperLimit)    assert(upperLimit>=2 && upperLimit<=3);    out = permute(in(2:1:upperLimit,:,:),[2,3,1]);    end MATLAB Coder generates:    --SNIP--     out_size[0] = 2;    out_size[1] = 1;    out_size[2] = (int)upperLimit - 1;    plast = (int)upperLimit - 1;    for (i = 0; i < plast; i++) {      out_data[2 * i] = in[3 * i + 1];      out_data[2 * i + 1] = in[3 * i + 2];    }     --SNIP-- which is incorrect when in is a 3-by-2-by-1 matrix and upperLimit is 2. This is because we assign elements 1 and 2 from in into elements 0 and 1 of out. However, the correct generated code would have assigned elements 1 and 4 from in into elements 0 and 1 of out. For example, the correct code may look like:    --SNIP--     plast = (int)upperLimit - 2;    for (i = 0; i < 2; i++) {      for (k = 0; k <= plast; k++) {        in_data[k + ((int)upperLimit - 1) * i] = in[(k + 3 * i) + 1];      }    }    out_size[0] = 2;    out_size[1] = 1;    out_size[2] = (int)upperLimit - 1;    plast = ((int)upperLimit - 1) << 1;    if (plast - 1 >= 0) {      memcpy(&out_data[0], &in_data[0], plast * sizeof(double));    }     --SNIP--  See attached example.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Description: MATLAB crashes during code generation when all the following conditions are true: An Outport block in a conditionally executed subsystem directly connects to a Model block. The same Outport block is accessed by a State Reader or State Writer block.

Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:The model contains a Merge block and a Unit Delay block. The output of the Merge block feeds into the Unit Delay block. The?Initial condition ?parameter of the Unit Delay block and the Initial output  parameter of the Merge block have different values.?

Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Some heat exchanger blocks might exhibit incorrect specific heats under certain conditions
Description: The Simple Heat Exchanger Interface (G) block might incorrectly calculate specific heat when you set the Gas Specification ?parameter to Real . The block calculates a specific heat at constant pressure (cp) value of 1 kJ/kg/K, which might limit the value of the specific dissipation to an incorrect theoretical maximum.  This bug also affects the Simple variant of these blocks: Heat Exchanger (G) Heat Exchanger (G-G) Heat Exchanger (G-TL)?
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Non virtual buses using update diagram mapping options might return incorrect error message
Description: Non virtual buses using the update diagram mapping options might return an incorrect error message as a result of the mapping operation.

Summary: A2L and ARXML generation might not be supported for models with lookup tables and breakpoint parameters which are fed through input ports
Description: A2L and ARXML generation might not be supported when the following conditions are true: The model contains a Prelookup block. The Prelookup block has a breakpoint with Specification  set to Explicit values ?and Source ?set to  Input port .
Workaround: For the Prelookup block, change the Source parameter to Dialog and specify the breakpoint data values in the provided field.

Summary: Import of File Log data from a target computer can report errors
Description: If a folder on the MATLAB path has the same name as the real-time application on the target computer from which File Log data is being imported, MATLAB reports an error. The error reports that MATLAB cannot change the current folder to a temporary folder on the development computer.
Workaround: Remove the folder with the same name as the real-time application.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: Increase memory usage on development computer during File Log import
Description: During File Log import of large datasets into the Simulink Data Inspector, memory usage on the development computer may increase considerably. This usage increase may cause long wait times for the import process to complete or may cause MATLAB to close. This issue appears more often on development computers that have less memory installed.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Description: Model Advisor check Check usage of the Saturation blocks ?(id: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0628 ) displays incorrect warning for Saturation and Saturation Dynamic blocks with same compiled input and output data types but has?type casting operations in their limits setting.

Summary: JMAAB check for jc_0623?does not report delay blocks used in?continuous type model or subsystem
Description: Model Advisor?check?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0623 (Usage of continuous-time Delay blocks and discrete-time Delay blocks) ?does not report?warning?for? delay ?blocks used in?continuous type model or subsystem as per the guideline.

Summary: Error when a function generated by matlabFunction is called with nonscalar input
Description: This error can happen only if both of these conditions are true: the input to matlabFunction  is not a scalar symbolic expression. the input to the function that is generated by matlabFunction  is also nonscalar.  Then MATLAB might throw the following error. "Error using reshape Number of elements must not change." For example, executing this code results in the error. A=sum(power(sym('a'),1:10));B=sum(power(sym('b'),1:10));C=sum(power(sym('c'),1:10));X=[A;B;C;2*A;2*B;2*C;3*A;3*B];f=matlabFunction(X,'File','Xfun',Optimize = false);f([1,2],[2,3],[3,4]) Meanwhile, f(1,2,3)  works as expected.?
Workaround: In R2021b you can work around this issue by using the name-value argument Legacy=true.For example, when you run the code example in the Description section, replace the call to matlabFunction with this line of code.f=matlabFunction(X,'File','Xfun',Optimize = false, Legacy = true);

Summary: Rebuilding a simulation target in an SLXC file from a previous release might fail
Description: Using SLXC files generated before R2020a_update8, R2020b_update7, and R2021a_update6 to rebuild simulation targets in a later release produces an error.     Unable to update models referenced by '***'.        Caused by:        Error using *** (line **)        Cannot find field dynamicEnumTypeChecksums in ***
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2022a

Summary: Inactive state writer block writing state to an outport block with initial condition may error when it receives propagated variant condition
Description: When?you perform an update diagram on a model, Simulink may throw an error stating that Outport block has inherited initial output value, though initial output value is specified.This error can occur when all below conditions are met: Outport block, with some initial value specified for initial output,  and connected from a block which has variant condition State Writer  block writes to the Outport block with the initial condition and propagated variant condition Outport block is inactive by variant block's whose 'Variant control mode'  is in 'expression' ?mode and 'Variant Activation time'  is not set to?'update diagram'   Sample error message shown below: Block 'model/Function-Call Subsystem/Out1' has attributes or configurations that are not supported by State Writer block 'model/Initialize Function/Subsystem/type_a/State Writer'.  Caused by: Outport block 'model/Function-Call Subsystem/Out1' is inheriting its initial output value from its sources(s). To correct this problem, ensure that the 'Source of initial output value' parameter is set to 'Dialog' and that the 'Initial output' parameter is set to a value different from the empty matrix([]).?

Summary: JMAAB check for jc_0626?does not report Lookup Table blocks not having recommended Interpolation method settings
Description: Model Advisor?check Check usage of Lookup Table blocks  (id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0626)? does not report warning for Lookup Table  blocks with Interpolation Method setting set to values other than recommended in the guideline.

Summary: Report Explorer Lookup Table component does not report table data and breakpoints specified by Simulink.LookupTable object
Description: To specify table data and breakpoints for a Simulink Lookup Table block, if you use a?Simulink.LookupTable ?object as the?Data specification  , the Report Explorer Lookup Table component does not report the data and breakpoints defined in that?object . Instead, it reports the data and breakpoints as if?Data specification ?were set to?Table and breakpoints . This causes incorrect data to be reported or the following error to occur:? Index exceeds the number of array elements. Index must not exceed 1 .??
Workaround: Set?Data specification?to Table and breakpoints and define the table data and breakpoints using the block dialog. Alternatively, if you are using release R2018a or later, use the Report API?slreportgen.report.LookupTable class to report information about Lookup Table blocks, instead of using the Report Explorer.?
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Aggregation of coverage data for subsystem into top-model might fail
Description: When you use Simulink Test  to aggregate subsystem-level coverage into the top-model, and one or more of the subsystems receives MCDC for cascaded logic, you get an exception similar to the following:Index in position n  exceeds array bounds (must not exceed m ).  This exception causes the coverage aggregation to fail and you do not receive the cumulative coverage results.

Summary: On Windows 11, Polyspace Code Prover might error out during the Software Safety Analysis Level 1?part of the analysis and provide less precise results
Description: If you run a Polyspace Code Prover analysis on a Windows 11 host, you might encounter an error during the Software Safety Analysis Level 1  part of the analysis. The error message in the log file is?exception SML_Pre_Basis.Fail("failure in job")(Fail: failure in job) raised . The error does not mean that the entire analysis failed. You can still see the Polyspace Code Prover results, but the results are from the previous level, Software Safety Analysis Level 0 . Because higher levels indicate greater precision, the results of level 0 might contain a greater number of orange checks (unproven results).

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink Coder might generate incorrect code for models with reusable subsystems that have function-call output signals
Description: Simulink Coder might generate incorrect code for models under the following circumstances: The model contains identical atomic subsystems with function-call output signals. The subsystems have auto  or reusable  function packaging. The function-call output signals connect to identical function-call subsystems. The subsystems initiating the function call have data connections to the function-call subsystems. The order of the data connection does not match between the various identical function-call initiator subsystems and function-call subsystem instances.  Simulink Coder might generate one instance for the function-call initiator subsystem, where multiple instances are required.

Summary: HDL code generation fails for Real Reciprocal HDL Optimized block
Description: HDL code generation fails if a model contains a Real Reciprocal HDL Optimized block with this error message: ˇ°The sample rate 4 of port 'Cout' in network ˇ®Real    Reciprocal HDL Optimized/Shift and cast to output type/Cast to Union of Types'    is different from the expected sample rate inf. Consider inserting a Rate    Transition block before the port.ˇ±
Workaround: Modify the sample time of the Constant block in Shift and cast to output type block from 'inf' to -1.1. Break the library link of Real Reciprocal HDL Optimized block:    a. Right click on the block. Navigate to Library Link then select Disable Link.    b. Right click on the block again. Navigate to Library Link then select Break Link. 2. Open the block by right clicking on the block, navigate to Mask and select Look Under Mask.3. Break the library link of Shift and cast to output type block and open it.4. Double click on the Constant 0 block and change the sample time from 'inf'?to?-1.A fixed block with the library link removed is attached.When updating to a newer version of MATLAB, the block with library link removed needs to be replaced with new block with library link to incorporate the updates.More information regarding Real Reciprocal HDL Optimized block and sample time setting can be found at the following reference pages.https://www.mathworks.com/help/fixedpoint/ref/realreciprocalhdloptimized.html    https://www.mathworks.com/help/hdlcoder/ug/check-for-infinite-sample-time-sources.html

Summary: MATLAB crashes when updating the model a second time during accelerator mode simulation
Description: Compiling a model twice during simulation in accelerator mode causes MATLAB to crash when the model contains a user-written S-function and one of the parameters of the S-Function block references a workspace variable. For example, during an accelerator mode simulation of the model with the simulation time set to inf , clicking Update Model  on the Modeling  tab twice causes MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Missing Simulation 3D Vehicle with Ground Following block Location and Orientation output ports
Description: If the Simulation 3D Vehicle with Ground Following block parameters are not defined, these block output ports are missing:Location  Orientation?
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: MATLAB might crash when generating an Embedded Coder report
Description: MATLAB might crash when you use Embedded Coder to generate code and then enable option Generate static code metrics ?when creating a report. This option is available under Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Report . The issue occurs when you generate code on Windows machines using the Visual Studio 2019 toolchain and the code contains a templated class with a static member variable whose initializer uses the ->  operator. This issue might also occur when using other toolchains.

Summary: JMAAB check for?db_0129?does not report supertransitions crossing states
Description: The?Sub-ID c? of Model Advisor?check?mathworks.jmaab.db_0129 (db_0146_c:?Transition lines shall not cross over states) ?does not report?warnings for? supertransitions crossing states.

Summary: JMAAB check for db_0129 does not report transitions crossing graphical functions or subcharts
Description: The?Sub-ID c? of Model Advisor?check?mathworks.jmaab.db_0129 (db_0146_c:?Transition lines shall not cross over states)  does not report?warning?for transitions crossing graphical functions or subcharts.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Data Store Read block that reads an array of buses that contains a string gives incorrect simulation results
Description: A Data Store Read block gives incorrect simulation results when all of these conditions apply: The Data Store Read block reads the entire data store from a Data Store Memory block. The Data Store Memory block specifies a Simulink.Bus  object data type. The specified Bus  object contains an element with a string  data type. The Data Store Memory block specifies a dimension greater than 1.  The Data Store Read block correctly reads only the first element of the array of buses. The other elements of the array of buses are set to zero.
Workaround: Read individual elements of the array of buses from the data store.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Yolov2Resnet18 predict incorrect results when ConvThread Number = 9,?25,?36,?49
Description: Networks output layer that is connected to the convolution layer will produce incorrect inference results when using ConvThreadNumber = 9,25,36,49.
Workaround: There isn't a easy workaround for this issue. Please use other ConvThreadNum. (e.g: 4,16)

Summary: Error during IP core generation in SoC Builder when FPGA model has reference models
Description: When you run SoC Builder for a model that contains one or more reference models in the FPGA model, you get this error during the IP core generation step. Could not apply model's setting to the Target Interface Table inTask 1.2 of the HDL Workflow Advisor for the following reason: Cannot connectto model 'soc_hwsw_fpga0'; please try Update Diagram (Ctrl-D). Default or lastvalid setting is applied instead. If you are using the HDL Workflow CommandLine Interface, consider making these changes in the HDL Workflow Advisor andusing 'Export to Script...' to automatically generate the appropriate 'hdlset_param' commands.?

Summary: MATLAB crashes when an existing model is simulated using Accelerator Mode in R2021a Update 4 or R2020b update 6
Description: MATLAB might crash when you simulate a model after installing R2021a Update 4 or R2020b Update 6, if all of the following conditions are true: The model is configured for Accelerator Mode simulation When using R2021a Update 4, a simulation cache folder and/or Simulink cache file generated using R2021a or a previous R2021a Update exist on the path When using R2020b Update 6, a simulation cache folder and/or Simulink cache file generated using R2020b or a previous R2020b Update exist on the path The model is unchanged since it was previously run  See also?Bug Report 2553782 ?and?Bug Report 2581114 .
Workaround: Before simulating the model in MATLAB R2021a Update 4 or R2020b Update 6, delete the following files and foldersModel Simulation cache folder. This folder is commonly named 'slprj'Model Simulink Cache file. This file is named <modelName>.slxcThis only needs to be done once, to remove cache files and folders generated by a previous MATLAB version.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When using any deep learning Simulink block, changes to the specified network MAT-file might not be reflected in the simulation or code generation results
Description: Consider a Simulink model containing a deep learning block that loads a trained network from a MAT-file. If the network in the MAT-file is updated but the rest of the model remains unchanged, Simulink might not rebuild the model when performing simulation or code generation. As a result, the deep learning block might produce incorrect output.

Summary: Adaptive AUTOSAR header file for stringview uses wrong file name
Description: As per the AUTOSAR standard for adaptive platforms(18_10, 19_03 and 19_11), an ara::core implementation file for datatype stringview should be named as string_view.h , but AUTOSAR Blockset generates a file with the name?stringview.h .

Summary: Unrecognized field name warning when running a custom training experiment in Experiment Manager
Description: When you use the Experiment Manager app to run a custom training experiment, the MATLAB Command Window might show a warning of the form: Error occurred while executing the listener callback for event MetricsUpdate defined for class experiments.Monitor:  Unrecognized field name "METRIC_NAME" .   ?After this warning, the results table in the app might display incorrect values for the metric.??

Summary: False discovery rate method applies incorrect denoising in wdenoise2 function
Description: Selecting the false discovery rate (FDR) method for the DenoisingMethod  value?in the? wdenoise2 ?function results in an output image with similar noise levels to the input image. FDR denoising is not correctly applied to the  image.? For example:? load flower  wdenoise2(flower.Noisy,2,'DenoisingMethod',{'FDR',0.01});  The denoised image and the original image have the same levels of noise.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: In Signal Editor, when converting fixed-point data type to or from enumeration data type, a busy icon appears
Description: When converting the data type of a signal from fixed-point to enumeration or enumeration to fixed-point, a busy icon appears and is not removed.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier incorrectly reuses model representation when?CovLogicBlockShortCircuit or CovMcdcMode parameter value is changed
Description: Simulink Design Verifier incorrectly reuses model representation in following scenario: ModelCoverageObjectives  option for Simulink Design Verifier analysis is set to MCDC  or EnhancedMCDC  and RebuildModelRepresentation  option for Simulink Design Verifier analysis is set to IfChangeIsDetected  and You toggle value of either CovLogicBlockShortCircuit  or CovMcdcMode  and rerun Test Generation analysis

Summary: When deleting points in Signal Editor user interface, spinner might display indefinitely
Description: When selecting and deleting multiple points that contain sparse selections, the Signal Editor user interface might display a persistent spinner.

Summary: Incorrect normal-mode simulation results for a referenced model with a parameterized for-each subsystem
Description: Simulating a model hierarchy in normal mode might produce incorrect simulation results when all these conditions apply:? The model hierarchy contains multiple instances of a referenced model that simulate in normal mode. That referenced model contains a parameterized for-each subsystem. That subsystem contains a Sine Wave block.

Summary: Incorrect signal might be logged by XCP-based external mode simulation
Description: ?An XCP-based external mode simulation might log an incorrect signal when, for a given block, all of these conditions hold: A signal from one of the block's output ports is marked for logging. A signal into one of the same block's input ports is marked for logging. The logged signal at the block's input port is not contiguous. For example, the output from a Mux block.  The waveform obtained from the block's output port might contain the data from the input port.
Workaround: For the affected block, insert a Signal Conversion block before the input port or after the output port.Mark the signal from the output port of the Signal Conversion block for logging.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Bulleted list formatting lost when importing requirements from Microsoft Word
Description: When you import requirements from Microsoft Word  and, in the Importing Requirements dialog box you select Ignore outline numbers in section headers , any bulleted list formatting within the requirement is also ignored. This setting is selected by default and intends to avoid capturing automatic section numbers as part of the imported requirements, however the bullet points are also removed.

Summary: Running Model Advisor on MATLAB Function block in a library then opening it causes assertion failure
Description: MATLAB throws an assertion error when you run Model Advisor check himl_0002 on a library that contains a MATLAB Function block, then try to open the MATLAB Function editor: falseComponent: Stateflow | Category: Assertion error  followed by an error of the form?  Error calling eml_man(open_editor): Java exception occurred:java.lang.RuntimeException: java.lang.NullPointerException
Workaround: Before trying to open the MATLAB Function editor, close the library model (e.g. with bdclose command), then reopen it.?
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Requirement links to test assessments are not recognized by the metrics of the Model Testing Dashboard
Description: When you link a requirement to a test assessment in Simulink Test Manager, the metrics in the?Model?Testing?Dashboard show the requirement as Unlinked . If you collect metrics programmatically using a metric.Engine  object, instances of metric.Result  show the requirement as not linked to test cases in the project.
Workaround: Instead of linking requirements to individual test assessments, link     requirements to?higher-level test cases in Simulink Test     Manager.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Invalid evaluation of classifiers in sbiompgsa
Description: The evaluation of classifiers in sbiompgsa  can fail or yield wrong values.?? When a classifier in sbiompgsa  references two or more model components whose names are a mix of qualified and non-qualified names, then the function can error. The error says that the classifier is invalid, even though the classifier is valid. An example of a qualified name of a species Drug  in the compartment named?Central ?is Central.Drug . When a classifier in sbiompgsa  references both constant and non-constant model components, then the function can return the wrong sensitivities without any error indication.
Workaround: To avoid the incorrect error about invalid classifiers, reference model components either by names or by qualified names, not a mix of names and qualified names in classifiers.To avoid wrong sensitivities being reported by sbiompgsa, specify numeric values instead of names of constant model components in classifiers.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: Save As might check out a Simulink Test license for models containing test harnesses
Description: Using Save As  to save a model that contains one or more test harnesses might check out a Simulink Test license.
Workaround: Make a copy of the model, open the model, and then save it. Do not use Save As because it might check out a license.

Summary: Advisor.Application class?run?method marks a compile check as passed although model compilation fails
Description: Using the run  method of the??Advisor.Application  class on? Model Advisor compile-time checks?wrongly? marks the check status as passed  on a model?that has a compilation error.

Summary: Dependency Analyzer throws an error when exporting a project archive
Description: In the Dependency Analyzer, the "Package As Archive" export option throws an error when both  of these conditions are true:? You opened the Dependency Analyzer from a MATLAB project. The project has files that depend on files stored outside the project root.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: JMAAB check for db_0141?doesn't have sub-id selection
Description: The guideline for the Model Advisor?check?db_0141?:?Signal flow in Simulink models ?has sub-ids but the Model Advisor check doesn't have the input parameters for sub-id selection.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when an existing model containing a MATLAB System Block is simulated in R2021a Update 4 or R2020b Update 6
Description: MATLAB might crash might when you simulate a model after installing MATLAB R2021a Update 4 or R2020b Update 6, if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains at least one MATLAB System Block configured for Code Generation When using R2021a Update 4, a simulation cache folder and/or Simulink cache file generated using R2021a or a previous R2021a Update exists on path When using R2020b Update 6, a simulation cache folder and/or Simulink cache file generated using R2020b or a previous R2020b Update exists on path The model is unchanged since it was previously run  When the crash happens, a stack trace similar to the following can be observed: [  4] [SNIP]\bin\win64\cgxe.dll CGXE::RuntimeBindingJIT[  5] [SNIP]\bin\win64\cgxe.dll CGXE::CGXEMgr::runtimeBinding[  6] [SNIP]\bin\win64\SimulinkBlock.dll SLCGXEExecBlock::InitializeSimStruct[  7] [SNIP]\bin\win64\SimulinkBlock.dll SLCGXEExecBlock::SetupRuntimeResources See also?Bug Report 2563472 ?and?Bug Report 2581114 .
Workaround: Before simulating the model in MATLAB R2021a Update 4 or R2020b Update 6, delete the following files and foldersModel Simulation cache folder. This folder is commonly named 'slprj'Model Simulink Cache file. This file is named <modelName>.slxcThis only needs to be done once, to remove cache files and folders generated by a?previous MATLAB version.

Summary: Propagate conditions outside of Variant Subsystem?may cause error during update diagram
Description: Variant Subsystem with condition propagation may cause update diagram error when all below conditions are metVariant Subsystem with?Propagate conditions outside of variant subsystem?  turned on Merge block connects output of conditionally executed systems (such as If action) Merge block output is feeding to the Variant Subsystem Variant Subsystem selects one of the signals of a bus  An example error message: Invalid connection from block 'untitled/Bus Creator' output port 1 to Merge block 'untitled/Merge' input port 1. An input signal to a Merge block must be connected and must originate from a conditionally executed subsystem that is not an Iterator subsystem"

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink Coder generates incorrect code for reusable subsystem with array of nonvirtual Bus Element inputs
Description: Simulink Coder generates incorrect code for a reusable subsystem under the following circumstances: The input port of a reusable subsystem is connected to the output of a Vector Element Selector block, where an element is selected that is not the first element in the source vector The input port of the Vector Element Selector block has a array of nonvirtual buses as its input type The input port block of the reusable subsystem is connected to a Bus Element Selector block The Bus Element Selector block is selecting a non-scale element from this bus  In the generated code, the element offset from the array bus selection is incorrectly subtracted from the Bus Element selection. This may lead to negative indexing or compliable code if the selected Bus Element is connected to a Vector Element Selection block that selects an element that is not the first element.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Data Store Read/Write blocks incorrectly inherit constant sample time when showing index port
Description: Simulating your model in normal mode may produce incorrect results when all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a Data Store Read or Data Store Write block. The Data Store Read or Data Store Write shows an index port. The index port is driven by a Constant block.?? The Sample time ?of the Constant block is set to inf .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Embedded Coder might generate incorrect code with Optimize global data access set to Minimize global data access
Description: Embedded Coder might generate incorrect code for a model under the following circumstances: The Optimize global data access  option is set to Minimize global data access  The model contains nested reusable subsystems (more than 1 layer deep)  The optimization might replace global variables with local variables that do not contain the same value as the replaced global variable.
Workaround: Set the Optimize global data access model parameters option to none.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Model with a shift by an explicit negative constant may experience incorrect Unsatisfiable or Valid objectives
Description: Models with a shift by a negative constant can experience one of the two scenarios:? Simulink Design Verifier  analysis completes normally and report incorrect Valid or Unsatisfiable  objectives. Simulink Design Verifier analysis reports an internal error without providing the full analysis results.  A few ways in which a model may experience a shift by a negative constant amount are:  Shift Arithmetic  blocks with the following configuration: The shift amount is a negative constant. The Check for out-of-range 'Bits to shift' in generated code  option is off   MATLAB functions that contain calls to shift functions with negative constant shift values. Stateflow charts with shifts by negative constants
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When targeting cuDNN with INT8 precision, the code generated for deep learning network having grouped convolution layers might produce incorrect results
Description: Generating cuDNN INT8 code for a deep learning network with grouped convolution layer might produce incorrect result if?all  these conditions are true  Number of filter groups is equal to 2?? Total number of filters from all the groups is not a multiple of 4 Number of filter channels is not a multiple of 4

Summary: MAXP document's getParent method crashes MATLAB
Description: Invoking an XML document's getParent method crashes MATLAB. For example, this script crashes MATLAB:import matlab.io.xml.dom.*d = Document('book');p = getParent(d);

Summary: PostgreSQL native interface returns incorrect data for double values with negative exponents
Description: The fetch  and sqlread  functions of the PostgreSQL native interface return incorrect values when the functions import data with the double  data type on the database and negative exponents. For example, the functions might import a 1e-6 value from the PostgreSQL database as 1.
Workaround: Create a connection to a PostgreSQL database using the database function to connect using either the ODBC or JDBC driver.Alternatively, for a PostgreSQL native interface connection, use the fetch function and cast the data from the?double?data type to a numeric data type in the SQL query. For example: SELECT CAST (doubleData AS numeric) FROM MyTable

Summary: Empty values in outputStruct.scan(index).precursorMz.value and loss of non-mzXML 2.1 attributes
Description: For an input mzXML file containing level 2 mass spectrometry scans (msLevel  ˇÝ?2), the function returns an empty array []  as the value for a field named "value" in the?precursorMZ  field of the output structure (outputStruct ) even if the value is not empty in the input file. That is, outputStruct.scan(index).precursorMZ.value = [] .? In addition, the scan  element attributes?that are not specified in the mzXML 2.1 specification document (http://sashimi.sourceforge.net/schema_revision/mzXML_2.1/Doc/mzXML_2.1_tutorial.pdf) were not included in outputStruct .

Summary: Rebuilding test harness slow when system being tested outputs bus objects and harness source is Chart or Test Sequence
Description: When a system under test has bus objects at its output interface, rebuilding its test harness whose source is a Chart or Test Sequence might be slower than test harness creation. If the number of bus objects at the output increases, the time taken to rebuild the harness also increases.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Dependency Analyzer does not list Polyspace as a required product
Description: When using Polyspace functions in your code, the Dependency Analyzer does not list Polyspace as a required product?in the?Products  section of the?Properties ?pane. The Dependency Analyzer also reports Outside project root ?problems in the?Problems? section of the?Properties? pane.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 5, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Antenna radiation patterns in Site Viewer have incorrect rotation
Description: When you change the elevation angle of a?txsite ?or rxsite  object by using the?AntennaAngle ?property, the antenna radiation pattern rolls instead of elevates. This bug affects Site Viewer radiation patterns in Antenna Toolbox(TM) and Communications Toolbox(TM).?

Summary: Summary of requirements in exported ReqIF file contains escaped HTML tags in plain text after import from PREEvision
Description:
Many third-party requirements management applications can export and import requirements using the ReqIF file format.?If you manage your requirements in a third-party tool, you can import the requirements to Simulink Requirements, edit or link the requirements, and export the requirements back to your third-party tool with ReqIF files. This procedure is referred to as a ReqIF round trip.When you perform a ReqIF round trip with PREEvision, the requirements in the ReqIF file exported by Simulink Requirements might contain escaped HTML tags in plain text. For example, a requirement in the ReqIF file might have a summary that says

Requirement 1

. When this requirement is exported back to ReqIF, the HTML tags are?escaped.?

Summary: Unused local variables in generated code with Variant blocks and Simulink functions
Description: Variant blocks and Simulink functions may result in generation of additional local variables in the code as the below example. The variable?rtb_in2  is assigned the value 0, when Choice_1~=0  , however is never used.?void test_f(void)  {  uint16_T rtb_in2;  uint16_T rtb_in1;  #if Choice_1 == 0  rtb_in2 = test_U.in2;  #else  rtb_in1 = test_U.in1;  rtb_in2 = 0U;   #endif  #if Choice_1 == 0  test_Y.Out1 = (uint16_T)(((uint32_T)rtb_in2 << 15) >> 14);  rtb_in1 = 0U;  #else  test_Y.Out1 = rtb_in1;  #endif  }  This behavior may occur when the inports and outports of the Simulink functions become conditional due to variant sink/source block within.

Summary: Incorrect Unsatisfiable objectives for Saturation blocks with a vector of Inf bounds
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might report incorrect?Unsatisfiable (or Dead Logic) objectives ?for a Saturation  block if one of the following conditions is met:The Upper limit   block parameter has a vector of at least 2?Inf   values specified The?Lower limit  ?block parameter has a vector of at least 2 -Inf  ?values specified
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: Clearing an XPath evaluator can crash MATLAB
Description: Using an XPath evaluator to evaluate a path on an XML file and then clearing the evaluator from the MATLAB workspace crashes MATLAB.? For example, this script crashes MATLAB:import matlab.io.xml.xpath.*xmlFile = fullfile(matlabroot,'toolbox/matlab/general/info.xml');ev = Evaluator;result = evaluate(ev,'//name',xmlFile);clear ev;n = getTagName(result);

Summary: Data Store Memory in referenced model with no tunable parameters might halt model build
Description: When a Simulink Real-Time model references a model using a model block, the model build might halt before compilation when all of the following apply: The referenced model uses a global or shared Data Store Memory The referenced model does?not?contain any blocks that would introduce a tunable Model parameter The default parameter behavior is?Tunable  The storage class for the model element category Model parameters ?in the Data Defaults ?tab of the Code Perspective is set to "PageSwitching"  For these conditions, the top-most model build halts after code is generated, before compilation with the error: The call to slrealtime_make_rtw_hook, during the after_tlc hook generated the following error:? ? Could not find pointer variable for type mg2546766_child_cal_type in header file
Workaround: For each model in the model reference hierarchy:?Open the Code Perspective.Select the Data Defaults tab of the "Code Mappings - C" view.Change the storage class for the model element category Model parameters from PageSwitching to Default.The model will then build, but the XCP Page Switching functionality will no longer be available.

Summary: Code generation fails for model with a root inport that outputs a nonvirtual bus
Description: Code generation can fail with an error for a model with a root inport that outputs a nonvirtual bus.? The error takes the following form: Simulink Coder Fatal: invalid SLTypeIdx -1 for struct

Summary: Clean Outlier Data can produce histogram plot with incorrect axes labels
Description: In the Clean Outlier Data Live Editor task, when a table is selected as the input and a histogram is selected as the plot type, the table variable name appears on the plot as the y-axis label when it should appear as the x-axis label.
Workaround: In the live script, add code directly following the Live Editor task (in the same section) to remove the y-axis label and create the correct x-axis label:ylabel("")xlabel("Var1")

Summary: Incorrect merged results when using three-way merge for Stateflow charts
Description: Three-way merge of Simulink models containing Stateflow charts creates incorrect merged charts for various chart objects. Incorrect merged result occurs when you perform these actions: Add, delete, or change properties of Stateflow data, event, or message objects.? Add or delete states, transitions, junctions under a box or a graphical function. Change junction positions. Add super-transitions crossing sub-chart boundaries.? Add or delete default and dangling transitions.?
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Vehicle Body 3DOF Longitudinal block has incorrect planar response for non-zero relative front and rear pitch angles
Description: The Vehicle Body 3DOF Longitudinal block incorrectlyapplies the suspension forces, resulting in incorrect planar response for non-zero relative pitch angles. Specifically, theblock uses the wrong sign for the internal longitudinal component of the front and rear axle forces. If you select?External forces , the issue does not apply.?
Workaround: Reroute these Info bus output port longitudinal suspension force signals:?BdyFrm.Forces.FrntAxl.FxBdyFrm.Forces.RearAxl.Fx?1. Subtract them.2. Multiply them by 2.?3. Input them to Fext input port.?See the attached example model (R2019a) and image.?
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 5, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Running sldvcompat on an externally saved test harness might error
Description: If you run sldvcompat  on an externally saved test harness, an error might occur.
Workaround: Create the test harness as internally saved, or convert externally saved test harnesses to internal before running sldvcompat. To convert externally saved test harnesses:Open the Apps tab at the top of the main model.In the Model Verification, Validation, and Test section, click Simulink Test.On the Tests tab, click?Manage Test Harnesses?>?Convert?to Internal Harnesses.A dialog box provides information on the conversion procedure and the affected test harnesses. Click?Yes?to convert the test harnesses.

Summary: Reaction-scoped parameters are not displayed in the SimBiology Model Builder app after adding a kinetic law to the SimBiology model
Description: The Parameters  table in the Browser  pane of the SimBiology Model Builder  app does not display all parameters on the model in the following cases: A reaction has reaction-scoped parameters, and you change the kinetic law to "None". When you undo the change, the original reaction-scoped parameters are added back to the model. The parameters do not appear in the Parameters  table in the Browser  pane. You use?copyobj  ?at the MATLAB command line to copy a kinetic law from a reaction with reaction-scoped parameters to another reaction while the model is open in SimBiology Model Builder . The copied reaction-scoped parameters are added to the other reaction, but they do not appear in the Parameters  table.
Workaround: Save the project, then close and reopen the project in?the SimBiology Model Builder app.

Summary: Sporadic MATLAB crashes during compilation of models with nonlinear terms and derivatives in constraint equations
Description: MATLAB might crash when you run or update a Simscape model where a constraint equation  contains a nonlinear term, which in turn contains a derivative of any variable and at least one more variable. Consider the following network equations: ??????? der(x) * u == u * u;? ? ? ? ? ? ? (1)??????? r == der(x);? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? (2)??????? y == der(r) + cos(u) * der(u);??? (3)??????? u == sin(time);? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?(4)  Equations (1), (2), and (4) are the constraint equations. When we differentiate equation (1) we get the following expression ? ? ?? der(der(x)) * u + der(x) * der(u) == 2.0 * u * der(u)   When applying derivative replacement method on this equation, der(x) * der(u)  term can cause the compilation to crash.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 5, R2021b

Summary: Setting Value or ItemsData with content of class timeseries or datetime results in an error
Description: Using complex datatypes like timeseries  might error when being used in the?Value  or ItemsData  property of a?Listbox , Switch , DiscreteKnob , or DropDown  component. For example, running this code results in an error. >> itemsData = [timeseries(2, 1); timeseries(0, 1)];>> c = uidropdown('ItemsData', itemsData); The first line of the error is:? Warning: JSON incompatible value has been used.

Summary: Simulink co-simulation with Unreal Engine??fails with 3D?simulation engine interface setup error
Description: If your user name contains non-ASCII characters, Simulink co-simulation with Unreal Engine fails with this error:3D simulation engine interface setup error: could not establish communication. Please restart the simulation.

Summary: Adding EtherCAT Async SSC/SoE Upload block to model generates errors
Description: Adding an EtherCAT Async SSC/SoE Upload block to a model generates errors or nonsense values. The error that the block returns vary depending on the configuration of block parameters.?In some cases, the block returns a nonsense value instead of an error.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when undefined, disallowed, or ambiguous units are reported at root output ports
Description: MATLAB might crash during update diagram of a Simulink model when the model configuration parameter for logging,?Format , ?is set to?Dataset . As a result, the root output port unit warns of any of the following: Undefined unit Disallowed unit system Ambiguous?unit
Workaround: To determine which root output ports have units warnings, set the?Format?model configuration parameter for logging to Array, Structure, or StructureWithTime. Then,?modify the units on root output ports such that undefined units, disallowed unit systems, and ambiguous unit warnings are eliminated. After the units warnings have been eliminated, restore the?Format?model configuration parameter for logging to?Dataset.

Summary: Creating a test harness with a large number of input or output ports might result in unconnected ports
