Description: When you save a default code mapping in a shared Simulink data dictionary, some mappings may be reset to the default values when you load the shared Embedded Coder Dictionary in another MATLAB session.

Summary: Specific port of FMU block unable to be hidden or shown when block has multiple bus signals as input or output
Description: When an FMU block has multiple bus signals at the interface (input port/output ports), the corresponding port of the FMU block might not honor the Visibility ?parameter setting in the FMU block parameter dialog.
Workaround: Use the command line API to set the visibility. For example:set_param(block, 'FMUInputVisibility', {true, false})This setting hides port2?for the model.

Summary: MATLAB can crash at an input prompt
Description: MATLAB can crash while at an input prompt. The crash could occur either when MATLAB is at an input prompt waiting for user input, or while processing the command entered at the input prompt.?

Summary: slwebview does not generate optional coverage view in Simulink Web View
Description: When you generate a Simulink Web View by using slwebview(modelName,'OptionalViews','coverage') , the button that you click to show coverage information is missing from the?lower-left palette of the web view.

Summary: "Not enough input arguments" warning appears when you open Simulink Blockset Designer example from the command line
Description: When you try to open the?Simulink Blockset Designer example by entering "slexBlocksetDesignerExample " on the command line, MATLAB throws a warning: "Not enough input arguments ."

Summary: Pedestrian and Bicyclist Classification Using Deep Learning example errors out
Description: The example titled Pedestrian and Bicyclist Classification Using Deep Learning errors out with this message: Unable to resolve the name phased.internal.getHumanBodyParts.
Workaround: Delete the backscatterPedestrian.m?file in the example folder.
Products: Phased Array System Toolbox, Radar Toolbox

Summary: Code generation may fail when custom Subsystems are added using TLC directives
Description: When custom subsystems are added using these TLC directives: '%addtorecord ::CompiledModel Subsystem xxxxxx ' '%addtorecord ::CompiledModel.BlockHierarchyMap Subsystem xxxxxx '  and LibSubsystemName  is called for these Subsystems with their indices, an error is thrown: Error: Simulink Coder Error: The Subsystem index <n> is out of range [0. . .<n-1>] for the current model '<modelname>'.?   Where n  is the index of the newly added Subsystem.

Summary: Setting the Test Sequence block path in the Test Manager Inputs section makes MATLAB unresponsive
Description: MATLAB becomes unresponsive If you set the block path of a Test Sequence block from the Inputs section of the Test Manager, and if these conditions are true: The Test Sequence block is in an external harness The external harness uses Simulation Data Inspector logging. The external harness was not loaded or opened before you set the Test Sequence block path in the Test Manager.

Summary: On Windows, MATLAB fails to process DLL file specified by Libraries?name-value argument if folder path contains spaces
Description: When you call clibgen.generateLibraryDefinition  or clibgen.buildInterface  with a DLL file using the?Libraries ?name-value argument, the folder name for the DLL file must not contain spaces. MATLAB does not process DLL files in folder names containing spaces.

Summary: Unexpected pipelining interactions between?AdaptivePipelining?and MapToRAM
Description: There are a couple issues with AdaptivePipelining  behavior and the recently added HDL Block Property?MapToRAM ?option for Lookup Table blocks. Pipelines related to Lookup Table blocks that map to RAM show up in the Adaptive Pipelining Report section of the Code Generation Report even when?AdaptivePipelining ?is set to?off. ?The report shows? AdaptivePipelining ?is on? for the Lookup Table block.  AdaptivePipelining ?set to on  can cause pipelines to be inserted for Lookup Table blocks even though MapToRAM ?is set to off  on the block.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier might report incorrect Unsatisfiable objectives for Relational Operator blocks where vector inputs are compared to a minimum or maximum values for a particular data type
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might report incorrect Unsatisfiable (or Dead Logic) objectives  when all the following conditions are met:Relational Operator block? Signal with non-scalar dimensions?? Exactly one of the inputs to the block is a constant value that is either the minimum or the maximum value for that data type, and this makes the condition always true or always false.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b

Summary: Interface elements are lost when you export an architecture model from version R2021a to a previous release
Description: When you export an architecture model that was created in R2021a to a previous release using the? systemcomposer.exportToVersion  function, interface element definitions contained within data interfaces are lost. Specifically, interfaces stored directly within the System Composer model are exported without any interface elements.

Summary: TCP Client block requires parameter values for Client IP address and Client Port
Description: When using the block for TCP communications, the TCP Client block requires parameter values for the Client IP address and the Client Port. This requirement produces some operational issues: Even when these parameter values are not necessary or not known, the parameter values must be specified values for the Client IP address and the Client Port. Because the TCP Client socket remains in the TIME_WAIT state for approximately 1 minute, the real-time application cannot be restarted soon after it stops. If the model is restarted during the wait period, the target computer issues an error:? TCP Client Connect Error:?Address already in use (248)

Summary: Code generation fmincon?fails to converge on a nonlinearly constrained problem, but MATLAB fmincon succeeds
Description: In rare cases, generated code for?fmincon ?on problems with many nonlinear constraints fails to converge, whereas the code converges when run in MATLAB. This failure can occur when the code is generated by using MATLAB Coder, Simulink Coder, or Embedded Coder products. The failure is due to disparities between fmincon  iterations in MATLAB and code generation iterations. This issue is more likely to occur with high dimensions (> 100 variables or constraints) and highly nonlinear constraints.

Summary: C Function block dialog shows duplicated symbol tables
Description: When you open a C Function block dialog by right clicking the block and selecting Block Parameters  from the context menu, the opened dialog might be rendered incorrectly, showing two duplicated symbol tables. Modifying symbol properties in the symbol tables might corrupt the C Function block or cause MATLAB to crash.
Workaround: Open the C Function block by double clicking on the block.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink.LookupTable?object displays incorrect data when table dimension or breakpoint value is 1
Description: A?Simulink.LookupTable ?displays incorrect data when all of the following conditions are true: Number of Dimensions ?is set to?1  Breakpoints specification ?is set to Explicit Values  Table ?value is of size 1 OR?Breakpoints (1) value is of size 1?

Summary: Error when using Simulink.Bus.createObject to create a Simulink.Bus object from a structure and save the object in a function?
Description: Simulink reports an error when you use Simulink.Bus.createObject  to create a Simulink.Bus  object from a structure and save the object in a function in either the base workspace or a data dictionary.? For example: Create a structure.? structForBus = struct('a',1,'b',2);  Save to a function in the base workspace.? Simulink.Bus.createObject(structForBus, format, fileName);?  Or, save to a function in the data dictionary.? ?dd = Simulink.data.dictionary.open(slddFile); Simulink.Bus.createObject(structForBus, format, fileName, dd);  ?In this case, the function reports an error similar to one of these:? Unrecognized method, property, or field 'identifyByName' for class 'Simulink.data.BaseWorkspace'  Unrecognized method, property, or field 'identifyByName' for class 'Simulink.data.Dictionary'
Workaround: Create the bus object by using Simulink.Bus.createObject, then save it in a file using Simulink.Bus.save.For example:?structForBus = struct('a',1,'b',2);busObj =? Simulink.Bus.createObject(structForBus);busName =? busObj.busName;Simulink.Bus.save(filename, format, {busName});or structForBus = struct('a',1,'b',2);busObj =? Simulink.Bus.createObject(structForBus);busName =? busObj.busName;dd = Simulink.data.dictionary.open(ddName);?Simulink.Bus.save(filename, format, {busName}, dd)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: 'Multi-Input Sum Block' configured with vector inputs may results in a numerical mismatch.
Description: HDL Coder generates incorrect HDL Code, when the 'Multi-Input Sum Block ' configured with vector inputs is used in a feedback loop. The generated HDL code may have a potential numerical mismatch.
Workaround: Consider modeling 'Multi-Input Sum Block' in terms of series of? 'Two Input Adder Blocks'.

Summary: The SimBiology model diagram can sometimes freeze, leading to a loss of the diagram layout
Description: Under some conditions, typically when a computer is low in memory, and interactions with the model diagram are done quickly, it is possible to save a project with a misconfigured block, such as a species or reaction block.? Upon loading that project, the misconfigured block produces an error that prevents the entire diagram from loading in the app. In addition, if you attempt to save the project once more while under these conditions, the diagram information is lost and cannot be retrieved.

Summary: Average-Value Voltage Source Converter (Three-Phase) block generates rather than dissipates power when converting from AC to DC
Description: The Average-Value Voltage Source Converter (Three-Phase) block generates rather than dissipates?power?when it converts the?electrical energy from AC to DC?voltage.

Summary: MATLAB crashes with an access violation in cef:cef_string_utf16_to_upper
Description: MATLAB occasionally crashes at startup with a crash report containing lines similar to the following: Stack Trace (from fault):[  0] 0x000007fecd8a87c0 cef_string_utf16_to_upper+01196685 at \bin\win64\libcef.dll+32475072 (no debugging symbols found) [  1] 0x000007fecc7b8f5c cef_string_map_size+14129644 at \bin\win64\libcef.dll+14716764 (no debugging symbols found)[  2] 0x000007fecc6390fe cef_string_map_size+12557198 at \bin\win64\libcef.dll+13144318 (no debugging symbols found)[  3] 0x000007fecc637a19 cef_string_map_size+12551337 at \bin\win64\libcef.dll+13138457 (no debugging symbols found)[  4] 0x000007fecd5b632f cef_string_map_size+28798399 at \bin\win64\libcef.dll+29385519 (no debugging symbols found)

Summary: Attempting to import cell array of timetables into the Diagnostic Feature Designer app generates an error
Description: To import data from their MATLAB workspace, the user clicks New Session ?to bring up the New Session  dialog box. In that dialog box, the user selects a workspace variable to import. If the selected variable is a cell array of timetables, the app generates the error message??Please try different input data format .
Workaround: Change the format of the data to be imported from a cell array of timetables to a table with timetables using the?cell2table function. Then, import the table into the?Diagnostic Feature Designer?app.

Summary: Failed model simulations result in incorrect scaling of first and total order indices reported by sbiosobol
Description: Failed model simulations result in incorrect scaling of the first and total order indices reported by?sbiosobol . If model simulations fail during the computation of Sobol indices, the reported Sobol indices are incorrectly scaled by the factor   s = number of non-failed model simulations / number of all model simulations .? If model simulations fail while executing sbiosobol , warnings about the failed model simulations are shown in the MATLAB command window. The relative importance ranking of Sobol indices is not affected by this bug, because all first and total order Sobol indices are scaled by the same factor.
Workaround: The scaling factor,?s, can be computed from the results, sobolResults, returned from sbiosobol as follows:  s = sum(all(sobolResults.SimulationInfo.ValidSample, 2)) / height(sobolResults.ParameterSamples);To correct the Sobol indices, divide the reported Sobol indices sobolResults.SobolIndices by s.However, this workaround does not correct the values of the Sobol indices stored on the?sobolResults. In particular, plotting the Sobol indices using the plot and bar methods still reports the incorrectly scaled Sobol indices.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Using the web command to view custom documentation?in the Help browser might result in a?Page Not Found?error
Description: When the Help browser is configured to view the documentation on the web, running the web  command provided by the Help Page Location dialog box to open the custom documentation results in a Page Not Found  error in the Help browser.
Workaround: To work around this issue, update the Help preferences to use the locally installed documentation: On the Home?tab, in the Environment?section, click Preferences.Select MATLAB > Help.Set the?Documentation Location?to?Installed locally.

Summary: Error when attempting to import a DOORS 9 requirements module
Description: When you attempt to import a module from IBM DOORS 9, an error message that says?Could not recognize the date/ time format...  might appear. ?The date and time string included in the error message shows "HH:mm:ss" inserted in between the module creation or modification date and time. The error prevents the requirements module from importing to Simulink Requirements. This only happens on some installations of DOORS. One known reported case was with DOORS version 9.5.2.4, but other versions may be affected.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: The ee.passive.instrumented_resistor class hasbeen removed and Simscape components converted by subcircuit2ssc in an earlier release might fail
Description: In R2021a, the ee.passive.instrumented_resistor  class hasbeen removed. Because of this change, simulation might fail for Simscape components that were converted by using the subcircuit2ssc  function in an earlier release.
Workaround: Inside a model saved in an earlier release thatcontains converted Simscape components, perform any of these actions: Run the?subcircuit2sscfunction again.Replace ee.passive.instrumented_resistor?by?foundation.electrical.elements.resistor.

Summary: Error during simulation of?export-function model with bus element port when Merge block?is driven by function-call Inport block
Description: When all of the following conditions are met, Simulink may incorrectly throw an error when you simulate your model:  The model is an export-function model.? The model contains a Merge block at its root level. The Merge block is driven by one or more function-call?root-level?Inport blocks. Each function-call root-level Inport block that drives the Merge block has a specified periodic sample time, that is, a sample time other than -1  (inherited)  or Inf . The model contains one or more In Bus Element or Out Bus Element blocks at its root level.?
Workaround: Change the sample time of at least one function-call?root-level Inport block driving the Merge block to inherited (-1).

Summary: systemcomposer.importModel?function does not import property values represented as strings when architectural elements are specified using MATLAB tables
Description: The systemcomposer.importModel  function does not import property values represented as strings when MATLAB tables are used to specify architectural elements and their associated property values. Properties, defined under stereotypes that exist on a profile, are customizable for each element the stereotypes are assigned to. For example, these steps create an architecture model where the component c1  does not have the correct value "a"  for the property myStringProp . Create a new System Composer model. Create a System Composer profile. To open the Profile Editor, on the?Modeling ?tab, under?Profiles ?click the bottom of the Import ?button and then click?Edit .? Name the profile SimpleProfile . Add a stereotype called TestComponent  and add a property called myStringProp . Save the profile. Create a MATLAB table to specify components and stereotype properties.componentTable =     ? 2ˇÁ5 table      ? ? ? ?Name? ? ? ? ID? ? ? ? ParentID? ? StereotypeNames? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? SimpleProfile_TestComponent_myStringProp   ? ? ___________? ? ___? ? ? ?________? ? _____________________________? ? ________________________________________   ??   ? ? "testModel"? ? "0"? ? ? ""? ? ? ? ? ?""? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?""? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ??   ? ? "c1"? ? ? ? ? ?"1"? ? ? "0"? ? ? ? ? "SimpleProfile.TestComponent"? ? "a"? ? ? ?     Import the model using the?systemcomposer.importModel ?function.

Summary: getImageData on CanvasRenderingContext2D?warning message in MATLAB Online and Linux systems
Description: Warning messages might appear when displaying graphics under either of the following conditions: Your graphics code is running in MATLAB Online. Your graphics code is running on a Linux system, and your code calls the uifigure  function.  This message, or a similar one, displays in the command window: Warning: An error occurred while drawing the scene: Error in web pick traversal: IndexSizeError: Failed to execute 'getImageData' on 'CanvasRenderingContext2D': The source width is 0.

Summary: Unable to clone submodules of a Git? repository
Description: Cloning a Git? repository using the ssh protocol fails to clone the included submodules. The error message is: Unable to clone the submodule 'xxxxx'?  Git internal error (Code = -1, Class = 23): Failed to authenticate SSH session: Unable to open public key file?
Workaround: To clone a Git??submodule, set the public or private ssh key explicitly. In the Command Window, enter:?>> s = settings>> s.matlab.sourcecontrol.git.PrivateKeyFile.PersonalValue = '~/.ssh/id_rsa'>> s.matlab.sourcecontrol.git.PublicKeyFile.PersonalValue = '~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub'

Summary: Full Array 3D pattern is not plotting after switching 'Enable Coupling' option
Description: In Antenna Array Designer App, unable to plot the Full Array 3D Pattern when the user clears and again selects the 'Enable Coupling' option. The App still plots PatternNC plots, instead of Full Array 3D Pattern.

Summary: Code inspection fails if a model contains multiple instances of a subsystem from the same library with a referenced model inside it
Description: Simulink Code Inspector fails to verify the code generated from a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains multiple instances of a subsystem from the same library. The subsystem contains a referenced model.  Simulink Code Inspector is unable to uniquely map buffers in the model for the different subsystem instances to the corresponding buffers in the generated code. Code inspection fails with a "Failed to verify" ?status.

Summary: Error when using functions for importing or converting ONNX or Caffe models on Windows
Description: A MEX-file error can occur when using functions of the Deep Learning Toolbox Converter for ONNX Model Format or Deep Learning Toolbox Importer for Caffe Models support package in Windows. The reason is that the support package MEX-file is unable to find its corresponding DLL, which is located in the directory ˇ°<Support Package Root>/bin/win64ˇ±. The software returns the following error message for Deep Learning Toolbox Converter for ONNX Model Format.? Error using nnet.internal.cnn.onnx.onnxmexInvalid MEX-file'C:\ProgramData\MATLAB\SupportPackages\R2021a\toolbox\nnet\supportpackages\onnx\+nnet\+internal\+cnn\+onnx\onnxmex.mexw64':The specified procedure could not be found.Error in nnet.internal.cnn.onnx.ModelProto (line 32)?????????ModelPtr = onnxmex(int32(FuncName.EdeserializeFromFile),?filename);Error in nnet.internal.cnn.onnx.importONNXNetwork (line 7)modelProto???????????????????????= nnet.internal.cnn.onnx.ModelProto(Filename);  Functions affected by this bug are listed below. importONNXNetwork  importONNXLayers  importONNXFunction  exportONNXNetwork  importCaffeNetwork  importCaffeLayers?
Workaround: As a workaround, move the DLL to the directory where the MEX-file is located.If you use Deep Learning Toolbox Converter for ONNX Model Format, type the following at the MATLAB command line.?>> source = fullfile(matlabshared.supportpkg.getSupportPackageRoot, "bin", "win64", "onnxpb.dll");??>> destination = fullfile(matlabshared.supportpkg.getSupportPackageRoot, "toolbox", "nnet", "supportpackages", "onnx", "+nnet", "+internal", "+cnn", "+onnx", "onnxpb.dll");??>> movefile(source, destination);?If you use?Deep Learning Toolbox Importer for Caffe Models, type the following at the MATLAB command line.?>> source = fullfile(matlabshared.supportpkg.getSupportPackageRoot, "bin", "win64", "caffelib.dll");??>> destination = fullfile(matlabshared.supportpkg.getSupportPackageRoot, "toolbox", "nnet", "supportpackages", "caffe_importer", "+nnet", "+internal", "+cnn", "+caffe", "caffelib.dll");??>> movefile(source, destination);??

Summary: Generated code for bwlabel?can cause "Index exceeds array dimensions" error for images less than 50 pixels wide
Description: Generated code for the bwlabel ?function can cause an "Index exceeds array dimensions" error for images less than 50 pixels wide. The error looks like: Error using bwlabel>labelingWu_parallel (line 291)  ? ? Index exceeds array dimensions. Index value 44 exceeds valid range [1-43].  Error in bwlabel (line 63)  ? ? [L, numComponents] = labelingWu_parallel(BW, mode, M, N, nThread);

Summary: MATLAB crashes when you import an unnamed MDF file into the Simulation Data Inspector
Description: MATLAB crashes when you try to import into the Simulation Data Inspector an unnamed file with a supported MDF file extension.

Summary: Cloning, fetching, pushing and pulling?Git??repositories always prompt users to enter their username
Description: MATLAB??always prompts for a username when interacting with remote repositories that do not specify a username in their SSH URL. An example SSH URL that does not specify a username is ssh://server/project.git .
Workaround: To avoid prompts to enter your username, add the username to the SSH URL. For example, change?ssh://server/project.git to?ssh://user@server/project.git?

Summary: Objects not deleted after generating PDF reports
Description: When generating PDF documents, MATLAB Report Generator fails to delete some objects that are no longer needed .

Summary: Code generation may take a long time when too many mask variables are present a model
Description: HDL Code generation may be slow when there are large number of mask variables present in the model. The mask variables could be explicitly specified by the user at the subsystem level, or they could also be from?some Simulink block implementations.

Summary: Using gpuArrays with live scripts can crash MATLAB
Description: If you have a live script open in the MATLAB editor and create gpuArray   variables in the workspace, then MATLAB can crash.  The most common occurrence of this crash is when using live scripts with live controls. In rare cases, the crash can happen when the live script does not contain live controls For example, if you have a live script open in the MATLAB editor with a live control, such as a slider, then running the following code causes MATLAB to crash. A = complex(randn(1024,"gpuArray"),randn(1024,"gpuArray"));   sum(abs(A). ^2,1:2)  exit ?

Summary: MATLAB might crash and report an issue related to startup_plugins::initializer::BundleFilter::filter
Description: A user preference might become corrupted and prevent MATLAB from starting. In this case, the MATLAB crash report indicates that the crash is coming from?startup_plugins::initializer::BundleFilter::filter .
Workaround: Make sure that the failing release of MATLAB is first on the path or specify the full path to the MATLAB executable in the following commands.Run the following from the terminal/command prompt to locate the location of the prefdir for the release that is failing:matlab -batch prefdirNavigate to the prefdir?folder.Remove the file matlab.mlsettings.Run MATLAB to verify that it starts correctly.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect HDL code may be generated for a trigger subsystem with non-boolean trigger value
Description: When the Design Under Test (DUT) used for HDL Code generation has a triggered subsystem and the trigger port is driven by a signal of non-boolean datatype, generated HDL code is syntactically correct but logically incorrect.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 6, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Build failure for DDS applications that model as only Publishers or Subscribers when using eProsima
Description: A build failure occurs when the following conditions are true:  The DDS application model is configured to use the vendor eProsima. The model has one or more inports but does not have outports, or the model has one or more outports but does not have inports.

Summary: Simulink data dictionary gets dirty on compatibility check for models with parameters in .sldd
Description: When you run Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check on a model which uses parameters from multiple data dictionaries across the model reference hierarchy then the check leaves one or more data dictionaries in dirty state even though the actual contents remain unchanged.

Summary: When state Jacobians function is unspecified in a?nlmpcMultistage?controller with a discrete-time plant,?the?nlmpcmove?command?generates an error
Description: If you leave the?Model.StateJacFcn ?field of a?nlmpcMultistage ?object empty, and specify a discrete-time transition function in the?Model.StateFcn ?field, the software fails to use numerical perturbations to calculate the Jacobians.  This leads to an unexpected error when you later use?nlmpcmove ?to calculate the controller moves.?
Workaround: There is no workaround unless an analytical Jacobian function is specified in "Model.StateJacFcn", which might not be available.

Summary: Setting lsqnonlin options using?optimoptions errors in a deployed application
Description: When code that sets options for lsqnonlin  using the optimoptions  function is compiled to a standalone application, the resulting application throws an error at run time. For example, consider the following code: options = optimoptions('lsqnonlin','Algorithm','levenberg-marquardt');  When compiled to a standalone application, the following error is thrown: Invalid solver specified. Provide a solver name or handle (such as 'fmincon' or @fminunc).
Workaround: Use the optimset?function instead of optimoptions.The following code produces the intended effect from lsqnonlin compared to the previous example:options = optimset('Algorithm','levenberg-marquardt');Some option names differ between optimset  and?optimoptions. For name mappings, see?Current and Legacy Option Names.

Summary: Loss of review information after running a database clean up script
Description: If you perform a database cleanup by running the clean_project  command in a PSCAUTO script, the operation might delete the review information for some findings (Status , Severity , and comment) that you previously entered in the Result Details ?pane. For instance, if you run a script that keeps only the 10 most recent runs for a project (clean_project projectPath MAXRUNS 10 ), the upload of an 11th run to that project triggers the cleanup operation and deletes all review information for findings that you reviewed after the first run.

Summary: Delay in MATLAB Coder Workflow
Description: Any of the MATLAB Coder configuration workflows may experience a delay in its execution. This delay depends on the number of support packages installed in MATLAB or Simulink.

Summary: Incorrect red Correctness condition?check with message invalid function call on C++ code
Description: A Code Prover analysis of C++ code can show red Correctness condition  checks with the message invalid function call . The associated message in the result details mentions a pointer to function, even though the highlighted expression does not seem to contain a pointer to a function. The message might look like the following: Argument ... of call has type pointer to const array but function expects type pointer to array.  The issue can occur in specific situations on function calls with template-dependent arguments, such as: std::find(std::begin(...), std::end(...), 0);
Products: Polyspace Code Prover, Polyspace Code Prover Server

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Triggered Subsystem that has no HDL optimizations configured, generates an incorrect HDL code
Description: For a triggered subsystem inyour design that has no HDL optimization configured on it, and when applied with the following settings, ˇ®Minimize Global ResetˇŻ isturned ˇ®OnˇŻ. ˇ®No-reset registers initializationˇŻis set either to ˇ®Generate initialization inside moduleˇŻ or ˇ®Generate anexternal scriptˇŻ.  HDL Coder generates an incorrect HDL code which results in a simulation mismatchwith testbench. No warning message is generated for this problem.?
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: MATLAB might crash when unit mismatch exists between?Simulink.Signal object and port
Description: When you update the diagram, MATLAB might crash while propagating data types and dimensions when:? A unit is specified on a Simulink.Signal  object assigned to a signal. The unit does not match the unit specified at the source or destination port of the signal.  The crash report might contain output similar to: [? 0] 0x00007ffc28512fe3 sl_units_diagnostics.dll+00143331 MLTerminate_sl_units_diagnostics+00122563[? 1] 0x00007ffc2850803d sl_units_diagnostics.dll+00098365 MLTerminate_sl_units_diagnostics+00077597[? 2] 0x00007ffc2851bf41 sl_units_diagnostics.dll+00180033 MLTerminate_sl_units_diagnostics+00159265[? 3] 0x00007ffc2853d9d1 sl_units_diagnostics.dll+00317905 slUnitTable::reportUnitMismatch+00000577
Workaround: Change the Unit?field of the Simulink.Signal object, or the source or destination port so that the units are the same.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: The Model Advisor checks?checks for?na_0016, na_0018 and?jc_0801 need Stateflow license to appear in the Model Advisor
Description: The Model Advisor checks for mathworks.maab.na_0016, mathworks.maab.na_0018  and  mathworks.jmaab.jc_0801 ?do not appear in the Model Advisor if Stateflow license is missing, though it is not needed to check blocks in Simulink.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Output strings might not truncate to specified maximum length during rapid accelerator simulation
Description: When a model containing string blocks simulates in rapid accelerator mode, the string signals might not truncate to the maximum length specified by the data type. For example, a string signal of type str20  might have more than 20 characters. The issue affects only the rapid accelerator mode; other simulation modes and generated code do not have this issue.?

Summary: Generated code contains usage of dynamic memory allocation when DynamicMemoryAllocation is disabled
Description: When generating code for a MATLAB function that contains: A binary operation where one of the operands is a concatenation expression. The concatenation expression contains bounded variable-size data.  MATLAB Coder might produce generated code using dynamic memory allocation even if the DynamicMemoryAllocation  configuration option is disabled. For example when generating code for the program:? function out = issue_2462856(val,n)    boundedData1 = generateBoundedData(val,n,2);    boundedData2 = generateBoundedData(2*val,n,1);    boundedData3 = generateBoundedData(4*val,n,1);    out = boundedData1 .* [boundedData2,boundedData3];endfunction out = generateBoundedData(val,n,dim2)    coder.inline('never');    assert(n <= 255);    out = val .* (1:n)';    out = repmat(out,[1,dim2]);end by using these MATLAB commands: cfg = coder.config('lib');cfg.DynamicMemoryAllocation = 'off';codegen -config cfg issue_2462856 -args {1,1} the generated code incorrectly contains usages of emxArray  APIs that use dynamic memory allocation. See the call to?emxEnsureCapacity_real_T  in the following generated code snippet:[SNIP]  b_tmp_size = tmp_size[0];  loop_ub = tmp_size[0];  i = r->size[0] * r->size[1];  r->size[0] = tmp_size[0];  r->size[1] = 1;  emxEnsureCapacity_real_T(r, i);  for (i = 0; i < b_tmp_size; i++) {    r->data[i] = tmp_data[i];  }[SNIP]? See also Bug Report ID 2511529 ?
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b

Summary: When a ROS service client is called multiple times using the?call?function, successive response messages get appended and increase the array size continuously.
Description: If a field in the ROS service response message is of type array, the items of that array field get appended to existing data with every call. For example, FrameName  is an array field and it is assigned every time with a frame name as shown below:?% Assigned as 1x1 cell in the service call back function>> resp.FrameName{1} = 'ABC'; Using the?call ?function to call the service client 10 times, the correct response should be a 1x1 cell {'ABC'} .  But the size of?FrameName ?field increases with each call and reaches 10x1, as illustrated below:??>> resp.FrameName {'ABC'}{'ABC'}{'ABC'}{'ABC'}{'ABC'}{'ABC'}{'ABC'}{'ABC'}{'ABC'}{'ABC'}

Summary: Simulink Test Manager errors when using [Model Settings] as the Signal Editor override scenario
Description: In the Inputs section of a test case, if you enable?Signal Editor scenario ?and set the dropdown to [Model Settings] , running the test case? produces this error: The MAT-file must contain a valid Simulink.SimulationData.Dataset object
Workaround: Select the name of an override scenario from the?Signal Editor scenario?dropdown.

Summary: Launching Model Slicer to debug or inspect a Simulink Design Verifier objective displays an error when the model has a?Referenced Configuration Set
Description: In a model with Referenced Configuration Set, setting up Model Slicer for debugging or inspecting Simulink Design Verifier?objectives (Design Error Detection, Property Proving or Enhanced MCDC) fails due to the following error:?? Error using Simulink.SimulationInput/getSetParamObj  Model is using a configuration reference. Set 'EnableConfigSetRefUpdate' to 'on' to set the parameters on the referenced configuration set.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Text between angle brackets in MATLAB Function block code is missing from Simulink Web View
Description: Simulink Web View does not display the text between angle brackets (<> ) in MATLAB Function block code. For example, suppose a MATLAB Function block contains this code: function y = fcn(u)y = sprintf('<stdio.h>'); Simulink Web View displays: function y = fcn(u)y = sprintf('');

Summary: Crash when deleting a root-level Inport or Outport block from the PostLoadFcn model callback
Description: If a model with C or AUTOSAR code mapping has a model post-load function (PostLoadFcn) which deletes a?root-level Inport or Outport block, MATLAB might crash with a stack trace that looks like the following: ?[ 10] 0x00007ffccd387fd9 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020b\bin\win64\codemapping_c.dll+00425945 coderdictionary::getCoderDataSectionNames+00120729  [ 11] 0x00007ffccd37f99b C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020b\bin\win64\ codemapping_c.dll+00391579 coderdictionary::getCoderDataSectionNames+00086363  [ 12] 0x00007ffccd425d58 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020b\bin\win64\codemapping_c.dll+01072472 coderdictionary::ModelMappingBase::getInports+00000024?  [ 13] 0x00007ffccd3b311b C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020b\bin\win64\codemapping_c.dll+00602395 coderdictionary::CoderDictionaryMapping::getValidationErrMessage+00002187?

Summary: Parking curves do not retain node rotation or Create Inner Markings values on scene reload.
Description: When loading a RoadRunner scene containing a parking curve, any custom node markings on the corners and sides of the parking spaces rotate back to their default orientation (pointing north). If you edit the curve, the node markings rotate to their appropriate orientation, but the curve reverts back to its default state each time you reload the scene. Values set on the Create Inner Markings option also revert back to their original state on scene reload.

Summary: Incorrect output for export-function model in accelerator and rapid accelerator modes when logged signal or root Out Bus Element block uses union sample time
Description: When simulating an export-function model in accelerator or rapid accelerator mode, a root-level Out Bus Element block or a logged signal might output incorrect data for bus elements with union sample time in either of the following situations: The Out Bus Element block uses a union sample time, and the model contains at least one other root-level Out Bus Element block corresponding to the same bus. An element of the bus being logged using signal logging at the root-level uses a union sample time.

Summary: Image Labeler returns an error when you export labeled ground truth to a MAT-file or to a variable in the MATLAB workspace.
Description: The Image Labeler App returns an error when you try to export ground truth to a MAT-file or to a variable in the MATLAB workspace. The error occurs when you load a session that was created using MATLAB R2020b into a MATLAB R2021a Labeler session, and then try to export the ground truth. The app returns the following error warnings on the MATLAB command prompt:?Error using vision.internal.labeler.annotation.ROIAnnotationStructManager/export2table  Index exceeds the number of array elements (1).  Warning: Error occurred while evaluating listener callback.

Summary: MATLAB Job Scheduler (MJS) stops running jobs and tasks
Description: If the following conditions are met An MJS independent job has its?NumWorkersRange  property set to something with a finite maximum, e.g. 10  or [1 10] , and You create a new task on the job while it is running OR a task of the job fails and is rerun because the task's MaximumRetries  property is set to 1 or more,  then the MJS cluster might hang and stop scheduling jobs and tasks.? Consequently, any jobs and tasks that are waiting to be run or are subsequently submitted to the cluster to be run will not run.? No error is thrown on the MATLAB client; these jobs and tasks simply never start running.
Workaround: As a workaround, you can eitherAvoid setting the NumWorkersRange property of MJS independent jobs to a value with a finite maximum, orAvoid creating new tasks (i.e. using createTask()) on a running job AND set the MaximumRetries property of tasks of MJS independent jobs to 0

Summary: Mismatch in HDL and simulation outputs of the RS Decoder block for back-to-back input frames
Description: When you provide back-to-back input frames to the RS Decoder block, for a set of (N, K) RS codes such as (255, 239) and (127, 121), the generated HDL code may not match the simulation output.

Summary: Code generation might fail when the code formatting configuration setting?CodeFormattingTool?is set to 'Clang-format'
Description: This issue occurs if both ?these conditions are true: The  CodeFormattingTool ?property?in the? coder.CodeConfig  or  coder.EmbeddedCodeConfig ?object is set to  'Clang-format'. ?Alternatively, in the project build settings in the MATLAB Coder app, on the Code Appearance  tab, the?Select code format to use ?parameter is set to  Always use clang format .  The generated code contains two consecutive > ?characters which are preceded by a <  character . For example, "x < a|b >> 1 ". The < ?character must precede the operands and >> ?characters .   In such situations, code generation might fail with the following error message: error: expected expression before ˇ®>ˇŻ token?  For example, the pattern of the return  statement of the generated code shown here triggers this error message:?? int main() {??int x = 0;???int y = 0;???return x < y - 1 >> 1;} ? The 'Clang-format'  option incorrectly inserts a space between the consecutive >  characters and produces this line of code:? return x<y - 1> > 1;?  This bug is caused by a known clang-format  issue captured in?https://bugs.llvm.org/show_bug.cgi?id=49868 . This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: Do any of the following:?Disable the use of the clang-format tool by setting the CodeFormattingTool?property to 'MathWorks'. Alternatively,?in the MATLAB Coder app,?set the?Select code format to use?parameter to Always use MathWorks code format tool.Alternatively, use a build of clang-format that includes the patch in?https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/commit/8fa56f7ededcbe28cbbb810b5d261b72ab0d8035.

Summary: Uninstalling a previous version of a toolbox that includes apps fails when multiple versions of the toolbox are installed
Description: If multiple versions of a toolbox that includes apps are installed, uninstalling a previous version of the toolbox fails silently.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Executables generated using clock-forwarding can output erroneous clock-times at block boundaries.
Description: When running a clock-forwarding executable (.dll or .so) in an EDA tool that supports IBIS BIRD204 and BIRD209, the clock-times output by the executable can contain invalid values. This only occurs when transitioning between wave_size blocks in GetWave and can occur when Rx_Use_Clock_Input  is set to either "Times " or "Waves ". This has no affect on the operation of the clock-forwarding model in Simulink.  The erroneous clock-times can be seen by  plotting the clock-times generated by the executable and looking for any non-monotonic sections on the y -axis.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation might occur for Simulink model when a data dictionary hierarchy is closed between simulations
Description: Simulation might result in a silent wrong answer when the following conditions are satisified: A model links to a data dictionary hierarchy. For example, a model links to adata dictionary, A.sldd , that links to data dictionary B.sldd .? The model uses a variable that is defined in the data dictionary hierarchy, but not in a dictionary that the model is directly linked to. For example, B.sldd .  ?Under these conditions, the silent wrong answer can occur when you take these actions:  Perform an update diagram or simulate the model. Close the data dictionaries by calling the function Simulink.data.dictionary.closeAll() . Modify entries defined in B.sldd  that are used by the model. Perform an update diagram or simulate the model again.
Workaround: To prevent incorrect simulation you can take either of these actions:Avoid using Simulink.data.dictionary.closeAll() between model compilations or simulations.Close model after calling Simulink.data.dictionary.closeAll().??
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Using?collisionMesh function leaks memory and can crash MATLAB
Description: Using the?collisionMesh ?object in a loop will leak memory in MATLAB and can cause MATLAB to crash with the following message in the crash log: Stack Trace (from fault):  [ 0] 0x00007ff9d5705975 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020b\bin\win64\libmwcollisionbuiltins.dll+00022901 shared_robotics::CollisionGeometry::CollisionGeometry+00001541  [ 1] 0x00007ff9d570bacd C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020b\bin\win64\libmwcollisionbuiltins.dll+00047821 shared_robotics::CollisionGeometry::sign+00010125  [ 2] 0x00007ff989574869 bin\win64\pgo\mcos_impl.dll+00346217  [ 3] 0x00007ff9895747e4 bin\win64\pgo\mcos_impl.dll+00346084
Workaround: Replace calls to collisionMesh?with approximate geometries such as a collisionBox, collisionSphere, or collisionCylinder.

Summary: Training a convolutional neural network on an Ampere card may result in a CUDNN_STATUS_BAD_PARAM error
Description: Training a network on a Linux system using Ampere cards can produce a  CUDNN_STATUS_BAD_PARAM  error during the backwards convolution. This error can occur when training using either the  trainNetwork ?function or a custom training loop with a  dlnetwork  object.
Workaround: To enable successful backwards convolution, disable tensor cores execution on the Ampere card:Open a terminal prompt and run setenv NVIDIA_TF32_OVERRIDE 0Restart MATLAB

Summary: MATLAB crashes or hangs when a v4 MAT-file is selected in the MATLAB Current Folder Browser window
Description: When a user selects a v4 MAT-file in the MATLAB Current Folder Browser  window  to view or list its contents in the MATLAB details window , the MATLAB session crashes or hangs.

Summary: When using the locally installed documentation on a localized system, the Help browser shows English content instead of the localized content
Description: On a localized system, if the documentation location is set to "Installed locally", the Help browser shows the English content instead of the localized content. The problem persists even after restarting MATLAB. This is due to problems with localization initialization.
Workaround: To work around this issue in the current MATLAB session, update the Help preferences and set the "Documentation Location" to "Web, on mathworks.com" then switch back to "Installed Locally".

Summary: Block name containing'/' character produces connection error during XCP external mode simulation
Description: Suppose your model contains configuration parameters with these settings: DefaultParameterBehavior : 'Tunable'  ExtModeTransport : 'XCP on TCP/IP' or 'XCP on Serial'   If the model has a block with tunable parameters and the block name contains the?'/' ?character, when you run the external mode simulation, the connection to the target executable file fails. For example, if the model modelName? contains a Constant block and the block name is set to Num/Den , the simulation produces this error: External Mode Open Protocol Connect command failed   Caused by:   External Mode Open Protocol SetParam command failed?   Unable to find parameter 'Value' of block on path 'modelName/Num//Den  '.?
Workaround: Modify the block name by removing the '/' character.

Summary: Row names in a table UI component might not populate if the table is created with empty data
Description: The RowName  property appears to have no effect when the table UI component is created with a Data  property that contains no data, such as in this code: t = uitable(uifigure, 'Data', [], 'RowName', 'numbered');
Workaround: 1. Set RowName after setting Data.or?2. Toggle the RowName property to something different and back to the desired row name.? For example, if RowName?is 'numbered', set RowName to empty and then back to 'numbered':tbl.RowName =? [];drawnow;?tbl.RowName = 'numbered';?

Summary: Watermark image covers text content in PDF documents generated by MATLAB Report Generator
Description: In PDF documents generated by MATLAB Report Generator, a watermark image covers the text content unless the image is transparent. For example, in the PDF document generated by the following code, the sine graph watermark image covers some of the "Hello World" paragraphs. plot(sin(0:0.001:4*pi))print -dpng watermark.pngd = mlreportgen.dom.Document('test','pdf')open(d)d.CurrentPageLayout.Watermark = mlreportgen.dom.Watermark('watermark.png')for i = 1:20    append(d, mlreportgen.dom.Paragraph('Hello World'))endclose(d)rptview(d)

Summary: DOCX report fields not updated
Description: If you do not use rptview  to view a DOCX report, its generated report fields, such as chapter numbers, are not updated. The same problem occurs when you generate a DOCX report from a standalone, compiled application.

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0736?displays an incorrect warning for conditional and loop statements in state actions
Description: Model Advisor?check?jc_0736 :?Uniform indentations in Stateflow blocks ?displays the following warning for conditional and loop statements in state actions.Warning :? The following Stateflow states do not have uniform indentation.  This warning message is incorrect as Stateflow performs auto-indentation for conditional and loop statements.
