The problem occurs due to an incorrect speed check implemented in these models that verifies whether the motor is running.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model containing a conditional subsystem that inherits initial conditions for output values
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:? The model contains a conditional subsystem. The output of the conditional subsystem inherits the initial condition and feeds into a Unit Delay block.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the conditional subsystem and the Unit Delay block, and select the?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization?property of the Signal Conversion block.

Summary: Assertion error when generating code for a model that computes the modulus of a negative value in a symbolic size expression
Description: When generating code for a model that evaluates the modulus of negative numbers in symbolic dimension for ports, MATLAB might cause an assertion error.  For example, suppose A and B are symbolic dimensions and the?Input Port  dimensions in Signal Properties ?is configured as? [((rem(-(A+B),C)))+9 4] .?When you build the model, you get this error: CGIR assertion 'In propCast: Assertion failed: propType.hasSameSize(inputType)' failed in 'utils/rtwcg_prop.cpp:2636'

Summary: Error when alternating coverage collection between normal simulation mode and SIL/PIL
Description: If you alternate coverage collection between normal simulation mode and SIL/PIL, and the model contains variants that changed between simulations, errors similar to the following might occur:? failed assertion in b:\matlab\toolbox\slcoverage\src\slvnv\utils\object.cpp at line 2525:  object[id]!=NULL  [ 0] 0x00007ffa82c38ff7 D:\ProgramFiles\MATLAB\R2020b\bin\win64\sl_lang_blocks.dll+08949751 SubsystemBlock::sDefineCoverage+00000215  [ 1] 0x00007ffa7f2aaaea D:\ProgramFiles\MATLAB\R2020b\bin\win64\sl_compile.dll+01682154 slcomp::BlockDoPostPropagationTasks+00000490  [ 2] 0x00007ffa7f2aaeaa D:\ProgramFiles\MATLAB\R2020b\bin\win64\sl_compile.dll+01683114 slcomp::DoBlockSpecificPostPropagationTasks+00000138 ?
Products: Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Coverage, Simulink Check

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated for MATLAB Function block calling Simulink Function block
Description: Incorrect generated code is possible when a model includes: Simulink Function block that writes to a global variable. MATLAB Function block inside an Atomic Subsystem and Function packaging  of the Atomic Subsystem is set to Reusable function . MATLAB Function Block has a loop that first calls the Simulink Function block and then reads the global variable.  The incorrect generated code has the read of global variable hoisted outside the loop. For example in the MATLAB code below, inside for  loop, the function writesG1  which writes to global variable g1  is called before reading from g1 . for i = 1:1:10    writesG1();    out(10) = g1(4);end Here is an example of incorrect generated code. The global variable rtd_g1  is read before the for  loop. The code is incorrect because we have changed the order in which the global variable is read and written. localB->out[9] = rtd_g1[3];  for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) {   example_writesG1();} The correct generated code is:? for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) {    example_writesG1();    localB->out[9] = rtd_g1[3];} For a model example that the bug occurs, see the example.zip  attached.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing a Data Store Memory block and a Function-Call Subsystem block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions:The model contains a Data Store Memory block and a Function-Call Subsystem block. The read or the write operation to the Data Store Memory block occurs inside the Function-Call Subsystem.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur when the output of a Stateflow chart inherits properties from a signal object
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:?The model contains a Stateflow chart. The output of the chart inherits properties from a signal object and feeds into a Unit Delay block.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the Stateflow chart and the Unit Delay block, and select the?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization?property of the Signal Conversion block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model?consisting of cascading Unit Delay and Probe blocks
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:The model contains a Unit Delay block and a Probe block. The output of the Probe block feeds into the Unit Delay block but does not match its Initial condition ?parameter value.

Summary: Ground stations cannot be added to the satellite scenario after performing certain actions on the satellite scenario
Description: Ground stations cannot be added to the satellite scenario after performing any one of the following: Calling any of the following methods: play  accessIntervals?  accessPercentage  accessStatus  without time  input? gimbalAngles  without time  input? states  without time  input? aer  without time  input? ebno  without time  input? linkIntervals  linkPercentage?  linkStatus  without time input?  Clicking the play ?button on the satellite scenario viewer   This bug is present in Aerospace Toolbox and Satellite Communications Toolbox. As an example, running the following code will result in an error:  >> sc = satelliteScenario;  >> sat = satellite(sc, 1e7, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0);  >> gs = groundStation(sc);  >> viewer = satelliteScenarioViewer(sc);  >> play(sc);  >> gs2 = groundStation(sc, "Latitude", 0, "Longitude", 0);?  Error using horzcat  Number of fields in structure arrays being concatenated do not match. Concatenation of structure arrays requires that these arrays have the same set of  fields.    Error in matlabshared.satellitescenario.internal.Simulator/addGroundStation (line 119)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? simObj.GroundStations = [existingGroundStations, gs];    Error in matlabshared.satellitescenario.GroundStation (line 472)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? simID = addGroundStation(simulator, lat(idx), ...    Error in satelliteScenario/groundStation (line 290)  gs = matlabshared.satellitescenario.GroundStation(names, latitude, ...
Workaround: Completely set up the scenario first, and then call the listed functions or interact with the play button of the satellite scenario viewer only when there are no more ground stations to be added.

Summary: Custom System Target File parameters might be loaded incorrectly from a MAT file
Description: When you load a configuration set object from a MAT file, some parameters might be reset to the default value. This might happen when all of these? conditions are true: The configuration set uses a custom System Target File (for example, dSPACE System Target File, dsrt.tlc). The MAT file storing the configuration set was created in an older version of MATLAB.

Summary: Static code metrics report shows an error
Description: The static code metrics report might not generate. Instead, the report shows the following error. Static Code Metrics Report not generated due to an internal error.Error using textscanFirst input can not be empty. Expected a non-empty character vector or a valid file-id.?

Summary: Standalone applications that use functionality from the Computer Vision Toolbox might fail to run on a Windows operating system
Description: Standalone applications that use functionality from the Computer Vision Toolbox might fail to run on a Windows operating system. The error is due to shared library conflicts that might arise in some Windows based deployment environments. Error messages might vary. Some example error messages are:? Error: "Bundle#280 start failed: Loading <MATLAB Runtime Root>\bin\win64\builtins\vision_builtins\mwvision_builtinimpl.dll failed with error: The specified procedure could not be found."  From Computer Vision toolbox:?Error using detectSURFFeatures>checkImage (line 128) Invalid function name '_visionIsObject'.?

Summary: Code inspection fails when a buffer is defined in an if statement and it changes the data type after the if statement
Description: If a buffer is defined inside the body of an if statement and it changes the data type after the if statement, then code inspection might fail with a "Failed to Verify " status.?For example, consider this code:? if (c) {   t =  int16(u); }y  = cast(t, 'int8') Here, the buffer t  is defined in an if statement. After the if statement, the data type of the buffer is changed from int16  to int8 . Code inspection might fail for this code.

Summary: createInputDataset?function returns error for models that use both Inport and In Bus Element blocks to load data for a bus
Description: The createInputDataset  function errors when you use it to create an input Dataset ?object for a model that loads bus data using both Inport and In Bus Element blocks.

Summary: A timetable with scalar values cannot be imported as a signal into the Diagnostic Feature Designer app
Description: In the?New Session ?dialog of the?Diagnostic Feature Designer ?app, the app interprets a timetable with scalar values as a feature by default. Although a checkbox allows the user to specify that the app import the timetable as a signal, the app does not honor that selection, and always imports the scalar data in the timetable as a feature.?
Workaround: Convert?the data in the timetable into a cell array and then create a table with the cell array as an input. You will then be able to import the timetable data as signal into the Diagnostic Feature Designer app. For example:tt = timetable(seconds(0:100)',(0:100)');?? ?%If this is a timetable that you want to importtt = table({tt});??? %Use this command to change the tt to table format and then import it within the DFD app.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation results from subscripted assignment of a bus array to a contiguous region of block output or data store of bus array type
Description: You might get incorrect results when simulating a model containing a MATLAB Function block, a Stateflow chart using MATLAB as the action language, or a MATLAB System block, when both of the following conditions are met:  The block or chart uses a variable that is a Simulink block output or a data store which is an array of?Simulink.Bus ?type. The block or chart uses subscripted assignment to assign an array of buses to a contiguous region of this variable.    For instance, either of the following lines of code, where aFcnReturnBusVector  is an internally defined MATLAB function that returns an array of buses: outputBusArray(:, 1) = aFcnReturnBusVector;  outputBusArray(2:10, 1) = aFcnReturnBusVector;?   In such scenarios, only the first element in the contiguous region of outputBusArray  might be updated.

Summary: The?los?function returns false for some low-elevation sites
Description: The?los ?function ?returns false when one or both of the input sites has a negative height value with respect to the WGS84 ellipsoid model. When this issue occurs, the?los? plot will appear red (indicating non-line-of-sight conditions) despite no obstacle appearing along the visibility path. This bug affects the?los ? function in Antenna Toolbox or Communications Toolbox.
Workaround: To compute line-of-sight visibility, use the?raytrace?function to create a?comm.Ray?object. Then, query the?LineOfSight?property of the object. Because the?raytrace?function returns?comm.Ray?objects in ascending order of the number of reflections, you only need to query the first object. This code determines whether line-of-sight visibility exists between a transmitter site?tx?and a receiver site?rx.pm = propagationModel("raytracing-image-method","MaxNumReflections",0);ray = raytrace(tx,rx,pm);status = ray{1}(1).LineOfSight;

Summary: Crash when generating code from export-function model with root-level non-function-call blocks in a loop
Description: In the root level of an export-function model, ?nonvirtual blocks must be part of an exported function or have?a constant rate. If your export-function model contains one more more blocks that violate this rule, and those blocks form a loop through graphical connections, MATLAB may crash when you attempt to generate code from your model.

Summary: To use MATLAB, Simulink Real-Time, and Simulation Data Inspector requires Simulink
Description: When using MATLAB and Simulink Real-Time to observe signals logged to the Simulation Data Inspector, an error reports that Simulink is required.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Option Ensure outport is virtual?not honored in generated code for root Outport block when driven by a signal with storage class
Description: Incorrect code generated from a model when all of the following conditions are present: The model contains a root Outport block with option Ensure outport is virtual  selected. The signal driving the input port of the root Outport block resolves to a Simulink.Signal  object. The StorageClass ?property of the CoderInfo  property of the Simulink.Signal  object has a value other than Auto .   In this situation the generated code should combine the memory for the root Outport block and the memory specified by the Simulink.Signal  object. However,?the root Outport block incorrectly makes an extra copy of input signal.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Test bench generation may fail when the bus selector block output signal having a? name is connected to the bus creator block.
Description: If the model has a bus selector block with its output signal having a name, and this output signal is connected to a bus creator block, in the generated model bus element names in the bus creator block will be different from those in the original model. This might cause failure in HDL test bench generation.
Workaround: In the generated model, manually update the names of the output signal of the bus selector block. These names should be the same as in the original model.

Summary: Incorrect error message when generating test cases for Simulink Test harness using existing coverage data
Description: If the user is generating test cases for a Simulink Test harness using Simulink Design Verifier and existing coverage data for the harness is specified, Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails with the following error message: The coverage objectives defined by the initial cvdata object STARTCOV are not consistent with coverage objectives on the model <harness_name>

Summary: Attempting to export new features that are computed after loading a saved session into Diagnostic Feature Designer results in an error
Description: After loading a saved session into the?Diagnostic Feature Designer ?(DFD) app, the user can compute new features using the loaded session data. However, if the user then selects a feature table that contains the new features and attempts to either create a feature table view plot or export the new features to the?Classification Learner  app, the DFD app generates an error.
Workaround: After computing new features, save the session. Then, load the session back into the DFD app. This sequence allows the features to then be plotted or exported without the error described in the previous sections.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for?polyfit might return different results than MATLAB for badly conditioned problems
Description: If a problem is badly conditioned, the code generated for the?polyfit  function might produce different results than the polyfit  function in MATLAB.

Summary: Cannot see base stereotype properties for interfaces in the Property Inspector
Description: If a stereotype is applied to an interface that has no stereotype properties, and inherits from a different stereotype that does have properties, the Property Inspector does not show the full list of properties when the interface is selected.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model containing a conditionally executed subsystem
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains a conditionally executed subsystem with multiple output ports. One of the output ports connects to a Terminator block located outside of the conditionally executed subsystem. At least one of the other output ports of the conditionally executed subsystem?has a different initial condition than the output port connected to the Terminator block.

Summary: Signal Routing distorted with load_system on a model containing Subsystem Reference block
Description: The input signals to the Subsystem Reference  block is distorted when the following conditions are TRUE:?Subsystem Reference ?block has few input and output signals. load_system ?used before open_system  command to open the model.  The input signal lines to the Subsystem Reference  block have incorrect layout, and when the spacebar is pressed to autofit the same, the zooming turns to 0%.?
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: Requirements comments in generated code do not show linked requirement summary
Description: When you generate code with requirements comments and the linked requirements are managed in .slreqx  files, the generated code displays text?Link #1 ?or  Link #2 , etc. ?instead of showing the ID and/or summary of the linked requirement. The generated code contains incomplete information even if the correct ID and summary appears when you right-click the linked Simulink object and select the Requirements ?context menu.
Workaround: You can manually populate the desired link destination label into the Description field of each link. You can edit this field in the Requirements Editor when you click Show Links, or you can use APIs. The script will need to iterate all links returned by slreq.find('type','Link'). You can update the Description field with the following:destinationInfo = link(i).destination; link.Description = destinationInfo.summary;

Summary: CustomFloat may not correctly convert to double or single for some values of ExponentLength
Description: When converting from CustomFloat  to double  or single , the converted value may be different than the original value.  For conversion to double , this occurs when the CustomFloat  has ExponentLength >= 12 , and one of ExponentReal  or ExponentImag == 2047 .  For conversion to single , this occurs when the CustomFloat  has ExponentLength >= 9 , and one of ExponentReal  or ExponentImag == 255 . Note that this bug may also impact the value that is displayed for a CustomFloat , but the underlying data stored by CustomFloat  is accurate. For example:? >> y = CustomFloat(1, 64, 51) % display a value converted from double-precision  y =?  ? ?2.2204e-16  ?  ? ? ? ? ? ?Data Type: Floating-point: Custom-precision  ? ? ? ? ? WordLength:? 64  ? ? ? MantissaLength:? 51  ? ? ? ExponentLength:? 12  ? ? ? ? ExponentBias: 2047?  >> d = double(y)  d =  ? ?2.2204e-16?  >> y + y % show that the stored value is actually accurate  ans =?  ? ? ?2   ? ? ? ? ? ?Data Type: Floating-point: Custom-precision  ? ? ? ? ? WordLength:? 64  ? ? ? MantissaLength:? 51  ? ? ? ExponentLength:? 12  ? ? ? ? ExponentBias: 2047?

Summary: Clicking the Help button for Binary Vector Conversion and Clock Jitter Measurement blocks do not open documentation.
Description: Clicking the Help  button for Binary Vector Conversion and Clock Jitter Measurement blocks?do not lead to their respective documentation pages.

Summary: HERE HD Live Map Reader errors when reading the AdasAttributes layer for multiple tiles
Description: When you use the hereHDLMReader  object to read the AdasAttributes  layer for more than one tile of data, MATLAB??displays an error that the downloaded Protobuf binary files are not in GZIP format.? Error using gunzip>gunzipwrite (line 217)   File "C:/filepath/AdasAttributes_12345.pb.gz"?is not in GZIP format.
Workaround: Create a new reader and read the AdasAttributes layer for each tile in the route. This code shows how to read the AdasAttributes layer for tiles in a sample route.route = load('geoSequenceNatickMA.mat');lat = route.latitude;lon = route.longitude;reader = hereHDLMReader(lat,lon);adasAttributes = {};for?idx = 1:numel(reader.TileIds)??r = hereHDLMReader(reader.TileIds(idx));??adasAttributes{idx} = read(r,'AdasAttributes');end

Summary: MATLAB crashes with custom table parameter in masking
Description: A custom table parameter with empty value crashes if the callbacks tries to query for number of rows or columns for the table data.An example of the crash snippet:? [? 0] 0x00007ffb13863a2a Z:\matlab\bin\win64\SimulinkBlock.dll+01325610 SLCustomTableParamData::numberOfRows+00000010 [? 1] 0x00007ffad7cca672 Z:\matlab\bin\win64\SimulinkMask.dll+00566898  [? 2] 0x00007ffb344f19c1 Z:\matlab\bin\win64\mcos_impl.dll+03348929 mcos::impl::terminateFcn+01050481  [? 3] 0x00007ffb344f244c Z:\matlab\bin\win64\mcos_impl.dll+03351628 mcos::impl::terminateFcn+01053180  [? 4] 0x00007ffb344f2631 Z:\matlab\bin\win64\mcos_impl.dll+03352113 mcos::impl::terminateFcn+01053665  [? 5] 0x00007ffb344f58bf Z:\matlab\bin\win64\mcos_impl.dll+03365055 mcos::impl::terminateFcn+01066607  [? 6] 0x00007ffb345f2e36 Z:\matlab\bin\win64\mcos_impl.dll+04402742 namedArgsToCell+00010134  [? 7] 0x00007ffb3460c871 Z:\matlab\bin\win64\mcos_impl.dll+04507761 namedArgsToCell+00115153  [? 8] 0x00007ffb72b523f8 Z:\matlab\bin\win64\mcos.dll+00140280 omDirectCallMethod+00000072 ?

Summary: Error during simulation of SOC Blockset coming from Memory Channel block: "Too many iterations while trying to stabilize state."
Description: When simulating model with large frame size creating more than 1000 bursts in Memory Channel block, the simulation will stop with the following error message: Too many iterations while trying to stabilize state
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: SimBiology Model Analyzer app sporadically stalls when rerunning a fit program
Description: The SimBiology Model Analyzer  might sporadically stall when rerunning a fit program if the project has a boxplot of the estimated parameters.
Workaround: Delete the existing boxplot before rerunning the fit program.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when moving an area with unconnected lines
Description: Moving an area by dragging it to a new location might cause MATLAB to crash if lines that extend outside the area are unconnected.?
Workaround: Delete the unconnected lines before moving the area.?

Summary: Incorrect SID and GraphicalName for bus element ports in data interfaces returned when using Code Descriptor API
Description: The DataInterface  object returned when using the Code Descriptor API contains properties such as SID  and GraphicalName  that describe the modeling element for which the data interface was created. For example,? >>  dataInterface = codeDescriptor.getDataInterfaces('Inports');   ans =   DataInterface with properties:              Type: [1x1 coder.descriptor.types.Type]               SID: 'rtwdemo_comments:99'     GraphicalName: 'In1'       VariantInfo: [1x0 coder.descriptor.VariantInfo]    Implementation: [1x1 coder.descriptor.DataImplementation]            Timing: [1x1 coder.descriptor.TimingInterface]              Unit: ''             Range: [1x0 coder.descriptor.Range] The SID  and GraphicalName  properties of the root Inport data interface may be incorrect when the root Inports in the block diagram are bus element ports.?

Summary: Generating code for concatenation operation inside parfor loop might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash during code generation if all ?these conditions are true:? Your MATLAB code contains a parfor  loop The body of the parfor  loop contains a concatenation operation In this operation, the dimensions that are not concatenated are variable-size  For example, the following MATLAB function is affected by this issue: function result = main(arg)result = [];parfor i = 1:arg    result = [result; zeros(1, i)];endend This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.

Summary: queryRequirements() object function for oslc.core.QueryCapability?object disregards query parameter
Description: After you create and authenticate an?oslc.Client , you can create an? oslc.core.QueryCapability ?object to query the service provider for available resources. You can use object functions like? queryRequirements() ?to query for available requirement resources. Additionally, you can use set? setQueryParameter () to apply a parameter to filter the query. However, when you use?setQueryParameter () with?queryRequirements() , the parameter does not filter the query correctly. Instead, all available requirement resources are returned by the query.

Summary: Aerospace Blockset Orbit Propagator block might output inaccurate results when Central body?is set to other than Earth?and State Output Coordinate Frame?is set to Fixed-frame
Description: Aerospace Blockset?Orbit Propagator ?block might output inaccurate results when?Central body ?is set to a value other than?Earth ?and?State Output Coordinate Frame ?is set to?Fixed-frame . ?These results do not account for historical leap seconds in the internal conversion between Universal Coordinated Time (UTC) and Terrestrial Time (TT). Since 1972, leap seconds are added to UTC as necessary.

Summary: Screenshot not copied to clipboard after selecting Screenshot?on a Mac
Description: On a Mac, selecting?Screenshot ?on the Format ?tab of the Simulink Toolstrip does not copy bitmap data to the clipboard.

Summary: Error when compiling an S-function generated with the Legacy Code Tool
Description: Compiling an S-function using the Legacy Code Tool may error out with "expected expression before ';' token " when all the following conditions are met: The S-function is using a Simulink.Bus  whose corresponding C struct type is defined as a header file. The C struct type has many scalar and array fields that are interleaved. The option convertNDArrayToRowMajor  is set to true.

Summary: Error when using Create Test for Model Component wizard in Test Manager for components inside function-call subsystems
Description: In the Create Test for Model Component wizard, when you specify a component that is inside a function-call subsystem, this error occurs: Specified component is within a triggered, enabled, function call or iterator subsystem. Automatic test creation is not allowed for such subsystems.

Summary: HDL code generation may fail for a subsystem containing a referenced model with certain bus configurations when traceability is enabled
Description: HDL code generation may fail with below error message for a Design Under Test (DUT) when the following conditions are met  The DUT has a referenced model? The referenced model has a bus-assignment block connected with bus element ports Generate traceability report  is enabled in the Report Options  node under HDL Code Generation  in Configurations Parameters dialog  The error message is: <Error using coder. internal. getNameForBlock The model 'DUT' must be compiled before it can be accessed programmatically>?

Summary: Execution of JIT MEX function might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: In a MATLAB session where no MATLAB Coder or Simulink commands have been run, the execution of a JIT MEX function generated by MATLAB Coder might cause MATLAB to crash.?? For example, this crash might happen if you perform these actions: Start MATLAB Generate a JIT MEX function Restart MATLAB Execute the JIT MEX function generated in step 2
Workaround: Do?any one of these:Generate a C/C++ MEX function instead of a JIT MEX functionBefore executing the JIT MEX function, execute a MATLAB Coder command like coder.typeof(0);?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for the rcond function might return NaN instead of 0 for some inputs
Description: For nearly-singular matrices, the code generated for?rcond ?might return NaN  in cases where MATLAB returns 0 . This behavior is caused by floating-point overflow in intermediate calculations.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Discontiguous index error when assigning multiple variables into a table or timetable
Description: When you assign?multiple variables into a table or timetable using parentheses and an array of variable subscripts, MATLAB might raise this error message, even when the syntax is correct. Cannot create a table variable with a discontiguous index. In some cases, this error occurs when the subscripts are nonconsecutive but otherwise valid. For example, in this code the first line creates a table with three variables. The assignment in the second line specifies a fourth table variable using an array of subscripts ([1 3 4] ) that does not refer to the existing second table variable. This syntax is valid. But instead of returning a table with four variables, MATLAB raises an error. >> T = table({'a'},{'b'},{'c'});>> T(:,[1 3 4]) = table({'x'},{'y'},{'z'})Cannot create a table variable with a discontiguous index.
Workaround: You can avoid this error by splitting the assignment into two separate assignments. First make an assignment that overwrites existing variables. Then make a second assignment that adds new variables to the table or timetable.For example, the assignment in this code, using [1 2 5 6] as variable subscripts, raises the error message.>> T = table({'a'},{'b'},{'c'},{'d'});>> T(:,[1 2 5 6]) = table({'w'},{'x'},{'y'},{'z'})Cannot create a table variable with a discontiguous index.Split this assignment into two assignments. First overwrite the first two variables.>> T = table({'a'},{'b'},{'c'},{'d'});>> T(:,[1 2]) = table({'w'},{'x'})T =  1ˇÁ4 table    Var1     Var2     Var3     Var4     _____    _____    _____    _____    {'w'}    {'x'}    {'c'}    {'d'}Then add the fifth and sixth variables as new table variables.>> T(:,[5 6]) = table({'y'},{'z'})T =  1ˇÁ6 table    Var1     Var2     Var3     Var4     Var5     Var6     _____    _____    _____    _____    _____    _____    {'w'}    {'x'}    {'c'}    {'d'}    {'y'}    {'z'}If the variable subscripts are not hardcoded, you might have to write more code to avoid this error. For example, in this line of code the variable subscripts?varsTarget and varsSource are numeric vectors.>> T1(:,varsTarget) = T2(:,varsSource);If the error occurs at this line of code, you can replace it with a for-loop that assigns the table variables one at a time.for i = 1:length(varsTarget)    T1(:,varsTarget(i)) = T2(:,varsSource(i));end

Summary: MATLAB crashes when you use the From Workspace block to load large data.
Description: MATLAB crashes when you use the From Workspace block to load data that occupies more than 2147483647  bytes of memory. You can determine whether your data exceeds this size by multiplying the product of the data dimensions by the memory requirement for the data type. For example, if you load a 400 -by-200  double matrix signal that contains 10000  samples, this is the calculation: 400 * 200 * 10000 * 8 bytes = 6.4e9 bytes   Because 6.4e9  bytes is greater than 2147483647  ( 2.14e9 )  bytes, loading this entire signal using the From Workspace block will result in a crash. If the simulation duration is shorter than the time range of the signal data, the crash may not occur. To determine the amount of the signal you can load before a crash occurs, find the index of the sample that exceeds 2147483647  bytes by dividing 2147483647  by the memory required for one sample in the signal: >>oneSampleSize = 400 * 200 * 8;  >>crashIndex = ceil(2147483647/oneSampleSize)  >>crashIndex =       3356   If the simulation time range only uses signal values before the time at index?3356 , the crash does not occur. You can determine the memory used by the signal data type by assigning one element of a sample to a variable and then using the whos  function to display information about the variable. For example, if the data uses the structure format, use this code: >>oneValue = data.signals.values(1);  >>  whos oneValue  >>ans =       Name? ? ? ?Size            Bytes  Class     Attributes    one_sample      1x1? 8? ? ?double   ?The memory used by this data type (double ) is 8 bytes.
Workaround: Use an Inport block at the root-level of the model to load the data instead of the From Workspace block.

Summary: Models containing Drive Cycle Source blocks with?Output sample period?set to non-zero values less than?1s can take from minutes to several hours to open
Description: If you set the Drive Cycle Source block Output sample period (0 for continuous)  parameter to a non-zero values less than 1s, models with the block can take from minutes to several hours to open. The opening time depends on the drive cycle length and sample period. The model does open, however, the wait time might be prohibitive.
Workaround: To control the output sample rate, consider using a Rate Transition blocks. Specifically, use Rate Transition blocks before the velocity input and immediately after the Drive Cycle Source outputs the signals. The Rate Transition blocks allow you to specify a sample time for the model that is different from the Drive Cycle Source block.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Short name uniqueness error after consecutive builds of AUTOSAR component model that inherits architecture model XML options
Description: Consecutive builds of a component model in an?architecture model hierarchy?might create duplicate parameter interfaces in the AUTOSAR dictionary if all of the following conditions are true: The component model inherits XML options from its parent architecture model. The component model uses an AUTOSAR.Parameter  object with a parameter interface. The parameter interface specified on the AUTOSAR.Parameter  object does not match the interface package specified in the XML options.  Model validation or code generation might display an error like the following: The call to autosar_make_rtw_hook,?during the entry hook generated the following error:Short name 'MyParameter' (ParameterInterface /MyModel/Interfaces/MyParameter) failed uniqueness tests,because short name 'MyParameter' (ParameterInterface /MyModel/Interfaces/MyParameter) already exists.AUTOSAR constraint 2508 (CONSTR_2508) requires uniqueness tests to be case insensitive.To comply with the constraint, change one of the short names.

Summary: Computing model checksum during simulation and code generation takes a long time for some model configurations
Description: For some model configurations, simulation or code generation can take a long time and may appear to be stuck when the status in the lower left corner of the Simulink window displays Compiling:?Computing checksums: Started . For example, a model that contains several Model blocks might display this performance issue.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: readMessages?errors when the DataFormat property is object, for custom message
Description: When reading messages as class objects from a rosbag file (calling readMessages  with DataFormat object ), readMessages   function throws the?"Reference to non-existent field" ?error for messages containing any of the following fields: 1. Message type name2. MD5CheckSum3. MessageType4. PropertyList5. ROSPropertyList6. PropertyMessageTypes

Summary: Test files with more than 1000 test suites cannot be opened
Description: If you try to open a test file that has more than 1000 test suites, Simulink Test Manager errors with File could not be opened

Summary: Push Button block does not work in external mode
Description: When you press the Push Button block during an external mode simulation, the value of the connected parameter or variable does not change to the On Value  specified for the block.

Summary: Polyspace Access X509 certificate error when using Redmine configured with HTTPS
Description: If you integrate the Redmine bug tracking tool with Polyspace Access, and Redmine is configured with HTTPS, you see this error message in the Create Issue  window when you try to create a Redmine ticket:? "An error has occurred. Please contact your Polyspace Access administrator and provide this message:?x509: certificate signed by unknown authority "

Summary: MATLAB might crash when comparing empty variable-size signals in the Simulation Data Inspector
Description: MATLAB might crash when you compare empty variable-size signals or runs that contain empty variable-size signals in the Simulation Data Inspector. The stack trace generated by MATLAB includes this line: [?0] 0x00007ffffabfb31c sdi::CastToAndFromDoubles::upcastToDoubles?

Summary: Using context menu to open Signal Properties dialog box for a signal line in Model Explorer causes MATLAB to crash
Description: In the Model Explorer Contents  pane, MATLAB crashes when you right-click a signal line and select Properties  to open the standalone Signal Properties dialog box.
Workaround: In the Model Explorer Contents pane, select a signal line and edit the signal properties in the Model Explorer Dialog pane instead.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code may be generated in model with multiple n-D Lookup Table blocks with fixed-point Table data?data type and floating point Intermediate results data type
Description: Incorrect code may be generated if all of the following conditions are true: The model has multiple n-D Lookup Table blocks The individual n-D Lookup Table blocks have: Number of table dimensions  set to 1  or 2 . Same input and table data types. Table data  data type parameter is a fixed-point data type. Intermediate results  data type parameter is a floating point data type.  The multiple n-D Lookup Table blocks differ in their fixed-point slope values for the?Table data  data type.
Workaround: Replace each affected n-D Lookup Table block with Prelookup block(s) connected to an Interpolation Using Prelookup block.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible unexpected behavior for Stateflow local events sent from a transition reachable from several states
Description: A Stateflow chart might not enter the expected destination state when the following conditions are true:? A path from the currently active source state contains a transition whose action sends a local event The transition also belongs to path whose source is some other state. For example, transitions from different source states come together at a junction, and then a transition from that junction sends a local event  After the local event is resolved, Stateflow unexpectedly returns to the source state, instead of completing the transition to the destination state, and any exit actions of the source state are not executed.?

Summary: The height of a geographic location must be non-negative in the?comm.Ray?object
Description: When the?comm.Ray ?object is specified as an input to the?raypl ?function, an error will be thrown from the?comm.Ray ?object when the?CoordinateSystem ?property is set to?'Geographic' ?and the third row of the?TransmitterLocation ,?ReceiverLocation , ?or?ReflectionLocations ?property has a negative element.
Workaround: If a negative height is specified or required, open the?comm.Ray?object and remove the code that enforces the error checking:In R2020a, remove?obj(i).TransmitterLocation(3) < 0?from line 864,?obj(i).ReceiverLocation(3) < 0?from line 872, and?any(obj(i).ReflectionLocations(3,:) < 0)?from line 880.?In R2020b, remove?obj(i).TransmitterLocation(3) < 0?from line 913,?obj(i).ReceiverLocation(3) < 0?from line 921, and?any(obj(i).ReflectionLocations(3,:) < 0)?from line 929.?

Summary: resample fails to generate code when given a nonscalar, non-fixed-size second input argument
Description: The resample  function fails to generate code and throws an error when given a second input argument that is not a scalar or a fixed-size vector. For example, if you define this function: function Y = resampleTest(X,Fs,Tstop)T = 0:1/Fs:Tstop;Y = resample(X,T,0.2);end  and attempt to generate code using this command: codegen resampleTest -args {randn(100,1),10,2}  you get this error: ??? Expected input number 3, Q, to be integer-valued.

Summary: C Caller block throws error when calling C function with both formal arguments and global variables as function interface
Description: In a custom code C function called by a C Caller block, when the function has both: An argument passed by pointer that is mapped to the block ports as an InputOutput ,  An accessed global variable that is mapped to the block ports as an Input ,   Simulink may throw an error during model simulation or code generation. You may see a message like the following:"c:\? matlab\? toolbox\? shared\? slcc\? src\? slcc\? src\? slccfunctionimpl.? cpp at line 1653In constructFunctionCall: Assertion failed: static_cast<int>(remainingRHS.? size()) == nGlobalOnInputSide"

Summary: Simulink crashes when a Queue block is inside an export-function model
Description: Simulink crashes when a Queue block is inside an export-function model.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 5, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: Range Response (phased.RangeResponse) throws a hard error when the input coefficient has all zeros values.
Description: Coefficient input with all zero values to phased.RangeResponse throws an error as shown below, Index in position 1 is invalid. Array indices must be positive integers or logical values.  Error in phased.RangeResponse/stepImpl (line 748)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? rngresp(1:sz_x(1)-obj.pRangeOffset,:) = x_rng(obj.pRangeOffset+1:sz_x(1),:);  Error producing code snippet:??  wav = [ones(50,1); zeros(50,1)];  coeff = zeros(50,1);  rngResp = phased.RangeResponse();  rngOut = rngResp(wav,coeff)?
Products: Phased Array System Toolbox, Radar Toolbox

Summary: Build failure for AUTOSAR model with unparented variation points
Description: Building an AUTOSAR model might fail if the model contains variation points?that are owned by a port interface. ARXML export incorrectly removes references to VariationPoint s that are not owned by any software component.

Summary: polyspace-configure fails silently on projects compiled with Keil ?VISION IDE
Description: If you use polyspace-configure  to trace the build configuration of a project that you compile with the Keil ?VISION IDE, the software fails to generate a Polyspace project and does not produce any error messages. The issue occurs whether you run polyspace-configure  in the Polyspace desktop interface or at the command-line.? If you rerun polyspace-configure  with the -debug  flag, you see a message similar to this message:? polyspace-configure: 0s: DEBUG: Found candidate: "options_108693", with pid: 108693

Summary: Priority mismatch error when Thread Trigger block or Speedgoat Interrupt Source block is connected to Model block
Description: When a Thread Trigger block or any Speedgoat Interrupt Source block is connected to a Model block, a priority mismatch error occurs. The function call signal a Thread Trigger block, or any Speedgoat Interrupt Source block does not contain the priority information that Simulink uses when connecting to a function call inport on a Model block. The lack of priority information produces a priority mismatch error.

Summary: parsim throws an error on non-local clusters for models in some projects
Description: parsim  throws ?"Error loading project on parallel workers " ?when running parallel simulations on a non-local cluster for a model that is part of MATLAB project that contains another reference MATLAB project inside a subfolder of the main project file.

Summary: High-integrity checks for hisl_0021,?hisl_0022, and?hisl_0102?need Stateflow license to appear in the Model Advisor
Description: The Model Advisor checks?Check for inconsistent vector indexing methods  (mathworks.hism.hisl_0021 ),?Check data types for blocks with index signals? ? (mathworks.hism.hisl_0022 )? and?Check data type of loop control variables? ? (mathworks.hism.hisl_0102 )? do not appear in the Model Advisor if Stateflow license is missing.

Summary: Error when updating or deleting tasks for common job scheduler (CJS) clusters
Description: After you use the vectorized?createTask() ?syntax to create multiple tasks at the same time on your local cluster or other common job scheduler (CJS) clusters, if you either update a task's?Function ?or?InputArguments , or delete one or more (but not all) of the tasks  then you can see a?'MATLAB:badsubscript' ?error or, in rare cases, can change a task's?InputArguments ?to incorrect values. In the latter case, the task might produce a silent wrong answer if the task runs successfully with the incorrect?InputArguments .
Workaround: As a workaround, you canAvoid updating the?Function or InputArguments?properties of tasks after you create them with the vectorized createTask() syntaxAvoid deleting some (but not all of) tasks created with the vectorized?createTask()?syntaxUse the non-vectorized createTask() syntax to create tasks in a loop
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: MATLAB may crash when using an S-function with tunable parameters that does not register run-time parameters
Description: MATLAB might occasionally crash when all of the following conditions are true: You are using an S-function with tunable parameters You do not register any run-time parameters through ssSetNumRunTimeParams , and  The parameters are n ot of type: double, single, integer, boolean, 1D char array or 2D char array?  For example, MATLAB might crash if the tunable parameters are fixed-point, struct, or char arrays of 3 or more dimensions, and no run-time parameter is registered.?
Workaround: Set the number of run-time parameters using ssSetNumRunTimeParams in your S-function with tunable parameters. Also, register the run-time parameters using?ssRegAllTunableParamsAsRunTimeParams, ssRegDlgParamAsRunTimeParam, or ssSetRunTimeParamInfo.?To detect if your Simulink model contains S-functions that could trigger this crash, set the?SfunCompatabilityCheckMsg?Configuration Parameter to?'warning'?or?'error'. Incompatible S-Functions will throw the error (or warning):"S-function "system" in "model" does not explicitly set the number of run-time parameters using?ssSetNumRunTimeParams. This compatibility diagnostic on the need for S-function upgrades can be set to?none,?warning, or?error?using the Configuration Parameters dialog".The stacktrace may contain lines similar to:[? 0] 0x00000000fe9ea270 libut.dll+00303728 ut_md5ChecksumRec::~ut_md5ChecksumRec+00000896[? 1] 0x00000000fe9eab7d libut.dll+00306045 ut_md5ChecksumRec::~ut_md5ChecksumRec+00003213[? 2] 0x00000001b825fdc7 sl_compile.dll+03210695 BusConnectionChecker::runChecks+00010407[? 3] 0x00000001b827626c sl_compile.dll+03301996 slcomp::slAddRTParamDataToChecksum+00000700[? 4] 0x00000001b8299a50 sl_compile.dll+03447376 slcomp::BdComputeChecksums+0002582??
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Interaction terms are incorrectly ignored when cross-validating a generalized additive model (GAM) using the fitcgam or fitrgam function when the interaction terms are specified via a formula
Description: Interaction terms are incorrectly ignored when cross-validating a generalized additive model (GAM) using the fitcgam  or fitrgam  function and the below workflow: Interaction terms are specified via a formula and Cross-validation is performed using one of the following name-value arguments: 'CrossVal' , 'KFold' , 'Holdout' , 'Leaveout'  or 'CVPartition' .  The Trained  property of the cross-validated model contains the trained compact models. The Interactions  property of all the compact models is incorrectly set to empty without any warnings.
Workaround: When specifying the interaction terms using a formula, perform?cross-validation using the crossval function instead of passing a cross-validation name-value argument to the fitcgam or fitrgam function.Specify interaction terms using the 'Interactions'?name-value argument instead of including interaction terms within a formula.

Summary: Running the model in example "Mapping MDF Channels to Simulink Model Input Ports" generates an invalid data type error
Description: Running the model in the example "Mapping MDF Channels to Simulink Model Input Ports" results in the following error message: Error loading external input data into root-level Inport ModelForMDFInput/triangle in model 'ModelForMDFInput' using Dataset format.Caused by:Invalid data type. The model is configured to load its root level ports from the workspace.  These external inputs have data type 'int8'.  This data type does not agree with the data type, 'double', that is being set for this root level port.
Workaround: To work around the error, execute the following code to convert the data types in dsObj to double, then run the model again:dsObj{1}.Data = double(dsObj{1}.Data);dsObj{2}.pwm.Data = double(dsObj{2}.pwm.Data);dsObj{2}.pwm_level.Data = double(dsObj{2}.pwm_level.Data);dsObj{2}.pwm_filtered.Data = double(dsObj{2}.pwm_filtered.Data);

Summary: Simulink Check qualification test suite requires Simulink Coder to execute
Description: Simulink Coder is required to successfully execute the qualification tests for Simulink Check. If Simulink Coder is not installed, this error message occurs: Error : Unrecognized field name "Name".  Report generation stopped. Follow the test execution procedure specified in the Simulink Check Test Cases and Procedures document.  ? "chapter" contains no content and will not be inserted into document ?

Summary: Test Harness using Inport source for component with Bus Element Ports using nonvirtual?buses in virtual bus hierarchy produces an error
Description: For test harnesses that uses an Inport source, if the component being tested has Bus Element ports that use nonvirtual buses in a virtual bus hierarchy, then when you compile or simulate the model, this error occurs: Block '<HarnessName>/<MainModelName>' is configured to require a nonvirtual bus or non-bus signal, however the type of the input signal to the block is virtual. Please see Simulink documentation for further information on composite (i.e. bus) signals and their proper usage. Alternately, if the input bus signal consists only of scalar elements, 1-D elements, or either row or column vectors; and all elements have the same data type, signal type, and sampling mode, consider inserting a Bus to Vector conversion block in the signal path. This message is related to a hidden ModelReference block.?

Summary: SIL simulation for AUTOSAR model with imported system constants might produce different results from Normal mode or might not return data
Description: SIL simulation results might be incorrect for an AUTOSAR model with imported system constants, if the imported ARXML contains a system constant that has multiple values defined (for example, values 2 and 3). When SIL simulation runs for the model, either of the following errors might occur: ### Starting SIL simulation for component: MyComponentWithVariantsrtw.connectivity.HostLauncher: started executable with host process identifier 31096rtw.connectivity.HostLauncher: stopped executable with host process identifier 31096### Stopping SIL simulation for component: MyComponentWithVariantsNo target application data received by development computer. There might be multiple reasons for this communications failure. or path /modelname /stub/Rte_Cfg.h(92): warning C4005: 'MySystemConst1': macro redefinition The SIL simulation might produce different results from Normal mode simulation or might not return any data.

Summary: Code inspection might fail with an "Out-of-bound access of array" error for a MATLAB Function block using coder.ceval
Description: Simulink Code Inspector fails verification of a MATLAB Function block that uses?coder.ceval  with an? "Out-of-bound access of array" error when all of these conditions are true:  There are two or more calls to the same function from a MATLAB Function block by using coder.ceval . The arguments at the same argument position in the two function calls differ in their size.  For example, consider the following two calls to a function foo ?that takes only one argument. coder.ceval('foo', coder.ref(u1))  coder.ceval('foo', coder.ref(u2)) ? If u1 ?and u2  have?different dimensions, the code inspection fails verification with an error message similar to the following: Out-of-bound access of array <variable name> at the following positions : 'FILE: <filename> LINE, <line number>, COLUMN, <column numbers>'  Here, <filename>  is the name of the generate C or CPP file,? <line number>  is the line number in <filename> , and? <column numbers>  are the columns numbers in line <line number> .

Summary: MATLAB might crash when calling find_system after a model with masked subsystems is closed
Description: MATLAB might crash when all of these events occur:The base workspace contains Simulink.LookupTable  objects.?? A model that contains a masked subsystem is compiled and then closed.? After the model is closed, the find_system  function is called.?

Summary: Invalid observable expression results in unexpected error at command line and silent error in SimBiology Model Builder
Description: If a SimBiology model contains multiple active observables and one or more of them have an invalid expression, you might see the following error while verifying or simulating the model at the command line:  All contents of the input cell array must be of the same data type. If you have such a model in the SimBiology Model Builder  app, you might experience a silent error. For example, if you click Verify , this error does not appear in the list of messages although the model verification has failed.
Workaround: To troubleshoot this issue, have only one active observable in the model at a time. If this active observable has an invalid expression, you will see a more informative error message that explains why the expression is invalid.

Summary: Simulink Check qualification test case SLCHK_SL_Q009 fails unexpectedly
Description: Simulink Check test case SLCHK_SL_Q009  in the DO Qualification Kit fails with the following error when Simulink tries to compile model DesignDelayedFcnCallSubsys_WARN.slx : Error:Input data dependency violation due to function-call or action subsystems. See Subsystem Examples in the Simulink library for valid and invalid examples of function-call and action subsystems.  Error:Input ports (1) of 'DesignDelayedFcnCallSubsys_WARN/ss' are involved in the loop.  Error:Input ports (FcnCall) of 'DesignDelayedFcnCallSubsys_WARN/fcss2' are involved in the loop.  Error:Simulink has inserted an implicit signal conversion block after the inport blocks 'DesignDelayedFcnCallSubsys_WARN/ss/In1' of subsystem 'DesignDelayedFcnCallSubsys_WARN/ss' to ensure that the corresponding signals are contiguous in memory. This may cause a one-step delay for the function-call initiator block(s) 'DesignDelayedFcnCallSubsys_WARN/ss/ss/sfcn' that are observing these signals as return values from the function-call subsystem(s) they are calling. To avoid this condition, ensure that each function-call return value originates from a single block in the function-call subsystem being called.   The model compile error occurs because Model Advisor check?Check for potentially delayed function-call block return values  (mathworks.design.DelayedFcnCallSubsys ) does not execute normally. As a result, the check cannot be qualified by using the DO Qualification Kit.
Workaround: Copy the matlabroot/toolbox/qualkits/do/slchk folder and subfolders to a location where you have write access.Open Excel spreadsheet qualkitdo_slchk_tcp.xls.Find test case SLCHK_SL_Q009 (column A).Remove the row and save the spreadsheet.Open qualkitdo_slchk_run.mlx and follow the workflow to execute the test procedure.

Summary: matlab.unittest.TestSuite.fromFolder?does not recognize test files using symbolic links
Description: When you use the?matlab.unittest.TestSuite.fromFolder ?method to create a test suite, MATLAB ignores any test files that are symbolically linked to your folder. For example, consider a test file C:\work\tests\myTest.m  on your Windows system. Create a symbolic link (or symlink) to the file by executing?this command in the command prompt: mklink "C:\work\symlink\myTest.m" "C:\work\tests\myTest.m"?  If you try to create a test suite using the symlink  folder, MATLAB ignores the test file when creating the suite. Running this command results in an empty test suite: suite = matlab.unittest.TestSuite.fromFolder('C:\work\symlink');

Summary: readtable, readmatrix, and readcell might throw an error when reading large text files with many lines that exceed 524288 bytes in length
Description: readtable , readmatrix , and readcell  might throw an error when reading large text files with many lines that exceed 524288 bytes in length. >> m = (1.23456789e123)*ones(147,65534);>> writetable(array2table(m), "numbers.txt"); >> opts = delimitedTextImportOptions("NumVariables",  65534);>> opts = setvartype(opts, "double"); >> t = readtable("numbers.txt", opts);Failed to convert character code. To hit this error condition, the text file must contain many lines whose lengths are?524288 bytes or greater.? In versions of MATLAB prior to R2020a, readtable ?does not produce an error if it is called without an Import Options object. For example, the following code does not error in R2019b: >> m = (1.23456789e123)*ones(147,65534);>> writetable(array2table(m), "numbers.txt");>> result = readtable("numbers.txt"); However, this code does error in R2020a and later releases.
Workaround: To work around this issue in R2020a and later releases, you can pass the Format name-value argument to readtable:>> m = (1.23456789e123)*ones(147,65534);>> writetable(array2table(m), "numbers.txt");>> t = readtable("numbers.txt", "Format", "auto");

Summary: Model build time much longer when generating A2L file
Description: The model build time is much longer when generating an A2L file.

Summary: Can't import some text timestamps on a system whose locale is not set to en_US
Description: On a system where MATLAB localizes to English but whose system locale setting is something other than en_US , you cannot import text timestamps that include time zone offsets specified as US localized abbreviations, such as EDT, by using the datetime  function. For example, if the system locale setting is not en_US , then this call to datetime  results in an error. >> d = datetime("26-Mar-2018 16:00:42 EDT","Format","dd-MMM-yyyy HH:mm:ss z","TimeZone","America/New_York","Locale","en_US") Error using datetime (line 649)Unable to convert '26-Mar-2018 16:00:42 EDT' to datetime using the format 'dd-MMM-yyyy HH:mm:ss z' and locale 'en_US'.

Summary: Aggregated coverage results show more than 100% for model with variable-dimension signals
Description: When the coverage results are aggregated from multiple simulations of a model that contains multiple blocks with variable-dimension signal inputs,? the Simulink Coverage tool might incorrectly calculate the total objective outcome count to be smaller than it actually is. Although the number of satisfied objective outcomes is correct, the coverage percentage for the model is artificially high and might be reported as greater than 100%.
Workaround: For the aggregated coverage results to correctly show the maximum possible total coverage count, when aggregating coverage results from multiple simulations, make sure that in at least one of the simulations, all the variable-dimension signals in the model use their maximum sizes.Note that all signals do not need to achieve their maximum sizes at the same time step, but they must achieve these maximum sizes during the same simulation.?
Products: Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Coverage, Simulink Check

Summary: Using ideal or low-loss channel in Analog Channel block in Simulink? generates error.
Description: In SerDes Toolbox? Simulink? model, the Analog Channel block supports both loss model and impulse response as channel sources.? For some low loss channels, such as the default ideal impulse response results in the following error after the simulation is complete: "Output argument "COM" (and possibly others) not assigned a value in the execution with "serdes.utilities.calculatePAMnEye" function."

Summary: fplot?and related functions may be susceptible to low-precision problems
Description: When plotting symbolic expressions that?matlabFunction ?cannot handle,?fplot ,?fplot3 ,?fsurf ,?fmesh ,?fcontour ,?fimplicit , and?fimplicit3 ?perform function evaluation at low precision. In some cases, this may cause numerical instabilities, potentially including incorrect results that look reasonable. ex = str2sym("piecewise(x < 100, (pi^2-107607675/10902937)*x)");??fplot(ex)
Workaround: Avoid the low-precision evaluation by explicitly calling?subs. Note that this significantly degrades performance.syms xex = str2sym("piecewise(x < 100, (pi^2-107607675/10902937)*x)");?fplot(@(xval) subs(ex,x,xval))
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Greater than expressions that use the duration operator on the left hand side do not return true at the correct simulation step
Description: Stateflow incorrectly evaluates greater than expressions when all of the following conditions are true: The Stateflow chart uses a fixed sample time The expression includes a duration  operator on the left hand side The right side of the expression evaluates to a value that is not a whole multiple of the chart's sample time  When these conditions are met, Stateflow evaluates the expression as?true  one time step later than expected.
Workaround: For charts that include the duration operator on the left side of a greater than expression, ensure that the right side of the expression specifies time that matches sample time of the chart.Alternatively, you can change the sample time of the chart to accommodate all values of time that are used in the chart.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated from models containing Assignment block may be incorrect
Description: If model uses assignment block, the assignment block may generate incorrect structured text code.

Summary: Update button in Mixed-Signal Analyzer app toolstrip does not update visual elements
Description: The?Update ?button in Mixed-Signal Analyzer app toolstrip only updates the data. The visual elements such as trend charts and waveforms are not being updated. Following are the specific bugs in the update button capability of the app: a.?????? Update/RefreshAnalysis Waveforms and Analysis Metrics are not being generated using either built-in or custom analyses.?? b.?????? Update/Refresh is not happening for Cadence AdeInfo database. c.?????? Update/Refresh is not updating the right-side "Plot Options"? legend/visibility table.

Summary: Unable to perform assignment error when opening block dialog of State Reader or State Writer block
Description: Simulink might throw the following error when you open the block dialog for a State Reader or State Writer block: Unable to perform assignment because the left and right sides have a different number of elements   The error occurs if the model contains a Stateflow chart that contains a Simulink Function.

Summary: Wireless Waveform Generator app might transmit a distorted signal constellation from lab instruments and result in incorrect EVM
Description: The Wireless Waveform Generator app can transmit generated waveforms using lab test instruments supported by the?rfsiggen  function (Instrument Control Toolbox, see https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/details/2413350 ). While using the app in the Transmitter mode, the signal constellation transmitted by an instrument might be distorted, leading to larger-than-expected EVM values. This occurs when provided IQ data values are greater than 1. The operation of the app in the Generator mode is not affected. This issue affects the output of RF instruments from all supported vendors, including: Rohde & Schwarz Keysight

Summary: A Simulink model that contains ROS Read Data block and a ROS Publish or ROS Subscribe block fails to simulate
Description: A Simulink model that contains ROS Read Data block and a ROS Publish or a ROS Subscribe block fails to run. An example of the error thrown in diagnostic viewer is shown below: An error occurred while running the simulation and the simulation was terminatedCaused by:For 'Output Port 2' of 'modelname/ Read Data/ SourceBlock', ?the method 'stepImpl' of the System object 'ros. slros. internal. block. ReadData' returns a bus type whose number of fields does not match the number of fields defined in the method 'getOutputDataTypeImpl'.

Summary: Using Parameter Overrides with a baseline test causes MATLAB to crash
Description: When you use Parameter Overrides to override the value of a variable used as a model argument in the model workspace, and then run a baseline test, MATLAB crashes.

Summary: Signal Analyzer does not show preprocessing information when Info context menu is selected
Description: The Signal table in the Signal Analyzer  app has an Info  column that shows whether any preprocessing has been performed on a signal. Right-clicking the row of a preprocessed signal and selecting the Info ?option currently fails to show a window enumerating the preprocessing actions.

Summary: Plotting many satellites or ground stations in the Satellite Scenario Viewer is very slow
Description: Plotting more than 50 satellites or ground stations with labels visible in the Satellite Scenario Viewer can take over a minute. This time increases as the number of satellites and ground stations increases. This bug is present in Aerospace Toolbox and Satellite Communications Toolbox.
Workaround: Hide the labels of satellites and ground stations before launching the satellite scenario viewer. This will significantly reduce the time needed to plot the graphics. For example:sc = satelliteScenario;sat = satellite(sc, 'leoSatelliteConstellation.tle');sat.ShowLabel = false;% Open the viewer after hiding labels as a workaround.viewer = satelliteScenarioViewer(sc);??

Summary: SLRT Overload Options block provides incomplete operation in referenced model or explicitly partitioned model
Description: The SLRT Overload Options block provides incomplete operation in a referenced model or in an explicitly partitioned model. When used in these types of models, the operations include: The block provides the expected control of the selected task thread. For example, the maximum overload number cannot be overwritten for the selected thread. The block cannot output the correct overload count.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when performing a cut and paste of a stereotyped component after launching the Analysis?Viewer
Description: MATLAB might crash if the Analysis Viewer is opened on an architecture model and a component with stereotypes applied is cut and pasted onto any layer of that architecture model. If MATLAB does not crash immediately, it might crash if the Analysis Viewer is relaunched.

Summary: Target computer might crash if?Default parameter behavior?is?Tunable and?Run on Target button starts real-time application
Description: If you set the?Default parameter behavior ?to?Tunable  and you start the real-time application by using the?Run on Target  button in the Simulink Editor (external mode operation), the target computer might crash.

Summary: Folders are created when opening a Simulink model?in the current directory
Description: Opening a Simulink model creates a folder named dig  in the current working directory. This folder contains folders such as blob_storage , cache , and Code Cache .

Summary: Test file cleanup callback runs incorrectly when using MATLAB Unit Test Runner
Description: If you run a Simulink Test file using the MATLAB Unit Test Runner, the test file cleanup callback runs multiple times instead of only once.

Summary: Unnecessary casts in the generated code might result in compilation error for C++ simulation targets in referenced models
Description: When building standalone executable for a model reference target whose language is set to C++, there might be unnecessary casts inserted during code generation causing compilation errors as shown in the error snippet below: "request for member "X" in "Y"??which is of non-class type "void *""

Summary: Editing MATLAB code that is also open in a MATLAB Coder project might produce an UnsupportedOperationException error
Description: If you edit MATLAB code that is part of an open MATLAB Coder project and add calls to MathWorks functions that do not support code generation, the stack trace from an?UnsupportedOperationException  error might appear in the MATLAB command window. If one such error appears, subsequent edits to your MATLAB code might trigger successive, identical errors. This error is purely cosmetic and does not affect either your MATLAB Coder project or your ability to progress through the MATLAB Coder app.
Workaround: When this error starts appearing, close and reopen your MATLAB Coder project.

Summary: Aerospace Blockset Orbit Propagator block incorrectly calculates transformation between fixed-frame coordinate systems and inertial coordinate system (ICRF) for some parameter configurations
Description: The Aerospace Blockset Orbit Propagator  block incorrectly calculates the transformation between fixed-frame coordinate systems and the inertial coordinate system (ICRF) when one of the following criteria is met, causing inaccurate propagation results: Propagation method  is set to  Numerical (high precision) , Central body   is set to a value other than Earth , and Gravitational potential model   is set to Oblate spheroid (J2)  or Spherical harmonics . Propagation method  ?is set to  Numerical (high precision) , ? Input external accelerations   is set to on , and?External acceleration coordinate frame   is set to Fixed-frame . Units  ?is set to English (kts) .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation when a Concatenate block is inside an Iterator Subsystem
Description: Incorrect code might be generated in some cases when an Assignment block feeds to a Concatenate block that is inside an Iterator Subsystem. This occurs while generating code from a Simulink model.

Summary: Simulation error in Condenser Evaporator blocks above the critical pressure
Description: When simulating the Condenser Evaporator (2P-MA) block or the Condenser Evaporator (TL-2P) block above the critical pressure of the refrigerant, you may receive the following error: ??In divide, the denominator must be nonzero.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a root Outport block that has a fixed-point data type with nonzero bias and an initial condition specified on its input signal
Description: Incorrect code might occur for a model that meets the following conditions: The Remove root level I/O zero initialization  configuration parameter is selected (that is,? ZeroExternalMemoryAtStartup ?is set to 'off '). The root Outport block has a fixed-point?data type with nonzero bias.? The root Outport block input signal connects to a subsystem Outport block with an initial condition.? The sum of the nonzero bias on the fixed-point signal and the initial condition on the subsystem Outport block is equal to zero.?
Workaround: Clear the Remove root level I/O zero initialization configuration parameter.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: On launching Model Slicer, an incompatibility message incorrectly indicates that a Model block inside a For Each subsystem is configured in normal mode
Description: Launching the Model Slicer on a model with a For Each subsystem containing a Model block throws an error of this form. The parameter 'Simulation mode' of the Model block <..> is set to 'Normal'. However, the Model block is inside subsystem <..>, which contains a For Each block. Simulink currently does not support this scenario. Consider changing? the parameter 'Simulation mode' of Model block <...> to 'Accelerator'.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Normal mode simulation and code generation results might not match if the model is composed of an atomic subsystem containing only a Bus Selector block
Description: When a model is composed of an atomic subsystem that contains only a Bus Selector block, block reduction might remove the buffer synthesized in that subsystem incorrectly in code generation. This may lead to a simulation result that is different from the normal mode simulation.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulation results might be incorrect in the Accelerator and Rapid Accelerator mode for reusable subsystem outport connected to constant sample time signal
Description: Incorrect code might be generated when all of the following conditions are met: Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator simulation mode is chosen. Reusable subsystem's outport is connect to block with constant (inf ) sample time? Configuration parameter BufferReusableBoundary  is checked
Workaround: Disable BufferReusableBoundary configuration parameter.

Summary: Running consecutive coverage analyses on a subsystem harness might cause an error
Description: If you perform consecutive coverage analyses on a subsystem harness and a signal datatype changes in between simulations, the following error occurs:  Error using cvdata (line 99)CV object #11654 does not existError in cvi.
