For example, the field specifies?values for only ?Verbosity level  ?and?Serial port ID : ?0, 'COM1' .??  You see this error: ?Reference to non-existent field 'PortName'
Workaround: Specify all supported arguments for MEX-file arguments (ExtModeMexArgs), namely, Verbosity level, Serial port ID, and Baud. For example: 0, 'COM1', 57600

Summary: Hierarchical signals are not plotted in the Simulation Data Inspector when you load a view
Description: When you load a view in the Simulation Data Inspector that includes plotted hierarchical signals, the hierarchical signals are not plotted by the view if the Block Path  property does not have a value. You can still plot the signals manually.

Summary: Linearization of Simulink models with non-ASCII block names might produce erroneous results on Windows
Description: The Simulink Model Linearizer app might produce the following confusing and misleading error message if the model contains blocks with non-ASCII names: The state order specified contains states that are no longer in the Simulink model <model name>. ?Remove these states from the Linearization State Ordering in Options dialog.?

Summary: Exporting simlog to Simulation Data Inspector shows incorrect values
Description: If a simlog containing variables with nonscalar values is exported to Simulation Data Inspector (SDI), the plots in SDI display wrong values.
Workaround: To view the data in Simulation Data Inspector, select Record data in Simulation Data Inspector?in the model settings instead of exporting the simlog from the MATLAB workspace.

Summary: Older versions of Simulink might crash when loading model that has?harnessInfo.xml file created or modified in newer release
Description: When you load a Simulink model in a R2020a or older release, Simulink might crash if these two conditions are true: The model folder contains a?harnessInfo.xml ?file. The?harnessInfo.xml ?file was created or modified in a newer release.  The stack trace for the crash contains slsHarnessInfoLoader::startSection  The newer? harnessInfo.xml ?file is incompatible with older releases of Simulink. This situation might occur if, for example, You export a Simulink project containing a model with external harnesses to an older release. After exporting, the main model has the same name as before exporting? and all external harnesses are converted to a internal harnesses. However, the?harnessInfo.xml ?file from the newer release still exists in the model folder. You export a Simulink model with external harnesses to an older release, and then, manually rename the exported model to its original name, with the original?harnessInfo.xml ?file still present in the model folder.?
Workaround: You can work around this issue by either:Deleting or renaming the?harnessInfo.xml?fileMoving the?harnessInfo.xml?file outside the model folder

Summary: dlgradient can return incorrect results for models using the gru function when calculating derivatives on the CPU with respect to the GRU state.
Description: When the gradient computation depends on the state output of the gru  function (the second output), the derivatives are incorrectly computed as zero. This issue does not occur when the input data contains a gpuArray  object.
Workaround: Instead of using the state output of the gru function, use the last time-step of the first output. For example, for data where the third dimension of the output corresponds to time, replace instances of the code[dlY,hiddenState] = gru(dlX,H0,weights,recurrentWeights,bias)withdlY = gru(dlX,H0,weights,recurrentWeights,bias)hiddenState = dlY(:,:,end);
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 4, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: When using UIFigure or App Designer, MATLAB sporadically crashes during shutdown
Description: When closing MATLAB after using UIFigure or App Designer, it might crash and leave a stack trace. Stack Trace (captured): 0x00007fff1b8e38b9                    D:\MATLAB\bin\win64\libmwms.dll+015873850x00007fff0cf21909                  D:\MATLAB\bin\win64\webwindow.dll+000719450x00007fff0cf40419                  D:\MATLAB\bin\win64\webwindow.dll+001976570x00007fff0d011d22 D:\MATLAB\bin\win64\libmwconnectorbinarycefimpl.dll+000729940x00007ffef2ea33db            D:\MATLAB\bin\win64\hgweb_interface.dll+00275419

Summary: MATLAB stops responding when opening the Signal Editor with a MAT-file
Description: MATLAB stops responding when opening the Signal Editor with a MAT-file containing signals with more than 30,000 channels.

Summary: The coder.checkGpuInstall and?gpucoderSetup?utilities are unable to find?cuBLAS libraries that are installed in the "system standard" location
Description: Starting with CUDA 10.1, the default installation of the cuBLAS file(s) on Linux platforms has moved from the CUDA toolkit install folder to a "system standard" location. The coder.checkGpuInstall  and?gpucoderSetup  utilities ? do not search for the cuBLAS libraries in the "system standard" location thereby throwing incorrect errors about missing libraries.

Summary: JMAAB check jc_0792 erroneously displays warning for enum types defined in the data dictionary
Description: The Model Advisor JMAAB check  Check unused data in Simulink Model ?(id:? mathworks.jmaab.jc_0792 ) erroneously displays?a warning for enum types used in the model that are defined in the data dictionary.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: Compilation error occurs if reusable Subsystem inside Variant Subsystem is connected to Scope block
Description: Compilation error might occur for a model that meets the following conditions: Model contains a reusable Subsystem placed inside a Variant Subsystem block.? Outport of reusable Subsystem is connected to Variant Subsystem's Outport.? The same signal also connects to a Scope block.?

Summary: Error when accessing contents of a subsystem named using the?Simulink.BlockDiagram.createSubsystem function
Description: Attempting to access the contents of a subsystem might produce an error when a script created the subsystem using the?Simulink.BlockDiagram.createSubsystem ?function with a value specified for the?Name ?argument.  Example actions that might produce the error include navigating into the subsystem and simulating the model that contains the subsystem.
Workaround: In scripts, do not use the Name argument of Simulink.BlockDiagram.createSubsystem. Rename the subsystem after its creation.?

Summary: Bug Finder might not detect some numerical defects involving float computations
Description: If you run a Bug Finder analysis on code that involves computations with floats, Polyspace might not detect some numerical defects, depending on the context of the computation. For example, in this code snippet, Polyspace does not flag the possible Float division by zero  when ctx2()  calls action(x,0)  and something()  returns true. Polyspace detects the division by zero on similar code constructs with integers. typedef float type;  int something();type action(type x, type y) {??? type z;??? if (something()) {??????? z = x * y;??? } else {??????? z = x * y - 5000;??? }??? return 10000 / z ; // Possible float division by zero coming from ctx2 when something() returns true}void ctx1(type x, type y) {??? action(x, y);};void ctx2(type x) {??? action(x, 0);};
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: BusPositionVelocity dimension mismatch error when Simulink model contains multiple Simulation 3D Lidar blocks
Description: When a Simulink model contains more than one Simulation 3D Lidar block, and the blocks contain different values for parameters such as Horizontal Resolution , Horizontal Field of View , and?Vertical Resolution , you encounter an error similar to this one: The method 'stepImpl' of the System object 'Simulation3DLidarSensor' returns a bus type whose field BusPositionVelocity.Position size does not match the field BusPositionVelocity.Position size defined in the method 'getOutputDataTypeImpl'.  This error is not known to occur in the following cases:  Models containing only one Simulation 3D Lidar block  Models containing multiple Simulation 3D Lidar blocks that have identical corresponding block parameter values

Summary: Code generation for?fsolve?fails on a scalar problem
Description: When you attempt to generate code for fsolve  with a scalar initial point x0 , the process can fail, issuing the following error message: ??? Index expression out of bounds. ?Attempted to access element 1. ?The valid range is 1-0. For example,   function x = mytest    opts = optimoptions('fsolve','algorithm','levenberg-marquardt');    f = @(x) x-1;    x0 = 0;    x = fsolve(f,x0,opts);  end The following attempt to generate code fails: cfg = coder.config('mex');codegen -config cfg mytest??? Index expression out of bounds. Attempted to access element 1. The valid range is 1-0.
Workaround: Call lsqnonlin?with infinite bounds instead of fsolve:?  function x = mytest    opts = optimoptions('lsqnonlin','algorithm','levenberg-marquardt');    f = @(x) x-1;    x0 = 0;    x = lsqnonlin(f,x0,-Inf,Inf,opts);  endYou must use infinite bounds, not [] as the bound arguments.? Code generation succeeds for this function:cfg = coder.config('mex');codegen -config cfg mytestCode generation successful.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for a subscripted matrix assignment in the last statement of an?if?or?switch body might be incorrect
Description: A variable?x ?might? obtain an incorrect value in the generated code if all? of the following conditions about your MATLAB code?are true : The MATLAB code contains an if  or switch ?block with a condition that is a compile-time constant The value of x  before the?if ?or?switch ?block?can be determined at compile time In the body of the active branch of the if  or  switch  block , a complete or partial assignment to x  occurs The last statement  in the active branch is a matrix subscripted assignment into  x: ? x(...) = ...  At least one of the matrix subscripts evaluates at compile time to an empty matrix or a logical value of false  The first statement after the?if ?or? switch ?block?reads from x    Example code that demonstrates this issue:  x = first_definition;  if (compile_time_constant_true)  ? ...  ? x = second_definition;  ? x([]) = ...;  ? % no additional statements here  end  % no additional statements here  use(x);   Here, the final use(x) ?call might incorrectly use the first_definition ?value for x ,  instead of second_definition . This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: In the active?if?or?switch?branch, after the last subscripted matrix assignment, add a dummy statement.For example, modify the code in the previous section to:x = first_definition;if (compile_time_constant_true)? ...? x = second_definition;? x([]) = ...;? 0; % dummy statement here avoids the bugenduse(x);

Summary: sbmlimport errors if a reaction name starts with UnnamedReaction_
Description: If you call sbmlimport  on an SBML file containing a reaction with the name starting UnnamedReaction_ , the function reports the error:  Invalid SimBiology reaction name. Name cannot begin with 'UnnamedRection_'.
Workaround: Edit the SBML file to rename any reactions whose name starts with UnnamedReaction_.

Summary: Error when using shuffle or subset on TransformedDatastore with multiple underlying datastores and multiple transformation functions
Description: A TransformedDatastore  with multiple underlying datastores and multiple transformation functions errors when the subset  or shuffle  method is used, as shown in the following example. >> imds = imageDatastore("peppers.png")>> tds1 = transform(imds, imds, @(x, y) x)>> tds2 = transform(tds1, @(x) x)>> tds2.subset(1)Error using matlab.io.datastore.TransformedDatastoreToo many input arguments. This error also occurs when passing a TransformedDatastore  with multiple underlying datastores to trainNetwork  with the "Shuffle"  option set to "once"  or "every-epoch" .
Workaround: Use CombinedDatastore to associate multiple underlying datastores together.

Summary: Slow loading or update diagram for AUTOSAR models when imported compositions contain a large number of components
Description: If you import AUTOSAR compositions that contain a large number of components from ARXML to Simulink, the resulting AUTOSAR models might be slow to load, update diagram, and close.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing a Selector block that connects to a Unit Delay block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a Selector block that directly connects to the input of a Unit Delay block. The input to the Selector block branches to another block aside from the Selector block.

Summary: Usingshow3D?function for uavScenario object could lead to missing meshes in visualization.
Description: Under a few conditions, you might observe missing meshes when using the show3D  function of uavScenario  object: UAV Scenario contains a?uavPlatform ?object with a trajectory ?that ends before the scenario ends Calling the?restart ?function after completing a full simulation on the scenario Calling the?show3D ?function immediately after calling the restart ?function.  Calling the setup ?function and then calling the show3D ?function with the FastUpdate ?name-value pair set to true .   For an example, see the code: scene = uavScenario("StopTime", 2);  plat = uavPlatform("UAV",scene,"Trajectory",waypointTrajectory([0 0 0; 0 0 -5],"TimeOfArrival",[0 1])); % Complete one full simulation first.  setup(scene);  while(true)  if ~advance(scene)  break;  end   end?  restart(scene);  ax = newplot(figure);  show3D(scene,"Parent",ax);?% Call show3D immediately after restart  setup(scene);while(true)  if ~advance(scene)  break;  end  show3D(scene,"Parent",ax,"FastUpdate",true);?%Observe that the figure doesn't contain any mesh  pause(0.1);   end
Workaround: Call the setup function on the uavScenario?object before calling the?show3D?function:scene = uavScenario("StopTime", 2);plat = uavPlatform("UAV",scene,"Trajectory",waypointTrajectory([0 0 0; 0 0 -5],"TimeOfArrival",[0 1]));setup(scene);while(true)if ~advance(scene)break;endendrestart(scene);?setup(scene); % Call setup before show3Dax = newplot(figure);show3D(scene,"Parent",ax);while(true)    if ~advance(scene)        break;    end    show3D(scene,"Parent",ax,"FastUpdate",true); % Observe that the figure contains a cuboid mesh initially    pause(0.1);end

Summary: MISRA-C:2012 Rule 10.3 checker does not detect conversions in structure initializations using initializer list
Description: MISRA C:2012 Rule 10.3 states that the value of an expression shall not be assigned to an object with a narrower essential type or a different essential type category. If you initialize a structure using a brace-enclosed initializer list, the Polyspace checker for rule 10.3 fails to detect narrowing conversions that happen during assignment to an individual structure member. In the following example, the checker fails to detect the narrowing conversion when the unsigned char  field a_  is initialized with the variable b , which has the wider type,?int . typedef unsigned char boolean; typedef struct tag_A{  boolean a_;  boolean b_;  uint16 c_[6];}A;int main() {    int b = 1;   A a = {b, 0, {0, 2000, 4000, 6000, 8000, 10000}}; }

Summary: CustomFloat produces incorrect values when ExponentLength >= 31
Description: When constructing a CustomFloat  object with ExponentLength  specified as >= 31 , the resulting object has incorrect values. For example:? >> CustomFloat(pi, 33, 1)ans =0Data Type: Floating-point: Custom-precisionWordLength: 33MantissaLength: 1ExponentLength: 31ExponentBias: 1073741823

Summary: Unexpected error when using model arguments on a masked Model block
Description: Simulink will incorrectly error when you have a model hierarchy with three or more levels for which all of the following conditions are true: A referenced model has a model argument. Its parent model is referenced in normal mode. The Model block in this parent model is masked. The value of the model argument in this parent model is a model argument specified in the model workspace of this parent model.  The error will be similar to: The symbol 'X' resolved to a parameter object that was created by the MaskInitialization code of 'mid/ModelBlock'. Parameter objects should only be created in the base workspace, data dictionary, or model workspace.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a

Summary: Logging signals in a model slows down compilation
Description: Logging signals slows down the model compilation process compared to not logging any signals. This behavior persists regardless of whether the Logic Analyzer is open and used to view the logged signals.

Summary: Figure produced by the Distribution Fitter Print To Figure?menu item does not have the figure toolbar
Description: The Print To Figure ?menu item of the Distribution Fitter app produces a figure that does not include the figure toolbar, menus, and figure name.
Workaround: Use the Generate Code?menu item to generate code for the current session of the Distribution Fitter app, and use the generated function to produce a figure that includes the missing functionalities.

Summary: analyzeNetwork does not check that time dimension propagates when 'TargetUsage' option is 'dlnetwork'.
Description: When performing a forward pass of a dlnetwork  object, the software applies the layer operation of each layer in the underlying layer graph, where some of the layer operations require the input data to be in a particular format.  For example, concatenation requires the input arrays to have the same format. The software errors, when the data passed to a concatenationLayer  object do not have the same format. The  analyzeNetwork  function does not check that time dimension propagates when 'TargetUsage'  option is 'dlnetwork' .? For example, create a layer graph that concatenates the output of a sequenceInputLayer  and a featureInputLayer . layers = [  ??? sequenceInputLayer(1,'Name','seqIn')  ? ? concatenationLayer(1,2,'Name','cat')];  lgraph = layerGraph(layers);  layer =  featureInputLayer(1,'Name','featIn');  lgraph = addLayers(lgraph,layer);  lgraph  = connectLayers( lgraph, 'featIn','cat/in2'); ? Analyze the layer graph using the analyzeNetwork  function and set the 'TargetUsage'  option to 'dlnetwork' . The function does not report any issues with the layer graph.  analyzeNetwork(lgraph,'TargetUsage','dlnetwork'); ? Convert the layer graph to a dlnetwork  object and perform a forward pass with some data. The predict  function throws an error.  dlnet = dlnetwork(lgraph);  dlX1 = dlarray(1,'CT');  dlX2 = dlarray(1,'C');  dlY = predict(dlnet,dlX1,dlX2); ? Error using dlnetwork/predict (line 415)  Layer 'cat': Invalid input data. Concatenated dlarrays must have the same dimension labels.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Translating Belt Pulley block exhibits invalid behavior for same-direction case
Description: The Belt Pulley block produces invalid calculations of motion when you set?Pulley translation? to? On , ?Belt direction ?to?Ends move in same direction, ?and? Belt type? to? Flat belt ?or? V-belt .? N onzero pulley centroid velocity?or nonzero belt end A force results in solution blow-up.
Workaround: To produce the correct calculations of motion, set Belt direction to Ends move in opposite direction. This changes the signs of the velocity and force at port A. Adjust the signs in connected blocks to make your system agree with this change.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code might be generated during a top model build when Coder Dictionary storage classes are used in an unsupported way inside one or more of its model references
Description: Incorrect code might be generated during a top model build if one or more of its model references contains an Outport block driven by a signal that is individually mapped to a Coder Dictionary storage class.

Summary: Error?when creating a project for Experiment Manager on a network drive
Description: When you try to create an experiment in a new project on a network drive, Experiment Manager produces the following error message: Failed to create experiment  Location should be within the project?

Summary: AUTOSAR-C++14 A03-03-02 false positive on a constexpr constructor with only constant arguments
Description: When static or thread-local objects with constexpr ?constructors are initialized with arguments that are compile-time constants (const , constexpr , or literals), false positive violations of AUTOSAR-C++14 A03-03-02 are raised. #include #include template class PS_ErrCat: public std::error_category {  public:    constexpr PS_ErrCat(const char* name) noexcept : mName(name) {}    std::string message(int code) const override { return mName; }    const char* name() const noexcept { return mName; }  private:    const char* mName;};std::error_code make_error_code(int code) {  static const PS_ErrCat sRed { "Red" };  // False positive violation  return std::error_code {      code,      sRed  };}

Summary: MATLAB might crash during Model Advisor metric check when no display is present
Description: MATLAB might crash when a state label position is queried if you started MATLAB using the -nodisplay  option. Model Advisor checks that do this include: mathworks. metrics. ModelAdvisorCheckIssues. maab mathworks. metrics. ModelAdvisorCheckCompliance. maab

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The result of eq (==)?or ne (~=) on handle objects whose allocation escapes the current function might be different between generated code and MATLAB
Description: Suppose that your MATLAB function foo  uses eq (==)  or ne (~=)  on a handle object MyHandle . Also, suppose that the allocation of?MyHandle?escapes the scope of foo ?in any  of these ways:  foo  reads?MyHandle?from one of its input arguments foo  writes?MyHandle?to one of its output arguments foo  reads?MyHandle?from a global or a persistent variable foo  writes?MyHandle?to a global or a persistent variable  In such cases, the result of eq (==)  or ne (~=)  on this handle object might be different between the generated code and MATLAB. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?

Summary: When switching views, the Architecture Views Gallery does not show the filter or updated property inspector for the view
Description: If the model has a large number of components, it takes time for the filter in the Architecture Views Gallery to appear when navigating between views. Additionally, the property inspector shows contents from the previous view selection.

Summary: Navigation to requirements in exported Web view file does not behave as expected
Description: When you generate a model Web view file that includes the model's requirements information, you can navigate to a requirement in two ways: Select a model element that is linked to a requirement. In the right pane, under Links , click the hyperlink to navigate to the listed requirement. In the Links ?tab, select a link. In the right pane, under Properties , a requirement might be listed as a?Destination ?item (or, rarely, as a Source? item). Click the hyperlink to navigate to the listed requirement.  When you try to navigate to a referenced requirement (slreq.Reference ?object), the navigation switches to the Requirements ?tab, but does not highlight or scroll to the referenced requirement. Additionally, this might happen sporadically for regular requirements ( slreq.Requirement ?objects).

Summary: dlhdl.buildProcessor fails for reference designs that are not supported
Description: If you specify a reference  design that is not supported for the?ReferenceDesign ?property of the dlhdl.ProcessorConfig  object, deep learning processor generation using dlhdl.buildProcessor ?fails with a message such as: "Invalid target interface 'AXI4 Master Activation Data Read'?for port 'dut_rd_data'"
Workaround: Specify a supported reference design for the?ReferenceDesign?property of the?dlhdl.ProcessorConfig?object. To find the supported reference designs for each target platform, inspect the files at this path:<supportPkgRoot>\toolbox\dnnfpga\supportpackages\<fpgaVendor>\buildHere "fpgaVendor" is "xilinx" or "intel" and "supportPkgRoot" is the installation location for MATLAB support packages. To determine the installation location, run this MATLAB command:>> matlabshared.supportpkg.getSupportPackageRoot?For example, on a Windows machine, the standard location for Xilinx boards is:C:\ProgramData\MATLAB\SupportPackages\R2020b\toolbox\dnnfpga\supportpackages\xilinx\build?

Summary: Parallel build failure when execution profiling is enabled and generated code is up to date
Description: The build process for your model might fail under these conditions: Parallel building of referenced models is enabled. The generated code is up to date. The shared utility folder contains at least one .c  or .cpp  file. Execution profiling is enabled.  You might see an error message like this:? Error using loadUnable to read file 'C:\ Users\...\_sharedutils\ buildInfo. mat'. ?No such file or directory. Error in coder. internal. instrumentationStage
Workaround: Disable parallel building of referenced models. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box,? clear the?Enable parallel model reference builds check box.

Summary: __MW_INSTRUM_?text in compilation error from SIL or PIL simulation configured for code coverage analysis
Description: Your software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation might fail under these conditions: Your Simulink model is configured to collect code coverage metrics by using Simulink Coverage. The build folder is from a previous SIL or PIL simulation. The newly generated code is identical to the previously generated code, which can occur if the Rebuild  configuration parameter is set to Always .  The simulation produces a compiler error message. For example: In function `myModelName':myModelName. c:(. text+0x22): undefined reference to `__MW_INSTRUM_myModelName_INIT_FILE' myModelName. c:(. text+0x30): undefined reference to `__mw_instrum_myModelName_phits'
Workaround: Delete the contents of the build folder, which contains myModelName.c.Run a new SIL or PIL simulation.

Summary: The Simulink Check Generic Tool Classification is incorrect for potential malfunctions or erroneous output [SLCHK_E5]
Description: In the ISO 26262 Simulink Check Tool Qualification?Package? (certkitiec_slchk_tqp) artifact, the generic Tool Classification table incorrectly identifies that the tool can claim TCL2 or TCL1 for potential malfunctions or erroneous output [SLCHK_E5], depending on which error prevention and detection measures is used:?  [M2a] -?Subsequent re-checking of the model for compliance with specified modeling guidelines. (TCL2)  [M2b]?- Subsequent dynamic verification (testing) of the model. (TCL2)  [M2c] - Subsequent comparison of the XML files exported from the original and fixed Simulink models and manual review of the comparison results (TCL1)   The correct, and only, error prevention and detection measure is:? [SLCHK_M2] Verification of the model afterautomatic fixing of reported issuesAfter automatic fixing the model, the tool user shall recheck the fixed model?for compliance with specified modeling guidelines andcompare the?original and fixed Simulink models?files.?  By using the correct measure for [SLCHK_E5], the Tool Error Detection is TD1 and the tool classification level is TCL1.

Summary: JMAAB check?Consistency in model element names?incorrectly warns about Truth Table block
Description: The Model Advisor check?Consistency in model element names ? (ID:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0602)? produces an incorrect warning about the inconsistency in model element names for a Truth Table block.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 3, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect results in generated code when Parameter Writer block writes to Model block parameters with non-auto parameter mapping in an AUTOSAR target
Description: Code generated from a model may produce incorrect results when all of the following conditions apply: A Parameter Writer block is inside an Initialization Function, Reset Function, or Terminate Function. The Parameter Writer block writes to a Model block parameter. The corresponding referenced model is set as an?AUTOSAR?target. The corresponding parameter in the referenced model has a parameter mapping other than auto .
Workaround: Do one of the following:Change the code generation target to an ERT-based target.On the Parameters tab of the Code Mappings editor, change the mapping of the parameter being written by the Parameter Writer block to auto.

Summary: Polyspace configset attachment popup halts the server workflow using polyspace.ModelLinkOptions
Description: When using polyspace.ModelLinkOptions ?to run Polyspace verifications from MATLAB, if you are using a configuration set reference, you may not have a pslinkoptions  object containing the Polyspace options attached to that Configset reference. In this case, a popup asks you to confirm the attachment, which can break the workflow using Continuous Integration with Polyspace Server product line.
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: Incorrect MISRA C:2012 rule 10.4 violation when operands are Boolean types
Description: If you use analysis option -boolean-types BOOL  to define type BOOL  as essentially Boolean, and then you compare BOOL  with an rvalue that is expanded from the TRUE  or FALSE  macros, Polyspace incorrectly flags the comparison as a violation of MISRA C:2012 Rule 10.4.

Summary: Polyspace-configure command fails on Windows
Description: The polyspace-configure ?command might fail to generate a project or options file if you run the command on Windows and the path to your compiler executable contains dots and ends with .exe . For instance: C:\path.with.dots\gcc.exe   In the command output, you see an error message similar to this: ERROR: Fatal error (exception): mwboost::filesystem::last_write_time
Workaround: To work around this issue, try one of the following:Remove the .exe extension from your compiler name:C:/folder.with.dots/gcc.exe --> C:/folder.with.dots/gccRename your compiler folder to remove all dots in the folder path:C:/folder.with.dots/gcc.exe --> C:/folder_without_dots/gcc.exe

Summary: Assertion or MATLAB crash during HDL code generation in the presence of unconnected output ports of Stateflow charts with input events.
Description: When using Stateflow charts that contain input events for HDL code generation, user may see the following assertion in the presence of dead or unconnected output ports of the chart: ?### Begin VHDL Code Generation for 'sfchart_event_dead_out'. failed assertion in matlab\ src\ cgir_hdl\ hdl_emitters\ CGIRCompEmitter. cpp at line 825:????In registerIOSignals: Assertion failed: sig[??0] matlab\ bin\ win64\ cgir_hdl. dll+09026218 pir::CGIRCompEmitter::globalPhase+00001482[??1] matlab\ bin\ win64\ cgir_hdl. dll+09129704 pir::PIREmitter::commonPostTransformPhase+00000360[??2] matlab\ bin\ win64\ cgir_hdl. dll+09497484 pir::VHDLPIREmitter::postTransformPhase+00000028[??3] matlab\ bin\ win64\ cgir_hdl. dll+09198422 pir::PIREmitter::runThroughNameUniquification+00001622[??4] matlab\ bin\ win64\ cgir_hdl. dll+09200034 pir::PIREmitter::startEmission+00000386[??5] matlab\ bin\ win64\ cgir_hdl. dll+05296563 metadatafactory::udd::UDDMetaDataFactory_MethodInfo::invokeMethod+00000259[??6] matlab\ bin\ win64\ udd. dll+00395264 UDMethodInfo::invokeMethodE+00000032[??7] matlab\ bin\ win64\ udd_mi. dll+00477095 ouAddBuiltInMethod+00001575 Users may also encounter the following MATLAB crash in some cases: [? 0 ]? pir::CGIRCompEmitter::registerIOSignals(pir::CGIRComp? const &, mwboost::shared_ptr<pir::PortAccessor>)? const ?at /matlab/src/cgir_hdl/hdl_emitters/CGIRCompEmitter.cpp: 816 ? [? 1 ] ?pir::CGIRCompEmitter::registerIOSignals(pir::CGIRComp&)? const ?at /matlab/src/cgir_hdl/hdl_emitters/CGIRCompEmitter.cpp: 842  [? 2 ] ?pir::CGIRCompEmitter::globalPhase()? const ?at /matlab/src/cgir_hdl/hdl_emitters/CGIRCompEmitter.cpp: 466 ?

Summary: The hydraulicToIsothermalLiquid function incorrectly returns an error that stops the conversion process
Description: The Hydraulic To Isothermal Liquid Conversion Tool incorrectly returns an error when updating a model that contains:A Counterbalance Valve block with the Orifice laminar transition specification ?parameter or the?Check valve laminar transition specification  parameter?set to Pressure ratio .  A Hydraulic Fluid block with the Hydraulic fluid ?set to Water-Glycol 60/40 .
Workaround: In the Counterbalance Valve block(s) in your model, set the?Orifice laminar transition specification parameter?and the?Check valve laminar transition specification?parameter to Reynolds number?prior to running the conversion tool.In the Hydraulic Fluid block(s) in your model:Delete or comment out the Hydraulic Fluid block before running the hydraulicToIsothermalLiquid function. Delete any connections to the Hydraulic Fluid block.After executing the function, add an Isothermal Liquid Predefined Properties (IL) block to the converted model. Connect the block to the Isothermal Liquid network. In the block, set:Isothermal liquid?to?Ethylene glycol and water mixture.Ethylene glycol volume fraction?to?0.4.Isothermal bulk modulus at atmospheric pressure (no entrained air)?to the same settings as the Hydraulic fluid block?Bulk modulus (Pa) at atm. pressure and no gas parameter.System temperature?to the same settings as the Hydraulic fluid block?System temperature (C)?parameter.Viscosity derating factor?to the same settings as the Hydraulic fluid block?Viscosity derating factor?parameter.

Summary: If patterns are used with multiple parpool('threads') sessions, MATLAB crashes
Description: If patterns are used with multiple parpool('threads')  sessions, MATLAB crashes. Functions that create patterns, such as digitsPattern , lettersPattern , and logical OR  on two strings, are affected. This problem also affects indirect uses of patterns, such as calling the?fileparts ?function (since fileparts  uses patterns).

Summary: Model block normal mode visibility selection not displayed when block path contains a line break
Description: After enabling normal mode visibility for a model instance with a line break in its block name or path, the corresponding check box in the Model Block Normal Mode Visibility dialog box does not appear to be selected.?

Summary: Adding For Each Subsystem block to Simulink model prevents A2L file generation
Description: Adding a For Each Subsystem  block to a Simulink model prevents A2L file generation during a model build.

Summary: MATLAB crash when using the two output form of sort with large gpuArray inputs
Description: MATLAB might crash when using the two output form of sort  with gpuArray  inputs that are sufficiently large such that the GPU memory becomes exhausted during the computation. For example:? % Create a large gpuArrayx = gpuArray(rand(2000000, 10));?% Use the two output form of sort[s,i] = sort(x);
Workaround: To avoid this issue, either use smaller gpuArray?inputs, or perform the computation on the CPU by gathering the gpuArray data before calling sort. ?For example:[s,i] = sort(gather(x));?

Summary: Calling solve?in parallel on an EquationProblem?might fail to find a solution
Description: For some?EquationProblems , solve ?might not find a solution when the?UseParallel ?option is set to true . For example: ep = eqnproblem;x = optimvar('x', 1, 2);F = optimexpr(2, 1); F(1) = (-(x(1)+x(2))).^2 - x(2)*(1+x(1)^2);F(2) = x(1)*(x(2)-3).^2 + x(2)*(x(1)-2).^2 - 0.5;ep.Equations = F == 0;x0.x = [0 0];opts = optimoptions('fsolve', 'UseParallel',true);sol = solve(ep, x0, 'Options', opts); This code returns: No solution found.fsolve stopped because the problem appears regular as measured by the gradient,but the vector of function values is not near zero as measured by thevalue of the function tolerance. However, when run in serial, solve  finds a solution. sol = solve(ep, x0); When called with UseParallel  set to its default value (false ), solve  returns the following: Equation solved.fsolve completed because the vector of function values is near zeroas measured by the value of the function tolerance, andthe problem appears regular as measured by the gradient.
Workaround: Do not specify the UseParallel?option or set it to?false:sol = solve(ep, x0);

Summary: Unable to import requirements from IBM DOORS Next using direct import
Description: You can directly import requirements from IBM DOORS Next by opening the Requirements Editor and selecting File  > Import . In the Importing Requirements dialog, set Document type ?to IBM DOORS Next . Enter your server information and credentials in the DOORS Server dialog. The Document location ?drop-down is auto-populated with project names from the server that you entered. However, sometimes this drop-down? is not populated with DOORS Next project names, and it is impossible to select the project to import from. The malfunction happens when DOORS Next Server is configured for "Basic Authentication" (as opposed to the "Form-based Authentication"), and the IBM Jazz server version is 7.0.1 or newer.

Summary: Code-generated fmincon never finishes in particular circumstances
Description: When you generate code for fmincon  using MATLAB Coder, Simulink Coder, or Embedded Coder products, the software runs indefinitely under the following circumstances. The problem has redundant linear equality constraints, and Some lower bounds equal the corresponding upper bounds (there are "fixed" variables).  For example, this function has fixed variables and redundant linear equality constraints: function solveNonlinearProblem()  %#codegen?    % Define consistent equality constraints with rank of one.  Aeq = [0, 1, 0, 1, 0, 0  ? ? ? ?0, 1, 0, 1, 0, 0];?  beq = [2; 2];?    % Fourth variable is fixed.  lb = [ 1e-6; -Inf; -Inf; 2; -Inf; -Inf];  ub = [ Inf;? Inf;? Inf;? 2;? 1e2;? Inf];    x0 = zeros(6, 1);?  options = optimoptions('fmincon','Algorithm','sqp','Display','none');?    [x,fval,exitflag] = fmincon(@vecnorm,x0,[],[],Aeq,beq,lb,ub,[],options);    fprintf('Solution norm: %4.5e\n', fval);  fprintf('Exitflag: %i\n', int32(exitflag));  end? Running this function in the MATLAB Command Window returns fval  = 2 and exitflag  = 1.? However, running the command>> codegen -config:mex solveNonlinearProblem  creates a MEX file called?solveNonlinearProblem_mex? that runs indefinitely when executed.
Workaround: Widen all fixed bounds by at least?options.ConstraintTolerance. In the previous example, setub(4) = ub(4) + options.ConstraintTolerance;The code-generated program then runs normally.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might not call handle class destructors
Description: If your MATLAB code uses handle classes, in certain situations, the generated code might not contain calls to the?delete  methods for some of the handle objects. This might cause the generated code to not execute the code that the omitted?delete  methods contain (for example,?code that performs clean-up actions such as closing a file handle or a socket.) This issue might occur if your MATLAB code satisfies both  ?these conditions: Your MATLAB code contains a handle object that has a property that is also a handle object. The second handle object has a property that is also a handle object.?  In such situations, the generated code might not call the delete  method of the innermost object (the object at the third level of nesting).? This issue affects MATLAB Coder and blocks that use MATLAB Code (for example, MATLAB Function block) in Simulink.?

Summary: writestruct?causes MATLAB to crash when the input struct contains a nested empty structure
Description: Calling the?writestruct ?function on a struct  containing a nested empty structure can cause MATLAB to crash, as shown in this code. >> S.foo = struct('bar',{})S =   struct with fields:    foo: [0ˇÁ0 struct]>> writestruct(S, 'test.xml')

Summary: NaNs appear at the output port of the Recursive Polynomial Model Estimator when system is overparameterized
Description: The problem occurs when the system is overparameterized (has too high an order) with respect to the variation in the data. The parameters of the identified system become insensitive to the data and this insensitivity drives the covariance to infinity.?  This results in Nans to appear at the output port of the Recursive Polynomial Model Estimator. Embedded processors may have difficulty in processing NaN values.

Summary: The Model Advisor check Check fundamental logical and numerical operations?displays an error when using?Simulink.AliasType
Description: The Model Advisor check Check fundamental logical and numerical operations ?(ID:mathworks.maab.na_0002 ) checks for appropriate usage of basic logical and numerical operations. Signals with Boolean alias type (Simulink.AliasType) connected to Enable subsystem or Trigger subsystem block is flagged by the check even though their base type is Boolean. For a similar issue see the following?bug report .
Fixed In: R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 3, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: International characters are not handled correctly for X3D virtual worlds
Description: When opening an X3D virtual world using the XML encoding, international characters may not be read correctly from the source X3D file. This may prevent the virtual world from being saved or edited.

Summary: FlattenHierarchy wrongly flattens subsystems with different BalanceDelays option from parent
Description: When controlling the scope of delay balancing ?on subsystems, some unexpected behavior may occur when also applying FlattenHierarchy to the design. Subsystems with different BalanceDelays options from the parent system can be wrongly flattened when Flatten Hierarchy is requested. Consider the case where the parent system has BalanceDelays=off and the child subsystem has BalanceDelays=on. If the expectation is that the child subsystem will participate in optimizations which require delay balancing, like Clock Rate Pipelining, then the generated HDL code will not be as expected. Flatten Hierarchy will have merged the child into the parent and now the resources from the child subsystem will not have delay balancing applied.
Workaround: Mark the subsystem such that is does not participate in Flatten Hierarchy.?>> hdlset_param(<path_to_child_subsystem>, 'FlattenHierarchy', 'off')?

Summary: MATLAB Online hangs after clicking Training Options in Deep Network Designer
Description: Clicking the Training Options button in Deep Network Designer can fail to open the Training Options dialog box and cause MATLAB Online to hang.  To recover from this state, you must restart MATLAB Online.

Summary: Update target computer generates Java FTP exception
Description: Update target computer can generate this exception: Cannot update software on target 'TargetPC1': Cannot copy file'C:\ProgramData\MATLAB\SupportPackages\R2020b\toolbox\slrealtime\target\supportpackage\qnxtools.tar' to'/home/slrt/.config/updateos/qnxtools.tar' on target 'TargetPC1': Java exception occurred:org.apache.commons.net.io.CopyStreamException: IOException caught while copying.at org.apache.commons.net.io.Util.copyStream(Util.java:129)at org.apache.commons.net.ftp.FTPClient.__storeFile(FTPClient.java:385)at org.apache.commons.net.ftp.FTPClient.storeFile(FTPClient.java:1379)
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Error when simulating a model with more than 17 SoC Blockset Hardware/Software connections
Description: If a model contains more than 17?SoC Blockset hardware/software?connections, such as 17 or more registers, Simulink can throw the following error in simulation: Simulink error: Error reported by S-function 'soc_data_pack' in 'xxxx/ xxxx/ Variant/ SIM/ HWSW_Message_Send/ Data_Pack': Too many bytepack blocks.?

Summary: Spurious error about conflicting symbols in the generated code
Description: The code generator may produce spurious errors about parameters used within reusable library subsystems. The error occurs if the model configuration set parameter 'GenerateSharedConstants ' is turned off and does not occur if the parameter is turned on. The errors contains text like this: Error computing symbol names in model model . ?The following symbol 'symbol ' needs to be changed or mangled to make symbol names?unique using the symbol format string 'storage class'. ?Consider changing one of the symbols or adding $M to the symbol format string.  The symbol referred to in the error message are unused in the generated code.?

Summary: The height of a geographic location must be non-negative in the comm.Ray object
Description: When the comm.Ray  object is specified as an input to the raypl  function or set to the?PropagationRays  property of a?comm.RayTracingChannel  object, an error will be thrown from the comm.Ray  object when the?CoordinateSystem  property is set to 'Geographic'  and the third row of the TransmitterLocation ,?ReceiverLocation  or?ReflectionLocations  property has a negative element.
Workaround: If a negative height is specified or required, open the comm.Ray object and remove the code that enforces the error checking:In R2020a, remove?obj(i).TransmitterLocation(3) < 0 from line 864,?obj(i).ReceiverLocation(3) < 0 from line 872, and?any(obj(i).ReflectionLocations(3,:) < 0)?from line 880.?In R2020b, remove?obj(i).TransmitterLocation(3) < 0?from line 913,?obj(i).ReceiverLocation(3) < 0?from line 921, and?any(obj(i).ReflectionLocations(3,:) < 0)?from line 929.?

Summary: Stateflow chart code not placed in _sharedutils directory when it contains another library-linked reusable chart
Description: If a reusable library-linked Stateflowchart in a model does not contain any modeling semantics that are describedas?limitations ?in the documentation,then the code for Stateflow chart will be generated in the _sharedutils directory.  If a parent Stateflow chart contains a reusable child chartthat is itself library-linked and doesn't contain any semantics described in the limitations above, then the parent child might be prevented from generating code in _sharedutils . This is an unintendedlimitation of the code generator optimizations. For more information, see?Generate Reusable Code from Library Subsystems Shared Across Models .

Summary: Grouping components in a System Composer model and then synchronizing the related allocation set causes error "Uniqueness constraint violation"
Description: The error "Uniqueness constraint violation" appears when synchronizing an allocation set in the Allocation Editor. The error appears if at least one of the following actions were taken on either of the allocated System Composer models: Select one or more components in the model editor and select "Group Selected Components" from the context menu. Select one or more components in the model editor and press CTRL+G on the keyboard.

Summary: rosgenmsg throws an error at the build time while creating a custom message for a message definition.
Description: When executing the rosgenmsg  function, a failure occurs during the build time. The build log contains the following error: CMake Error: CMake was unable to find a build program corresponding to "Ninja". ??CMAKE_MAKE_PROGRAM is not set. ??You probably need to select a different build tool. CMake Error: CMAKE_C_COMPILER not set, ?after EnableLanguage CMake Error: CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER not set, ?after EnableLanguage?  You may encounter this issue when your OS is Linux and the default shell of MATLAB is tcsh .

Summary: Generating custom messages gives an error: Ambiguous output redirect.
Description: When executing the rosgenmsg  function, an error message is returned: ? ? ?Ambiguous output redirect.?  You may encounter this issue when your OS is Linux and default shell of MATLAB is tcsh .

Summary: Custom messages with time or duration as a field errors out in ROS Simulink Blocks
Description: When you add a Blank Message block and set the message type to your own generated custom messages, Simulink gives the following error: Error evaluating 'MaskDialog' callback of ROS Blank Message block (mask) 'BasicModel/ Blank Message'. Callback string is 'ros. slros. internal. block. MessageBlockMask. dispatch('entityTypeEdit', ?gcb);'   This happens when generated custom message definition has time or duration as a field in it.?

Summary: The Interpreter?property of label UI components?does not respond correctly to Enable, FontSize, or specific label text
Description: When using the Interpreter  property with a label UI component, there are the following inconsistencies. When Interpreter  is 'html'  or 'latex' , the Enable  property has no effect. When?Interpreter ?is 'html'  or 'latex' , the FontSize  property has no effect when it is set before or at the same time as?Interpreter . When?Interpreter ?is 'html' , the label disappears from the App Designer design view when changing other properties. When?Interpreter ?is 'latex' , display equations in multiline input might appear on an incorrect line.
Workaround: Here are possible workarounds for some of the issues above.To update the font size, set?FontSize after creating the label or in the startupFcn of App Designer. The value of FontSize?must change for this workaround to have an effect. For example, the code below would not work if the font size was already 25.>> drawnow>> app.Label.FontSize = 25;If the label in App Designer disappears, resize the label in the design view.To place LaTeX display equations on separate lines, specify Text as a cell array of character vectors or a string array, with each display equation as a separate element. For example:>> l = uilabel(uifigure,'Interpreter','latex','Position',[100 100 100 70]);?>>?l.Text = ["Inline: $f(x)= x^2$" "Display:" "$$f(x)= \frac{1}{x}$$"];

Summary: Configuration reference in a data dictionary might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash when a data dictionary has a configuration reference object that points to a configuration object in the data dictionary, and the configuration reference object is updated to point to a different configuration object in the data dictionary.

Summary: Dynamics calculations using the rigidBodyTree?object functions?might return wrong answers when using DH parameters
Description: If you specify DH?parameters ("dh" ) when using the setFixedTransform  object function for rigidBodyTree  objects, there might be a transformation error. This transformation error results in incorrect dynamics calculations for functions such as forwardDynamics , inverseDynamics , or geometricJacobian .
Workaround: To get correct dynamics calculations, specify your transformations using MDH (mdhparams) or homogenous transformation (tform) inputs. For more information, see the?setFixedTransform?object function.
Products: Robotics System Toolbox, ROS Toolbox, Navigation Toolbox

Summary: Simulation 3D Vision Detection Generator block outputs inaccurate velocity and angular velocity values of detected actors
Description: The Actor Truth bus output by the Simulation 3D Vision Detection Generator block reports inaccurate Velocity  and AngularVelocity  values of detected actors.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur when code for DSP System Toolbox blocks is replaced with library code
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a model that meets all of these conditions:The model contains a block from the DSP System Toolbox, such as an FIR Interpolation block. At least one of the block parameters is set to a structure field of variables that belong to the model workspace or a mask workspace. The model uses one of these code replacement libraries: ARM Cortex-A  ARM Cortex-M?  ARM Cortex-M (CMSIS)  ARM Cortex-M (Optimized)  Intel AVX (Windows)  Intel AVX (Linux)?   The code for the DSP System Toolbox block is replaced with the library code specified in one of the code replacement libraries above.

Summary: velodyneROSMessageReader errors out or crashes MATLAB when an empty message is provided as input
Description: When providing an empty message as an input to velodyneROSMessageReader? function, you might experience the following error message: Error using velodyneROSMessageReader>iParseAndValidateInputs  (line 478) Unsupported data type. The input message must be velodyne_msgs/VelodyneScan  ROS messages.? Or MATLAB might crash with the following stack trace:? Summary: Runtime error from generated code that includes a pcfitcuboid function call
Description: When a point cloud passed to the pcfitcuboid  function has only a single valid point or index, MATLAB might crash or throw an error when running generated code that includes this function call.

Summary: webcam object creation might fail in latest Linux distributions having GStreamer 1.14 if video format conversion is required
Description: If you install the MATLAB Support Package for USB Webcams on recent Linux distributions having GStreamer version 1.14, the webcam  object might not be able to establish a connection with a USB webcam that supports compressed video formats. This is because the libv4l2 library, which is used by the support package for video format conversion, is disabled by default in GStreamer version 1.14 and above.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use one of the following procedures for your MATLAB release.For MATLAB R2020b, update the MATLAB Support Package for USB Webcams to version 20.2.1 by uninstalling it from the Add-On Manager and reinstalling it. For MATLAB releases prior to R2020b, use the following steps:Open the Terminal application.?Execute the following command to set the environment variable that enables the?libv4l2?library:export GST_V4L2_USE_LIBV4L2=1Or, if you are using a C shell, use the following command:?setenv GST_V4L2_USE_LIBV4L2 1?Ensure that the environment variable is set properly. Execute the following command:printenv??GST_V4L2_USE_LIBV4L2If the environment variable is set properly, this command returns 1.Navigate to the?<MATLAB>/bin?folder in Terminal.Start MATLAB by using the following command:./matlabThis allows you to use the?webcam?object to establish a connection with your USB webcam that supports compressed video formats.?

Summary: Error from instantiating a model for analysis after performing Create Component from Selection on a component and its connections
Description: You might not be able to instantiate a model for analysis if you performed the Create Component from Selection  action on the model. A model can get into this incorrect state after the following actions: Select one or more components and connectors, then right-click to bring up the context menu.?? Select the Create Component from Selection  menu item to create a new component.  The following steps to instantiate the model for analysis, generate an error: From the Views section of the toolstrip, select either Analysis Model  or Analysis Viewer . Select the profiles and stereotypes to instantiate the model with. Click Instantiate .

Summary: MATLAB might crash when opening Simulink model or subsystem that contains Simscape blocks
Description: When you try to open a model containing Simscape blocks and?certain files are missing from the MATLAB path, MATLAB might crash with?the following error:? ? ? ? ?Assertion in physmod_common_gl_sli_kernel:isIconStylingEnabled  This issue can occur if toolbox files are missing or if the toolbox path cache needs to be updated.?
Workaround: First, try updating the toolbox path cache. In the Preferences dialog box, under?General,?click Update Toolbox Path Cache.?Alternatively, execute the following command at the MATLAB command window:? rehash toolboxcacheIf the issue persists, try regenerating MATLAB preferences. See the following link for instructions:? ?https://www.mathworks.com/matlabcentral/answers/99625-how-do-i-regenerate-my-matlab-preferences?If the issue persists, please contact The MathWorks licensing and installation support team:? ?https://www.mathworks.com/support/contact_us.html??

Summary: Large CAD assemblies imported from cloud storage might affect simulation performance
Description: Models containing large assemblies that are stored on the cloud (such as, in OneDrive) might have degraded performance during simulations subsequent to the first simulation. The degraded performance is a slowdown in the simulation compared to runtimes when files are stored on a local or network directory. The slowdown occurs even if the files have not been changed since last being read. Normally, when models with CAD assemblies are loaded into a Simulink session for the first time, the operation takes some time. Subsequent simulations of the model during the same session run faster because they do not require reimporting the files unless they have changed since last being read.?
Workaround: Move the CAD files to a local or network directory.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 8, R2020b Update 4, R2021a

Summary: Nonfinite support files might not be present in generated C++11 code
Description: If you generate C++11 code from your MATLAB code, the code generator might not produce these supporting files: rtGetNaN.h  rtGetNaN.cpp  rtGetInf.h  rtGetInf.cpp?   In such situations, when you build the generated code to produce libraries or executables, the absence of these files might cause a build failure.
Workaround: Change the code generation configuration settings to generate C++03 or C code.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility checking fails for model containing AUTOSAR Blockset lookup table blocks
Description: If you check Simulink Design Verifier compatibility for a model that contains AUTOSAR Blockset lookup table blocks, the function fails with the following error message: Dot indexing is not supported for variables of this type.Component:simulink | Category:Design Verifier compatibility error
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 4, R2021a

Summary: Functions in MATLAB classes do not receive coverage
Description: Functions defined as part of a?MATLAB class do not receive coverage.

Summary: Simulation Data Inspector incorrectly maps labels to values for enumerated data types defined in MATLAB packages
Description: When you import enumerated data into the Simulation Data Inspector, labels are mapped to values incorrectly if the enumerated data type is defined in a MATLAB package.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model reference targets may fail to rebuild when they have unsaved changes and parallel builds are used
Description: If a model references models that contain unsaved changes and parallel building is enabled for the referenced models, the parallel build process does not consider the unsaved changes. This can result in model reference simulation targets and code generation targets that are out of date. For example, if a model contains a Model block with Simulation mode  set to Accelerator  or? Software-in-the-loop (SIL) , simulation might produce incorrect numeric results.

Summary: ROS 2 nodes are unable to be created if external ROS environment is sourced
Description: Creation of ROS 2 nodes error if ROS or ROS 2 libraries are on the system PATH or library path. The error also occurs if the RMW_IMPLEMENTATION environment variable is set. The following error message is returned: Error using ros2node/createNode (line 319)  Error creating the ROS 2 node <name>.  Error in ros2node (line 119)  ? ? ? ? ? ?createNode(obj, parser.Results.name, parser.Results.id);?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect type for Constant-scope data in a Stateflow chart if the initial value is an enumeration
Description: In Stateflow charts using MATLAB action language, if you use an initial value for a Constant-scope data that is an enumeration, both simulation and generated code might use the enumeration type instead of the specified data type for the Constant-scope data, if the data is a scalar. If the data is non-scalar, Stateflow might throw an assertion error for both C and MATLAB action language charts when you run or update the model.
Workaround: Explicitly cast enumerated initial values to the specified type of the constant data.

Summary: Lateral Driver block incorrectly calculates the cumulative lateral tracking error signal, ErrSqrSum,? ?when Lateral Control type is set to Stanley
Description: If you set the Lateral Driver block parameter?Lateral Control type  to?Stanley , the block inc orrectly calculates the cumulative lateral tracking error signal,?ErrSqrSum . The  ErrSqrSum  signal is in the Info bus output port.

Summary: Simulation 3D Scene Configuration block onlyloads Unreal Engine??executable?files in the local?folder
Description: In the Simulation 3D Scene Configuration block, if you set?Scene source ?to?Unreal Executable ,? the block only loads Unreal Engine? executable?files that are in the local?folder. If you try to load the Unreal Engine??executable?file from a network path, the block does not load it.

Summary: Unable to save Simulink.SimulationData.Dataset?object that contains empty fixed-point signal to v7.3 MAT file
Description: When you try to save a Simulink.SimulationData.Dataset ?object to a v7.3 MAT file, no data is saved and this warning occurs if the Dataset ?object contains an empty signal with a fixed-point data type:  Warning: While saving an object of class 'Simulink. SimulationData. Dataset':MatFileDatasetStorage::locPreprocessData data is empty   For example, if you log a fixed-point signal inside an enabled subsystem using the Dataset ?format, the logged dataset contains an empty fixed-point signal if the enabled subsystem remains inactive throughout the simulation.?
Workaround: Save the Dataset?object to a v6 or v7 MAT file.

Summary: When EtherCAT blocks are present in a referenced model, model build generates a compile error.
Description: When EtherCAT blocks are present in a referenced model, a model build of the model that contains the referenced model block generates a compile and link error. The build process does not create variable <modelname>_rtm. When the variable cannot be found, the build fails.

Summary: Generated code may be incorrect if a mask parameter in reusable subsystems is used in multiple places
Description: In reusable subsystems if a mask parameter is used in multiple places and one of them has logical expression then the generated code may be incorrect. This is especially true if the value of mask parameter exceeds underlying data type of boolean  (which is unsigned char ). In such a case, overflow may occur and generated code may not produce correct result.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Structures of fi?types used as block parameters incorrectly duplicated in?model_data.c file
Description: When multiple variables that are structures with identical shape and embedded.fi  values are used in a reusable subsystem, the generated code in the model_data.c  file might incorrectly assign the same value to each of them.

Summary: Parameters inheriting data type from CAN Pack or CAN Unpack blocks might halt model build
Description: When a Simulink Real-Time model contains one or more CAN Pack or CAN Unpack blocks, the model build might halt during compilation when all of the following apply: The storage class for model elements of category Model parameters  is set to PageSwitching  in the Data Defaults  tab of the Code perspective. The default parameter behavior is Tunable . A block parameter has inherited the CAN_MESSAGE ?data type.  For these conditions, the model build halts during compilation with the error: error: 'CAN_DATATYPE' does not name a type
Workaround: To resolve the block issues that halt the model build:Open the Code perspective and expand the Code Mappings - C pane.On the Data Defaults tab, change the Storage Class for Model parameters to Default.

Summary: Error when using parallel functionality on MAC OS?10.15.7?machines with a non-ASCII computer name
Description: When you try to use Parallel Computing Toolbox functionality on a machine running MAC OS?10.15.7, if the machine has a non-ASCII computer name, you see an error message similar to the following. The list of cluster profiles failed to load and so they are unusable. To recover, check the reason and restart MATLAB. Reason: Invalid character code sequence detected.  You might also encounter the error if the computer name only contains characters which are part of the ASCII character set.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Code Generation:?GPU Coder generated code might produce incorrect output in the presence of gpucoder.reduce with large inputs
Description: GPU Coder generated code produces incorrect output when the following conditions are met: The design function contains gpucoder.reduce.?  The number of elements in the input array to gpucoder.reduce  is greater than 67107841 (?numel(inputArray) > 67107841 ).
Fixed In: R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 3, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Cryptic error in the presence of filter blocks and when?Generate resource utilization report enabled
Description: HDL Coder generates an unhelpful error message when the following conditions are true:  1) FlattenHierarchy ?is turned on in the hierarchy of the filter block/s 2)?Generate resource utilization report  is turned on?HDL Code Generation ?>?Report ?pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box. The error message is as follows:? ?Error  :?"Index exceeds the number of array elements (0). "?  ? List of filter blocks fall under above category:?  Biquad Filter CIC Decimation CIC Interpolation Digital Filter? Discrete FIR FIR Decimation, FIR Interpolation
Workaround: consider disabling "Generate resource utilization report" option in the global settings.

Summary: Incorrect violations in AUTOSAR C++ 14 A8-5-2
Description: For the following example, Polyspace Bug Finder raises some incorrect violations of AUTOSAR C++ A8-5-2: #include <memory>class A {public: void bar() {} };std::shared_ptr<A> foo(); // No need of braces as it is a function declarationstd::shared_ptr<A> foo() { return std::make_shared<A>(); }int main(int argc, char* argv[]) {  auto a = foo(); // No need of braces as it is 'auto' initialization  std::shared_ptr<A>& b = a; // No need of braces as it is a reference declaration  return 0;}

Summary: Bug Finder or Code Prover compilation stops at the linking phase with the message:?The process "ps_cxx_fe" received the signal 11
Description: When compiling C/C++ sources, in some cases, the analysis might stop with one of the messages: The process "ps_cxx_fe" received the signal 11  double free or corruption (!prev)  realloc(): invalid next size  ps_cxx_fe: edg/src/preproc.c:1544: void new_logically_defined_set(): Assertion `logically_defined_ptr < LOGICALLY_DEFINED_STACK_SIZE' failed.  Error: The process "ps_cxx_fe" received the signal 6.   The issue occurs during the preprocessing of the sources.  To make sure that it is the preprocessing phase that crashes, you can add the -verbose  option to the analysis. One of the previous error messages must occur during the raw preprocessing phase like this: ********* Phase 3.1.1.1.2.1 (raw preprocess)The process "ps_cxx_fe" received the signal 11********* Phase 3.1.1.1.2.1 (raw preprocess) took 0.09real, 0.05u + 0.01s (0gc)
Workaround: The following workarounds are dependent on the code and might not always work. The bug occurs in code that uses a lot of macros.In R2020a and later: use the options -no-macro-in-pp-db and -no-macroIn R2018b to R2019b: use the options -no-macro?and -cfe-extra-flags --no_macro_expansion_in_pp_DBFor releases before R2018a, there is no workaround.
Products: Polyspace Code Prover, Polyspace Code Prover Server

Summary: Missing support for atomic subsystem traceability in execution profiler report
Description: When you generate this information: Create an atomic subsystem Configure for non-reusable function Change profiling level to Detailed    The timing information of each subsystem is shown on the report. To access the timing information, click the white button that appears on the profiling report. When you click the button, the profiler does not highlight the line of code in the generated code report.

Summary: Final state not restored for referenced model when a noncontinuous derivative output changes at the final state
Description: Simulation results might be incorrect if all the following conditions are true:? The top model in a model hierarchy references a model that does not reference other models. The referenced model outputs a noncontinuous derivative signal. For example, the referenced model might contain a Pulse Generator block that connects to an Integrator block that connects to a root output port. The referenced model simulates in accelerator mode. The referenced model Type ?configuration parameter is set to?Variable-step . The referenced model?Final states ?and?Initial state ?configuration parameters are selected.  Simulation does not restore the final state when the referenced model output changes at the final state.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated CUDA code for the sign function returns 0?instead of NaN for?NaN?input
Description: If the input to the sign  function is NaN , the CUDA code generated for  sign ?incorrectly returns  0 . In? MATLAB execution, the sign  function returns NaN ?for a NaN  input.

Summary: PLC compatibility check error when bus element name contains NaN
Description: If a Simulink model has a bus element name which contains NaN , Simulink PLC Coder will error out with the following message:Unable to generate code as Simulink PLC Coder detected NaN in this model.

Summary: Polyspace Bug Finder shows error: The process "ps_cxx_fe" received the signal 11
Description: If you analyze code using Polyspace Bug Finder, in some cases, a crash can occur with the following output: Error: The process "ps_cxx_fe" received the signal 11. This message indicates that the phase did not complete successfully and that some rules could not be detected.

Summary: MATLAB Job Scheduler errors on?Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7 (Maipo) and CentOS 7
Description: The following MATLAB Job Scheduler operations fail on?Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7 (Maipo) or CentOS 7 operating systems: Starting worker processes? Launching Admin Center?  The operations fail with the following error: Symbol lookup error: /home/local/MATLAB/R2020b/bin/glnxa64/../../sys/os/glnxa64/libstdc++.so.6:undefined symbol: __cxa_thread_atexit_impl

Summary: Reshape block with matrix input may generate unintuitive error during Floating Point HDL code generation for Intel/Altera Target
Description: When the input Simulink design has a Reshape block with matrix input and model configuration setting ' FloatingPointIPLibrary ' is configured to ' Altera Megafunctions (ALTFP) ', the following error appears during HDL Code Generation:  Error using hdlcoder.pirctx/earlyElaborate?Error: Evaluation of elaborate function on class hdldefaults.Reshape?failed with the error message:?MATLAB:UndefinedFunction: Undefined function 'setSourceBlock' for input?arguments of type 'hdlcoder.reshape_comp'.   FloatingPointIPLibrary  setting is present under HDL Code Generation > Floating Point > Floating Point IP Library

Summary: fft and ifft?can leak small amounts of memory when called with a vector input.
Description: The fft  and ifft  functions can leak memory when operating on vector inputs. The amount of unfreed memory after each call is small, but can accumulate over many calls, leading MATLAB to consume larger amounts of memory over time.
Workaround: A workaround is to call fft on a non-vector input and then extract the portion of interest.For example, replace a call that operates on a vector x,y = fft(x);with a call that operates on a matrix:y = fft([x x]);y = y(:,1);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur when an export-function model contains a Constant block that feeds a block executing in a different task
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for an export-function model that meets all of these conditions: The export-function model contains a Constant block. The output of the Constant block feeds a block which executes in a different task. At least one of these blocks executes in a function-call subsystem that executes at an inherited rate.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Multi-rate subsystem updates discrete state in an incorrect order
Description: When output and update code is in one function in the generated code, code generator may try to force the simulation execution order to be the same as the code generation order. The simulation execution order may not match the order in the generated code under all these conditions:? The model configuration parameter?"ForceCombineOutputUpdateInSim " is enabled.? The multi-rate subsystem contains Mux block. For the Mux block: Output is connected to a block with discrete state. One input is connected to Rate Transition block inside subsystem whereas other input is connected to Rate Transition block outside of subsystem.?  The simulation updates the buffer values in order such that output and update function for this subsystem cannot be combined.?
Workaround: Use Vector Concatenate block instead of Mux block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated from a combination of coder.const and coder.extrinsic?in MATLAB might be incorrect
Description: You can generate code from MATLAB code that simultaneously:? Declares a function foo ?as extrinsic by using  coder.extrinsic('foo')  Uses  coder.const  to constant-fold an expression that directly or indirectly invokes the extrinsic function  foo    This MATLAB code pattern might produce incorrect C/C++ code if the size of the return value of?foo  is different from what the caller of foo ?assumes (for example, based on a previous assignment). Specifically, this issue occurs if all  of these conditions are true about your MATLAB code:? The main function uses coder.const  to call a function Foo  Foo  contains a call to another function Bar  that is declared as extrinsic The extrinsic function Bar  returns an array A  The host function?Foo ?assigns this return value to a variable that has been assigned previously That variable is previously assigned from a scalar or from an array of size smaller than A   In such situations, the code generator drops the extra returned elements, and truncates the returned (larger) array so that it fits the size of the initially assigned array A . This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?

Summary: Index output of min and max can be incorrect for multi-column gpuArray inputs with a dimension of size 8200 or greater.
Description: Using the min   and max  functions with a single input array argument can result in an incorrect answer for the optional index output under the following conditions:? Input array is a gpuArray;  input array has two or more non-singleton dimensions; one or more of the dimensions to operate along is the first dimension?or the?first non-singleton dimension; and? that dimension is of size 8200 or greater.  ?For example, computing the min or max by operating along the first dimension of a gpuArray  of size 8200-by-2 can produce an incorrect index.
Workaround: To avoid the issue, permute the dimensions of the input array so that the dimension to operate along is not the first non-singleton dimension. Then modify the dimension to operate along as appropriate and permute the result back.For example, to compute the maximum of a matrix X use the following code:[M,I] = max(X',[],2);M = M';

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Changing a model argument value might not trigger rebuild of model reference target
Description: Simulation and code generation might use an out-of-date model reference target when a model argument value has changed and Simulink adds hidden Model blocks to the model. For example, Simulink adds hidden Model blocks when: The output of a Model block is a virtual bus. A Model block receives input from a?function-call input port.
Workaround: Delete the?slprj?folder and?.slxc?file from the previous build, then build the model again.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 5, R2021a

Summary: Validating?Simulink.SimulationInput with a sim-only parameter set throws an error
Description: After a sim-only parameter, like?TimeOut , is set on a?Simulink.SimulationInput ?using the? setModelParameter  function, then using the? validate  function throws an error similar to the following :? Error using Simulink.Simulation.ModelParameter/validateblock_diagram does not have a parameter named 'TimeOut'Error in Simulink.SimulationInput/validate - Show complete stack trace

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model that contains a referenced model
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:The model uses model referencing. There is a feedback loop where the output is fed to the input.  In the generated code, one or more temporary local variables might be incorrectly eliminated. Incorrect elimination of local variables might occur when a function in the generated code has pointer arguments and the caller of the function passes the same memory address for the pointer arguments.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, clear the?Eliminate superfluous local variables (Expression folding)?(ExpressionFolding) parameter.

Summary: Segmentation fault in generated ROS node with Read Point Cloud Block
Description: A ROS node generated from the Simulink model containing Read Point Cloud block fails at run-time with a segmentation fault error. The segmentation fault is due to the Read Point Cloud block trying to access memory out of bounds when running the node.

Summary: MATLAB might crash during code generation when a model contains a parameter with symbolic dimensions and a custom identifier
Description: MATLAB might crash during code generation when a model contains parameter that has a symbolic dimensions, an imported storage class and a custom identifier.

Summary: JMAAB check for jc_0531?does not comply with its JMAAB 5.1 modeling guideline??
Description: Model Advisor?check?Check default transition placement in Stateflow charts? (id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0531)? does not support sub-id selection configuration as per?JMAAB 5.1 modeling guideline??.

Summary: undefined function error when you click Cancel Build in Build Status window
Description: If the Build Status window is open when parallel building of referenced models is in progress, clicking the Cancel Build  button can produce an?undefined function ?error.

Summary: SLibIfVariantConditionForm16b error for S-Function blocks using?LibAddToCommonIncludes
Description: Simulink model containing S-Function results in the build error Attempt to call a non-function value: SLibIfVariantConditionForm16b ?when the below conditions are TRUE :The S-Function includes a header(example TestHeader. h) using the LibAddToCommonIncludes . This header is also included by another parameter/ dwork/ signal which is variant.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 4, R2021a
