Summary: Datastore construction time has regressed for hierarchical folder structures on Microsoft Azure and Amazon S3.
Description: Datastore construction has regressed for hierarchical folder structure with the files residing on Microsoft Azure blob storage and in Amazon S3 buckets.

Summary: Unable to access results in Polyspace Access repository with Polyspace Desktop application from an older release
Description: Opening results from a Polyspace Access repository with Polyspace Desktop application from an older release might fail. Similarly, uploading results might fail. This issue occurs if you are using the following products together:? Polyspace?desktop application (Bug Finder or Code Prover) R2019b Polyspace Access R2020a or higher  In Polyspace Access, when you click Open in Desktop , the connected Polyspace?desktop application does not open and the following message is displayed:  No result found on the Polyspace Access repository for the run ID <runId>.  In a Polyspace desktop application (Bug Finder or Code Prover): If you select Access > Open Result , the window?Open result from Polyspace Access repository ?appears empty instead of showing all projects in Polyspace Access.? If you select?Access > Upload Result,? ?the window?Upload result to Polyspace Access repository ?is also empty.

Summary: Extraneous AUTOSAR array data types exported when shared base data type has min or max values
Description: Array data types exported from an AUTOSAR model might differ from the corresponding imported array data types if both of the following conditions exist: Imported ARXML descriptions contain AUTOSAR array data types and a ServerCallPoint  argument that reference the same base data type. The shared base data type specifies min or max values.  When you generate code, the exported ARXML contains additional unnecessary array data types.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 4, R2021a

Summary: Continuous acquisition with DataAcquisition object consumes memory until exception or crash
Description: When starting a DataAcquisition  (daq) object in continuous mode in a context other than the MATLAB command prompt (for example, from a function, an app, or from a loop in a script), computer memory usage increases until control is returned to the MATLAB command prompt. When available memory is depleted, MATLAB might throw an exception or crash. For example, the following script uses an NI USB 6351 device ("Dev1" ): dq = daq("ni")addinput(dq,"Dev1", 0:7, "Voltage");start(dq,"continuous");pause(1)ticn = 0;% Memory usage increases for the duration of this loop:while n < 12000    t = toc;    if t >= 2        disp(dq.NumScansAcquired)        scanData = read(dq, "all");        disp(height(scanData))        disp(datetime)        memory        tic;    end    pause(0.01)    drawnow    n = n+1;end% Memory is freed only when script is complete.
Workaround: To avoid this issue with continuous operation, you can use the session-based interface with a callback function:s = daq.createSession("ni");addAnalogInputChannel(s, "Dev1", 0:7, "Voltage");s.IsContinuous = true;aiLH = addlistener(s, "DataAvailable", @ai_callback);startBackground(s)function ai_callback(src, evt)    % Read data (actual code would do something with it)    src.UserData = evt.Data;end

Summary: rtw.asap2SetAddress fails with the DWARF file when used to set the addresses in the ASAP2(a2l) file
Description: When you use the?rtw.asap2SetAddress? function? with the DWARF file to set the addresses in the ASAP2(a2l) file, a failure occurs with the following error message: ?"Index exceeds the number of array elements (0)".

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model containing a Unit Delay block and an Assignment block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:The model contains a Unit Delay block and an Assignment block. The output of the Assignment block feeds into the Unit Delay block. In the Assignment block parameters dialog box, the Initialize output (Y)? parameter is set to Specify size for each dimension in table .? The Initial condition  parameter of the Unit delay block is set to a non-zero value.

Summary: Running Model Advisor checks for an AUTOSAR model with a Stateflow Chart receiving messages from a root-level Inport block might crash MATLAB
Description: Running Model Advisor checks for a model with Stateflow Chart satisfying the following conditions crashes MATLAB: In Configuration Parameters ?>? Code Generation  section, System target file  is?autosar.tlc  or autosar_adaptive.tlc  . Stateflow Chart receives messages from a message input port. The message input port is connected with a root-level Inport block.

Summary: CurrentPoint?value is incorrect?for horizontally scrollable figures
Description: The value of the CurrentPoint ?property scales incorrectly in scrollable figures created using the uifigure  function. The value is correct when scrolled all the way to the left, but is incorrect after scrolling horizontally. For example, this code creates a horizontally scrollable figure that displays the location of the mouse pointer. If you scroll to the right and mouse over the figure, the displayed values are incorrect. >> f = uifigure;  >> pnl = uipanel(f,'Position',[1000 100 100 100]);  >> f.Scrollable = 'on';  >> f.WindowButtonMotionFcn = @(src,event)disp(src.CurrentPoint);

Summary: Error while importing OpenDRIVE file into Driving Scenario Designer app
Description: If an OpenDRIVE file contains negative laneoffset values, and you import this file into the Driving Scenario Designer ?app,the app returns this error:'Index in position 1 exceeds arraybounds (must not exceed 1) '.

Summary: The Stereo Camera Calibrator app errors when attempting to load RGB stereo pair images
Description: The?Stereo ?Camera ?Calibrator ?app returns the following error when attempting to load RGB stereo pair images for calibration. Error  using  vision.internal.calibration.tool.StereoBoardThumbnails/resizeToThumbnailStereo  (line 134 ) Dimensions  of  arrays  being  concatenated  are  not  consistent.  Warning:  Error  occurred  while  evaluating  listener  callback.

Summary: Assertion error when generating code for MATLAB function when variable sized matrix is assigned a scalar value
Description: MATLAB might display an assertion error message when you generate code for a MATLAB function that meets all of the following conditions: The left-hand side of an assignment contains a variable sized matrix The right-hand side of the above assignment evaluates to a scalar value and a full write of the variable sized matrix is performed using this scalar value  Assertion at dom_core/analysis/range/LocalAnalysisImpl.cpp line 2098: In rangeOfElementwiseCall: Assertion failed: matInput != nullptr
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b, R2021b Update 3

Summary: Pasting model components fails unexpectedly in SimBiology Model Builder
Description: If any compartment names of the model are not valid MATLAB identifiers and you paste model components in the diagram, an error dialog appears with the message "Compartment NAME is being used by another SimBiology model component object. Specify a different NAME."
Workaround: Rename compartments so that their names are valid MATLAB identifiers.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Objects?such as?string?that overload the?isequal method?might produce incorrect result in generated code
Description: Suppose that your MATLAB code uses objects that overload the isequal  method, string  being a common example of such an object. When such an object is contained within a struct, a cell array, or another object, the generated code might not call the overloaded isequal   method. The generated code might instead call the default isequal ? method which only compares the properties of each object. In particular, this issue might produce incorrect answers when comparing strings with character arrays and cell arrays of character vectors, especially when comparing empty strings or character arrays.

Summary: The?linprog?'interior-point' algorithm returns an incorrect result
Description: In rare cases when the problem to be solved has an inequality constraint, the?linprog ?'interior-point' ?algorithm can return an incorrect result such as: An (invalid) "solution" to an infeasible problem, or A declaration that a feasible problem is infeasible, or An invalid solution to the specified feasible problem.
Workaround: To check that a?linprog 'interior-point'?result is valid, solve the problem using the?linprog?dual-simplex?algorithm.options = optimoptions('linprog','Algorithm','dual-simplex'); [x,fval,exitflag,output] = linprog(f,A,b,Aeq,beq,lb,ub,options);?

Summary: App Designer apps saved in R2020b and loaded into earlier releases are missing containers that have UIAxes
Description: App Designer apps that are saved in R2020b, and opened with App Designer in R2019b or earlier, will lose any containers (e.g., panels, tabs, grid layouts) that contain UIAxes  objects. This issue affects apps opened in App Designer in R2019b and earlier releases. Running apps works correctly in all supported releases.

Summary: Generation of C++ code from MATLAB Code might take a long time
Description: For some large MATLAB designs, generation of C++  code from MATLAB code might take a long time, where generation of C  code takes significantly less time. If you encounter a very long code generation time, and switching the target language from C++  to C  speeds up code generation time significantly, consider updating to the release that contains the fix for this issue.

Summary: Creating uavPlatform with waypointTrajectory that uses rotation matrix for orientation errors out
Description: When a waypointTrajectory ?object with the?"OrientationMatrix" ?name-value pair set to a rotation? matrix is specified to the uavPlatform ?object , the object returns an error. For example:      scene = uavScenario;    rotm = zeros(3,3,3);     traj = waypointTrajectory("Waypoints", [-5 -5 1; 0 -5 1;5 -5 1], "TimeOfArrival", 0:2,"Orientation",rotm);    plat= uavPlatform("UAV", scene, "Trajectory", traj); Error returned:      Dot indexing is not supported for variables of this type.    Error in uav.internal.scenario.Platform/setupTrajectory (line 268)                    initMotion = [traj.Waypoints(1,:), traj.Velocities(1,:), zeros(1,3), traj.Orientation(1).compact, zeros(1,3)];    Error in uav.internal.scenario.Platform (line 106)                    obj.setupTrajectory(traj, rTraj2body);    Error in uavPlatform (line 235)                    obj.PlatformImpl = uav.internal.scenario.Platform(...
Workaround: To avoid this issue use the waypointTrajectory?function with an orientation specified as a quaternion?object:    scene = uavScenario;    rotm = zeros(3,3,3);     traj = waypointTrajectory("Waypoints", [-5 -5 1; 0 -5 1;5 -5 1], "TimeOfArrival", 0:2,"Orientation",quaternion(rotm,'rotmat','frame'));    plat= uavPlatform("UAV", scene, "Trajectory", traj);

Summary: Run with Stepper?button for test debugging cannot be used at test file or test suite level
Description: The Run with Stepper  button is disabled for debugging tests at the test file and test suite levels. Interactive debugging by stepping through the model is allowed only at the test case level.

Summary: Error dialog box appears when updating requirements imported from Excel
Description: When you update requirements imported from an Excel file, an error dialog might appear when there is a period (. )  in the file name, apart from the file name extension period. The error dialog box says customId ... does not correspond to a valid item .

Summary: High-Integrity check?Inport interface definition?incorrectly analyzes root model when run on subsystem
Description: The Model Advisor check??Inport interface definition  (ID:?mathworks.hism.hisl_0024)? incorrectly analyzes root model and displays warning found on root model when run on subsystem.

Summary: Empty simulation output with failed equivalence test case
Description: If the alignment of signals fails during equivalence criteria evaluation of an equivalence test case, then the simulation output of the first simulation is empty in the test case result.

Summary: Switching error in dropdown menu of Power Accounting Bus Creator block
Description: In a non-English locale, if you open the block dialog box of the Power Accounting Bus Creator block?and switch the value of the Associated Block  parameter from Parent  to Parent reference block , you get ?a warning message in the command window. The message says that the popup menu has an invalid setting.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL Coder sharing semantics might be incompatible with conditional subsystems
Description: If all ?these conditions are true for a subsystem in your model, sharing that subsystem might cause generation of incorrect HDL code:? The subsystem contains control ports (Enable , Reset , or Trigger ) The subsystem contains state elements such as delay registers  In such situations, the generated validation model might show a simulation mismatch between the generated model and the original model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: QoS settings for ROS 2 in Simulink get reset in code generation
Description: The Quality of Service (QoS) settings for ROS 2 Publish block and Subscribe block in Simulink are reset to default values in generated code.

Summary: Simulink crashes when same identifier is used for root-level Inport or Outport of a subsystem or referenced model that use Auto storage class
Description: Simulink crashes when these conditions are met:?Root-level Inport or Outport of a subsystem or referenced model use Auto  storage class. Same identifier is used for ports or signals inside the subsystem or referenced model.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020b Update 4, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unit conversions on PS-S converters are not captured in the implementation model when using Simscape to HDL Workflow Advisor
Description: When using the Simscape to HDL Workflow advisor if the the PS-S converters have the Output signal unit ?Property set to a value other than '1'  or 'inherit'  the unit conversion may not be captured in the implementation model. Instead the outputs will be the native unit for the Simscape network.  This can lead to the output of the implementation model to be different than expected.?
Workaround: Change the setting on all PS-S Converters in the model to '1'.To keep the correct units add the appropriate simulink blocks on the output of the PS-S converter to perform the desired unit conversion.?

Summary: Drive Cycle Source block Calculating Error Bounds wait bar appears multiple times
Description: When you select Enable fault tracking  in the Drive Cycle Source mask, you will encounter multiple wait bars displaying the message Calculating Error Bounds.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when trying to access an empty sparse matrix returned from an out of process MEX function or an out of process C++ Engine application
Description: If an empty sparse matrix is either sent to MATLAB or returned from MATLAB using an out of process MEX function or an out of process C++ Engine application, MATLAB crashes.

Summary: centrality(G,'betweenness') gives wrong result if G?has?many paths connecting a pair of nodes
Description: The function call?centrality(G,'betweenness') , where?G  is a graph  or digraph  object, can give a wrong result when at least one pair of nodes in G are connected by more than 1.8e19  distinct paths and each of those paths is a shortest path between the nodes.

Summary: Error extracting an operating point from a model with 6-DOF joint
Description: Attempting to extract an operating point from a model with a 6-DOF joint generates the following error: 6-DOF Joint block (mask) does not have a parameter named 'VelocityTargetInFollowerFrame'
Workaround: If the model can be simulated at the start time, the following steps can be used to create the OperatingPoint using the "Start" values.1) Enable Simscape logging.2) Simulate the model with the stop time set to the same value as the start time.3) Extract the OperatingPoint from the simlog, e.g.>> op = simscape.op.create(simlog, startTime);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: C Caller, C Function, MATLAB System, and Dataflow Subsystem blocks may generate wrong simulation results with bus type data
Description: The following blocks may yield wrong simulation results when using bus type data of any scope: C Caller C Function MATLAB System (when simulating using code generation) Dataflow Subsystem
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 3, R2021a

Summary: Error?Unrecognized function or variable 'ExampleHelperTurtleBotVisualizer'?occurs when trying to run "Control the Turtlebot with Teleoperation" example
Description: When running the "Control the Turtlebot with Teleoperation" example, the following error is thrown: ? ? ?Unrecognized function or variable 'ExampleHelperTurtleBotVisualizer'.  This occurs because example files are not available if you do not have Robotics System Toolbox installed.?

Summary: Polyspace Access server crashes when User Manager imports users with empty ID attributes
Description: You can customize the LDAP attribute that the Polyspace Access?User Manager  service uses as a user ID by modifying the LDAP ID attribute ?setting in the Admin settings. If any of the user entries that the LDAP server returns does not contain the attribute you specify in the?LDAP ID attribute  setting, for instance if you specify attribute foo  but no entries have attribute foo , the Polyspace Access server might crash. This issue is not present in the R2020a release.
Workaround: Add a clause to the?LDAP search filter?setting to filter out entries with a missing ID attribute.For example, if you set the LDAP ID attribute to samAccountName, and your LDAP search filter?is set to (objectCategory=person), update the search filter like this: (&(objectCategory=person)(samAccountName=*)) The updated filter selects only user entries that contain a non-empty?samAccountName attribute.

Summary: Scenario Reader block produces code generationerror during simulation when reading scenario from workspace variable
Description: Whenthe Scenario Reader block is configured to read a driving scenario from aworkspace variable and to generate code, during the second simulation run, the blockthrows this error: ?'Nontunableproperty 'pIsScenarioSourceWorkspace' may only be assigned once.'

Summary: MATLAB crash during HDL code generation when parametrized code generation is enabled for masked Triggered subsystem
Description: HDL code generation crashes when mask parameters are used inside a triggered subsystem and HDL configuration setting Generate Parameterized HDL code from masked subsystem ? under HDL Code Generation > Global Settings > Coding style > RTL Style ? is enabled.
Workaround: consider either disabling?Generate Parameterized HDL code from masked subsystem?or consider removing masks can help.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated VHDL code fails to simulate with generated test bench code in HDL simulator tools
Description: When the Target Language ?is set to?VHDL? in Configuration Parameters > HDL Code Generation pane, and the HDL Code Generation > Global Settings > General > Instance Postfix ? is set to a value that ends with an underscore, the generated test bench code has invalid syntax and fails to simulate the code in HDL simulator tools.

Summary: Setting the optimization option of ports of s-function blocks as SS_NOT_REUSABLE_AND_LOCAL does not prevent the code generator from optimizing the corresponding buffers away
Description: The generated code might not contain local buffers for the input andoutput ports of an S-Function block even though the ssSetInputPortOptimOpts  andssSetOutputPortOptimOpts  SimStruct functions contain a value of SS_NOT_REUSABLE_AND_LOCAL .

Summary: Live Editor Tasks become blank after clear all?command
Description: After running the command clear all ? ?in MATLAB, all Live Editor Tasks in open live scripts become blank.?

Summary: System Composer systemcomposer.arch.ComponentPort connectors and isConnected properties return incorrect results
Description: System Composer systemcomposer.arch.ComponentPort  connectors and isConnected  properties return incorrect results. The "Connectors" property may return connectors that are not valid for the given port. The "isConnected" property may return that the port is connected when the port is not connected.

Summary: Simulink Compiler fails to compile models and incorrectly reports usage of products
Description: When compiling a model with Simulink Compiler, compilation might fail with an error reporting that some products which are not currently used are not supported by Simulink Compiler. For example: Error while determining required deployable files. Compilation terminated. Details:  Error using matlab.depfun.internal.SimulinkModelInspector  The following list of products used in your model are currently not supported for deployment through Simulink Compiler:  RF Blockset

Summary: During import for some OpenDRIVE scenes, the computed corners would appear incorrect and offset.
Description: Some imported corners from OpenDRIVE were created with incorrect heights at their ends. This was due to a lack of constraints applied on the newly created corners.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect answers in rapid accelerator mode when Stateflow charts call multi-instance model blocks in export-function mode
Description: Incorrect results are possible in rapid simulation mode fora model that meets the following conditions: The model contains two or more identical Stateflow charts. The Stateflow charts call multi-instance Model blocks.  The multi-instance Model blocks are in export-function mode.  For the above scenario, incorrect answers might occur at the model block outputs.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Test generation shows objectives status as?Undecided with Testcases for certain models with Block Reduction off?
Description: Certain Simulink models can be optimized further with?Simulation Target->Block Reduction ?parameter is off. For these models, SLDV test generation shows objective status as?Undecided with Testcases.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Fixed-Point Tool does not honor the data type override settings defined on the model if the option?Collect Ranges >?Use current settings?is used for range collection
Description: When using the Fixed-Point Tool in the Iterative Fixed-Point Conversion  and Range Collection  workflows, if the option Collect Ranges >?Use current settings ?is used for range collection, the tool turns off the data type override settings set by the user on the model.

Summary: System Composer Architecture Views Gallery shows incorrect filter for a view
Description: System Composer Architecture Views Gallery shows the filter for a view incorrectly after closing and reopening the model when the filter specified includes a custom clause in the filter builder.

Summary: Incorrect error message regarding Simulink code generation folder thrown during IP core generation
Description: When running IP core generation from a Simulink model, the following error may be thrown: Simulink code generation folder in the current folder was created for a different release. ?The 'slprj' subfolder is not compatible with the current release. ?Manually remove the 'slprj' folder and generated code files that the folder contains.   In certain cases, the same error continues to be thrown even after removing the Simulink code generation folder, "slprj". In those cases, the underlying issue is masked by this error message.
Workaround: To determine the underlying issue that is blocking IP core generation run the following command in MATLAB:>>?report =?checkhdl('DUT')where "DUT" is the path of the Simulink model, model reference, or subsystem for which you are generating code.The underlying issue is captured in the output argument "report". For more information on checkhdl, refer to the following page:https://www.mathworks.com/help/hdlcoder/ref/checkhdl.html

Summary: Specifying? Number of iterations to a value other than Inherited (-1) leads to assertion
Description: Specifying?ˇ®Number of iterationsˇŻ other than ˇ®-1ˇŻ is unsupported and leads to an assertion in HDLCoder.? ? ??Error using hdlcoder.pirctx/doForEachSubsystemTransformation Assertion failed: pir_transforms/ForEachSubsystem.cpp:748:(numElementsToPartition + partitionData.offset) % (partitionData.width + partitionData.offset) == 0  Starting R2021a release the following validation error is generated ? ?The 'Number of iterations' dialog parameter on the For Each block <path to the forEach token> must be set to '-1' to continue with HDL code generation.  when setting the Numer of Iterations parameter to inherited HDL Coder automatically computes the iteration length using this method? ? ? NumIterations = (partitionDimensionSize + partitionOffset) / (partitionWidth + partitionOffset)   ??
Workaround: Set? Number of iterations to Inherited (-1).

Summary: Abnormal analysis termination when MISRA-C++ or AUTOSAR-C++14 coding rules are activated
Description: When any of the following coding rules is activated, the analysis might terminate abnormally if the analyzed source contains a specialization of the template std::hash . MISRA-C++ 14-7-3 AUTOSAR-C++14 A14-7-2 AUTOSAR-C++14 A18-1-6

Summary: Rectangular images result in complex-valued shearlet coefficients for real transforms and NaNs in reconstruction
Description: Shearlet systems with images where the aspect ratio deviates significantly from 1:1 exhibit numerical instabilities.   The aspect ratio is the ratio of the image's? width to height. For example, an image with a width of 1200 and height of 600 has an aspect ratio of 2:1. You can create a shearlet system for this image size using code similar to this: shs = shearletSystem('ImageSize',[600 1200]);?  In the above shearlet system, the value of the?TransformType ?property? is 'real' , but because the image's aspect ratio deviates significantly?from 1:1, you might still obtain significantly large imaginary parts in the shearlet coefficients. Also, the inverse shearlet transform? might result in  NaN s?i n the reconstructed image.
Workaround: Pad the image to an aspect ratio closer to 1:1 as detailed in this example:https://www.mathworks.com/help/releases/R2020b/wavelet/ug/boundary-effects-in-real-valued-bandlimited-shearlet-systems.htmlYou can remove the padding after reconstruction or ignore the padded coefficients during analysis.?

Summary: A bug in 64-bit Windows 10 Version 2004 system math library can cause MathWorks products to return incorrect numerical?results.
Description: There is a bug in one Microsoft library included in the Windows 10 Version 2004. Some calculations performed using this library can cause later calculations to sporadically return NaN. For more background on this bug, see?this page on Microsoft's community developer forum .
Workaround: Unzip and run the attached MEX file ("ucrt_2004_fmod_patch.mexw64"). This MEX file modifies the system library in memory which should avoid the bug. You will need to run this file in each MATLAB session. You can automate this process by adding a call to this MEX-file in your?startup.m?file; see https://www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/ref/startup.html.This MEX file looks for the signature of the fmod error and if it does not recognize that signature, it prints this text (Actual Bytes might be different):INFO: ucrt_2004_fmod_patch was not applied.Expected signature of DD D8 DD D9 not recognized.Actual bytes:DD C0 DD C1?If you see this, it means you may have an older or newer version of the UCRT unaffected by this issue.

Summary: Warning Index exceeds the number of array elements (0) when you save a model that uses signal logging
Description: Simulink might throw the following warning to the Command Window when you save a model that uses signal logging: Index exceeds the number of array elements (0).
Workaround: Follow these steps:Save a backup copy of the model.Select all blocks. (Ctrl+A)Cut all blocks from the model. (Ctrl+X)Save the empty model. (Ctrl+S)Paste the blocks back into the model. (Ctrl+V)Save the model again. (Ctrl+S)

Summary: Custom bitstream generation could fail for certain values of the ConvThreadNumber property
Description: When you generate a custom bitstream by using a custom deep learning processor configuration with the ConvThreadNumber set to 9, 25, or 49 the following error message? is generated : Cannot connect to model 'testbench'; please try Update Diagram (Ctrl-D).?
Workaround: There is no workaround. To avoid the error, use other ConvThreadNumber property configurations when generating bitstream. (e.g: ConvThreadNumber value of 4,16, or 64)

Summary: Incorrect ARXML data constraints exported for an AUTOSAR model containing multiple lookup tables
Description: Incorrect ARXML data constraints might be exported for an AUTOSAR model when all of the following conditions are true: The model contains multiple Simulink lookup tables. The lookup tables use the same field names for some of their table values or breakpoints. Table values or breakpoints that have the same field name specify different Simulink data constraints. For example, the table values or breakpoints use different min and max values or different data types.  In the exported ARXML for the model, the data constraints for table values and breakpoints that use the same field name are not correctly differentiated.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a

Summary: SoC Builder errors out during validation step with message "Cannot resolve the name??newfpgaparams.fdesObj.CustomDesignTclHooks.ProcessingSystem"
Description: SoC Builder errors out during validation step when user followed the below steps. Register a custom board with MPSoC device family. Select the registered board in any SoC Blockset example or model compatible with the SoC Builder workflow. In Simulink toolstrip, click on Configure, Build & Deploy  button. Follow the SoC Builder workflow until the validation screen. Click Validate . The following error is thrown in the SoC Builder screen: "Cannot resolve the name??newfpgaparams.fdesObj.CustomDesignTclHooks.ProcessingSystem"

Summary: When the first input is a vector, the repelem function requires repetition count values to be 'double'?type
Description: When generating code for the repelem  function where the first input is a vector, the repetition count values must be 'double'  type. All other built-in numeric types used as repetition count values cause the code generator to issue an error. For example? ...?  u = repelem(v,n);?  ...  Here v  is the input vector; n  is the repetition count variable; u  is the resultant vector or matrix.? 'n'  must only contain?double  type values.?

Summary: In some locales, the Deep Learning HDL Toolbox errors on compile
Description: In some locales, when you try to compile with Deep Learning HDL Toolbox, you see an error message of the form: Error using dnnfpga. tool. Text/ concatToLine The text only has 23 lines.  Error in dnnfpga. apis. Workflow/ displayCompileStartBanner  Error in dnnfpga. apis. Workflow/ compile   The issue can occur with European and Japanese locales.

Summary: Using runtests with GenerateBaselines option for a MATLAB-based Simulink test without a Simulink Test license errors
Description: If you do not have a Simulink Test license and use?runtests(<test>,' GenerateBaselines',true) ?for a MATLAB-based Simulink test, the baselines are not generated or updated. The following error occurs: Error using baselines.plugins.BaselineGenerationPlugin  The specified superclass 'matlabtest.internal.plugins.ExcludedFromParallelSupportValidationMixin' contains a parse error, cannot be found on MATLAB's search path, or is shadowed by another file with the same name.  Also, if you do not have a Simulink Test license, but can access Simulink Data Inspector, you can generate baselines, but this feature should only be available with a Simulink Test license.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Data might be incorrectly mapped when using atomic subcharts and either nested buses or an array of buses
Description: Data mapped to an atomic subchart in Stateflow might be incorrectly read, written, or assigned if the data comes from a bus with a nested bus or an array of buses.
Workaround: Create a local variable in the parent chart for the bus data that you want to mapMap the local variable to the atomic subchart variableWrite to the local variable as needed so its value always matches the bus data

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect number of bytes used for array of structures memcpy
Description: To implement a copy of an array of structures, MathWorks C/C++ code generators use a memcpy  call if the number of bytes to copy exceeds the Memcpy threshold (bytes)  model configuration parameter. Under rare circumstances the code generator might produce a memcpy  call for the array of structures with the incorrect number of bytes to copy. When this issue occurs the incorrect memcpy  corresponds to an array of structures copy with the following properties: Individual elements of the array are assigned as a sequence of statements A subset of array element copies are direct structure copies A subset of array element copies are composed of copies of the structure's fields  The following is a sample pattern where the code generator produces an incorrect memcpy . Note that element 0 and 1 of the array are assigned using structure's fields while elements 2 and 3 are direct copies: Str1[0].f1 = Str2[0].f1;Str1[0].f2 = Str2[0].f2;Str1[1].f1 = Str2[1].f1;Str1[1].f2 = Str2[1].f2;Str1[2]?= Str2[2];Str1[3]?= Str2[3]; When this issue occurs the code generator produces the memcpy : ? ? ?memcpy(Str1[0] , Str2[0], 2 * sizeof(struct));   Which only copies 2 structure elements, whereas the original code was copying 4 struct elements.  To check for an array of structures assignment as described above, the steps in the workaround section can be applied to disable memcpy  generation.? This issue affects the following workflows:? Simulink model to C/C++ code generation using Simulink Coder MATLAB to C/C++ code generation using MATLAB Coder
Workaround: Set?Use memcpy for vector assignment?to?Off, or, set the?Memcpy threshold (bytes)?parameter to a very high value such as?inf. Note that both of these approaches prevent the code generator from using memcpy for any array copy.

Summary: Creating a software-in-the-loop (SIL) harness for a subsystem block might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: When you create a software-in-the-loop (SIL) test harness for a subsystem block, and the?GenerateMakefile ?model configuration parameter is set to?off , MATLAB might crash.

Summary: The?edfread?function and?edfinfo?object throw hard errors when an EDF file has the number of data records as -1?and have unsorted annotations
Description: The EDF/EDF+ file readers have following issues:? 1.? When an EDF file has the number of data records equal to?-1 , the edfread  function and edfinfo  object throw this error:?  Error using signal.internal.edf.validateEDF (line 44)    File size in bytes is 848. File size must be 688 as specified in "EDFFile41.edf" EDF/EDF+ file header.    ? ? The byte values and file name may vary between error messages. 2. The annotations from the edfread  function and edfinfo  object are always unsorted.

Summary: Adapter ports are reset to default positions when updating interface element mapping
Description: When using the Adapter block in System Composer models, and positioning it at a non-default position between components, updating the interface element mapping resets the Adapter port positions to the default. This results in sub-optimal line routing. The user can take the following steps:? Add an Adapter block between components. Connect the Adapter block to each component. Double-click the Adapter block to open the Interface Adapter. Set line routing information. Add interfaces that do not match to each component port connected to the Adapter block. Double-click the Adapter block and open the Interface Adapter. Map the corresponding interface elements son the two interfaces o they can communicate. Save the? configuration. Notice the positioning of the Adapter block has changed between the components preventing optimal line routing.

Summary: Error: Unable to create a service client for a service gazebo_msgs/GetModelState when trying to run "Apply Forces and Torques in Gazebo" example
Description: The "Apply Forces and Torques in Gazebo" example does not work with the latest virtual machine image provided in prerequisite examples. This example is failing as it is unable to create a service client for a particular service of type?gazebo_msgs/GetModelState.

Summary: Code generation for an S-Function block can fail if the current directory contains non-ASCII characters,
Description: Code generation for an S-Function block can fail if the current directory contains non-ASCII characters. More specifically, you might get the following error:?Block "<block path>"' is a non-inlined S-function, ?which is not supported with the current configuration. ?Consider selecting Support non-inlined S-functions.

Summary: Crash while compiling a model with missing referenced subsystem file
Description: Simulink may crash during model compile if the model is referencing a subsystem file which is missing or not there on the MATLAB path with below stack-trace:-? [? 5] 0x00007f2274f41901 sltopo::connectPorts(slPort*, slPort*, sltopo::XformableTopo&) [? 6] 0x00007f2274f41a01 slsr::SlTopoConnectionRestorer::~SlTopoConnectionRestorer() [? 7] 0x00007f2274f3f60d std::default_delete<slsr::SlTopoConnectionRestorer>::operator()(slsr::SlTopoConnectionRestorer*) const [? 8] 0x00007f2274f46087 (anonymous namespace)::updateSSRefBlock(SubsystemBlock*, bool)?

Summary: Calling setVariable on Simulink.SimulationInput with a variable unsupported by data dictionary errors out
Description: For a model using a data dictionary, trying to set a variable that is not supported by the dictionary using setVariable ?on Simulink.SimulationInput , and then calling applyToModel  produces an error. The error is produced even if the model has access to base workspace.Error using assigninGlobalScope  Values of class '<class_name>' are not accepted in the 'Design_Data' section of the dictionary .

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier may terminate early in cases where the dead logic and at least one other design error detection parameter is enabled
Description: This is a bug where analysis may terminate early (with about 40% of the time left) in the following scenarios: 1) The analysis mode is Design Error Detection 2) Dead Logic (partial)? with Run exhaustive analysis ?and at least one other design error detection parameter is enabled 3) All of the Dead Logic objectives have been decided ?In this case, the non-dead logic design error checks may not be fully analyzed.
Workaround: Increase the value of the Maximum analysis time parameter. Although analysis may continue to erroneously terminate early, this will allow you to scale up the analysis time for the non-dead logic design error checks.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Enable local block outputs configuration generates incorrect code for a model with Probe block.
Description: Incorrect code is generated when all these conditions are met:A Probe block feeds into a Concatenateblock and both blocks are inside a reusable subsystem.? The Probe block causes awrite to one element in the subsystem's initialize function and write toanother element in the subsystem's step function. The model configuration parameter Enablelocal block outputs  is selected.

Summary: Unexpected error encountered when using multiple frame input in predict function of dlhdl.Workflow object
Description: When providing more than 1 frame input to the predict() function of dlhdl.Workflow workflow, the predict() function errors out. For example: >> hW = dlhdl.Workflow('Network',net,'Bitstream','zcu102_single.bit','Target',hTarget); >> hW.predict(img) when img contain 15 frame of input data  Error message:? Error using dnnfpga.apis.Workflow/predictOnNetwork  Input frame number 15 exceeds the limit. To fix the problem, provide an input frame number that is less than or equal to 1. Or you can re-compile the network with a larger InputFrameNumberLimit property value in the input argument, for example hW.compile('InputFrameNumberLimit', 50).?

Summary: When rostopic list is executed, MATLAB returns an error: "Could not retrieve the system state of the ROS master"
Description: The ROS network introspection API,?rostopic list ,?fails when the user creates multiple publishers and subscribers and then deletes them.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when opening a read-only data dictionary that had been closed with unsaved changes
Description: MATLAB might crash when opening a data dictionary file from Model Explorer under the following conditions:The data dictionary file is read only. You previously made changes to the data dictionary and attempted to save and close the dictionary by using the UI.  Simulink is unable to save the changes to the read-only file, but no error is reported. When you attempt to opening the dictionary file again,?a crash might result.

Summary: Simulation Deployment does not timeout after the specified time
Description: Even when the timeout parameter is specified for a simulation in deployed mode, the simulation continues to execute after the period specified by the timeout parameter.

Summary: Test case for Model Advisor check "Check usage of Assignment blocks" is incomplete
Description: Model Advisor check Check usage of Assignment blocks  (mathworks.misra.AssignmentBlocks ) analyzes the content of library blocks and masked subsystems. The qualification tests for the check do not?verify the content of linked library blocks.

Summary: Complexity of S-Function Builder port might be set incorrectly
Description: In the S-Function Builder block, the complexity of a port is set incorrectly if you perform these actions in the S-Function Builder editor: Change the data type of the port Change the complexity to complex  Change the datatype again?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: If Action Subsystem block with function packaging using absolute time may produce incorrect results in generated code
Description: An If Action Subsystem block may produce incorrect results in generated code if all the following conditions are true:? * The If Action Subsystem block is placed inside a triggered subsystem * The If Action Subsystem block contains an Integrator block * The Sample time ?parameter of the If Action Subsystem block is set to a value other than -1  or Inf ? * The Function packaging  parameter of the If Action Subsystem block is set to?Nonreusable function  * The model configuration parameter Underspecified Initialization mode  is set to Simplified

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for?a model that contains a?Data Store Write block inside a conditional subsystem
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:The model contains a conditional subsystem. There is a Data Store Write block inside the conditional subsystem. The input signal of the conditional subsystem connects to the Data Store Write block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might be incorrect when Constant block output is used as the input to an exported Stateflow chart function
Description: The generated code might be incorrect when a Constant block output is used as an input to an exported Stateflow chart function.? As a consequence, the behavior of the code may not match the behavior of the simulation.
Workaround: You may configure the Constant block output signal as a test point.? Alternatively, you may assign a non-Auto storage class to that Constant block output signal.

Summary: plot(simscape.logging.Series) displays incorrect data in the plot if the series values are nonscalar
Description: If you use the?plot ?object? function to plot a?simscape.logging.Series ?object that contains nonscalar values, the plot displays incorrect data.
Workaround: If the simscape.logging.Series object contains nonscalar values, you can plot the data using plot(simscape.logging.Series.time, simscape.logging.Series.values).

Summary: Running MATLAB-based Simulink tests may cause warning about "SimulationCompleted" callback error
Description: After you execute a MATLAB-based Simulink test, the warning 'The 'SimulationCompleted' callback did throw an error'  appears in the command window. The warning appears under these conditions:You executed the test in Simulink Test Manager The Model Testing Dashboard has been opened at least once in the project

Summary: Unable to edit lanes of maneuver roads
Description: Lanes that existed on maneuver roads are unable to be edited using the standard lane tools (Lane Tool, Lane Width Tool, etc). This issue occurs because the expandable road view for the maneuver road is not initialized, causing none of the editable lane views to be created.

Summary: Polyspace analysis of C++ code stops with error: function returning function is not allowed
Description: In some cases, Polyspace analysis of C++ code might stop with the following error: error: function returning function is not allowed  The error might happen when analyzing header files that come from the GCC 8?compiler.

Summary: Exporting model containing Model block as 32-bit FMU causes configuration mismatch error
Description: Using a model that contains a Model block to generate a standalone FMU with a 32-bit library causes the following error if the parent model and subsystem model reference contains mismatched configuration sets in Code Generation  and Hardware Implementation : The following Configuration parameter option(s) do not match between the models 'model (Parent)' and 'Subsystem (Child)'.  The issue is exclusive to generating a standalone FMU with a 32-bit library on Microsoft Windows. Generating a tool-coupling FMU does not cause this issue.

Summary: Simulink Real-Time models that contain Simulink Function blocks fail to compile
Description: When a Simulink Real-Time model contains one or more Simulink Function blocks, building the model fails during compilation.  The compile errors are available in the?ModelInfo.hpp ?file.

Summary: Unexpected model rebuild in code generation and simulation for referenced model
Description: Unexpected model rebuild might occur?when all of the following conditions are satisfied:You simulate or generate code for a top model with at least one referenced model on the current computer after you have simulated or generated code for the referenced models on a different computer. You copy simulation or code generation artifacts to the current computer, so that they can be reused in a top model simulation or in code generation. The computer that you use for simulating or generating code for the referenced models has different datetime or locale settings from the current computer.

Summary: MATLAB started with the -nodesktop option hangs when the terminal window is resized
Description: Resizing the terminal window causes MATLAB to hang if it was started without the desktop (using -nodesktop  or -nojvm ). This issue occurs only on Mac OS X and Linux.

Summary: Simulation errors out on complex signals with custom-defined type
Description: If you configure a complex-valued signal to use a user-defined custom data type, Simulink throws an error when you try to update or run the model.

Summary: Parallel Bayesian optimization experiments highlight incorrect trial as optimal
Description: When you run a Bayesian optimization experiment in parallel, the incorrect trial might be highlighted as the best trial.
Workaround: Sort the results table to manually compare your results.

Summary: Incorrect size of a printed Simulink model when using a print frame
Description: If print frames are used when printing a Simulink model, the frame and model content does not center and fill the printed area on the printed pages or in the exported PDF file.

Summary: For certain denoising methods, the wdenoise2 function errors when 'NoiseEstimate' value is a partial string and returns coefficients as a vector instead of a matrix
Description: ?The wdenoise2  function errors out when 'NoiseEstimate'  is set to a partial string and 'DenoisingMethod'  is set to 'Minimax' , 'SURE' , or 'UniversalThreshold' . For example, execute these commands:>> x = randn(681,1034);>> imden = wdenoise2(x,2,'DenoisingMethod','SU','NoiseEstimate','LevelDep'); The commands result in these error messages:Unable to perform assignment because the size of the left side is 1-by-1 and the size of the right side is 2-by-1.Error in wavelet.internal.DonohoJohnstone2 (line 57)? ? sigmahat(:,nt) = tempSigmahat;Error in wdenoise2 (line 211)? ? [imden,Cden,C,S] = wavelet.internal.DonohoJohnstone2(temp_im,level,...  The wdenoise2  function returns the original and denoised coefficients as vectors instead of matrices when?'CycleSpinning'  is set to a positive integer and?'DenoisingMethod'  is set to 'Minimax' , 'SURE' , or 'UniversalThreshold' .?F or example, execute these commands:>> load flower>> [imden,denoisedcfs,origcfs] = wdenoise2(flower.Noisy,'DenoisingMethod','Uni','CycleSpinning',1); The wdenoise2  function returns the coefficients denoisedcfs  and origcfs  as row vectors.
Workaround: To avoid generating the error, set the value of 'NoiseEstimate' to a full string. For example:>> load flower>> imden = wdenoise2(flower.Noisy,'DenoisingM','SURE','NoiseEstim','LevelDependent');To obtain the coefficients in matrix form, use the value of 'CycleSpinning' to reshape the coefficients vector and then take the transpose. For example:>> cyc = 2;>> load flower>> [imden,dencfs,origcfs] = wdenoise2(flower.Noisy,'DenoisingM','SURE','CycleSpin',cyc);The denoised and original coefficients, dencfs and origcfs, respectively, are row vectors. Execute these commands to reshape and transpose the coefficients:>> dencfs = reshape(dencfs,size(dencfs,2)/(cyc+1)^2,(cyc+1)^2);>> dencfs = dencfs';>> origcfs = reshape(origcfs,size(origcfs,2)/(cyc+1)^2,(cyc+1)^2);>> origcfs = origcfs';The matrices dencfs and origcfs have the correct dimensions.

Summary: serialport write operations might cause MATLAB to hang
Description: When calling a?write operation on a?serialport ?object from within the?BytesAvailableFcn  callback function, MATLAB hangs indefinitely. The write operations for the?serialport ?interface are?write ,?writeline ,?writebinblock , ?and?writeread . (The writebinblock  and writeread  functions require an Instrument Control Toolbox license.)

Summary: MATLAB crashes when a Simulink component outputs messages carrying bus data to a Queue with function connector
Description: When a Simulink model contains a component that outputs messages carrying bus data, MATLAB crashes if the following conditions are met: The messages carrying bus data are sent to a Queue block calling a Simulink function. The function connectors are turned on. The  Bus Creator  block is configured to output virtual bus.
Workaround: Turn off function connectors or in the component, revert the Bus Creator blockˇŻs configuration to output nonvirtual bus by selecting the Output as nonvirtual bus checkbox.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when you import nonnumeric data into the Simulation Data Inspector from an MDF file
Description: MATLAB crashes when you import data into the Simulation Data Inspector from an MDF file if the file contains data with a data type other than integer or float.?

Summary: Simulink stops responding when editing name of child signal in array of buses in Signal Editor
Description: Simulink stops responding when you edit and then try to save the name of a child signal in a nested array of buses in Signal Editor.?

Summary: HDL Code generated for a For Each Subsystem block that contains a black box subsystem might be incorrect
Description: If a For Each Subsystem block contains a Subsystem block that has the black box architecture, the generated code might incorrectly contain an unrolled representation of each iteration of the For Each Subsystem block in addition to a for-generate loop.
Workaround: In the HDL block properties of the For Each Subsystem block, set FlattenHierarchy to 'on'. This will prevent unnecessary instantiations of the For Each Subsystem block logic in the generated code.

Summary: Referencing copied dictionaries can lead to missing content in the Interface Editor
Description: Interface data dictionaries in System Composer are stored in SLDD files. SLDD files that are copied from other SLDD files and then referenced by the original SLDD file result in missing content in the Interface Editor. This situation can occur from these steps: Create dictionary Top.sldd  and add interfaces and interface elements to that dictionary. Copy Top.sldd  to Bottom.sldd . Link Top.sldd  to Bottom.sldd  through the Model Explorer.  Now all content in Bottom.sldd  does not appear in the Interface Editor.

Summary: Flattening a Black Box subsystem generates incorrect HDL code
Description: HDL Coder incorrectly generates code when 'Flatten-Hierarchy' ?option is set on Black-Box subsystem that is configured to generate generics.
Workaround: Consider disabling Flatten Hierarchy on black-box subsystem.

Summary: Non-ASCII strings in Simulink Coverage reports may be corrupted
Description: Non-ASCII strings in Simulink Coverage reports may display incorrectly when viewed in a browser.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for?a model containing a triggered Model Reference block and a Data Store Write block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:The model contains a triggered Model Reference block. The output of the triggered Model Reference block connects to a Data Store Write block. The initial condition of the output is not the same as the initial condition of the Data Store Write block.

Summary: Code generation compilation error when referenced model has S-Function block calling TLC function LibMdlStartCustomCode() for custom code
Description: Generated code might produce compilation errors when all of the following conditions are met: Model reference hierarchy is configured for code generation. The referenced model has the configuration parameter?Underspecified initialization detection  set to Simplified . The referenced model contains an S-Function block which uses .tlc  file for code generation. The S-Function block implements TLC function Start()  that?calls LibMdlStartCustomCode()  to insert custom code to the generated?Start() ?function.

Summary: getpdb fails to retrieve data files from PDB with old URLs
Description: RCSB PDB modified the download URLs for entries in the Protein Data Bank (PDB) in November 2020. Consequently, getpdb  fails when retrieving data files. For example, this line of code generates an error: pdbinfo = getpdb('4hhb')
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the PDB file from the updated URL using websave, and read the PDB information using pdbread. More details on the updated URLs can be found at http://www.rcsb.org/pages/download/http.websave('4hhb.pdb', 'https://files.rcsb.org/download/4hhb.pdb');pdbinfo = pdbread('4hhb.pdb')

Summary: Simulation does not stop at breakpoints in MATLAB code files called from MATLAB Function blocks or Stateflow blocks in Simulink model
Description: If your model contains more than one Stateflow or MATLAB Function block, and those blocks call more than one MATLAB script, function, or class file, Simulink might not pause simulation at breakpoints within those files.?

Summary: In Japanese environment, the build log of S-Function Builder might not refresh correctly.
Description: After building an S-Function, the content of the build log of the S-Function Builder block might not update correctly when you set?Desktop language ?to Japanese.

Summary: Highlighted blocks might save their highlighting as foreground and background colors
Description: If a model is saved while blocks are highlighted, the highlighting might be saved as foreground and background colors for the blocks. For example, you might see this behavior when highlighting blocks with the?hilite_system ?function, Execution Order ?overlay, or Timing Legend.
Workaround: Clear the highlighting on all the blocks before saving a model by performing one of these actions:Right-click the canvas of the Simulink model and select?Remove Highlighting. Alternatively, press?Ctrl+Shift+H.In the MATLAB Command Window, enter this command, replacing modelName?with the name of your model:hilite_system(find_system(modelName,'FindAll','on','type','block'),'none');

Summary: Reselecting a GPU device in a thread-based parallel pool can produce a CUDA_ERROR_INVALID_CONTEXT?error
Description: Reselecting a GPU device in a thread-based parallel pool can sometimes result in the following CUDA_ERROR_INVALID_CONTEXT error: Error using gpuDevice (line 26)An unexpected error occurred during CUDA execution.  ?The CUDA error was: CUDA_ERROR_INVALID_CONTEXT   For example, running the following code might produce the error. parpool('threads');gpuDevice([]);fetchOutputs(parfevalOnAll(@gpuDevice, 0, 1));

Summary: MATLAB crashes when opening a System Composer model with Views that references missing models
Description: MATLAB might crash when loading or opening a System Composer architecture model that has Architecture Views created, when a View includes components from referenced models that are missing or not found on the MATLAB path.

Summary: ncread function performs slowly when reading NetCDF-4 files
Description: ncread  and other MATLAB NetCDF functions might exhibit slow performance with NetCDF-4 files due to a memory leak in the NetCDF library.

Summary: Training a neural network using the 'multi-gpu' or 'parallel' execution environment will fail if the machine has no connected network interface
Description: When you use a parallel pool on your local cluster to train a neural network in parallel, you see the following error if your machine has no connected network interface. Error using trainNetwork (line 183)Failed to retrieve host address.  Caused by:  ? ? Error using nnet.internal.cnn.assembler.setupExecutionEnvironment>iValidateWorkerLoad (line 156)? ? Error detected on workers 1 3 4 6.? ? ? ? Error using parallel.internal.general.HostNameUtils.getLocalHostAddress (line 122)? ? ? ? Failed to retrieve host address.?  ?You encounter this error when you try to train a neural network using training options with?ExecutionEnvironment ?set to?'multi-gpu'  or 'parallel' .

Summary: MATLAB might crash because of reusable subsystems in different tasks
Description: MATLAB might crash during model update when all following conditions are met:  Model contains multiple subsystems that are configured to generate reusable code. One of subsystems execute in multiple tasks. For example, it has both periodic and constant rate. Not all of these subsystems execute in the same number of tasks.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: External mode simulation with Variant Subsystem blocks cause unexpected simulation result with generic targets
Description: Variant Subsystem causes incorrect simulation result with external mode simulation when the Variant Subsystem block has:Variant activation set to update diagram analyze all choices ?or code compile  Code generation target is set to grt.tlc  Model is configured for external mode simulation with grt.tlc

Summary: Premature termination of a simulation created using?Simulink.SimulationInput throws a warning message on subsequent runs
Description: When a simulation that is initialized using?Simulink.SimulationInput ?ends prematurely, for example during the execution of the?PostSimFcn , the following warning message is displayed on subsequent runs and the simulation does not proceed. Warning: Associated data with name "SL_SimulationInputInfo" is already registered
Workaround: Close the model.Or?Execute the following commands in the Command Window:?modelHandle = get_param(modelName, 'Handle');dataId = 'SL_SimulationInputInfo';if Simulink.BlockDiagramAssociatedData.isRegistered(modelHandle, dataId)? ? Simulink.BlockDiagramAssociatedData.unregister(modelHandle, dataId);end

Summary: Out of memory error when model enables custom code global variables as function interface
Description: If you select?Enable custom code globals as function interface ,?located on the Simulation Target ?pane of the model configuration parameters dialog, Simulink may throw an error like the following when you try to compile or simulate your model: Out of memory. The likely cause is an infinite recursion within the program.

Summary: snr does not accept complex-valued or N-D input for the syntax r = snr(x,y)
Description: The snr  function throws errors when given complex-valued or N -D signals as input for the syntax?r = snr(x,y) .  When the input signal is complex-valued, as in?r = snr(complex(rand(1,3)),rand(1,3)) , the error message is: Error using snr  Expected input number 1, X, to be real.  When the input signal is an N -D array, as in?r = snr(rand(1,3,2),rand(1,3,2)) , the error message is:? Error using snr  Expected input number 1, X, to be a vector.?

Summary: Code generation error occurs when referenced model contains Overload Options block
Description: When an Overload Options block appears in a referenced model in a Simulink Real-Time model, a code generation error occurs.  The error during code generation is: undefined reference

Summary: Application installers created using the?compiler.package.installer?function?fail to complete installation
Description: On Windows operating systems, when you create an application installer using the?compiler.package.installer ?function with the option "RuntimeDelivery " set to "installer ",? the generated installer fails with the following error: "Call to download server failed (InternetCrackUrl failed: 12006). Try to rerun the installer. If that fails, check your internet connection, firewall, proxy server for virus scanner settings. "?

Summary: Deep Learning code generation with TensorRT and FP16 data type errors out for GPU hardware with compute capability of 5.3, 6.0, and 6.2
Description: GPU Coder incorrectly requires a minimum compute capability (CC) of 7. 0 for FP16 code generation with the TensorRT library. Per NVIDIA documentation, TensorRT supports FP16 data type on GPU hardware with compute capability of 5.3, 6.0, and 6.2. However, TensorRT does not support FP16 data type on a GPU that has a compute capability of 6.1.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Rapid accelerator returns wrong result for Selector block connected to a Multiport Switch block
Description: Rapid accelerator simulation might have incorrect output values for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains a Multiport Switch block with a non-scalar control input signal. A data input of the Multiport Switch block connects to a Selector block, that performs a non-linear indexing operation into the non-scalar source signal.

Summary: MATLAB might crash during startup when signed in to your MathWorks account and using a proxy set in Windows settings
Description: MATLAB might crash during startup if the Windows proxy settings include a list of hosts that should bypass the proxy, and that list ends in a semicolon. The generated stack trace always includes a line that mentionsfoundation::httpproxy::windows::`anonymous namespace'::getProxySettings  For example: [ 43] 0x00007ff9cb071839 foundation::httpproxy::windows::`anonymous namespace'::getProxySettings at b:\matlab\foundation\httpproxy\interface\src\windows\windowsproxyselector.cpp:244
Workaround: Specify the proxy you need to use in the MATLAB preferences, rather than using the the Windows proxy settings.?If that is not possible,Edit the Windows registry.Find HKEY_CURRENT_USER\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings.Edit the?ProxyOverride entry to remove the trailing semicolon from the list of hosts.

Summary: Inconsistent staleness results when collecting model testing metrics using the API
Description: If you execute a lower-level metric separately from higher-level metrics using the command line API, the staleness results might not be updated correctly.For example, staleness results are inconsistent if you follow this workflow:Configuration: collecting the metric TestCaseStatus  and the metric TestCaseStatusDistribution . TestCaseStatusDistribution  is derived from metric TestCaseStatus  . Results for TestCaseStatus  and TestCaseStatusDistribution  exist in the database, but the results are stale (for example, there are new test results to analyze).? You execute the metric TestCaseStatus    by using the command line API. Results for TestCaseStatus    are updated. Separately, you execute the metric TestCaseStatusDistribution . Metric results?TestCaseStatusDistribution  are not updated because all metric dependencies are considered to be up-to-date.    Old results are shown on the dashboard.
Workaround: - Perform an action that makes metric TestCaseStatus  stale again (e.g., export new results). After that run metric TestCaseStatusDistribution , which implicitly runs metric TestCaseStatus  .

Summary: JMAAB check for db_0141 displays an incorrect warning for feedback loops
Description: The Sub-ID a  of JMAAB check for  db_0141  reports the below warning for feedback loops with signal flow from right to leftThe signal flow of diagrams in the following subsystems can be improved for subsystems having feedback loops.  This warning message is incorrect as right to left signal flows are excluded for this check.

Summary: Fixed-point data type strings might be generated incorrectly in test harness global stub functions
Description: Autogenerated test harnesses might error when compiled. The error occurs because fixed-point data type strings stored in Alias Type  parameters are incorrectly prefixed with "fixdt. "? The incorrect prefixes?cause the fixed-point data types to be generated incorrectly in test harness global stub functions. The error message is: Invalid setting in '<Argin/Argout block in Stub Function>' for parameter 'OutDataTypeStr'.  Caused by:  Error evaluating parameter 'OutDataTypeStr' in '<Argin/Argout block in Stub Function>'  Unrecognized data type name '<Fixed-Point Alias Type>'.
Workaround: Remove the "fixdt" prefix from the data-type field in the Signal Attributes tab of block indicated by the error message.

Summary: Import error for certain TLOG files in Flight Log Analyzer App
Description: The Flight Log Analyzer App errors when importing a TLOG file witha message containing a single row in the timetable and the data is of type Char, while formultiple rows the data is of type Cell array. This causes issue while mergingtwo different versions of MAVLink messages. The App throws an error in the MATLABcommand prompt.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier may error out for models containing Lookup Table (n-D) blocks with certain parameter values
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may error out under the following conditions: The model contains Lookup Table (n-D) block, and The?Number of table dimensions ?parameter?of?Lookup Table (n-D) block is set to 1  The following error message appears:?Error while generating data, due to the following issue:Can not resolve: <expression> .  Here, <expression>  is the value entered for?Breakpoints 2  ?parameter of the Lookup Table (n-D)?block.?
Workaround: Temporarily change the value of Number of table dimensions?parameter?of?Lookup Table (n-D) block from 1 to 2. Then set the value of Breakpoints 2 parameter as []. Now change the value of?Number of table dimensions?back to 1, and invoke Simulink Design Verifier.

Summary: ARXML exported for COM_AXIS lookup table in AUTOSAR model contains extra SwRecordLayoutGroup
Description: In the ARXML exported for a COM_AXIS lookup table in an AUTOSAR model, the SwRecordLayout  contains an extra SwRecordLayoutGroup .?The additional SwRecordLayoutGroup  is not required because the lookup table implementation data type is an array.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 4, R2021a

Summary: Setting Simulink preference for Code generation folder structure to Target environment subfolder causes issues
Description: When the Simulink preference Code generation folder structure  is set to Target environment subfolder , issues can occur with Simulink Real-Time when: Building a model that loads data to root-level input ports Running a real-time application on the target computer Opening Simulink Real-Time Explorer

Summary: Error using the coder.getDeepLearningLayers function because of missing dependencies
Description: When using the coder.getDeepLearningLayers  function with dependencies missing, an error is thrown similar to the following: >> coder. getDeepLearningLayers('mkldnn')  Insufficient number of outputs from right hand side of equal sign to satisfy assignment.?   This error is only thrown if any of the following dependencies are missing.? 1. Deep Learning Toolbox Importer for TensorFlow-Keras Models?2. Deep Learning Toolbox Converter for ONNX Model Format?3. Reinforcement Learning Toolbox This issue does not affect code generation of deep neural networks using either MATLAB Coder or GPU Coder.
Workaround: To resolve the issue, install the missing toolbox and support packages. You can also use the following documentation links to determine the layers supported for code generation.Supported Layers?(MATLAB Coder)Supported Layers?(GPU Coder)?

Summary: Passing a string argument to mdfVisualize generates an error
Description: Passing in an MDF-file name as a string argument to mdfVisualize  results in the following error message: Expected input number 1 to be one of these types:char, string

Summary: Simscape to HDL Workflow Advisor produces an algorithm containing a delay
Description: The state space algorithm used by the Simscape to HDL Workflow Advisor introduces one cycle of latency which the advisor does not report. This added delay can change the behavior of the model, especially if the model contains feedback loops. When modeling HDL systems with Simscape to HDL Workflow Advisor, take this behavior into consideration.

Summary: The rosinit or rosgenmsg function fails with error message:?Unrecognized function or variable 'pythonPkgSrc'.
Description: When executing the rosinit  or rosgenmsg  function, an error message is reported in the ros.internal.createOrGetLocalPython  function: ?Unrecognized function or variable 'pythonPkgSrc'.  You may encounter this issue if you installed the ROS distribution on the computer you are running MATLAB.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with Stateflow charts
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:The model contains Stateflow charts. One Stateflow chart has an exported function that is defined inside it. The exported function is reused in another Stateflow chart.? The model configuration parameter?Optimize block operation order in the generated code? parameter is set to?Improved code execution speed.
Workaround: In Configuration Parameters dialog box, set?Optimize block operation order in the generated code??to?None.

Summary: Enabling 'Flatten Hierarchy' on a masked subsystem generates incorrect warning message.
Description: HDL Coder generates warning message when 'Flatten Hierarchy' is enabled on masked subsystem configured to generate generics using MaskParameterAsGenerics option.? This warning can be ignored as generated HDL Code is valid.  See 'Flatten Hierarchy' report for limitations of flattening masked subsystems with generics. The following is the warning message shown in 'CheckReport ' :? Inlining of 'path_to_subystem ' failed: Model contains blocks configured to use generics style parameters in the generated code.?? This message will be removed in the future release.?

Summary: CubeSat Simulation Library?Orbit Propagator block optional output port q_icrf2ff has incorrect units in certain block configurations
Description: CubeSat Simulation Library?Orbit Propagator? block optional output port q_icrf2ff  (transformation?between the International Celestial Reference Frame (ICRF) coordinate system and fixed-frame) has units set to day ?if the block previously enabled, then disabled parameter?Output current date/time (UTC Julian date) .

Summary: MATLAB might crash when navigating between autogenerated chart and state transition table
Description: Performing these actions with an automatically generated chart for a state transition table might cause MATLAB to crash:? Navigating the states in the generated chart in the Simulink Editor. Closing the window of the generated chart. Generating the chart again after closing it.

Summary: mdfVisualize crashes MATLAB when specifying an MDF-file name that is not in the current folder
Description: If you specify an MDF-file name without a full path for mdfVisualize , MATLAB crashes if the file is not in the current folder. This occurs even if the file is on the MATLAB search path.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, always specify a full path or make sure the file in the current folder.

Summary: Generated code applies incorrect casting when ImportedDefine storage class is applied to a Simulink.Parameter object with a structure value
Description: When you apply the storage class?ImportedDefine  to a Simulink.Parameter  object with the Value  property specified as a structure and the DataType  property specified as a Simulink.Bus  object, the generated code applies incorrect casting. For example, given this definition of a Simulink.Parameter object: >> p1 = Simulink.Parameter;?  >> p1.Value =?slexpr("struct('max',int16(150),'min',int16(0))");  >> p1.StorageClass = 'ImportedDefine';  >> p1.DataType = 'busType' % `busType` being also a Simulink.Bus object with both the 'max' and the 'min' bus elements.  The generated code applies this incorrect casting: ? ? ... = (busType)p1.max;  ? ? ... = (busType)p2.min;  Instead the generated code should either not apply casting or apply this casting:? ? ? ... = ((busType)p1).max;  ? ? ... = ((busType)p2).min;

Summary: The find_mdlrefs?function fails to find the active variant block with variant filters post model compilation
Description: When a model is either in the Accelerator  or Rapid Accelerator  modes, the find_mdlrefs  function fails to identify the active model with variant filters blocks post-compilation. Example:? The code below is expected to return only active model blocks, however inactive model blocks will also be noticed.  load_system(modelName);?  % load top model with model reference hierarchy set to accelerator mode set_param(modelName,'SimulationCommand','update');?  [models,blocks] = find_mdlrefs(modelName,'MatchFilter',@Simulink.match.activeVariants);   The code below is expected to return only code active model blocks, however inactive model blocks will also be noticed.  load_system(modelName); ? % load top model with model reference hierarchy set to accelerator mode modelName([],[],[],'compileForCodegen');?  modelName([],[],[],'term');?  [models,blocks] = find_mdlrefs(modelName, 'MatchFilter',@Simulink.match.codeCompileVariants);?

Summary: Code generation for the?filtfilt?function errors out for constant inputs when dynamic memory allocation is turned?"Off"
Description: Standalone static library/dynamically linked library/Simulation-in-the-Loop (SIL)/Processor-in-the-Loop (PIL) code generated for the?filtfilt ? function returns an error when the input arguments are constants and the configuration settings satisfy these conditions: Dynamic Memory Allocation is turned?"Off"   For example, define a function?foo ?that calls?filtfilt : function y = foo() %#codegenx = rand(100,1);b = [0.9876 -0.9876];a = [1 0 -1];y = filtfilt(b,a,x);end Generate a standalone C static library for the function?foo ?using these commands: >> cfg = coder.config('lib');>> cfg.DynamicMemoryAllocation = "Off";?>> codegen foo -config cfg The commands result in this error message: ????Dynamic memory allocation must be enabled in order to create a sparse matrix.

Summary: Nested bus types with C Function block might produce an assertion
Description: Using a nested bus type in a C Function block without defining the bus type in an external header?might result in an assertion if the inner bus type is not specified in the Symbols  table for the block. The assertion might be of the following form: Unexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR:  SanityCheckerImpl. cpp:????ValidCore violated: Type:????type??SubBus_1 = struct SubBus_1_tag {x = myEnum};?
Workaround: Specify an unused symbol of Persistent?scope of each bus type inside the top bus in Symbols?table for the C Function block.?or?Specify the definition of the top bus type in the header code specified in the Model Configuration Parameters in?Simulation Target > Header Code.

Summary: Quantizing alexnet based YOLO detection network results in an error
Description: Using the quantization workflow to quantize an alexnet based YOLO detection network produces this error when the compile function is used Error: dnnfpga. compiler. codegenfpga (Line 69) The specified key does not exist in this container.   Error: dnnfpga. apis. Workflow/ compileNetwork (Line 53)     deployableNW = dnnfpga. compiler. codegenfpga(this. Network,      this. hBitstream. getProcessor,  . . .   Error: dnnfpga. apis. Workflow/ compile Line 533)                 this. DeployableNet = this. compileNetwork(. . .

Summary: sim function does not reset LSTM states between simulations of DQN agent that uses recurrent neural network as its critic
Description: When you use the sim  function to run multiple simulations of a DQN agent that uses a recurrent neural network as its critic, the LSTM states of the critic network are not reset between simulations. Instead, the final LSTM states from the previous simulation are used as the initial LSTM states of the next simulation. The LSTM states of the critic network are reset properly at the beginning of the first simulation. This issue affects only simulation using the sim  function. LSTM states are reset correctly between training episodes.
Workaround: To reset the LSTM states of the critic between simulations, configure the simulation options to run one simulation at a time:simOptions = rlSimulationOptions('NumSimulations',1);Then, you can run multiple simulations using a loop. For example, to simulate the agent 10 times:for ct = 1:10? ?experience(ct) = sim(env,dqnAgent,simOptions);end

Summary: S-Function Builder block might produce an error when it is built after adding a new parameter
Description: The S-Function Builder block might produce the 'parameter mismatch ' error if you perform these actions: Launch the S-Function builder editor Assign a valid name and build the S-Function Add a new parameter? Build the S-Function again??

Summary: sharing applied on a subsystem with matrix as output results in GM compilation issue
