Description: HDL Coder generates the following cryptic error during GM model compilation, when sharing is applied on the subsystem? modeled using Reshape block driving? matrix as output instead of vector. Invalid port dimensions [MXN] has been specified for '<port_name> '? of '<gm_model_name> ' . In multidimensional array mode, the sizes of dimensions other than theconcatenate dimensions must match for all inputs and output ports. Therequirement? is violated along thedimension ˇ®NˇŻ of this input port because the specified size ˇ®MˇŻ is not the sameas the size ˇ®NˇŻ specified by some other port. Error in port widths or dimensions. '<port_name>'  of<ˇŻgm_model_name>'  is a [MXN] matrix.?
Workaround: consider using reshape block to convert matrix types at interfaces to vector types.

Summary: The'ni' adaptor does not appear in the list of installed VISA adaptors
Description: The  instrhwinfo  function does not detect  'ni'  as an installed adaptor  even though NI-VISA is installed. This?happens under the following conditions : NI-VISA driver and another VISA (Keysight VISA, R&S VISA, or TekVISA) are installed.  Preferred VISA is setto the other VISA (Keysight VISA, R&S VISA, or TekVISA)?in NI MAX software or it is not ?explicitly set in NI MAX after VISA installation.  Since the?NI-VISA?adaptor is not detected, visa  object creation returns an error in MATLAB. For example, the following code causes an error during object creation: % This machine has NI-VISA and Keysight VISA?installed.  >>visaInfo = instrhwinfo('visa');  % NI-VISA does not show up in the list of installed VISA adaptors.?  >>visaInfo.InstalledAdaptors  ans =  ? 2ˇÁ1 cell array  ? ? {'keysight'}  ? ? {'agilent' }  >> instrhwinfo visa ni  Error using instrhwinfo (line 331)  Specified ADAPTOR was not found or could not be loaded.?  % VISA object creation returns an error because NI-VISA is not detected.?  >> v = visa('ni','ASRL1')  Error using visa (line 444)  Unable to create VISA object.? The required drivers may not be installed properly.  Type 'instrhelp visa' for more information.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the following steps to set the preferredVISA to "National Instruments VISA" in the NI MAX software. Open NI MAX and click Tools > NI-VISA > VISA Options.?Click on ConflictManager in the VISA Options tab (center).Select "National Instruments VISA"?from the drop-down list for Preferred VISA.Click on Save and then close NI MAX.Launch MATLABand try 'instrhwinfo visa' to see that 'ni' appears in theInstalledAdaptors field.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using enumeration generated from "Active State Data" monitoring of Stateflow charts can result in generation of incorrect Verilog code.
Description: In Stateflow charts, enabling "Active State Data" monitoring results in an extra output port that can be used to monitor the current state of the chart. Enabling "Active State Data" introduces an enumeration that can be used in the Simulink model, like other user defined enumerations. Using the "Active State Data" enumeration value in other parts of the Simulink model can result in incorrect Verilog code being generated.

Summary: Warning message generated when using dlhdl.buildProcessor
Description: The following warning appears when using dlhdl.buildProcessor: ### Start Workflow### Load setting from ModelWarning: There is no interface assigned to model 'wr_reqCounter'. > downstream. tool. displayValidateCelldownstream. DownstreamIntegrationDriver/ validateTargetInterfacehwcli. runWorkflowhdlcoder. runWorkflow (Line 27)dnnfpga. build/ DLBitstreamBuild/ runBitstreamBuilddnnfpga. build. buildProcessor dlhdl. buildProcessor (Line 33)?The warning message does not affect the generated code and can be ignored.

Summary: Code generation might produce uncompilable code for a model containing Nonreusable subsystems with Allow arguments (Optimized) interface
Description: Uncompilable code due to an undeclared local variable might be generated for a model that meets all of the following conditions:The model contains nested subsystems. All these subsystems have the block parameter?Treat as atomic unit ?enabled,?Function packaging ?parameter set to Nonreusable function  and? Function interface  set to  Allow arguments (Optimized)  One of the Subsystems in this hierarchy of nested subsystems has more number of outports than optimized threshold decided by  Allow arguments (Optimized)  interface.  The Model configuration parameter Reuse local block outputs  is enabled

Summary: Running the Shift Schedule Optimization produces an error indicating that a supporting .slxc file cannot be found
Description: Reference Applications use .slxc files to get up and running quickly. .slxc files are pre-built executables made from referenced models. Some .slxc files were not shipped with the Powertrain Blockset. This results in an error when Parallel Computing is used to speed the shift schedule optimization process by attempting to make a copy of the pre-built executable on local workers when those files are not present.
Workaround: When opening up the Conventional Vehicle shift optimization project, go to the OptimizeShiftScheduleForBestFuel.m file in the utilities folder, and comment out lines 137-138. This will prevent the project from requiring the .slxc files for parallel running dependency.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation for function-call subsystem with reusable function packaging inside a triggered subsystem
Description: When a model contains a function-call subsystem configured for reusable function packaging inside another triggered subsystem, the initialization code for the reusable subsystem might not be generated. This incorrect code generation occurs when the function-call port of the function-call subsystem is not connected or is grounded.

Summary: VariableNamingRule errors in parquetread
Description: T = parquetread(filename,'VariableNamingRule','preserve');?  Issues an error: ?'VariableNamingRule' is not a recognized parameter.?
Workaround: Use "PreserveVariableNames",true/false instead.T = parquetread(filename,"PreserveVariableNames",true);??

Summary: FPGA blocks in referenced models do not produce FPGA data in generated real-time application MLDATX files
Description: When FPGA blocks appear in referenced models, building the top-model to generate a real-time application MLDATX file does not include FPGA data in the real-time application.

Summary: Incorrect results from SimFunction objects that include both sensitivities and observables
Description: SimFunction  objects return incorrect simulation results if they include both sensitivities and observable objects in the simulation outputs. This can also lead to incorrect fitting results when using sbiofit  on a model that includes observables, if sensitivity analysis is enabled.
Workaround: If you need to simulate with sensitivities, create a SimFunction that computes sensitivities but not observables. Then, add observables to SimData results using addobservable.If you need to fit a model that contains observables, call sbiofit with the?SensitivityAnalysis name-value argument set to false.

Summary: Simulation 3D Vehicle with Ground Following block outputs incorrect pitch value
Description: In the Simulation 3D Vehicle with Ground Following block, the Orientation output port returns a pitch angle (Y -value) with a reversed sign. For example, an expected output of [10 5 0] is returned as [10 -5 0].
Workaround: To correct the sign of the pitch value, pass theoutput of the Orientation port through a Gain block that has a [1 -1 1] value.

Summary: MATLAB errors when a multi-display system is configured such that the primary display is not bottom-aligned with others to its left or right
Description: If the primary display is not bottom-aligned with a secondary display next to it, it's lower-left corner will not lie at the point (1, 1) which causes problems for the hgrc.m  initialization script. This causes MATLAB to throw the following error: Warning: Initializing MATLAB Graphics failed. ? This indicates a potentially serious problem in your MATLAB setup, which should? be resolved as soon as possible.? Error detected was: MATLAB:badsubscript  Index exceeds the number of array elements (0).  > In hgrc (line 165)  In matlab.graphics.internal.initialize (line 15)
Workaround: Open the hgrc.m?initialization script by running this command in the MATLAB Command Window:? ? open hgrc.m?In hgrc.m, replace these lines:?? ? ? ??screens = get(groot, 'MonitorPositions');? ? % Get the screen positioned at [1 1]? ? screen = screens(and(screens(:,1)==1,screens(:,2)==1),:);? ? width = screen(3);? ? height = screen(4);with these:?? ? screen = get(groot, 'ScreenSize');? ? width = screen(3) - screen(1);? ? height = screen(4) - screen(2);

Summary: Missing parameters in the Predict and Image Classifier block dialog box on non-English installations of MATLAB
Description: The block parameters dialog box for the Predict  and Image Classifier  blocks do not display the File path  and MATLAB function  parameters even after selecting the appropriate value for the Network  parameter. This issue affects only non-English installs of MATLAB.
Workaround: To set these parameters, use their programmatic equivalents.To specify a network from a MAT-file, at the MATLAB command prompt, enter a command of this form:>> set_param(gcb, 'Network', 'Network from MAT-file');>> set_param(gcb, 'NetworkFilePath', '/path/to/mat/file');?To use the network from a MATLAB function, at the MATLAB command prompt, enter a command of this form:>> set_param(gcb, 'Network', 'Network from MATLAB function');>> set_param(gcb, 'NetworkFunction', 'function-name');

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model that contains a Variant Source block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:The model contains a Variant Source block, an Assignment block, and a Bus Assignment block. These blocks are connected in a cascade, with the Variant Source block feeding the input initialization signal (Y0) of the Assignment block, which in turn feeds the assignment input port of the Bus Assignment block.? The model configuration parameter Perform in-place updates for Assignment and Bus Assignment blocks ?(BusAssignmentInplaceUpdate ) is selected.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, clear the Perform in-place updates for Assignment and Bus Assignment blocks (BusAssignmentInplaceUpdate) parameter.

Summary: Deployed applications containing Data Acquisition Toolbox functions run slowly
Description: If you use the daq.createSession()  function from the Data Acquisition Toolbox in your deployed applications, they will run slowly.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for?a model containing a Data Store Memory block with variant conditions
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a Data Store Memory block with variant conditions and a Variant Source block. The Variant Source block is connected to a Data Store Write block. The input to the Data Store Write block is not controlled by the variant conditions of the Variant Source block.

Summary: Deep Network Designer errors when clicking the help button next to a layer name that contains parentheses
Description: When you click the help button next to a layer in Deep Network Designer, an error occurs if the layer name contains parentheses. For example, ˇ°maxPooling2dLayer (for unpooling)ˇ± or ˇ°resize2dLayer (for scale)ˇ±.

Summary: Spurious compatibility warning message occurs when opening generated apps in App Designer
Description: Apps generated in R2020b using simulink.compiler.genapp  throw a spurious warning message about the app being auto-updated to work in R2020b when you open them in App Designer. The warning message also shows a link to display the difference between the files. When clicked, it shows that the files are identical.?

Summary: Parameters not found when using Test Manager "Test from Spreadsheet" for Model reference or block diagram harness
Description: When you use "Test from Spreadsheet in the Test Manager and the system under test is a Model reference or a block diagram harness, the Model reference variables are not found. The following error occurs: No parameters found in the system under test

Summary: The Simulink Signal Logging Selector displays incorrect strings in non-ASCII locales
Description: The Model Hierarchy  panel title in the Simulink Signal Logging Selector  dialog box might be rendered incorrectly when running MATLAB in a locale with non-ASCII characters.

Summary: Test execution with parameter overrides in SIL/PIL simulation mode errors
Description: If you configure a test case to override parameters from a referenced model workspace, and the simulation mode is Software-In-the-Loop (SIL) or Processor-In-the-Loop (PIL), then test execution might produce an error about unrecognized variables or parameters.

Summary: Generated HDL code for the Discrete FIR HDL Optimized block returns a synthesis error when the input data is complex and the coefficients are real
Description: Generated HDL code for the Discrete FIR HDL Optimized block errors out during synthesis when the input data is complex and the coefficients are real. The error indicates that the HDL code drives the same signal with two drivers. The synthesis error from Intel Quartus may look like:? Error (12014): Net "Rx_Filter:u_Rx_Filter|FilterBank:u_FilterBank|doutVld", which fans out to "Rx_Filter:u_Rx_Filter|FilterBank:u_FilterBank|validOut", cannot be assigned more than one value??

Summary: Coverage not recorded for the referenced model during simulation of the generated harness
Description: Coverage is not recorded for the referenced model during simulation of the generated harness when any of the below scenarios is true:You create the harness by setting?Reference input model in generated harness?parameter  to Off  in the?Design Verifier? >?Results? pane of the Configuration Parameters window. You create the harness using ?sldvmakeharness ?with?harnessOpts.modelRefHarness? set to ?false.

Summary: Setting block parameters on block icons and resizing blocks using?Fit to Content?might be disabled
Description: Setting Constant, Gain, and From Spreadsheet?block parameters by clicking the blue underlined value on the block icon fails. Automatically resizing Constant, Gain, and From Spreadsheet blocks after editing the parameter value fails for these actions: In the Simulink Editor, using the interactive cue to resize the block. In the Simulink Toolstrip, on the Format ?tab, selecting?Fit to Content . In the Simulink Editor, selecting the block, pausing on the ellipsis that appears, and selecting?Fit to Content ?from the action bar. Right-clicking the block and selecting?Arrange > Fit to Content .

Summary: Continuously using?clear?after fft?or?ifft with gpuArray inputs can result in an out-of-memory error
Description: Using?clear  or  clear all ? after calling fft ?and other Fourier transform functions? with?gpuArray   inputs?can cause subsequent calls to Fourier transform functions? with gpuArray   inputs to display an out-of-memory error. ?The following functions are affected:?? fft?  ifft?  fft2?  ifft2?  fftn?  ifftn?   For example, the following code generates an out-of-memory error on a GPU with 12GB memory:for ii = 1:10????for j = 2.^(8:13)????????X = rand(j,'gpuArray');????????ifft2(X);????end????clear all;end
Workaround: To avoid this issue, create a dummy gpuArray variable and use clearvarsinstead of clear all to clear all variables except the dummy variable.dummy = gpuArray(1); for ii = 1:10????for j = 2.^(8:13)????????X = rand(j,'gpuArray');????????ifft2(X);????end????clearvars -except dummy;end

Summary: Opening an app in App Designer might result in incorrectly sized components
Description: Some App Designer apps created in previous releases of MATLAB will load with incorrect component size and location. The components appear compressed inside their containers.?The incorrect layout makes it difficult to interact with components in the app.  This issue most often occurs for apps that use a sequence of nested containers, alternating between GridLayout and non-GridLayout containers.?The issue shows an increased rate of occurrence?when the non-GridLayout containers span over multiple cells in the container's parent grid.

Summary: Code generation might produce uncompilable C++ code that uses renamed versions of the type?coder::array?such as?coder::b_array?that are not defined
Description: In some situations, the generated C++ code might contain references to renamed versions of the type?coder::array ?such as?coder::b_array ?or more generally coder::<any prefix>_array ?that are not defined anywhere. Such references makes the generated source code uncompilable. This issue might occur if all? these conditions are true: Your MATLAB code contains a variable-size array? You generate C++ code from your MATLAB code This variable-size array contains a type which is renamed in the generated C++ code (relative to the name of the type in your MATLAB code)  For example, this issue might occur if a variable-size array in your MATLAB code contains a structure, and either this structure itself or its fields are renamed in the generated C++ code. When such renaming happens, code generation might incorrectly rename the?coder::array ?type (that handles dynamically allocated arrays) to coder::<any prefix>_array  in the generated C++ code?without producing a definition for the renamed??type . If this issue occurs, the C++ compiler produces errors saying that the renamed?coder::array  type , like?coder::b_array,  is undefined.?
Workaround: Perform any one of these actions:Instruct the code generator to use the emxArray type instead of the?coder::array?type to handle dynamically allocated arrays by doing one of the following:In the code generation configuration object, set the DynamicMemoryAllocationInterface property to 'C'In the MATLAB Coder app, on the Memory tab, set Dynamic memory allocation interface to Use C style EmxArrayAvoid renaming of the types during code generation. To achieve this, modify your MATLAB code to use unique names for the types (for example, structures and structure fields). A name is unique if it does not match any other name in your MATLAB code or any reserved identifier name in the C++ language.Write a post processing script that automatically renames all instances of?coder::<any prefix>_array?to coder::array?in the generated C++ code. ?

Summary: Crash when choosing "Update Model" in the Outdated Profile Report for a System Composer model
Description: A crash can occur when choosing "Update Model" in the Outdated Profile Report or the "Fix It" option for the outdated profile warning when loading a System Composer model.

Summary: Missing block icon for Predict and Image Classifier
Description: The block icons for the Predict  and Image Classifier  blocks are not displayed in Simulink.

Summary: Empty timetable returned by MDF read has incorrect data type for RowTimes property
Description: When the MDF read  function returns an empty timetable , its RowTimes  property is the incorrect type of datetime . The type should be duration .

Summary: sound and soundsc fail to play audio in MATLAB Online
Description: Using the sound  or soundsc  function in MATLAB Online in non-English browsers generates an error. For example, the following code errors: >> load handel >> sound(y, Fs);  The English translation of the error is: Error: sound (line 79)The index exceeds the number of array elements (0).
Workaround: Use the audiodevinfo and audioplayer?functions to work around the error, as shown in the following steps:1.? Get information about the audio output devices:? ?>> info = audiodevinfo;?2. Examine the fields info.output.Name and info.output.ID to identify the correct output device and its corresponding ID.??3. Call audioplayer with the chosen ID to play the audio in MATLAB Online:?? >> aObj = audioplayer(y, Fs, 16, ID);? >> aObj.play();?

Summary: Calling mean on an empty?OptimizationExpression?returns an error
Description: mean(e)  returns an error when?e  is an empty?OptimizationExpression .

Summary: fileread and other functions return out of memory error for files that cannot be queried for size
Description: When reading files that do not provide an explicit file size, such as the /proc  filesystem, fileread  returns an Out of Memory  error. This also occurs for functions such as?textscan , fread , fscanf , fgets , and fgetl .
Workaround: To avoid this issue, specify the encoding when opening the file. For example:[fid, msg] = fopen(['/proc/' pid '/cmdline'], 'r', 'n', 'UTF-8');out = fread(fid,'*char')';

Summary: Signal Editor fails to import more than one signal from a CSV file
Description: If a CSV file with Signal Builder-format signals contains more than one signal in a sheet, only one signal is imported.
Workaround: Save the CSV file as a .xls or .xlsx file and import the new file.

Summary: When applying aerial imagery onto terrain surfaces, texture coordinates might not accurately reflect a top-down orthographic projection
Description: When applying aerial imagery onto surfaces, texture coordinates might not accurately reflect a top-down orthographic projection. When viewed from above, texture coordinates might appear unexpectedly skewed or rotated to better fit the plane.

Summary: Writing to a subset of a MATLAB created HDF5 file that allocates a large contiguous dataset might corrupt the file
Description: Using h5create  to create an HDF5 file that allocates a large dataset, and then writing to subset of the dataset, generates a file that will error out the next time the file is opened. For example: >> h5create(filename, '/images', [512, 512, 100]);>> h5write(filename,'/images',1,[1,1,1],[1,1,1]);>> h5info(filename); Error using h5infoc  The filename specified was either not found on the MATLAB path or it contains unsupported characters.
Workaround: To work around this issue, create and write the HDF5 file using the HDF5 low-level Interface by first setting the sec2 driver. Set the HDF5 sec2 driver using the MATLAB low-level interface before opening the file with?H5F.open.?For example: %Create the file with dataset 'images' using HDF5 low-level APIfapl = H5P.create('H5P_FILE_ACCESS');%Use the default sec2 driver using low-level API to get around the bugH5P.set_fapl_sec2(fapl);fid = H5F.create("test2.h5",'H5F_ACC_TRUNC','H5P_DEFAULT', fapl);space_id = H5S.create_simple(3, fliplr([512,512,100]), fliplr([512,512,100]));lcpl = H5P.create('H5P_LINK_CREATE');dcpl = H5P.create('H5P_DATASET_CREATE');dset_id = H5D.create(fid,"/images",'H5T_NATIVE_DOUBLE',space_id,lcpl,dcpl,'H5P_DEFAULT');H5D.close(dset_id);H5P.close(dcpl);H5P.close(lcpl);H5S.close(space_id);H5F.close(fid);%Write the data using 'fapl' created abovefid = H5F.open("test2.h5",'H5F_ACC_RDWR',fapl);dataset_id = H5D.open(fid,"/images",'H5P_DEFAULT');space_id = H5D.get_space(dataset_id);H5S.select_hyperslab(space_id, 'H5S_SELECT_SET', ...fliplr([1 ,1 ,1]), fliplr([1,1,1]), ...fliplr([1, 1, 1]), fliplr([1,1,1]));memspace_id = H5S.create_simple(3,fliplr([1,1,1]),[]);H5D.write(dataset_id,'H5ML_DEFAULT',memspace_id,space_id,'H5P_DEFAULT',1);H5S.close(space_id);H5D.close(dataset_id);H5F.close(fid);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using memcpy function in a Stateflow chart generates incorrect code
Description: When you use the?memcpy ?C function in a Stateflow chart as a custom code C function, Stateflow writes the result of the function to a temporary variable. However, the temporary variable is not used to write to the intended destination variable, producing incorrect results in both simulation and generated code.

Summary: Incorrect pointer level in assignment generated from modeling pattern containing a = foo(a) variable-size structure field
Description: When generating code for a MATLAB function that: Contains a structure variable with a variable-size field The field is passed to a function as a a = foo(a)  argument  MATLAB Coder might generate code with an incorrect pointer assignment. This assignment results in C/C++ compiler warnings/errors and at runtime has undefined behavior. For example in the attached issue_2336299.zip  the script repo_2336299.m  generates code for an example with this issue. The MATLAB code contains the function call: ? ?[in1(1).f1,t1] = localFun(in1(1).f1,in3(1:6,1) | in3(1:6,1),in2);  which uses??in1(1).f1  as a  a = foo(a)  argument. The?generated code contains the assignment:?  ? ?r = &in1->f1;?  which has a mismatch in pointer level. The left-hand side of the assignment is emxArray_boolean_T*  and the right-hand side is?emxArray_boolean_T**.  The behavior of proceeding uses of  r  is undefined.?When compiled with?MSVC 2019 this code results in the warning :? ? ?warning C4047: '=': 'emxArray_boolean_T *' differs in levels of indirection from 'emxArray_boolean_T **'

Summary: Graphically or tabularly removing points from signal in table might not always succeed
Description: In some cases, graphically or tabularly removing points from a signal in a table might not succeed. The data point might reappear after checking and unchecking the signal.

Summary: In R2020b, opening and running a model saved in a previous release that contains the Condenser Evaporator (2P-MA) block might result in a compilation error
Description: If you open a model that contains the Condenser Evaporator (2P-MA) block and run the model, you might receive the following error: Error evaluating parameter 'cross_flow_arrangement' in <block name>  This occurs if the model was saved in a release prior to R2020b and the parameter Cross flow arrangement ?in the block was set to Two-Phase Fluid 1 unmixed & Moist Air 2 mixed.
Workaround: In R2020b, change the Cross flow arrangement?parameter to a different option, and then change it back to Two-Phase Fluid 1 unmixed & Moist Air 2 mixed. Then save the model.

Summary: Problem setting value of parameters with Unicode prompt on Windows platform
Description: The value of the parameter does not get set correctly from the dialog when the parameter prompt has Unicode character. This problem is seen only on non-English Windows machine.

Summary: Constant radius reference application model update might error
Description: In the constant radius reference application, if you set the Reference Generator block Maneuver  parameter to Double Lane Change  or Swept Sine , model updates might result in errors.

Summary: ga and gamultiobj?throw an error when clicking the plot function Stop button
Description: Clicking the Stop  button in a?ga  or gamultiobj  plot function window causes the solver to throw an error and not return the current iteration's output. This error occurs for any plot function selections. The error stack varies depending on the solver and problem type, but always includes: Reference to non-existent field 'Generation' .
Workaround: You can programmatically stop ga and gamultiobj?early by specifying certain options,?such as: MaxGenerations, MaxTime, MaxStallGenerations, and MaxStallTime.?For stopping criteria options details, see?https://www.mathworks.com/help/gads/genetic-algorithm-options.html?s_tid=srchtitle#f8335.

Summary: MATLAB Web App Server sessions might be rendered as blank pages in a web browser
Description: MATLAB Web App Server sessions are rendered as blank pages in a web browser. Eventually, these blank pages show the following error message: "Connection to the server was lost. Attempting reconnection... "  This issue occurs when the server is experiencing excessive load and is running out of prewarmed workers.

Summary: Loading auto-reflow apps in App Designer causes incorrect layouts to be displayed
Description: When an auto-reflow app created in a previous release of MATLAB is loaded in MATLAB R2020b, the auto-reflow panels are sized incorrectly in the App Designer design environment. This causes the app's components to be difficult to interact with in App Designer.

Summary: Populating a cell or a struct array with a large number of elements using .NET MWArray API in MATLAB Compiler SDK results in poor performance
Description: Populating a cell or a struct array with a large number of elements using .NET MWArray API in MATLAB Compiler SDK results in poor performance. This is because adding an element results in a traversal operation on an entire array which is performance intensive.

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector fails to verify an incompatible non-inlined user-defined function in a MATLAB Function block and the function call-site
Description: If a non-inlined user-defined function in a MATLAB Function block is incompatible with Simulink Code Inspector, the code inspection fails with a "Failed to verify" overall status. Since the user-defined function cannot be verified, the function call-site also fails verification.

Summary: stftmag2sig?function initializes phases incorrectly in some cases
Description: When?'InitializePhaseMethod' ?is specified as?'random' , or when the user specifies the?'InitialPhase' ?argument, the  stftmag2sig ?function incorrectly?initializes the phase angles used to initialize the complex short-time Fourier transform estimate.

Summary: Incomplete rendering of Debugging Results?while debugging a failed signal using Model Slicer in Simulink Test Manager
Description: Incomplete rendering of?Debugging Results  is?observed while debugging a failed signal using the Model Slicer debug affordance. This issue is observed when there are a large number of signals in the test case result.

Summary: PLL model with PLL testbench encounters error in accelerator mode
Description: When you run PLL model with a PLL testbench in accelerator mode, sometimes it encounters an error. The Lock Time Measurement block inside the PLL testbench oversamples the PLL output clock at 4 times the frequency. This causes the model to run slower and results in the error in accelerator mode. The error persists even when lock time measurement is disabled on the PLL Testbench block parameter dialog box.

Summary: Simulink Test Manager performance degrades when using custom criteria
Description: When a test case has custom criteria enabled and the model produces a large amount of simulation data, the test case execution slows down significantly. For sizes over 1 GB, this causes MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Gain block whose gain constant is a boolean vector?generates internal assertion during HDL code generation
Description: A Gain block whose gain constant is a boolean vector throws this error during HDL code generation:  Error: Evaluation of elaborate function on class hdldefaults. Gain failed with the error message:MATLAB:nonLogicalConditional: Operands to the logical and (&&) and or (||) operators must be convertible to logical scalar values.

Summary: An application installer created using the compiler.package.installer function contains an incorrect custom logo size
Description: When you create an application installer using the the compiler.package.installer  function, and specify a custom logo for the "InstallerLogo"  property, the logo shown when running the generated installer is sized 150 by 340 pixels instead of 340 by 150 pixels.

Summary: Error?'exampleHelperTurtlebotEnableColorCamera' is used in "Track and follow an object"?occurs when trying to run "Track and Follow an Object" example.
Description: When running the "Track and Follow an Object" example, the following error is thrown:? 'exampleHelperTurtlebotEnableColorCamera' is used in "Track and follow an object"  This occurs because example files are not available if you do not have Robotics System Toolbox installed.??

Summary: Error generated by read on an MDF-file channel containing empty byte arrays
Description: Reading MDF-file data from a channel containing empty byte arrays, the read  function results in the following error message: Invalid argument.

Summary: Running an Unreal simulation in Simulink fails with an error message
Description: When running a model with the Simulation 3D Scene Configuration ?Unreal block from UAV Toolbox, the simulation fails with the error message: ?Windows cannot find ... Make sure you typed the name correctly, and then try again .? This error occurs if MATLAB is installed in a folder with a space in it, e.g. in C:\Program Files .??
Workaround: In MATLAB, run the following stepsFind out where your MATLAB is installed by typing matlabroot?in the MATLAB Command Window. Write down the path that's returned, e.g. C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020b.Open the block mask of the Simulation 3D Scene Configuration?Unreal block by double-clicking it.Under Scene source, select Unreal Executable.Under File name, click Browse.Go to the folder from step (1).Navigate to the toolbox\shared\sim3d_projects\aero_project\UE4\win64 folder and select the MathWorks_Aerospace.exe?executable.Under Scene, enter "/Game/Maps/USCityBlock" (if it's not already there).Click Okay?on the block mask to close it.Your Simulink model should now simulate without error. You have to make this change to the Simulation 3D Scene Configuration?block in every model that contains it.

Summary: Breakpoints in MATLAB function Block might slow down simulation
Description: When MATLAB Function Blocks within a Simulink model have breakpoints, the simulation might significantly slow down after reaching the breakpoints. The simulation might remain slow after resuming, even if the breakpoints are never reached again.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when opening a reference component in a System Composer model
Description: A MATLAB crash might occur when opening a reference component in a System Composer model when the referenced model is missing or not on the MATLAB path.

Summary: Cannot?use Configset Reference on a Simulink Model when analyzing the generated code with Polyspace
Description: An issue prevents use of Configset Reference on a Simulink Model when analyzing the generated code with Polyspace.  This also prevents the use of the second argument (pslinkoptions ) when using the polyspacePackNGo  function.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, store your Configset Reference with Polyspace options already embedded.

Summary: Assertion error when using persistent variables in external MATLAB functions called from continuous time Stateflow charts
Description: Simulink might throw an assertion error when all of the following conditions are true: A Stateflow chart with the Update method ?set to Continuous .  A call to a MATLAB function external to the chart. The external MATLAB function uses a persistent variable.  The error message looks similar to: ?failed assertion in b:\matlab\toolbox\stateflow\src\stateflow\utils\object.cpp at line 2319:   id < pool_capacity?

Summary: In Simulink Real-Time Explorer, clicking?View Value button for string signal generates error
Description: In Simulink Real-Time Explorer, clicking the View Value  button for a string signal, such as a signal from a String Constant block, generates an error. The value does not appear. Instead, this warning appears in the MATLAB Command Window: Warning: Error occurred while executing the listener callback for event internalTimer defined for class slrealtime.Instrument:Error using num2str (line 47)Input to num2str must be numeric.

Summary: Web browser link href values containing matlab: might not work on Mac
Description: Web browser link href values starting with the string matlab:  might not work on Mac systems. You might encounter this issue when including the string matlab:  in links to create: Hyperlinks to open one or more files. Normal interactive documentation links. Clickable links in the MATLAB Command Window, such as when creatingcustom error recovery links or "next step" links for users to follow.

Summary: Event bindings for aperiodic partitions from reference models are not editable
Description: When an event binding exists and the model is updated, the event bindings for partitions from reference models might not be editable. These bindings are shown in the Schedule Editor, but the "unbind" button is disabled.
Workaround: Affected bindings may be edited from when they are created until the next update diagram. If a binding is not editable, you may delete the aperiodic partition and re-create it.?This is done by changing the subsystem to schedule as "sample time", updating the diagram, and then changing the subsystem's schedule as back to "Aperiodic partition".?Note that any other scheduling information will also be lost for the affected partitions when using this procedure.

Summary: Using trainNetwork with the option 'ExecutionEnvironment','multi-gpu'?can open a parallel pool with incorrect number of workers
Description: When you use?trainNetwork  with the option? 'ExecutionEnvironment', ?'multi-gpu ' and no parallel pool is currently open, the software attempts to open a pool with a number of workers equal to the number of NVIDIA GPUs in the machine.? If there are any NVIDIA GPUs that are unsupported or are unavailable for MATLAB workers to use, this can result in opening a parallel pool with an incorrect number of workers, which will lead to a warning message similar to the following: Warning: Unable to assign workers with indices 2 in the parallel pool their own GPUs, so these workers will be unused. When opening the pool, specify a number of workers equal to the number of available GPUs.  This issue can occur for any GPU in your machine for which the following is true: GPU has unsupported compute capability; or GPU is marked for exclusive use by another application or program.?
Workaround: To avoid this issue, open a parallel pool with the correct number of workers before using the trainNetwork function. To determine the correct number of workers for your available GPUs and open a parallel pool, use the following code:totalGPUs = gpuDeviceCount;availableGPUs = nnz(parallel.gpu.GPUDevice.isAvailable(1:totalGPUs));parpool(availableGPUs)?

Summary: Some labels created, updated, or deleted in Signal Labeler Fast Navigation?mode are not plotted in main app
Description: Some labels created, updated, or deleted in Signal Labeler  Fast Navigation mode are not plotted in the main app app after the mode is closed. This happens when the fast labeling uses a channel not plotted in the main app.  For example, if a signal has two channels and only one of them is plotted in the main app, a user can enter Fast Navigation mode, plot the unplotted channel, and use it to add or modify labels, but upon exit the main app will not show those changes. Changes made to the channel plotted in the main app will be reflected upon exit. This bug affects only the display axes. The data and labels are correct in both the Labeled Signal Set Browser  and any labeled signal sets exported from the app.
Workaround: To reset the axes to their correct state, unplot and plot back all the signals whose labels you created, updated, or deleted in Fast Navigation mode.

Summary: Example models have incorrect speed measurementduring the transients due to incorrect Hall sensor A configurations
Description: The speed measurements for the models in the example "Field-Oriented Control of PMSM by Using Hall Sensor" (mcb_pmsm_foc_hall_f28069m.slx ?and?mcb_pmsm_foc_hall_f28379d.slx ) are incorrect during transients. This is due to an incorrect edge?detection configuration of the Hall sensor A interrupt. ?

Summary: Fixed point type in bus used with C Function block results in a CGIR assertion
Description: Using a fixed point type inside a bus type variable in a C Function block might produce an assertion of the form: CGIR assertion 'In printTypeUseByConstr: Unsupported type:'
Workaround: Use the base integer type instead of the fixed point type when passing data to or from the C Function block.? The C Function block does not support variables of fixed point type.

Summary: lime?stores simple models inconsistently depending on the simple model type, and its plot breaks when at least one predictor has the same value for all observations
Description: If SimpleModelType  is 'tree'  (decision tree), then the SimpleModel  property of lime  contains a simple model trained on all predictors, not a model trained on selected predictors. If?SimpleModelType ?is?'linear' ?(linear model), then the?SimpleModel ?property contains a simple model trained on selected predictors. This inconsistency makes switching from one model to another nontrivial. The plot  object function of lime  throws warnings or displays misaligned bars when at least one categorical predictor has the same value for all observations in the input data (X  or CustomSyntheticData ) or generated synthetic data. This issue is less common with continuous predictors but can still occur.

Summary: Training TD3 or DDPG agents that use a Gaussian noise model generates bad policies
Description: The Gaussian noise model used by TD3 and DDPG agents generates only positive noise values, which results in poor exploration during training.
Workaround: Instead of using a Gaussian noise model, use an?OrnsteinUhlenbeckActionNoise?object for both DDPG and TD3 agents.DDPG agents use an?OrnsteinUhlenbeckActionNoise?object by default. Access the noise model options using an rlDDPGAgentOptions?object:ddpgOptions = rlDDPGAgentOptions;ouNoiseOptions = ddpgOptions.NoiseOptions;To specify an?OrnsteinUhlenbeckActionNoise?object for a TD3 agent, replace the exploration model and target policy smoothing model with the?OrnsteinUhlenbeckActionNoise?object from the DDPG agent options:td3Options = rlTD3AgentOptions; td3Options.ExplorationModel = ouNoiseOptions;td3Options.TargetPolicySmoothModel = ouNoiseOptions;

Summary: getInstallationLocation returns incorrect installation path for?additional software installed with toolbox
Description: After a toolbox (.mltbx file) that requires additional software is installed, +<toolboxname>.getInstallationLocation ?returns the incorrect installation path for the installed? additional software.
Workaround: After installing the toolbox, open the file:<toolbox installation folder>/+<toolbox name>/getInstallationFolder.mlx??Remove the stray ".zip" appended to the end of the installation folder path and save the file.?

Summary: Crash when copying SimBiology objects with?UserData storing other objects
Description: MATLAB might crash if you call?copyobj  on a SimBiology object that has its UserData  property storing other objects. Because SimBiology Scenarios  objects use copyobj , this crash can also occur when creating scenarios from dose objects that contain other objects in their?UserData ?properties.
Workaround: Instead of storing objects in the UserData property of SimBiology objects, consider using a struct that contains the needed information.

Summary: Simulink message blocks display an error when signal range checking is enabled
Description: Simulink message blocks do not support signal range checking. If the Signal Range Check  parameter is not set to none , message blocks display an error.
Workaround: Disable Signal Range Check:? In Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Data Validity, set Simulation range checking to none.

Summary: 'Normalization' option ignored for dlnetwork objects with multiple input layers.
Description: For dlnetwork  object objects with multiple input layers, the software applies the specified normalization of the first input layer to all other input layers. For example, create a dlnetwork  with two image input layers and specify different normalizations for each. branch1 = [  ? ?imageInputLayer([6 6 1],'Normalization','none','Name','in1')  ? ?fullyConnectedLayer(2,'Name','fc1')  ? ?concatenationLayer(1,2,'Name','cat')];?  lgraph = layerGraph(branch1);?  branch2 = [  ? ?imageInputLayer([6 6 1],'Normalization','zerocenter','Mean',1, 'Name','in2')?  ? ?fullyConnectedLayer(2,'Name','fc2')];  lgraph = addLayers(lgraph,branch2);?  lgraph = connectLayers(lgraph,'fc2','cat/in2');?  dlnet = dlnetwork(lgraph);  Make predictions using the network. dlX1 = dlarray(ones([6 6 1]),'SSCB');?  dlX2 = dlarray(ones([6 6 1]),'SSCB');? ?  [dlY1, dlY2] = predict(dlnet,dlX1,dlX2,'Outputs',["in1" "in2"]);  Because the software applies the normalization specified for the first input layer, the outputs dlY1? and?dlY2 ?are the same. The normalization specified by the second input layer?'in2' ?is not applied to the second input.
Workaround: Manually normalize the data before passing the data to the predict?and forward?functions. For example, to use zero-center normalization for the second input with mean mu. Subtract the mean from the input data manually before calling predict.dlX2 = dlX - mu; [dlY1, dlY2] = predict(dlnet,dlX1,dlX2,'Outputs',["in1" "in2"]);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Certain HDL configuration parameter values might produce Verilog code that has same instance name and register name in the module
Description: Suppose that you modify the HDL configuration parameter values by performing these steps: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, you set HDL Code Generation ?> Global Settings  > Coding Standards  > HDL coding standard  to Industry .  For any  of these two parameters, you set?Maximum ?to a nondefault value: Check module,?  instance,?  entity name length  Check signal,?  port,?  parameter name length     Generating Verilog code with these configuration parameter values might produce code that has the same instance name and register name in the module. This issue could produce an error during generated HDL code simulation.

Summary: Displaying a cell array created using the mxArray API (C/C++) or mwArray API (C++, Java, .NET) throws and exception error
Description: Displaying a cell array created using the mxArray API (C/C++) or mwArray API (C++, Java, .NET) throws and exception error.? For example, the expected display of a 1x2 cell array where the two cells contains a numeric row vector is:? ? ? ? ? [1x9 double]? ? [1x9 double]?    But an exception is thrown for the display of? the cell array: For a C/C++ driver code, the following exception is thrown:?   vector<T> too long    For a .NET driver code, the following exception is thrown:   Unhandled Exception: System.ApplicationException: Error formatting array output    For a JAVA driver code, the following exception is thrown:   java.lang.RuntimeException: Raised from C++ code using jproxy::throwNamedException
Workaround: Iterate through each elements that are not of type cell array and display them individually.

Summary: A structure array created using the mxArray API (C/C++) or mwArray API (C++, Java, .NET) does not expand field values when displayed
Description: A structure array created using the mxArray API (C/C++) or mwArray API (C++, Java, .NET) does not expand field values when displayed.?? For example, consider a structure containing a numeric array in two of its fields. The expected display of the structure is: foo: [1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 9]  bar: [1 2 3 4 5 67 8 9]   But the actual display of the structure is: foo: [1x9double]   bar: [1x9double]?

Summary: NaN from sensor when sensor sample time is not a multiple of the UAV scenario sample time
Description: For the UAV scenarios, you can construct sensors with update intervals that are not a multiple of the scenario update interval. This can lead to sensor output NaN results. For example: s = uavScenario("UpdateRate", 5, "StopTime", 2); p = uavPlatform("UAV", s, "Trajectory", waypointTrajectory([0 0 0; 1 0 0], "TimeOfArrival", [0 1]));  gps = uavSensor("GPS", p, gpsSensor("SampleRate", 2));? % GPS update interval is 0.5 second, which is not a multiple of the scenario update interval 0.2 second. setup(s);  isRunning = true; ?for idx = 1:5  isRunning =  advance(s);  ? ? ?  updateSensors(s);  ? ? ? [isUpdated,  ?t,  ?lla] =  read(gps)? % lla output could be NaN in certain steps.  end?
Workaround: To avoid this problem, change your scenario or sensor update rate so that the sensor update interval is a multiple of scenario update interval:?s = uavScenario("UpdateRate", 10,?"StopTime",?2);p = uavPlatform("UAV",?s,?"Trajectory",?waypointTrajectory([0 0 0; 1 0 0],?"TimeOfArrival",?[0 1]));gps = uavSensor("GPS",?p,?gpsSensor("SampleRate",?2));?

Summary: Model advisor check Usable characters for Inport blocks and Outport block?erroneously flags bus elements
Description: The Model Advisor check?Usable characters for Inport blocks and Outport block  (mathworks.jmaab.jc_0211 ) flags the bus elements if it is a bus. For example, if Bus1 and Bus2 are two Simulink bus signals and var1 and var2 are elements of Bus1, and Bus1 is element of Bus2. Then one of the port is "Bus2.Bus1.var1" which is flagged by the check mathworks.jmaab.jc0211 .

Summary: AUTOSAR-C++14 A10-1-1 checker does not recognize some interface classes and incorrectly flags some multiple inheritances
Description: AUTOSAR-C++14 A10-1-1 ?states that a class shall not be derived from more than one base class which is not an interface class. The AUTOSAR-C++14?standard considers an interface class as a class with these properties: All member functions are public pure virtual All data members are public static constexpr  The rule does not separately mention special member functions such as constructors or destructors. However, you might need to explicitly set one of these special member functions to =default  or =delete ,?and because of the rule of five, do the same for all five special member functions. In this case, the Polyspace checker for AUTOSAR C++A10-1-1 no longer considers the class as an interface class and incorrectly flags some multiple inheritances where such a class acts as a base class.

Summary: Displaying cvdatagroup object causes unrecognized property error
Description: An error occurs when displaying a cv.cvdatagroup  object at the MATLAB command-line. For example, the cvsim  command below performs a coverage analysis on a model reference hierarchy (with a top model named "mTopModel") and returns a cv.cvdatagroup  object to capture the results.? However, displaying the object causes an error. >> cvdg = cvsim('mTopModel');>> cvdgcvdg = ... cv.cvdatagroup? mTopmodelUnrecognized method, property, or field 'isObserver' for class 'cvdata'.Error in cv.cvdatagroup/display (line 27) ? ? if(this.get(names{ii}).isObserver)  This bug only affects displaying the cv.cvdatagroup  object at the command-line, and does not affect the validity of the results. The object can still be used with any of the relevant coverage functions such as cvhtml  to generate a report or?cvmodelview  to highlight results.
Workaround: If you wish to see what cvdata objects are contained within the cv.cvdatagroup object, you can use cv.cvdatagroup.getAll instead.

Summary: NR Polar Encoder block output is incorrect for the first simulation run when Configuration source is From property and the parameters are defined by workspace variables
Description: If the NR Polar Encoder block has?Configuration source  set to From property  and you use a workspace variable to define either of the parameters, then the internal configuration does not update correctly when the workspace variable is changed. The first simulation run after changing the variable returns the wrong answer. A second run with no changes returns correct results.
Workaround: Set the Configuration source?parameter to From port and use Constant blocks to define the K and E values on the ports.

Summary: Potential crash during Update Diagram with System Object block
Description: Simulink can potentially crash during Update Diagram if the model contains System Object block where the System Object definition has been changed since the last Update Diagram.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code that uses row-major array layout and contains BLAS calls might return incorrect answers for certain functions
Description: Code generated for certain MATLAB functions might return incorrect answers if all  of these conditions are true: You generate a static library, a dynamically linked library, or an executable. You generate code that uses the row-major array layout. You instruct the code generator to include calls to an external BLAS library in the generated code.  Functions for which the generated code might return incorrect answers include?cov ,  nancov ,  pca ,  sgolay , and? sgolayfilt .

Summary: MATLAB might sporadically crash if the user exits MATLAB while debugging MATLAB code
Description: MATLAB might sporadically crash if the user exits MATLAB while debugging MATLAB code

Summary: GPU Coder generated MEX leaks memory when there is a mismatch between the compile-time and run-time?gpuArray sizes
Description: Running generated MEX files will leak GPU Memory when all of the following conditions are true: gpuArray ?object(s) are used in the input The MEX is called with a different runtime size for the gpuArray  then what it was compiled with  For example: ? ? ?function z = simpleAdd(x, y) ? ? ? ? z = x + y;??? ?? end? ? ? ?codegen -config coder.gpuConfig('mex') -args {gpuArray(zeros(1, 10)), gpuArray(zeros(1, 10))} simpleAdd?  Executing the following line would then incur a leak of GPU Memory:? ? ? ?simpleAdd_mex(gpuArray(zeros(1, 15)), gpuArray(zeros(1, 15)))?  In addition, the following error message would appear:? ? ? ?Incorrect size for expression 'x': expected [1x10] but found [1x15].  ? ? ?Error in simpleAdd_mex?

Summary: GPU Coder leaks GPU memory when executing a MEX function that uses one or more?gpuArray variables as both an input and an output
Description: Executing generated MEX files that use a gpuArray  variable as both an input and output will result in a leak of GPU memory. For example: ? ? ?function x = simpleAdd(x)??? ? ? ? x = x + 2;?? ? ?end?  ? ? ?codegen -config coder.gpuConfig('mex') -args {gpuArray(zeros(1, 1e8))} simpleAdd  Executing the following line would then incur a leak of GPU Memory:? ? ? ?simpleAdd_mex(gpuArray(zeros(1, 1e8)));?

Summary: Functions generated for a Stateflow chart use the wrong memory section
Description: The functions generated for a Stateflow chart use the memory section for shared utility functions instead of the memory section for the chart. This results in incorrect code behavior when the memory section for the shared utilities differs from the memory section specified, either directly or indirectly, for the chart: Directly - The chart parameter Memory section for execution functions  is set to Default   or the name of a memory section. Indirectly - The chart parameter?Memory section for execution functions?is set to Inherit from model , and the Execution  memory section for the model is used.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Size of variable-size data incorrectly modified prior to generated code that reads and writes the same data
Description: For the following MATLAB coding pattern An assignment statement completely writes a variable-size matrix The right-hand-side of the assignment statement contains an access of a single element of the same variable-size matrix using a subscript  MATLAB Coder might incorrectly generate code for this statement that modifies the size of the variable-size data prior to the code that reads the variable-size data. This is incorrect when the read of the variable-size data also uses the modified size to compute a subscript. This generated code might result in reading uninitialized memory and/or incorrect numeric results. The following MATLAB function exemplifies this issue: function out = issue_2329962(x,y,z)    assert(isa(x,'double'));    assert(size(x,1) <= inf);    assert(size(x,2) <= inf);    assert(isa(y,'double'));    assert(size(y,1) <= inf);    assert(size(y,2) <= inf);    assert(isa(z,'double'));    assert(size(z,1) <= inf);    assert(size(z,2) <= inf);    y = x(2,3) .* y(4,4) + z;    out = max(42,y(end));end When code is generated for this function using the command:?? ?codegen -c -config:exe issue_2329962 ? The generated code contains   loop_ub = z->size[0] * z->size[1];  i = y->size[0] * y->size[1];  y->size[0] = z->size[0];  y->size[1] = z->size[1];  emxEnsureCapacity_real_T(y, i);  b_x = x->data[x->size[0] * 2 + 1] * y->data[y->size[0] * 3 + 3];  for (i = 0; i < loop_ub; i++) {    y->data[i] = b_x + z->data[i];  } The MATLAB statement?y = x(2,3) .* y(4,4) + z  corresponds to the coding pattern described above where y is the variable-size data both written and read. The expression in the generated code?x->data[x->size[0] * 2 + 1] * y->data[y->size[0] * 3 + 3]  corresponds to the MATLAB expression?x(2,3) .* y(4,4) . Note that the size of y  has been incorrectly modified prior to this expression. Instead code should have been generated such that the size of y  is updated after the multiply expression:   loop_ub = z->size[0] * z->size[1];  i = y->size[0] * y->size[1];  b_x = x->data[x->size[0] * 2 + 1] * y->data[y->size[0] * 3 + 3];  y->size[0] = z->size[0];  y->size[1] = z->size[1];  emxEnsureCapacity_real_T(y, i);  for (i = 0; i < loop_ub; i++) {    y->data[i] = b_x + z->data[i];  }

Summary: Table UI component duplicated from a grid layout manager in App Designer is not visible
Description: When you duplicate a table UI component from a grid layout manager in App Designer using the "right-click-drag" gesture, the table is not visible and cannot be deleted.
Workaround: Save and reload the app. You will then be able to delete the duplicated table.

Summary: Requirements authored in Excel, imported to Simulink Requirements, and exported as ReqIF file might contain undefined XML namespace
Description: When you author requirements in Microsoft Excel, import those requirements into Simulink Requirements, and then export the requirements as a ReqIF file, the exported file might contain undefined XML namespace. This happens when cells in your Excel document contain formatting such as centered or bold text. Some third-party requirements management tools use an XML validator to validate a ReqIF before importing it. A ReqIF with undefined namespace will fail the validation.?

Summary: Code generation fails with error "Unable to find Interface within the ModelReferenceInterfaces scope"
Description: Code generation might fail for a referenced model that only has event based rates with the following error message:"Unable to find Interface within the ModelReferenceInterfaces scope"

Summary: Incorrect parameter override values applied during test execution
Description: If a test case has a parameter override, and uses inputs from a Simulink Design Verifier MAT file that specifies the same parameter, then during simulation the MAT-file parameter value is applied, but the test case result shows that the parameter override is applied.

Summary: Production and target properties of a coder.HardwareImplementation object synchronize with each other on modification
Description: The behavior of the coder.HardwareImplementation ?object changes in R2020b. This change in behavior might cause incorrect code generation for your target hardware if you execute any code generation script that relies on the pre-R2020b behavior of the?coder.HardwareImplementation ?object . Prior to R2020b, modifying a production hardware property of the?coder.HardwareImplementation ?object did not affect the corresponding target hardware property and vice-versa. For example, modifying the ProdHWDeviceType  property had no effect on the TargetHWDeviceType  property. In R2020b, if the ProdEqTarget   property is set to?true , the target and production hardware properties synchronize with each other when either one is modified: Modifying a production hardware property automatically modifies the corresponding target hardware property Modifying a target hardware property automatically modifies the corresponding production hardware property  For example, consider this MATLAB code: hw = coder.HardwareImplementation;hw.ProdHWDeviceType = 'Generic->8-bit Embedded Processor';disp(hw.TargetHWDeviceType); Prior to R2020b, this code displays 'Generic->MATLAB Host Computer' .  In R2020b, this code displays?'Generic->8-bit Embedded Processor '.  This behavior change causes a backwards incompatibility which might affect your legacy code that manipulates coder.HardwareImplementation  objects. Also, in R2020b, when you convert a MATLAB Coder project to an equivalent script of MATLAB commands, the generated script might be incorrect if any one  of these conditions is true: Your project uses a custom?coder.HardwareImplementation ?object in which?ProdEqTarget ? is set to?false  Equivalently, in the MATLAB Coder or GPU Coder app, in the All Settings  section of the More Settings  dialog,? Test hardware is the same as production hardware ?is set to No .   Also, in R2020b, a script generated from a MATLAB Coder project might behave differently compared to previous releases if both  these conditions are true: You generated this script by using a previous release In this script, the?ProdEqTarget ?property is set to?false ?

Summary: Model Advisor check Display model metrics and complexity report fails for models containing Stateflow Charts defined in a library
Description: If a model contains Stateflow charts defined in a library, the Model Advisor check Display model metrics and complexity report? (Check ID: mathworks.iec61508.MdlMetricsInfo) shows this error: 'Model Advisor couldn't retrieve information from the model correctly'?

Summary: Compiling a model with an observer reference might crash MATLAB when the observer model has an asynchronous sample time
Description: When you compile an observer model that has an asynchronous sample time, MATLAB might crash instead of Simulink showing an error that the sample time is unsupported.

Summary: The edfread function and edfinfo object throw hard errors when an EDF+ file has signal data but has no actual signal-specific annotations other than record times
Description: When an EDF+ file has signal data and data record start times as annotations, but no other signal annotations, the?edfread ?function and?edfinfo ?object throw this error: Error using timetable (line 470)  RowTimes property must contain one time for each row in the timetable.

Summary: Generating HDL code from MATLAB Function blocks and Stateflow charts may result in violation of HDL Coding standard rule 2.F.B.1?in the generated code
Description: Generating HDL code from MATLAB Function blocks and Stateflow charts can result in code of the form: foo(){  ???? if (cond1){  ???????? ...  ???? }  ???? if (cond2){  ???????? ...  ???? }  }  This pattern results in violation of HDL Coding standard rule?2.F.B.1.
Workaround: For MATLAB Function blocks, this issue can be avoided by setting the HDL block property "Architecture" as "MATLAB Datapath".

Summary: Lidar 3-D Object Detection Using PointPillars Deep Learning example has a dependency on Statistics and Machine Learning Toolbox
Description: augmentData  is a helper function in the Data Augmentation section of the example. It uses a function called randsample , which requires Statistics and Machine Learning Toolbox. As a result, you will not be able to run the Train Model section of the example without installing Statistics and Machine Learning Toolbox.
Workaround: In the helper function augmentData.m, replace the following code (line 45) :? if randsample([true,false],1) with this code:?if randi([0,1])

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for MATLAB code in which the loop bounds are unknown at compile time and the lower bound is greater than the upper bound
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for MATLAB code in which the loop bounds are unknown at compile time and the lower bound is greater than the upper bound. In the incorrect code, SIMD optimized code incorrectly computes the scalar loop bounds. ?Consider this MATLAB function: ?function out = dynamicBounds(a, b, start, stop)  ??? assert(isa(start, 'int32'));   ??? assert(isa(stop, 'int32'));   ??? out = zeros(size(a), 'like', a);  ??? for i = start:stop  ??????? out(i) = a(i) * a(i) + b(i) * b(i);  ??? end  ?end  When start > stop +1, the loop should execute zero iterations, but in the incorrect generated code, SIMD optimizations might result in one or more iterations executing at run time.
Workaround: To obtain correct behavior without disabling SIMD optimizations, you can modify the bounds of loops exhibiting this issue. For the preceding example, review the problematic case and alter the loop bounds to ensure that there is zero iteration.?if start > stop+1? ? start = int32(1); ? ? stop = int32(0); ?end ?for i = start:stop ? ? ... ?endThis modification ensures zero iterations of the loop without encountering the error condition start > stop+1.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier Results Summary window may include Dead Logic objectives that were not proven to be dead.
Description: Simulink Design Verifier Results Summary  window may include Dead Logic objectives that were not proven to be dead under the Valid  total, when the following conditions are met:?The mode is Design error detection   Dead logic (partial)   option is enabled Run exhaustive analysis  option  is disabled At least one other Design error detection option (such as Out of bound array access  ) is enabled  For example, if the following text is in the Results Summary  Window:? Objectives Processed:? 30/32  Valid: 30  Falsified: 0  Elapsed Time: 10:00  The total of 30  for Valid  would incorrectly include Dead Logic objectives for which Simulink Design Verifier failed to prove that they were dead

Summary: MATLAB might hang or crash when using a deep learning network inside a resettable subsystem
Description: Triggering the reset signal to a subsystem that contains a deep learning network can cause MATLAB to hang or crash. The crash report may contain the following assertion error:Assertion `it == m_output.end()' failed

Summary: MATLAB crashes when changing a non-string stereotype property value on an architecture model after the model has been instantiated via the Analysis Viewer
Description: MATLAB crash occurs in the below scenario:Find or create an architecture model containing stereotypes and stereotype properties on model elements. Launch Analysis Model to open the Instantiate Architecture Model dialog. Select all stereotypes and click Instantiate . The Analysis Viewer opens up.? On the architecture model, change the value of a non-string stereotype property using the Property Inspector.

Summary: Variant Subsystem block '<block name>' contains no active variant controls?error with Allow zero active variant controls
Description: For model which contains a Variant Subsystem with Allow zero active variant controls  parameter turned on, update diagram will throw the error Variant Subsystem block '<block name>'?contains no active variant controls , when the following conditions are satisfied:All choice blocks are coming from custom libraries. None of the variant expressions are active. The Variant Subsystem block itself or the dialog is not opened.
Workaround: Explicitly open the Variant Subsystem dialog.

Summary: Import from IBM DOORS Next not working when configuration management is enabled for project
Description: When attempting to import an IBM DOORS Next module from Jazz server version 6.0.6.1 or later, and when Configuration Management is enabled for the DOORS Next project, the? Importing Requirements dialog in Simulink Requirements fails to display the list of modules. After selecting the project and selecting?Full module hierarchy ?in the Importing Requirements dialog box, the UI takes a while to refresh the DNG Module ?selector, but no entries appear in the drop-down list.

Summary: Issues with modifying session file location and renaming session file in Flight Log Analyzer app
Description: In the Flight Log Analyzer app, if you modify the location ofthe previously saved session file and load the session file with the app tosave it again, the app tries to save the session file in the previously savedlocation.  Also, if you rename the session file and load the session file with the app to save it again, the app tries to save the session file with the previouslysaved session name.

Summary: Unable to set data type for lookup table object in model workspace to alias data type in base workspace
Description: When you have a lookup table object in a model workspace and an alias data type in the base workspace, the data type for the lookup table object breakpoint cannot be set to the alias data type.

Summary: Enum file is missing from automatically generated project for protected model
Description: An enum file that is required by the protected model, but is not used by the root inputs or outputs of the model, is not included in the automatically generated project archive (.mlproj ) that is created for the protected model and its dependencies.
Workaround: The creator of the protected model can:Unpack the automatically generated project archive (.mlproj).Add the missing enum file to the project.Create a project archive from the project that contains the enum file.

Summary: Example model for FOC Autotuner Block on real-time systems uses obsolete bitstream file to configure Speedgoat hardware
Description: The model  mcb_pmsm_foc_autotuner_speedgoat.slx , provided as part of the example  Tune PI Controllers Using Field Oriented Control Autotuner Block on Real-Time Systems , uses an older version of bitstream file to configure FPGAs on Speedgoat hardware. As a result, the inverter in the Speedgoat electric motor control kit might not turn on.

Summary: ROS Master is not reachable in case of Wi-Fi or other slower connections.
Description: The default timeout for checking if a ROS master is available is 2 sec. This default is not enough for some Wi-Fi connections or other slower connections.

Summary: Incorrect update of target critic representation when training DQN agent
Description: Training a DQN agent can produce a bad policy due to incorrect updates of the target critic representation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: For an RNN whose sequence input layer?accepts 3-D image sequences, code generated for the cuDNN target might produce different output size than MATLAB
Description: In R2020b, deep learning code generation for?SequenceInputLayer  ?using the ?cuDNN library supports only vector and 2-D image sequence inputs. However, the code generator does not throw an error for 3-D image sequence inputs and proceeds with code generation. Suppose that you try to generate code that takes advantage of the cuDNN library for a recurrent neural network (RNN). If the sequence input layer?of the network accepts 3-D image sequences, the?generated code might produce a different output size compared to MATLAB.

Summary: Analysis may terminate with an?Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier back end?error message.
Description: Analysis might terminate without producing the results due to floating-point computations in the model that produce very large values. When you run the analysis with a user interface, it displays the following error message: Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier back end.  When you run the analysis in the?Test Generation ?mode, the error message displayed at the MATLAB Command Window is: Test generation produced errors  In releases beginning in R2018a, the below stack trace is produced: src/data_graph/graph_rat_prop-new.c:475 err=data_graph_err_rat_val_too_big_for_double    src/data_graph/graph_rat_prop-new.c:110 err=data_graph_err_rat_val_too_big_for_double    src/tempo/tempo_safe_induction.c:1343 tempo_error=tempo_err_unknown  When this issue is hit, the stack trace will contain data_graph_err_rat_val_too_big_for_double
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 5, R2021a

Summary: Block LoadFcn callbacks do not execute in test harnesses
Description: When blocks inside an internal test harness contain a LoadFcn callback, they might not execute when a test harness is opened or loaded.

Summary: Masked block with Custom Table errors out during simulation with open mask dialog
Description: Model containing masked block errors out when simulated with mask dialog open.The behavior is seen when all of the following conditions are TRUE: The masked dialog is open The masked block contains Custom Table Parameter The Custom Table Parameter has an associated parameter callback The Parameter callbacks explicitly modifies a specific cell in the Table  ?Error message observed in the diagnostic viewer: 'Model/withMask' has unapplied changes. Please apply or cancel these changes before running the simulation?

Summary: Simulink Check Tool Operational Requirements contains incorrect hyperlink to the high-integrity guidelines
Description: In the Simulink Check Tool Operational Requirements  artifact (qualkitdo_slchk_tor ), the hyperlink for the high-integrity system modeling guidelines directs you to the R2019b version of the document.? ?Correct links are: For R2020a:?Modeling Guidelines for High-Integrity Systems, R2020a  For R2020b:?Modeling Guidelines for High-Integrity Systems, R2020b

Summary: MATLAB might crash when using a context menu in Simulink or Stateflow on macOS Big Sur
Description: On macOS Big Sur (11.0), using a context menu in any Simulink or Stateflow window might cause MATLAB to crash. For example, control +clicking a block in the Simulink Editor, an element in the Model Explorer, or a parameter in a block dialog box might cause the crash.

Summary: NI device names containing "do" might not be recognized
Description: If your NI device name contains the substring "do", accessing it in MATLAB might cause the following error:Unrecognized method, property, or field 'doWriteDataLastBlock' for class 'daq.ni.ChannelGroupAnalogInput'.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, use the NI MAX tool to rename the device so it does not contain the substring "do". Then either restart your MATLAB session or run daqreset.

Summary: Code compiled with MATLAB Compiler SDK or executed with MATLAB Job Scheduler on Linux can crash due to an assertion failure in?dl-open.c
Description: Some code compiled with MATLAB Compiler SDK or executed with MATLAB Job Scheduler on Linux can sporadically crash with the following assertion failure:? Inconsistency detected by ld.so: dl-open.c: 265: dl_open_worker: Assertion `_dl_debug_initialize (0,?args->nsid)->r_state == RT_CONSISTENT' failed! For MATLAB Compiler SDK, the failure can occur with multi-threaded applications. For MATLAB Job Scheduler, the failure causes worker MATLAB processes to occasionally hang. For example, when running code that starts a large parallel pool, the formation of the pool can fail and one or more workers in the MJS cluster become unusable.
Workaround: You can work around the issue by setting the KMP_INIT_AT_FORK environment variable false before running your code.On all machines using MATLAB Compiler SDK, set the environment variable before starting MATLAB or running the compiled application:  KMP_INIT_AT_FORK=falseFor the MATLAB Job Scheduler, set the environment variable in the mjs_def.sh file on all nodes in your cluster:Edit the mjs_def.sh file.Add the following on a new line at the end of the file:export KMP_INIT_AT_FORK=falseRestart the MJS service on each node in the cluster.

Summary: Compatibility check fails for models containing blocks with ports at top or bottom
Description: If a model contains blocks with ports at top or bottom then Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check can fail with following errors:Simulink Design Verifier failed to initialize: Checking compatibility failed  Component:sldv | Category:Design Verifier compatibility error  The destination port already has a line connection  Component:sldv | Category:Design Verifier compatibility error
Workaround: Locate the block with ports at top or bottom and move those ports to left or right side of the block

Summary: Incorrect adjacency matrix for some SimBiology models using reversible reactions
Description: The adjacency matrix is incorrectly constructed when calling getadjacencymatrix  on a SimBiology model containing reversible reactions that have the same species on both the left-hand and right-hand sides of the reaction, for example "null <-> a + null " or "x + y <-> x + z ".
Workaround: Replace each reversible reaction that has the same model species on both sides of the reaction with two equivalent irreversible reactions. For example, replace "x + y <-> x + z" with reactions "x + y -> x + z" and "x + z -> x + y".

Summary: Model reference simulation in Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator mode ignores updated default model argument
Description: After a model reference simulation, if you change the default value of a model argument at the point of definition, during subsequent simulations that use Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator mode, a top model that uses the default value ignores updates to that value .

Summary: Clock Rate Pipelining with Scoped Delay Balancing may result in MATLAB crash/freeze during HDL code generation
Description: If a model uses Scoped Delay Balancing  and Clock Rate Pipelining , and that model contains a feedback loop between two or more subsystems which are not participating in Flatten Hierarchy , a crash may occur during HDL code generation. An example model which exhibits this crash has the following HDL properties set. 'Subsystem' and 'Subsystem1' are connected to create a feedback loop. ?%% Set Model 'crash_example' HDL parameters  hdlset_param('crash_example', 'BalanceDelays', 'off');  hdlset_param('crash_example', 'HDLSubsystem', 'crash_example/DUT');  hdlset_param('crash_example', 'Oversampling', 40);  ?  % Set SubSystem HDL parameters  hdlset_param('crash_example/DUT/Subsystem', 'BalanceDelays', 'on');  hdlset_param('crash_example/DUT/Subsystem', 'StreamingFactor', 3);  ?  % Set SubSystem HDL parameters  hdlset_param('crash_example/DUT/Subsystem1', 'BalanceDelays', 'on');  hdlset_param('crash_example/DUT/Subsystem1', 'StreamingFactor', 3);?

Summary: MATLAB crashes and displays error when deleting element in System Composer model with enumeration class file missing from MATLAB path
Description: MATLAB crashes and displays the error: The architecture model has become inconsistent. ?Contact The MathWorks for assistance.  Caused by: Enumeration class not found.  This crash occurs when all of the following are true: The model being edited has an imported profile. The imported profile uses an enumerated class. The enumerated class file is not on the MATLAB path.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for the Sqrt block with fixed point input may mismatch simulation behavior
Description: Generating HDL Code from a subsystem with a Sqrt block results in incorrect code? under the following conditions: 1) The input to the block is fixed-point type?? 2) The Intermediate results  in the Datatype pane of block properties is set to a value other than Inherit:inherit via internal rule.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL Code generated for the Signed Sqrt block with double data type may be incorrect
Description: Generating HDL Code from a subsystem with a Sqrt block results in incorrect code when following conditions are met Input is double data type. Floating Point IP Library?is set to ?Native Floating Point   Under these circumstances, the generated VHDL has compilation errors while the generated Verilog has simulation mismatches during simulation with an HDL Simulator.

Summary: CPU utilization in code execution profiling report is 0%
