Description: If you run a processor-in-the-loop (PIL)?or XCP external mode simulation with execution profiling enabled and TimerTicksPerSecond ?set to a?uint64  value , the generated profiling report can show CPU utilization?values that are fixed at 0%.

Summary: Labeler apps render a blank dialog box when you add a new scene label definition.
Description: The Define new scene label ? dialog renders a blank dialog box when you try to add a new scene label definition in the Ground Truth Labeler , Image Labeler , Video Labeler,  or the Lidar Labeler . The problem occurs after loading a saved labeler session that contains 60 or more scene label definitions. The?Label Name ,?Color, Group , and?Description? fields are missing from the dialog box, and only the?OK? and?Cancel? buttons are shown. When you click?OK  , the labeler returns the following error: Dot indexing is not supported for variables of this type.    Error in vision.internal.labeler.tool.FrameLabelDefinitionDialog/onOK (line 89)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? isValidName = this.SessionLabelSet.validateLabelName(newLabelName) ...    Error in vision.internal.uitools.OkCancelDlg>@(varargin)this.onOK(varargin{:}) (line 39)  ? ? ? ? ? ? this.OkButton = uicontrol('Parent', this.Dlg, 'Callback', @this.onOK,...  ?  Error while evaluating UIControl Callback.

Summary: Performance problems when running experiment trials with large data in parallel
Description: When your experiment uses large training or validation data sets, training in parallel takes longer than expected. This issue is caused by unnecessary transfer of data between the workers and the client. For example, with imagedatastores larger than 1 GB, the data transfer can take many minutes.

Summary: MATLAB running in a terminal window may crash when the terminal window is resized
Description: If you launch MATLAB within the terminal emulator on Mac or Linux using one of these commands: matlab -nojvm?  matlab -nodesktop?  then resizing the terminal window can crash MATLAB.??

Summary: Persistence: mps.cache.DataCache put?function fails silently when attempting to write objects larger than 512 MB to Redis cache
Description: Redis cache does not support writing objects larger then 512 MB. The?mps. cache. DataCache ?put ?function fails silently when you configure MATLAB to use Redis for data persistence and use the?put ?function to write to the Redis?cache an object larger than 512 MB. The large object is not written to the Redis cache, and MATLAB does not throw an error to indicate so. This issue does not affect MATLAB variables and objects smaller than 512 MB, which the?put ?function can successfully write to the cache.

Summary: Polyspace linking phase for C++ coding standards shows error: The process "ps_cxx_fe" received the signal 6
Description: During a Polyspace analysis, the linking phase might crash with the following error message:  Linking phase for C++ coding standards...Error: The process "ps_cxx_fe" received the signal 6. The problem occurs in trying to detect violations of these coding rules related to linking: MISRA-CPP-3-2-2 MISRA-CPP-3-2-3 MISRA-CPP-3-2-4 MISRA-CPP-7-5-4 MISRA-CPP-15-4-1 JSF-46 JSF-137 JSF-139 AUTOSAR-CPP14-2-10-4 AUTOSAR-CPP14-2-10-5  The message indicates that the phase did not complete successfully and that some rules could not be detected.?
Workaround: To avoid the error in releases R2016a and later, you can use the option -no-linkage-cpp-codingrules. The option causes this phase of the analysis to be skipped. The associated coding rules are not detected but the error message no longer appears.
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: The Model Advisor check?Check usage of floating-point expressions in Stateflow charts does not check ~= and != operators
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check usage of floating-point expressions in Stateflow charts? (ID: mathworks.maab.jc_0481) does not check ~=  and !=  operations as expected by jc_0481 MAB guideline. The check is meant to analyze equality and inequality operations (==, ~=, !=) on floating-point data types as per the MAB guideline jc_0481.?

Summary: EnableTestpoints can disable ClockRatePipelineOutputPorts
Description: Turning EnableTestpoints  on creates extra output ports which might disable ClockRatePipelineOutputPorts .

Summary: Code view does not show generated code for a Stateflow chart
Description: When you navigate into a Stateflow chart inside of a model block, the Code view fails to load the generated code.
Workaround: Navigate out of the Stateflow chart to the parent model and the Code view will refresh to show the code.

Summary: Tooltips in Model Testing Dashboard are incorrect for Japanese locale
Description: When you hover over a widget in the Model Testing Dashboard, the tooltip does not display correctly. For example, placeholders like?[[‚Ž]] appear in the tooltip.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier ignores certain justification rules in filter files
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may ignore certain justification rules for individual objective outcomes while running Test Generation or Dead Logic Detection.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 2, R2021a

Summary: MATLAB might crash when linked library blocks are opened and closed repeatedly without clearing global data objects
Description: MATLAB might crash under these conditions:Masked blocks from custom library are used in model. After compilation, if the model is closed and opened again without clearing global data objects such as LookupTable objects. Masks are opened.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model that contains a Model block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:The model contains a referenced model. An output signal of the referenced model connects to a block that has an update method, such as a Unit Delay block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: X.^0 and X.^1 with fi input can give different data types and values in simulation and code generation when the exponent is not constant.
Description: In MATLAB simulation, X.^0  and X.^1  always return solutions with the smallest possible datatype. In code generation, this only occurs if the exponent is a constant. For example, define the function myPow .  ? ? function y = myPow(x, k)?  ? ? ? y = x.^k  ? ? end  Call myPow(X, 0), ?with the input X  defined as a fi  value as shown below. >> X = fi(0.5, 1, 18, 18, 'ProductMode', 'SpecifyPrecision');  >> myPow(X, 0)  ans =?  ? ? ?1  ? ? ? ? ? DataTypeMode: Fixed-point: binary point scaling? ? ? ? ? ?? Signedness: Unsigned? ? ? ? ? ??WordLength: 1? ? ? ??FractionLength: 0? Generate code for this function with a non-constant exponent k .? >> codegen myPow -args {X, 0}  Calling the resulting mex function gives: >> myPow_mex(X, 0)  ans =?  ? ? ?1  ? ? ? ? ? DataTypeMode: Fixed-point: binary point scaling? ? ? ? ? ?? Signedness: Signed? ? ? ? ? ??WordLength: 32? ? ? ??FractionLength: 30 which has a different datatype than the simulation result.? This disagreement is not present if the exponent is a code generation constant. For example, >> codegen myPow -args {X, coder.Constant(0)}?  >> myPow_mex(X, 0)?  ans =?  ? ? ?1  ? ? ? ? ? DataTypeMode: Fixed-point: binary point scaling? ? ? ? ? ?? Signedness: Unsigned? ? ? ? ? ??WordLength: 1? ? ? ??FractionLength: 0?
Workaround: Wrap the fi power function in another function that takes in a desired output data type as an additional argument. This output data type must be constant for all code paths in the case when the exponent k is non-constant.?For example, define the function myPow2:?function y = myPow2(x, k, outType)? ? if k == 0? ? ? ? y = ones(size(x), 'like', outType);? ? elseif k == 1? ? ? ? y = cast(x, 'like', outType);? ? else?? ? ? ? y = cast(x.^k, 'like', outType);? ? ? ? end?endSimulation and code generation with myPow2 produce the same output:>> outType = fi(1, 0, 1, 0);?>> codegen myPow2 -args {X, 0, coder.Constant(outType)}?>> myPow2(X, 0, outType)ans =?? ? ?1? ? ? ? ? DataTypeMode: Fixed-point: binary point scaling? ? ? ? ? ??Signedness: Unsigned? ? ? ? ? ??WordLength: 1? ? ? ??FractionLength: 0>> myPow2_mex(X, 0, outType)ans =?? ? ?1? ? ? ? ? DataTypeMode: Fixed-point: binary point scaling? ? ? ? ? ??Signedness: Unsigned? ? ? ? ? ??WordLength: 1? ? ? ??FractionLength: 0?

Summary: Incorrect simulation results when using array of buses for in-place permutation in Stateflow
Description: Simulationresults might be incorrect when using an array of buses in a Stateflow chart and all of the followingconditions are met: The chart action language is set to MATLAB. The array of buses is aninput or output of the chart (not a local symbol). The array of buses is directly used for any in-place permutationoperation, such as an in-place swap of elements.

Summary: Model advisor S-function checks might incorrectly flag the S-functions generated by the Legacy Code Tool
Description: Model Advisor S-function checks incorrectly flag S-functions generated from the Legacy Code Tool, even if the the S-function does not have state data or multiple sample times. The checks can also incorrectly flag user-defined S-functions where SS_OPTION_CAN_BE_CALLED_CONDITIONALLY  is true.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when opened in -nodisplay mode when a data dictionary has unsaved changes
Description: When running MATLAB in?-nodisplay ?mode on a Linux or MacOS machine, MATLAB reports a warning and crashes when you call a function that changes a data dictionary without saving or discarding changes before exiting.On Windows, calling the same function using the '-batch ' option incorrectly opens up a dialog which asks whether to save changes to the data dictionary.? The warning reported on Linux machines is similar to the following: Warning: dialog is no longer supported when MATLAB is started with the  -nodisplay or -noFigureWindows option or there is no display. For more  information, see "Changes to -nodisplay and -noFigureWindows Startup Options"  in the MATLAB Release Notes. To view the release note in your system browser,  run web('www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/release-notes.html#br5ktrh-3',  '-browser').  > In warnfiguredialog (line 21)  In dialog (line 39)  In questdlg (line 166)  Warning: MATLAB Connector is not running.  > In message.subscribe  In connector.internal.webwindowmanager (line 6)  In connector.internal/webwindowmanager/instance (line 14)  In matlab.internal.webwindowmanager.get.windowList (line 117)  In matlab.internal/webwindowmanager/findAllWebwindows (line 88)  In matlab.ui.internal.dialog/DialogUtils/disableAllWindowsSafely (line 24)  In questdlg (line 430)  Warning: uiwait is no longer supported when MATLAB is started with the  -nodisplay or -noFigureWindows option or there is no display. For more  information, see "Changes to -nodisplay and -noFigureWindows Startup Options"  in the MATLAB Release Notes. To view the release note in your system browser,  run web('www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/release-notes.html#br5ktrh-3',  '-browser').  > In warnfiguredialog (line 21)  In uiwait (line 40)  In questdlg (line 431)

Summary: S-Function Builder displays an error when building an S-Function with non-double parameters
Description: When building an S-Function with the S-Function Builder block that contains a parameter with a data type other than double, the block might display an error similar to the following: Error reported by S-function 'system' in 'model/S-Function Builder': The data type and or complexity of parameter 1 does not match the information specified in the S-function Builder dialog. For non-double parameters you will need to cast them using int8, int16, int32, uint8, uint16, uint32 or boolean.
Workaround: Use the following steps to avoid the issue:Double-click the S-Function Builder block to open the parameters dialog. Manually edit the parameter values that are not double type, to cast them to the appropriate type.Click OK.For example, for an int8 parameter with a value of 2, change the parameter value to int8(2) and click OK.? Then update the diagram or simulate the model to build the S-Function.

Summary: Simulation 3D Vision Detection Generator block does not detect some actors within Unreal Engine environment.
Description: The Unreal Engine environment?reports incorrect velocities for some actors. Depending on how the environment is configured, these incorrect velocities can be extremely large, prompting the Simulation 3D Vision Detection Generator block to filter detections from those actors out based on the Maximum detectable object speed ?parameter.

Summary: The WindXYZ inport of the Vehicle Body 3DOF Longitudinal?block can become wrongly connected internally to the external forces port when External suspension is selected
Description: When the External suspension  option of the block is selected, the WindXYZ inport can be incorrectly connected internally to the external forces port Fext, causing a units propagation error to occur.
Workaround: Check the External forces checkbox, then uncheck it. This will fix the miswiring issue.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: The los?function returns false for some low-elevation sites
Description: The?los  function ?returns false when one or both of the input sites has a negative height value with respect to the WGS84 ellipsoid model. When this issue occurs, the los  plot will appear red (indicating non-line-of-sight conditions) despite no obstacle appearing along the visibility path. This bug affects the los   function in Antenna Toolbox or Communications Toolbox.
Workaround: To compute line-of-sight visibility, use the raytrace function to create a comm.Ray object. Then, query the LineOfSight property of the object. Because the?raytrace?function returns comm.Ray objects in ascending order of the number of reflections, you only need to query the first object. This code determines whether line-of-sight visibility exists between a transmitter site tx and a receiver site rx.pm = propagationModel("raytracing-image-method","MaxNumReflections",0);ray = raytrace(tx,rx,pm);status = ray{1}(1).LineOfSight;

Summary: Real-time test cases in Test Manager for models with Stateflow chart using verify keyword errors
Description: When you run real-time test cases in Test Manager and the?models or harnesses in the system-under-test contains Stateflow charts that use the verify  keyword, then this error occurs when processing data from the target: Not enough input arguments  The test case fails and no verify signals are shown in the results.

Summary: Serialization fails for objects that are serialized as structs
Description: It is possible for class definitions that overload the loadobj ? method, which generates the serialized representation of objects of that class, to represent the object as a different data type in the serialized form.? Before the final serialization (to JSON or Protobuf) takes place, the object is converted to MATLAB data array representation.? A bug in that conversion results in an exception being thrown for these types of objects.

Summary: Requirements Traceability Report for MATLAB code file mentions obsolete file type
Description: When you generate a Requirements Traceability Report for a .m ?file, the section title includes an incorrect reference to an obsolete file type. The section title is? "Requirements from PATH_TO_FILE.req" ?but should be? "Requirements from PATH_TO_FILE.slmx."  Similarly, when you generate a report for a .m  file without traceability links, the displayed message says "PATH_TO_FILE.req does not exist or has no links"  instead of "PATH_TO_FILE.slmx does not exist or has no links."

Summary: When running a web app, slider UI components do not show up
Description: When running a web app, slider UI components in the app do not show up. The rest of the app appears as expected.
Workaround: A workaround is to reload the web page hosting the web app.

Summary: solve returns an error?for some problems containing?an empty OptimizationExpression
Description: solve  can return an error when both of the following conditions are met: The OptimizationProblem  calls diff , which then returns an empty OptimizationExpression . The?OptimizationProblem ?objective or constraint derivatives are calculated using automatic differentiation. See?Automatic Differentiation in Optimization Toolbox .  The fault is in the gradient calculation for diff  in reverse mode automatic differentiation. This calculation is not robust to empty inputs.

Summary: Custom template makefile on Windows does not fully honor?'MAKEFILE_FILESEP = /'?macro
Description: On Windows, the file separator is by default the  \ ? (backslash) character. If you use a custom template makefile that defines the file separator with this macro:  'MAKEFILE_FILESEP = /'??   then, the source-to-object rules in the generated makefile can contain paths with mixed file separators. For example:?  C:\MATLAB\R2019a\toolbox\coder\rtiostream\src\utils/rtiostream_utils.c   Only the last file separator is set to the? /  (forward slash) character, which results in compilation errors: . ../llvm-9.0.1.1/WIN64/bin/clang -c -D__VXWORKS__ ....... -O2 C:\MATLAB\R2019a\toolbox\coder\rtiostream\src\utils/rtiostream_utils.c  clang: error: no such file or directory: 'C:MATLABR2019atoolboxcoderrtiostreamsrcutils/rtiostream_utils.c'  clang: error: no input files  make.exe: *** [rtiostream_utils.o] Error 1
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 3, R2021a

Summary: Load of real-time application fails for non-English locale
Description: The load of the real-time application fails for non-English locale due to incorrect timestamp on the real-time application MLDATX file.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mask popup parameter values may not apply correctly on refreshing the linked blocks
Description: Mask popup parameter values are lost when thelinked block is refreshed after making changes to the libraryblock.? The behavior is observed when the following conditions are TRUE: The popup parameter options aredynamically controlled using another parameter'scallback  The linked block is visible when making thechanges to the library block

Summary: Unable to create rosmessage for the service request and service response for custom services
Description: When custom messages are generated for services, service request and service response are not shown in rosmsg list . They are not available in the custom message registry.

Summary: Cannot create a service client for a custom ROS service
Description: When calling the rossvcclient  function for a service that uses custom messages , you will see this error: ? ? ? ?Failed to create a service client.  This happens when the ROS master needs to connect with real hardware.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB code with System objects stored in cell arrays generates HDL code with only one instance for all objects in the cell array
Description: When System objects are stored in cell arrays and step method is called by dynamically selecting one element from the cell array, the generated HDL code does not create instances for all System objects. In generated HDL code, one instance gets reused for all the elements in the cell array. For the following example MATLAB code only one RAM is created. function y = fcn(u, c)    persistent objs  if isempty(objs)  ? ? objs = cell(3,1);  ? ? for ii=1:3  ? ? ? ? objs{ii} = hdl.RAM;  ? ? end  end    y = step(objs{c}, u, uint8(1), true);
Workaround: Store System objects in individual variables and not in cell arrays. Do not assign to System object variables conditionally.function y = fcn_cell_workaround(u, c)persistent obj1 obj2 obj3if isempty(obj1)? ? obj1 = hdl.RAM;? ? obj2 = hdl.RAM;? ? obj3 = hdl.RAM;endy = coder.nullcopy(uint8(0));if c == 1? ? y = step(obj1, u, uint8(1), true);endif c == 2? ? y = step(obj2, u, uint8(1), true);endif c == 3? ? y = step(obj3, u, uint8(1), true);end
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 8, R2020b Update 4, R2021a

Summary: Upload might fail when destination project uses custom Quality Objectives definition
Description: If you apply a custom?Quality Objectives  definition to a Polyspace Access project, uploads to that project might fail. The issue occurs when the custom?Quality Objectives  definition uses non-default SQO thresholds for any of these?Run-time Checks > Global Criteria for RTE checks  entries: No systematic run-time errors (red checks) No? unreachable branches (gray checks) No non-terminating?function calls and loops  To view the Quality Objectives  definition, go to the?Configuration ?tab of the?Quality Objectives Settings ?window.

Summary: Generated custom messages with empty nested messages errors out in ROS Simulink Blocks
Description: When you add a Blank Message block and set the message type to your own generated custom messages, Simulink gives the following error: Error evaluating 'MaskDialog' callback of ROS Blank Message block (mask) 'BasicModel/ Blank Message'. Callback string is 'ros. slros. internal. block. MessageBlockMask. dispatch('entityTypeEdit', ?gcb);'?   This happens when a custom message definition has empty nested messages.?

Summary: Compiling a model containing a subsystem reference block with physical ports and unsaved changes may crash Simulink
Description: Compilation of a model containing a subsystem reference block may crash if the following conditions are met  The subsystem reference block contains physical port(s) as part of its interface? There are unsaved changes inside the subsystem reference block?

Summary: Simulation of a SLDV generated harness containing a Stateflow chart does not use JIT
Description: If you are simulating a Simulink design Verifier generated harness that contains a Stateflow chart, the simulation falls back to non-JIT model even if the Stateflow chart otherwise qualifies for JIT simulation.

Summary: ROS 2 Publish block publishes messages at a slower rate
Description: The ROS 2 Publish block sometimes publishes messages at a slower rate than the sample time of the block. When using messages with multiple nested messages in the fields, the rate of publishing is greatly reduced.

Summary: Deep Network Designer errors when exporting layers with function handle properties
Description: Deep Network Designer errors when exporting a layer or network containing a property specified using a function handle. This error also appears when analyzing or training the network in Deep Network Designer. For example, construct a layer with a function handle initializing the weights and import the layer into Deep Network Designer. layer = convolution2dLayer(5,3,'WeightsInitializer',@rand);?  deepNetworkDesigner(layer)?  Try to export the layer by clicking?Export . Deep Network Designer displays the error: Expected input to match one of these values:?  'narrow-normal', 'glorot', 'he', 'zeros', 'ones'?  The input '@rand', did not match any of the valid values.

Summary: The Simulation 3D Vision Detection Generator reports a signal read error for large numbers of road sample points
Description: The Simulation 3D Vision Detection Generator reports the following error when computing lane boundaries for a large number of distances (approximately 40 or more sample points/distances): Error while reading GroundThruth signal: Error: This statement is incomplete.

Summary: Closed data dictionary might cause unnecessary rebuild of model reference simulation or code generation target
Description: Simulating, generating code, or updating the diagram of a top model in a model hierarchy might cause an unnecessary rebuild of a model reference simulation or code generation target when all of the following conditions are true: The referenced model is configured to simulate in accelerator mode. The referenced model is?not loaded. The referenced model uses a data dictionary that is not loaded. The referenced model contains a MATLAB Function block or a Stateflow block that uses a data type defined in the data dictionary. The first build of the model hierarchy successfully completed.  The rebuild message looks like: ### Model reference simulation target (aModelRef_msf.mexa64) for model aModelRef is out of date because a MATLAB file used by a Stateflow block or a MATLAB Function block has changed.
Workaround: Load the referenced model or the linked data dictionary.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 2, R2021a

Summary: Simulation 3D Probabilistic Radar does not use?Detection range parameter to detect objects
Description: The Simulation 3D Probabilistic Radar does not use the?Detection range ?parameter value to detect objects.??Instead, if you specify a Detection Range  parameter value, the block incorrectly returns detections within the default 1 m to 150 m range.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model produces incorrect answer when one or more reusable subsystems receive 1-D input and N-D input in row-major layout
Description: Incorrect code is generated under these conditions:? Model has reusable subsystem that has Function packaging set to Auto or Reusable function. Model has multiple copies of the reusable subsystem. One or more of these subsystems receive 1-D signals. One or more of these subsystems receive N-D signals configured in row-major array layout.

Summary: writePort?function errors with negative values on AXI4 Slave interface
Description: When writing negative values to an AXI4 Slave interface on the?fpga ?hardware object using?writePort , the following error message may appear: Error using matlabshared.libiio.aximm.base/validateData (line 202)  Expected input to be an array with all of the values <= 2.14748e+09.  If you have a model that has signed ports mapped to an AXI4 or AXI4-Lite interface and enable the option "Generate MATLAB software interface script " during IP Core generation, the generated script may have this issue. The error may be triggered when running a?writePort ?command in the generated script with negative values.

Summary: Coverage might return incorrect information when run on a Stateflow chart that contains an enumeration of a custom base type
Description: Coverage results might be incorrect for Stateflow charts for which all of the following conditions are true:? The chart contains a graphical, MATLAB, or Simulink function At least one input argument to the function is defined as an enumeration The chart passes an integer or other base type when calling the function

Summary: The command window password prompt for Matlab Job Scheduler does not correctly wait for input
Description: When using a Matlab Job Scheduler configured with security level greater than zero, certain operations require the user to interact with a password prompt to confirm their identity. If the MATLAB client is running in nodisplay mode, this prompt switches from an interactive window to a running in the command window. For some operations, this command window prompt does not correctly wait for the user input. This means the prompt cannot be correctly completed, and instead the user input is executed as a command in the MATLAB console.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Simulink model containing MATLAB System block that uses sparse matrices is recompiled during every simulation
Description: Suppose that your Simulink model contains a MATLAB System block. If this block uses certain functions (for example, reshape ) on sparse matrices, the model recompiles every time you simulate the model.
Workaround: Use any of these workarounds:Isolate the problematic MATLAB System block in its own model and use model reference to refer to that model. Use rapid accelerator simulation mode and disable incremental code generation check. Every time you make a change that is related to the MATLAB System block, you must manually recompile the model that contains this block.?Use a MATLAB Function block instead of a MATLAB System block. You still can use MATLAB System block classes in a MATLAB Function block:?function y = my_matlab_function_block_top_level_function(...)    if isempty(p)        p = MySystemBlock(...);        p.setup(...);    endy = p.step();

Summary: MATLAB might crash with an assertion message during code generation of program containing concatenation of variable-size data
Description: When generating code for a MATLAB program containg concatenation of variable-size arrays, MATLAB might crash with this message: Assertion `In check_inoutdata_sanity: Assertion failed: echild->outData() == aNode' failed.  A stack trace similar to the following occurs with the error message  [  6] client_assertion_failed at matlab\src\cg_ir\util_prim\cg_assert.cpp:49[  7] CG::CfgClassic::cg_cfg_delete_dead_edges_and_nodes at matlab\src\cg_ir\classic\cg_cfg.cpp:537[  8] cg_cfg_make_compact at matlab\src\cg_ir\classic\cg_cfg.cpp:1188 [ 10] CG::transform::PeepholeCore::processFcn at matlab\src\cgir_xform\xform\peepholecore.cpp:41[ 11] CG::FcnTransform::apply at matlab\src\cgir_support\xform\fcntransform.cpp:28[ 12] CG::FcnTransform::processScopeWithService at matlab\src\cgir_support\xform\fcntransform.cpp:38[ 13] CG::ScopeTransform::apply at matlab\src\cgir_support\xform\scopetransform.cpp:36

Summary: writePort?function of?fpga hardware object errors with vector data on AXI4 Slave interface
Description: When writing vector data to an AXI4 Slave interface on the fpga  hardware object using writePort , the following error message may appear: Error using hdlshared.internal.VectorAddressUtils.validateStartAddrForBlock (line 125)  Address for vector port must start from the boundary of an address block (integer multiple of a power of two number), for example: 'x"FFFFC"'.  If you have a model that has vector ports mapped to an AXI4 or AXI4-Lite interface and enable the option "Generate MATLAB software interface script" during IP Core generation, the generated script may have this issue. The error may be triggered when running a writePort  command in the generated script with vector data.

Summary: MATLAB might crash for models with Code Mappings
Description: MATLAB might crash while copying blocks or creating a test harness for models with Code Mappings for signals, Data Store Memory blocks, or blocks with states. The stack trace generated by MATLAB includes these lines:? [ 14] 0x00007ffb1dbb6f1b?C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020b\bin\win64\codemapping_c.dll+00945947?coderdictionary::InternalDataMapping::InternalDataMapping  [ 15] 0x00007ffb1dc01a05?C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020b\bin\win64\codemapping_c.dll+01251845?coderdictionary::StateMapping::StateMapping  [ 16] 0x00007ffb1db181b1?C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020b\bin\win64\codemapping_c.dll+00295345?coderdictionary::StateMapping::Copy  [ 17] 0x00007ffb3770988d?C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020b\bin\win64\codermapping_core.dll+00104589?BlockToTargetMapInfo::BlockToTargetMapInfo

Summary: Model Explorer Block Parameters section shows an error when a Simscape Multibody Block is selected
Description: When a Simscape Multibody block is selected in Simulink Model Explorer, the Block Parameters pane shows the following error message: Error Id: PMDialogs:DynDlgSource:internalGetPmSchema This method must not be called from the base class. ??Only the subclass's overriden version can be invoked Failed to create dialog schema due to errors above. ?Contact technical support.

Summary: Code generation assertion when using matrix multiply operation in MATLAB Function Block with variable sized matrices
Description: Code generation asserts when the model has:MATLAB Function Block with variable sized matrix of structs passed as arguments. MATLAB Function block uses matrix multiply operator on the function arguments which are created as variable sized matrices.
Workaround: MATLAB Function Block with functions accepting matrix arguments, should use for-loop to apply matrix multiply operation on individual elements of the matrix.

Summary: ?rosgenmsg or ros2genmsg?function throws confusing error when message type definition is missing.?
Description: The?rosgenmsg ?or?ros2genmsg ?function?throws a confusing error when generating a custom message definition with: 1.? A misspelled message name in the .msg file. 2.? ?Missing .msg files.  ?The error returned is: ? ? Specified message package does not exist. Check the message package specified.?

Summary: Code generation might crash when accessing property of a class if that class is assigned as initial value of another class's property
Description: Code generation might crash if all   of these conditions are true:? In your MATLAB function, you create an instance of a class A?  A property of class  A ?is assigned an initial value that is an instance of another class  B  Class  B ?has a property that is not? assigned anywhere except in the classdef  declaration Your MATLAB function accesses this property of class  B   For example, this issue occurs when you try to generate code for the MATLAB function xMcosInitVal :? function result = xMcosInitValobj = McosInitVal;result = obj.property.property;  % obj.property has an initial value of "SimpleClass"end classdef McosInitVal    properties        property = SimpleClass    endend classdef SimpleClass    properties        property = 42;    endend This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function Blocks or MATLAB System Blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.

Summary: MATLAB may crash when opening a System Composer model saved in R2020a or R2020b
Description: Opening a System Composer model saved in R2020a or R2020b, may cause an error to appear with the message "Error using open (line 146) UUID Conflict".? After closing the error dialog and reopening the model, MATLAB crashes.? This issue occurs because the saved System Composer model has become corrupted.??
Workaround: Download the attached zip file 'Fix_2313177.zip' and extract the p-coded patch 'fixSystemComposerModel_UUID_20a.p'. Run the following lines of code to fix a corrupted System Composer model:?>> modelName = 'myBrokenModel';>>?fixSystemComposerModel_UUIDConflict_20a(modelName); % This will add a model myBrokenModel_tmw_fixed.slx to the working directory??

Description: HDL code generation for a Stateflow chart might generate an assertion when the following conditions are met: MATLAB  is set as the Action Language  in the Chart Properties dialog box The chart contains floating point operations The Floating Point IP Library  in the HDL Code Generation > Floating Point  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box is set to Native Floating Point??   The assertion looks like the following:In cast: Assertion failed: isa(aValue)   [? 0] ../matlab/bin/glnxa64/builtins/sl_main/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcg_ir.so+13427972 _ZN2CG11DoubleValue4castEPKNS_5ValueE+00000086 [? 1] ../matlab/bin/glnxa64/simulink_plugins/sl_pir_cpp/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_hdl.so+25912710  [? 2] ../matlab/bin/glnxa64/simulink_plugins/sl_pir_cpp/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_hdl.so+25919655  [? 3] ../matlab/bin/glnxa64/simulink_plugins/sl_pir_cpp/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_hdl.so+25921796  [? 4] ../matlab/bin/glnxa64/simulink_plugins/sl_pir_cpp/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_hdl.so+20326342  [? 5] ../matlab/bin/glnxa64/simulink_plugins/sl_pir_cpp/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_hdl.so+20590166?

Summary: Crash might occur when a function in MATLAB Compiler SDK Python package is called repeatedly
Description: If a function in a MATLAB Compiler SDK Python package is called repeatedly in a tight loop for thousands of iterations, a crash might eventually occur.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Subsystem configured with?Treat as atomic unit?containing bus element ports generates internal assertion during HDL code generation
Description: HDL Coder generates the following cryptic error during code generation when Subsystem containing bus element port is set with the?Treat as atomic unit  parameter: Error using slhdlcoder.HDLCoder/pirSanityCheck (line 39)  Internal Error: Could not connect the blocks in the model. Look for unconnected blocks or other warnings or errors and  correct them to continue.
Workaround: Select the Subsystem block property as Treat as grouped when propagating variant conditions?and then generate HDL code.

Summary: Real-time kernel fails to install on macOS Big Sur
Description: The Simulink Desktop Real-time kernel fails to install on an Intel-based macOS Big Sur (11.0) computer. The installation ends with an error message and the real-time kernel may not be installed.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated C/C++ code might miss property validation code if property validator is specified in an abstract property in a base class
Description: When generating C/C++ code from MATLAB code that uses the MATLAB class property validation feature, the generated code might have incorrect assignments to properties that use property validation.  This issue occurs if all  the following conditions are true: The property is declared as an abstract property in a base class? The validation information is supplied in the base class The property is defined in a derived class?  In such situations, the generated C/C++ code ignores the property validation rules. For example, define the MATLAB classes Base  and Derived  and the function main  in their respective files: Base.m:?  classdef Base    properties(Abstract)        Foo(1,1) {mustBeNumeric, mustBeFinite}     end ?...?? Derived.m:  classdef Derived < Base    properties        Foo = 0.2    endend main.m:  function mainx = Derived;x.Foo = Inf;end Execution of the function main  in MATLAB produces an error because the value assigned to the property Foo  is Inf , but the validation rule requires the value to be finite. Code generation succeeds and the generated code does not check for this validation rule. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB Functions in Stateflow.?

Summary: find_mdlrefs function with @Simulink.match.codeCompileVariants filter returns incomplete results for expression or sim/codegen switching mode
Description: For model reference hierarchy containing variant subsystem with variant control mode in expression or sim codegen switching, invoking find_mdlrefs  with MatchFilter as @Simulink.match.codeCompileVariants  may return incomplete list of referenced models.? Consider a top model containing a hierarchy of referenced models, where one of the referenced models has a variant subsystem with model block choices. The variant subsystem variant control mode is expression  or sim codegen switching . After the top model has been compiled for codegen using feval(topModel, [],[],[], 'compileForCodegen') , the post-compile variants match filters can be used with find_mdlrefs   or find_system  functions as below.  find_mdlrefs(topModel, 'MatchFilter', @Simulink.match.codeCompileVariants)  find_system(topModel, ..., 'MatchFilter', @Simulink.match.codeCompileVariants, 'BlockType', 'ModelReference')   These above calls can return an incomplete set of referenced models or blocks for the above two calls respectively.?

Summary: The cumulative distribution function (CDF) calibration curves are incorrect in the example "Physical Layer Abstraction for System-Level Simulation"
Description: The large-scale path loss calculation is incorrect for the generated plots "Calibration: CDF of SNIR per?subcarrier" and "Calibration: CDF of effective SINR per reception," causing the calibration curves to not match their references.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect outputs from Simulink based state in discrete-time Stateflow charts
Description: Outputs from blocks in a Simulink based state may be delayed by one step if all of the following conditions are true: The Stateflow chart has a discrete sample time The Simulink based state contains a block that does not require direct feedthrough, that is,?outputs in a given time step depend only on the state of the block At least one input of the chart is mapped to the input of the Simulink based state

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model?containing a bus data type that is used across nested reusable atomic subsystems
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions:?? A bus data type is specified on the input and output signals of a nested reusable atomic subsystem. In the Configuration Parameter dialog box, the?Eliminate superfluous local variables (Expression folding) ?parameter is selected.?  In the generated code, one or more temporary local variables might be incorrectly eliminated.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameter dialog box, clear the?Eliminate superfluous local variables (Expression folding)?parameter.

Summary: The architecture model has become inconsistent?error post renaming a component in a System Composer model
Description: The error The architecture model has become inconsistent. ?Contact The MathWorks for assistance ? reported when a component is renamed within a reference component in a System Composer model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur when comparing a signed integer of negative value with unsigned enum if the CastingMode parameter is set to 'Standards'
Description: Incorrect results might be generated from MATLAB code in which a signed integer of negative value is compared using any of > ?(greater than), <  (less than), <=  (less than or equal to), >=  (greater than or equal to) with an unsigned enum when the CastingMode  parameter is set to 'Standards ' In the below example, a signed integer is compared with an unsigned enum Sample MATLAB code:  classdef SampleEnumClass < uint8  ? ? enumeration    ? ? ? ? val(23)    ? ? end    ? ? methods (Static)    ? ? ? ? function retVal = addClassNameToEnumNames    ? ? ? ? ? ? retVal = true;    ? ? ? ? end    ? ? end    end?    function [out1] = func_swa(in1)    ? ? out1 = (in1) > (SampleEnumClass.val) % int16 > uint8(enum)    end?  In the generated C code,? an incorrect unsigned int  cast is added which gives unexpected results when the integer is negative valued. Generated C Code:?  boolean_T func_swa(int in1)  {  ? return (unsigned int)(unsigned int)in1 > (unsigned int)SampleEnumClass_val; // incorrect (unsigned int) cast over in1  ? /*? int16(negative) > uint8(enum) */  }?
Workaround: Do not set the CastingMode property to 'Standards'

Description: A numeric matrix created using the mxArray API (C/C++) or mwArray API (C++, Java, .NET) unexpectedly displays the elements column-wise. Column 1  ????1   ????2   ????3  ?Column 2  ????4   ????5   ????6  ?Column 3  ????7   ????8   ? ? ?9?

Summary: Help browser is unresponsive after search
Description: When a search is performed in the Help browser for a term that does not exist in the documentation, the Help browser becomes unresponsive.
Workaround: Close and reopen the Help browser.

Summary: In charts that use MATLAB as the action language, empty initial values may not be treated as default values
Description: During simulation of charts that use MATLAB as the?action language, empty initial values for data are not treated as default values in some cases. The following cases are known to be affected: The chart is contained in a reset subsystem. When the subsystem is reset, local and output data without a specified?initial value are not reset. State-parented data without a specified?initial value are not reinitialized when the state becomes active.
Workaround: Enter default initial values for data with empty initial value fields. For example, for data of type double, enter a default value of 0.?

Summary: Selecting Calibrate Maps?to virtually calibrate block maps with Model-Based Calibration Toolbox results in callback errors
Description: Selecting the block?Calibrate Maps? option to virtually calibrate the maps with Model-Based Calibration Toolbox can result in callback errors. The calibration option is available with these blocks: Mapped CI Engine Mapped SI Engine Mapped Motor  If you use the?kinematics and compliance virtual test laboratory reference application, you may encounter an error when the software invokes the Model-Based Calibration Toolbox.
Workaround: Before using Vehicle Dynamics Blockset to virtually calibrate the block maps:At the MATLAB command-line, type mbcmodel?to open the Model-Based Calibration Toolbox Model Browser.?If prompted, fill in the User Information dialog.Close the Model Browser.

Summary: Non-English characters in ASAP2 (A2L) file are mangled after address replacement
Description: If you specify an address replacement for an ASAP2 (A2L) file using the rtw.asap2SetAddres  function with ASAP2Fil e and InfoFile  as inputs, the non-English characters are mangled and incorrectly displayed in the A2l file as a result.?

Summary: Code inspection fails for a model containing a MATLAB Function block that accesses a nested bus
Description: A compatible model containing a MATLAB Function block fails code inspection if the MATLAB Function block accesses a nested bus.

Summary: Simulation fails due to an Embedded Coder Dictionary error
Description: Simulating a model may fail and return an error like the following: Data dictionaries (.sldd files) 'one.sldd' and 'two.sldd' contain different Embedded Coder dictionaries and are in the same data dictionary hierarchy. A data dictionary hierarchy can contain only one coder dictionary.?
Workaround: Use the coder.dictionary.move?function to move a coder dictionary from one data dictionary to the other, or use the coder.dictionary.remove function to delete a coder dictionary from one of the data dictionaries.

Summary: Code inspection fails for a model containing a partially assigned nested bus that passes to an if-else decision logic or enabled subsystem
Description: A model containing a partially assigned nested bus fails code inspection if the bus passes to an if-else decision logic or an enabled subsystem.

Summary: MATLAB?does not recognize the normalization insensitivity of MacOS11 (Big Sur) file system
Description: The macOS file system type Id attribute has updated to anew value in MacOS11 (Big Sur) which can cause incorrect behavior in MATLAB whenused with file names containing canonically equivalent characters, such as the character ¨ą , which is canonically equivalent to the sequence u  andˇ§ . MATLAB treats the MacOS11 (Big Sur) volume as Normalized Decompose, that is, characters are decomposed by canonical equivalence.

Summary: Error during code update when referenced models are configured to be never rebuilt?and system target file is?ert.tlc or?slrt.tlc
Description: After generating and building code for a referenced model, if you set the configuration parameters Rebuild  to Never  and Never rebuild diagnostic  to Error if rebuild required and i f the configuration parameter System target file  is set to?ert.tlc  or slrt.tlc , then the next simulation produces this error: The model '<model name> ' has the Rebuild parameter on the Model Referencing pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box set to Never. However, the following referenced models are out-of-date for the following reasons. Consider resetting the Rebuild parameter to a value other than Never.  Caused by:  Model reference code generation target (<referenced model >.c) for model <referenced model >?must be recompiled because either <referenced model >.c has changed since the last build, or it has never been compiled.
