Summary: C++ coding rules checking stop at linking phase with message The process "ps_cxx_fe" received the signal 11
Description: When the C++ coding rules are computed with the option -misra-cpp , -jsf-coding-rules , or -autosar-cpp14 , in some cases, analysis might stop with the message: Global compilation phase...Linking phase for C++ coding standards...Error: The process "ps_cxx_fe" received the signal 11.Received signal 'SIGSEGV'. Exiting... The issue occurs because of a bug in the C++ linker used internally for linking translation units.?
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the option -no-linkage-cpp-codingrules, but be aware that in this case some coding rules are lost. The computation of these coding rules involve more than one translation unit. For instance, MISRA C++ rule 3-2-3 suggests that types used in multiple translation units shall be declared only once (in a header file preferrably). The checker for this rule looks for multiple declarations in separate translation units. You lose this and similar checkers involving multiple translation units when using the option??-no-linkage-cpp-codingrules.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Direct Lookup Table(n-D) block with complex floating point table data might generate incorrect HDL code
Description: The generated HDL code for a Simulink model might be incorrect if all  these conditions are true: The model contains a Direct Lookup Table(n-D) block The values in the?Table data ?block parameter?are complex floating point types. Examples of floating point types include single, double, and half-precision types.   During verification of the generated code, the incorrect HDL code might cause simulation failure in ModelSim.

Summary: Code generation may error with a 'Unrecognized method, property, or field 'Identifier' for class 'RTW.DataImplementation' message
Description: For a model containing a variant block and a message block that connects to a Root Inport or Root Outport block, code generation might error with this message: 'Unrecognized method,  property,  or field 'Identifier' for class 'RTW. DataImplementation'?

Summary: NCO HDL Optimized HDL simulation doesn't match Simulink simulation when reset is applied at reset input port
Description: This problem occurs with an NCO HDL Optimized block configured to have a reset input port and a phase increment input port.?? When you simulate the generated HDL code, the behavior when the reset signal is applied in the HDL simulation doesn't match the Simulink simulation.? The same problem also occurs with the dsp.HDLNCO System object when it is configured to use a reset input argument and a phase increment input argument.??

Summary: Incorrect simulation results when referencing a function-call model in accelerator mode
Description: Simulation results might be incorrect when all of these conditions apply: The model hierarchy contains at least three levels of models. The top model of the model hierarchy simulates in normal mode. A referenced model contains a Model block that has a function-call or trigger port and a triggered sample time type. That Model block's?Simulation mode  is Accelerator . The number of sample times varies among the models in that Model block's path. For example, the top model might have three discrete sample times, and the model that references that Model block might have two discrete sample times.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a

Summary: Code generation report creation fails when using rtwreport function
Description: When you use the rtwreport  function to create a code generation report for a model with?Simulink?Report Generator, the report generation fails and displays this error: Error running reportCannot find an exact (case-sensitive) match for 'Path'.

Summary: Training reinforcement learning agents using parallel computing might crash MATLAB
Description: MATLAB can crash while training a reinforcement learning agent using parallel computing. The crash is due to a communication race condition between the client and worker processes. The crash is more frequent for larger parallel pools or if the environment has a short simulation time.? On Windows, the report for this crash can contain a line similar to the following: [? 2] 0x00007ffa47bd675b? bin\win64\pgo\libmwlxeindexing.dll+00026459 MathWorks::lxe::IIndexingContext::HasSubsrefForParen+00000075?   On Linux, the crash report can contain a line similar to the following: [? 1] 0x00007fd688d8160c /usr/local/MATLAB/R2020b/bin/glnxa64/libmwlxeindexing.so+00972300 _ZNK9MathWorks3lxe16IIndexingContext18HasSubsrefForParenEPK11mxArray_tag+00000060?
Workaround: To bypass the communication race condition for PG, DQN, DDPG, TD3, SAC, and PPO agents, use synchronous parallel training and configure the workers to wait until the end of the episode before sending data to the host. To do so, configure your?rlTrainingOptions?object as shown in the following code:trainOptions = rlTrainingOptions;trainOptions.UseParallel = true;trainOptions.ParallelizationOptions.Mode = "sync";trainOptions.ParallelizationOptions.StepsUntilDataIsSent = -1;Using?StepsUntilDataIsSent = -1?is not supported for AC agents. To avoid a communication race condition for these agents, consider using a PPO agent with experience-based parallel training or a PG agent with gradient-based parallel training.

Summary: GPU Code generation for the function pcsegdist errors out for input point-clouds containing more than 1000 points
Description: GPU Coder fails to generate CUDA code for pcsegdist  function when the input point-cloud contains more than 1000 points. GPU Coder throws the following error message for this failure: ??? Maximum variable size allowed by the program is exceeded

Summary: MATLAB might crash while using a test harness for a model that has Code Mappings
Description: Closing a subsystem test harness may cause MATLAB to crash if the parent model has Code Mappings for signals, Data Store Memory blocks or blocks with states. The stack trace generated by MATLAB includes these lines:?? [? 0] 0x00007ffc42a94636?C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020b\bin\win64\SimulinkBlock.dll+00673334 SLBlock::getFullPath+00000006  [? 1] 0x00007ffc14f84821?C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020b\bin\win64\codemapping_c.dll+01984545 coderdictionary::MappingUtils::remapOutport+00254641  [? 2] 0x00007ffc14f8d303?C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020b\bin\win64\codemapping_c.dll+02020099 coderdictionary::MappingUtils::remapOutport+00290195  [? 3] 0x00007ffc14f8f9f3?C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020b\bin\win64\codemapping_c.dll+02030067 coderdictionary::MappingUtils::remapOutport+00300163

Summary: MATLAB crashes when extending manually generated test cases using Simulink Design Verifier
Description: Simulink Design Verifier supports the extension of manually generated test cases?to aid in test generation using a .mat file.However, a crash is observed when: TestCases.paramValues  field is missing in the given .mat file.?? TestCases.paramValues = [];   The crash occurs with an error log shown below:? Abnormal termination:  Access violation  Snippet from Stack Trace (from fault): [? 0] 0x00000000fe13ab89 matrix::detail::noninlined::mx_array_api::mxIsEmpty at b:\matlab\foundation\matrix\src\matrix\array.cpp:793  [? 1] 0x00000002a8cc5cf2 DV::MX::CGTestCaseFromMx::initParamsFromMxArray at b:\matlab\toolbox\sldv\src\sldv\analyzer\mxresults.cpp:721  [? 2] 0x00000002a8cbf9ef DV::MX::ResultsFromMx::addResults at b:\matlab\toolbox\sldv\src\sldv\analyzer\mxresults.cpp:1578  [? 3] 0x00000002a8cbc57f DV::ExternalLoader::Analyzer::load at b:\matlab\toolbox\sldv\src\sldv\analyzer\loadexternaldataanalyzer.cpp:50  [? 4] 0x00000002a8c79c8c DV::DvInternal::loadTestCases at b:\matlab\toolbox\sldv\src\sldv\analyzer\dvinternal.cpp:1309  [? 5] 0x00000002a8eed489 TestComponent::loadTestCases at b:\matlab\toolbox\sldv\src\sldv\udd\methods\avt_testcomponent_methods.cpp:84  [? 6] 0x00000002a8ee0e3f TestComponentloadTestCasesMI::invokeMethod at b:\matlab\toolbox\sldv\src\sldv\udd\autogen\avt_testcomponent.cpp:247  [? 7] 0x000000001f355a36 UDMethodInfo::invokeMethodE at b:\matlab\src\udd\udd_util\udmethodinfo.cpp:103
Fixed In: R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: dateshift returns the wrong answer for the nearest end of a time unit
Description: The calculation of the nearest end of a time unit in dateshift  is incorrect, adding an extra increment to the result. For example, this call to dateshift  returns a datetime  value for the end of September 2019 (30-Sep-2019 )?when it should return the end of August 2019 (31-Aug-2019 ). >> dateshift(datetime(2019, 8, 17),'end','month','nearest')ans =   datetime   30-Sep-2019 Furthermore, for units of 'week' , 'month' , 'quarter' , and 'year' , the nearest value is determined based on the beginning of the week, month, quarter, or year rather than on the end of the specified time unit. This issue has an impact on results within a day of the midpoint of the specified time unit.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Getter function for bus arrays, which return by value, used as a pointer return in the algorithm code
Description: The return type of getter function in the algorithm code is treated as pointer in the algorithm code for bus arrays in which the bus has arrays as fields. The invalid get function usage appears when an element of the field array is read directly within algorithm code.

Summary: Simulink may crash during simulation for variant subsystem blocks with?true?and?false?as variant control
Description: Model containing variant subsystem with? true? and? false? as variant control may crash during simulation when all the below conditions are met:  Variant subsystem blocks has variant activation set to?code compile   Variant subsystem has?Specify output when source is unconnected ?set for the output port  Variant subsystem should have on choice set to  true  and rest all choices to  false   Snippet from the stack trace:  [[? 0] 0x00007efaf729d758?D:/ProgramFiles/MATLAB/R2020b/bin/glnxa64/builtins/sl_main/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwsl_compile.so+14153560 [? 1] 0x00007efaf729895a?D:/ProgramFiles/MATLAB/R2020b/bin/glnxa64/builtins/sl_main/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwsl_compile.so+14133594  [? 2] 0x00007efaf72976fe?D:/ProgramFiles/MATLAB/R2020b/bin/glnxa64/builtins/sl_main/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwsl_compile.so+14128894  [? 3] 0x00007efaf7297730?D:/ProgramFiles/MATLAB/R2020b/bin/glnxa64/builtins/sl_main/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwsl_compile.so+14128944  [? 4] 0x00007efaf724e31d?D:/ProgramFiles/MATLAB/R2020b/bin/glnxa64/builtins/sl_main/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwsl_compile.so+13828893 _ZN14VariantCompile27ConfigureVariantsForCompileEv+00000045

Summary: MATLAB might crash if an error occurs when simulating models that contain Stateflow charts or S-Function blocks
Description: If an error occurs during initialization of a model containing Stateflow charts or S-Function blocks, the error might cause MATLAB to crash.The crash report may contain output similar to this: +09050739 slAccRegPrmChangeFcn+00041411  +02241817 SLExecBD::modelCleanupRuntimeResources+00000105

Summary: Multiply-Accumulate block in Serial architecture generates internal assertion during HDL code generation when input bit width exceeds 63 bits
Description: HDL Coder generates the following cryptic error during code generation when Multiply-Accumulate block is configured with inputs greater than 63  bits in Serial  architecture: Error Assertion failed: b:\ matlab\ src\ cgir_hdl\ emlinterface\ emlinterfaceforhdl. cpp:52:Unsupported signal type detected.?
Workaround: Consider configuring the block to use input bit widths less than or equal to 63 bits for HDL code generation.

Summary: Additional interfaces are applied on ports in error, when a model is imported using systemcomposer.importModel() API.
Description: When customer imports a model into System Composer using the importModel() API, additional interfaces are applied on ports when a port is connected to another port which has an interface applied to it.

Summary: Code generation might fail with the assertion message?Assertion failed: cdr_node_returns_mx_array(rhs)
Description: Code generation might fail with an assertion if your MATLAB code satisfies both  these conditions: Uses a global variable anywhere in the code Passes the output of an extrinsic function to another extrinsic function directly (that is, not through an intermediate variable)  The assertion is: Assertion at dom_core/lowering/TransformMatlabAccess.cpp line 2133:  In transform_ml_assign: Assertion failed: cdr_node_returns_mx_array(rhs)??   Example MATLAB code that is affected by this issue: % Defined in file: foo.mfunction foolocal1;local2;function local1global x;x = x + 1;function local2coder.extrinsic('baz');disp(baz);% Defined in file: baz.mfunction y = bazy = 1;end

Summary: Requesting quarterly data with end of quarter start date causes data mismatch
Description: If you request quarterly data and specify a start date that falls on the last date of the quarter, the returned data is misaligned for the expected date range. For example: % Store connection to ASREPGDPc = haver('R:\_appl\Haver\DATA\ASREPGDP.DAT');% Store the fetch parametersseriesID = 'GDP7812';startDate = '31-Mar-1978';endDate = '31-Dec-1978';% Download the data with and without a start datedataWithStart = fetch(c,seriesID,startDate,endDate); In this case, the data is shifted one date into the future. The fetch  function returns data for the date 31-March-1978 in the record of the date 31-Jun-1978. The function shifts each subsequent data point in the same way.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Models with booleans in nested bus inputs exported as Tool-coupling FMUs might send incorrect data to remote MATLAB sessions
Description: When a model is exported as a tool-coupling FMU, it might send incorrect data to the remote session during simulation if the following conditions are satisfied: The FMU block accepts as its input a bus signal. The input bus signal is a nested bus that contains a boolean and a sub-bus.  For example, if an FMU has as input the following signals in a non-virtual input bus: --Subbus----double----int--boolean--double--double--double. . . The number after the boolean received by the remote MATLAB session differs from the number sent by the originating MATLAB session.??

Summary: Web-based UI controls (Simulink Toolstrip, Stateflow Symbols pane, Interface Editor, etc.) might appear blank
Description: Web-based controls on Simulink, such as Simulink Toolstrip, Stateflow Symbols pane, System Composer Interface Editor, might appear blank after launching Simulink or in an active session on Simulink. This might be due to a third-party application, maybe an anti-virus, killing some essential Simulink sub process responsible for managing the rendering of web-based widgets.
Workaround: If the problem is encountered in a running MATLAB session, save the model using?Control+S?and relaunch MATLAB with command line option -cefdisablegpu.If the problem occurs frequently, use the command option -cefdisablegpu?on every MATLAB launch.

Summary: Error running clibgen.generateLibraryDefinition or clibgen.buildInterface with Visual Studio 2019 C++ compiler updates 16.4.x and 16.7.x
Description: If you run clibgen.generateLibraryDefinition  or clibgen.buildInterface  with certain updates (16.4.x or 16.7.x) of VS 2019 C++ compiler, then you might get errors such as the following errors. Errors parsing header file.C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2019\Professional\VC\Tools\MSVC\14.27.29110\include\utility:137: expected an identifier.C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2019\Professional\VC\Tools\MSVC\14.27.29110\include\utility:136: not a valid member class or functiontemplate declaration.C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2019\Professional\VC\Tools\MSVC\14.27.29110\include\utility:136: "explicit" is not allowed.C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2019\Professional\VC\Tools\MSVC\14.27.29110\include\utility:138: expected a ";".C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2019\Professional\VC\Tools\MSVC\14.27.29110\include\utility:218: expected an identifier.C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2019\Professional\VC\Tools\MSVC\14.27.29110\include\utility:218: expected a ")".C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2019\Professional\VC\Tools\MSVC\14.27.29110\include\utility:218: not a valid member class or functiontemplate declaration.C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2019\Professional\VC\Tools\MSVC\14.27.29110\include\utility:218: "explicit" is not allowed.C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2019\Professional\VC\Tools\MSVC\14.27.29110\include\utility:218: expected a ";". These errors do not occur with VS 2019 initial version (16.0).

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When motorParam structure is present in workspace, Motor Control Blockset example models use RMS value of motor nominal current instead of peak value to compute base value (per-unit).
Description: Running the motor parameter estimation models available in Motor Control Blockset creates the motorParam  variable structure in the workspace. For computing the base values (per-unit), Motor Control Blockset examples convert the motor nominal current value available in the motorParam  structure into an RMS value. However, base value calculations need peak value of the motor nominal current.
Workaround: In the file mcb_SetPMSMMotorParameters.m,replace the expressionpmsm.I_rated= motorParam.nomCurrent / sqrt(2); with pmsm.I_rated= motorParam.nomCurrent;

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model that contains a Simulink Function block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:The model contains a Simulink Function block. The Simulink Function block has more than one caller block such as a Stateflow chart, a Function Caller block, a Function-Call Generator block, and so on. The caller blocks are on different levels of the model hierarchy. The model configuration parameter?Optimize block operation order in the generated code  (OptimizeBlockOrder )?is set to?Improved code execution speed.
Workaround: Set the model configuration parameter?Optimize block operation order in the generated code?(OptimizeBlockOrder) to Off.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for?a model that contains a?Unit Delay block connected to Stateflow chart output
Description: The generated code might contain incorrect initialization of a Unit Delay block state for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains a Stateflow chart. A Unit Delay block has an input that directly connects to the Stateflow chart output.? The Unit Delay block has a nonzero initialization value.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when executing test file in SIL mode
Description: If you execute a test file in software-in-the-loop (SIL) mode and your Simulink model contains Stateflow charts, a crash can occur. The crash generates a stack trace similar to: [0] 0x00007ffd53f97b12 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\libcovrt.dll+00162578 covrtSimulinkSetTopModelInterface+00011410[1] 0x00007ffd53f9d6a0 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\libcovrt.dll+00186016 covrtDeleteStateflowInstanceData+00000048
Workaround: Call cvexit in between test cases. For example, you can put a call to cvexit in the test suite cleanup callback in the Simulink Test Manager:Open the test fileOn the left pane, click Test BrowserClick Test Suite: <Your test suite name>Expand CALLBACK In the CLEANUP section, enter cvexitClick Save

Summary: The Capacity vector and Head vector?parameters?in the Centrifugal Pump (TL) block cannot be set to Run-time
Description: In the Centrifugal Pump (TL) block, the?Capacity vector ?and?Head vector? parameters?do not currently support run-time capabilities. However, the block dialog incorrectly provides the option to choose between the?Compile-time ?or?Run-time ?settings. Selecting?Run-time ?and simulating the model results in a compilation error: Function 'fluids_functions. polynomial_fit' must be an unprotected MATLAB function. ?In fluids. thermal_liquid. pumps_motors. centrifugal_pump
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: JMAAB check Prohibited use of implicit type casting in Stateflow reports issue when comparing same enumeration type
Description: The Model Advisor check Prohibited use of implicit type casting in Stateflow  (Check ID: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0802 ) reports an issue when comparing enumeration types without class names.
Workaround: When using enumeration type use it with complete class name. E.g. className.EnumType

Summary: Error during model generation when generating HDL code for a model with large number of test points
Description: HDLCoder generates code for models that have test point signals and connects them as output ports when?EnableTestPoints? property ?is set to?on . These test point signals are routed to the top of the DUT and are connected to a Simulink scope for a rate in the testbench outside the DUT during model generation. However, if the number of test points exceeds a large value such as 96, a cryptic error is generated as Simulink scope does not allow more than 96 input ports.  Invalid setting for property 'NumInputPorts' in 'gm_testpoint/TestpointScope'.??
Workaround: Limit the number of test points for every rate to be less than or equal to 96.

Summary: Calling a private superclass method from a property access method using a subclass instance might not succeed
Description: When a class property has an access method that calls a private method of that same class, accessing the property from an instance of a subclass might cause unexpected results. If a function on the path has the same name as the superclass private method, MATLAB might call the function instead of the method.? For example: % Superclass  classdef Super     properties         P            end         methods           function obj = set.P(obj, value)             obj.P = value;             obj = reset2(obj);           end     end     methods (Access = private)         function obj = reset2(obj)             disp('in private reset2 method');         end     end  end% Subclass  classdef Sub < Super end  % Function on path with same name as private method of superclass  function x = reset2(varargin)     disp('in function reset2')     x = varargin{1}; end% Create an instance of subclass and set the property >> obj = Sub;>> obj.P = 14; % Should call the private method Super/reset2>>
Workaround: Use one of the following to work around this issue:?Use dot-notation to invoke the method:      methods         function obj = set.P(obj, value)             obj.P = value;             obj.reset2;         end      end  Make the class method protected instead of private:      methods (Access = protected)          function obj = reset2(obj)          end      end   If the method must be private, invoke it from a protected method instead of a public method:      methods          function obj = set.P(obj, value)              obj = reset1(obj);          end      end      methods (Access = private)          function obj = reset2(obj)          end      end         methods(Access = protected)          function obj = reset1(obj)              reset2(obj);          end              end

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might produce incorrect results for AUTOSAR model containing For Each subsystem
Description: Generated code might produce incorrect results for an AUTOSAR model that meets all of these conditions: The AUTOSAR model uses export-function modeling, represented by a function-call subsystem at the root level of the model. The function-call subsystem contains a For Each subsystem, which is directly connected to the inport of the function-call subsystem. The inport of the function-call subsystem also connects to another branch. The AUTOSAR data access mode of the root-level inport is set to ExplicitReceive .
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2020b Update 4, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: Compilation error when Dynamic compressibility is set to Off?for heat exchanger sample variants in the?thermal liquid domain
Description: This issue occurs if Dynamic compressibility ?is set to Off  in: Heat Exchanger (TL), Simple Variant Heat Exchanger (TL-TL), Simple Variant Heat Exchanger (TL-G), Simple Variant? Simple Heat Exchanger Interface (TL)  In such situations, simulating the model generates this error message: Number of equations exceeds number of variables .?
Workaround: Set Dynamic compressibility?to On.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Calling the write function for characteristic objects without specifying the response type fails to write data to the BLE device
Description: Some BLE devices have characteristics that support the "Write"  response type, but not "WriteWithoutResponse" . For these characteristics, calling the write  function without the optional response type input argument results in a failed write operation, but might not return an error. For example, the following command might fail to write to the BLE device.? >> write(c,1:5)
Workaround: If your characteristic object supports "Write" but not "WriteWithoutResponse", specify?"withresponse"?as an input argument for write. You can check whether your characteristic object supports "WriteWithoutResponse" by examining its Attributes property. For example, use the following commands to view a characteristic object's attributes and complete a write operation:>> c.Attributesans =   1ˇÁ2 string array    "Read"    "Write">> write(c,1:5,"withresponse")

Summary: When computing AUTOSAR C++14 or other C++ rules, analysis gets stuck on the message?Verifying sources ...
Description: When the AUTOSAR C++14 coding rules are computed with the option -autosar-cpp14 , the analysis might be stuck on messages such as the following: Verifying sources ...file1.c (1/100)file2.c (2/100) The issue? might also happen with the options?-misra-cpp  or -jsf-coding-rules . The issue occurs because of a bug in how data types in template instantiations are?internally?constructed.
Workaround: If you are checking for defects along with AUTOSAR C++14 rules and encounter this issue, check for defects only. If you are checking for AUTOSAR C++14 rules only and encounter the issue, there are no workarounds.

Summary: MATLAB Web App Server is unable to launch sessions on macOS Catalina (10.15)
Description: MATLAB Web App Server is unable to launch sessions on macOS Catalina (10.15) resulting in a blank session page. This is because MATLAB Web App Server uses a self-signed certificate and macOS Catalina (10.15) has stricter requirements for self-signed certificates.?

Summary: Requirement annotation might not have tip of arrow after drag-and-drop linking
Description: If you drag a requirement from the requirement browser and drop it onto a block for creating a traceability link, you can show a requirement annotation with a connector by clicking an annotation template.  The connector might not have a tip on it if you display the annotation by clicking the annotation template after drag-and-drop.
Workaround: If you hide and show the requirement annotation by either of the following operations, the tip will be displayed correctly.Disable and enable the Requirements Manager app Selecting hide and show from? the requirement badge?

Summary: Dependency Analyzer incorrectly displays a file path with Unicode characters on machines with non-UTF8 locales
Description: In a project, the Dependency Analyzer incorrectly reports the path of a file  if both   of these conditions are true:The file is stored in a folder with Unicode characters The machine uses non-UTF8  locale settings

Summary: Failed port conversion and incorrect warning when linking component model in AUTOSAR architecture model
Description: In an AUTOSAR architecture model, when you link a Software Component block to an implementation model, the software might fail to convert a port to a bus port and generate an incorrect message similar to the following: Warning: Converting port 'mTestIRVEmptySingleInputNoExpFcn/ModeReceiveIn1' to a bus port is not supported because the port specified anonvirtual bus. The cited port does not have a selected bus type as the message states.
Workaround: In the implementation model, the?Output as nonvirtual bus?property is set for the cited Inport or Outport block. But because a bus type is not selected for the port, the option is not visible in the block dialog box. Issue the following MATLAB command to clear the block property.>> set_param('path/to/block','BusOutputAsStruct','off');

Summary: Selecting Calibrate Maps?to virtually calibrate block maps with Model-Based Calibration Toolbox results in callback errors? ??
Description: Selecting the block?Calibrate Maps? option to virtually calibrate the maps with Model-Based Calibration Toolbox can result in callback errors. The calibration option is available with these blocks: Mapped CI Engine Mapped SI Engine Compressor Turbine Mapped Motor Three-Phase Voltage Source Inverter?  If you use the CI engine dynamometer or SI engine dynamometer reference application Generate Mapped Engine from Spreadsheet  test option, you may encounter an error when the software invokes Model-Based Calibration Toolbox.
Workaround: Before using Powertrain Blockset to virtually calibrate the block maps:At the MATLAB command-line, type mbcmodel?to open the Model-Based Calibration Toolbox Model Browser.?If prompted, fill in the User Information dialog.Close the Model Browser.

Summary: PLL models result in error in Accelerator mode.
Description: When running PLL models in Accelerator mode, it results in a sporadic error. This is true for both PLL models provided under reference architecture and PLL models created using basic building blocks in the Mixed-Signal Blockset?.The error is caused because the decrease in the propagation delay in Variable Pulse Delay block under VCO in the PLL model is more than the increase in the?variable step discrete input sample time, causing the output sample times to occur out of sequence.

Summary: JMAAB check Comment position in transition label reports issue for correctly positioned comment
Description: The Model Advisor check Comment position in transition label  (Check ID: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0771 ) reports issue for sub ID a1 when the comments are positioned correctly and [condition], {condition_action}, or {transition_action} are enclosed within parenthesis.

Summary: MATLAB crashes during simulation for model containing variant source/sink and buses
Description: MATLAB may crash when we simulate a model containing variant source/sink block, selecting bus signal with bus-selector or bus-creator with different bus hierarchy.  The stack trace may look like this: [? 0] 0x00007fa6cf37f4be std::__cxx11::basic_string<char16_t, std::char_traits<char16_t>, std::allocator<char16_t> >::_M_assign(std::__cxx11::basic_string<char16_t, std::char_traits<char16_t>, std::allocator<char16_t> > const&)  [? 1] 0x00007fa587022f8a std::__cxx11::basic_string<char16_t, std::char_traits<char16_t>, std::allocator<char16_t> >::assign(std::__cxx11::basic_string<char16_t, std::char_traits<char16_t>, std::allocator<char16_t> > const&)  [? 2] 0x00007fa58700f7ac GraphicalBlockSignalProp::BusSelectorSignalProp::fwdPropagateFromSrcToAllDsts(HierLabelInfo const*)  [? 3] 0x00007fa5870108f3 GraphicalBlockSignalProp::BusSelectorSignalProp::fwdPropagate(SLBlock*, int, SLBlock*, int)  [? 4] 0x00007fa587058262 SigNameHandler::FwdPropagateSignalLabel(SLBlock*, int, SLBlock*, int)  [? 5] 0x00007fa587058b97 SigNameHandler::propagateSignalLabelFromBlk(SLBlock*)  [? 6] 0x00007fa587059b38 SigNameHandler::propagateSignalLabels()  [? 7] 0x00007fa5870922ca VariantConditionPropagator::PropagateConditions(slGraph*, SLCompBD*)  [? 8] 0x00007fa58707e69a InlineVariantConditionPropagation(SLCompBD*)  [? 9] 0x00007fa5869560dd SLCompLibrariesMdlRefAndVariants(SLCompBD*)

Summary: The xcpA2L function errors if the A2L file has GROUP without keyword ROOT
Description: If the xcpA2L  function is used to read an A2L file containing the GROUP  keyword, but that GROUP  does not include the optional ROOT  keyword, an error is thrown.

Summary: Assertion thrown during HDL code generation from a Simulink model containing Gain blocks that are operating in "u*K" mode
Description: When performing HDL code generation from a Simulink model containing a Gain block, we throw the following assertion if the gain mode is "u*K", the input vector is unordered and the gain factor is a square matrix: Error: Evaluation of elaborate function on class hdldefaults. Gain failed with the error message: pir_core:pirudd:assertionFailed: Assertion failed: dom_pir_udd/ paramset. cpp:65:orientation. size() == 1 ?

Summary: The input parameter settings and corresponding modify actions for sub-ids B & D of the JMAAB check?Check Model font settings?(ID: mathworks.jmaab.db_0043)?do not work as expected?
Description: The input parameters for sub-id B and D for check Check Model font settings  (ID: mathworks.jmaab.db_0043) on the Model Advisor Configuration editor are not being considered correctly by the check. Due to this, it is possible that the check gives incorrect results when these input parameters are set differently in Model Advisor Configuration Editor. As a result of this issue, the modify action of the check also fails to modify the model correctly.

Summary: JMAAB check "Check usage of transition conditions in Stateflow transitions" (ID: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0772)?incorrectly flags single internal transitions in a Stateflow chart
Description: The Model Advisor check "Check usage of transition conditions in Stateflow transitions " (ID: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0772 ) does not consider execution orders of each set of transitions separately when a Stateflow chart contains a state with internal as well as external transitions sourced from it. This leads to an erroneous violation being shown when the check runs on such models.?

Summary: JMAAB check Prohibition of logical value comparison in Stateflow?incorrectly flags transitions where no logical constants are used
Description: The Model Advisor check?Prohibition of logical value comparison in Stateflow ?(ID:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0655 )?incorrectly flags transitions where no comparisons between logical variable and logical constants are made.

Summary: readMeasurement returns incorrect values for multi-dimensional objects
Description: The readMeasurement  function returns incorrect values for objects of two or more dimensions. Because the axes read from the A2L file are incorrectly assigned, the row and column dimensions of the response are exchanged.

Summary: Incorrect simulation results from elapsed time in multi-level Model block mixed mode simulation
Description: Simulation results could be incorrect when a multi-level model contains one or more blocks?that need elapsed time, such as Discrete-Time Integrator, in the model referencing hierarchy, when all of the following conditions exist: The model simulates in Normal mode. The model contains a first Model block with Simulation mode ?set to Normal . The model referenced by the first Model block contains a second Model block with Simulation mode ?set to Accelerator . The model referenced by the second Model block contains a third Model block. The model hierarchy from the?second Model block on down contains one or more blocks with a triggered sample time that need elapsed time. The model hierarchy from the?second Model block on down contains a Model block that has selected to schedule rates via ports.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when querying the input elements of a Bus Selector block with?get_param
Description: Querying the input elements of a Bus Selector block with get_param  might cause MATLAB to crash when all of these conditions apply: One of the elements of the input bus for the Bus Selector block originates from a Function Caller block. Any blocks between the Function Caller block and Bus Selector block are virtual blocks.  The query might look like: get_param('model/Bus Selector','InputSignals')
Workaround: If the output of the Function Caller block is not a bus, connect a Gain block with Gain set to 1?between the Function Caller block and the Bus Selector block.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 8, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Tabs misaligned when first opening a Test Sequence editor with more than two scenarios
Description: If you use API commands to create a Test Sequence block and create more than two scenarios in that block, when you open that Test Sequence block for the first time, the Test Sequence scenario tabs in the editor are not aligned correctly.

Summary: Incompatibility reported for models with custom block replacement rules to stub Initialize, Reset or Terminate Function blocks
Description: Simulink Design Verifier reports incompatibility with model consisting of Initialize, Reset or Terminate Function blocks even if the user has provided custom block replacement rule to stub such blocks. An example of such block replacement rule can be the one that replaces these blocks with empty subsystems.
Workaround: The incompatibility can be worked around with following two step approach:Generate replacement model using sldvblockreplacement?Run Simulink Design Verifier analysis on the replacement model generated in step 1.

Summary: Graphics flicker when hovering mouse over the figure
Description: Figure content might flicker as you hover the cursor over the figure while the content is being updated. For example, if you display video frames in a loop as you hover the cursor over the figure, the content might flicker.

Summary: False positive violations of AUTOSAR C++14 rule M8-4-4 on function calls to static methods from objects
Description: Autosar C++14 rule M8-4-4 states that a function identifier shall either be used to call the function or it shall be preceded by & .  However, the Polyspace checker for rule M8-4-4 might show some false positive violations on function calls from instantiated objects to static methods. For example, the checker shows a violation on a.foo() ?below even though the identifier foo  is used to call the function: class A{public:  static void foo (void) {};};// ...intmain (void){  A a;  a.foo();       // False positive on this call.  A::foo();      // No false positive on this call.}
Workaround: Since there is no false positive from function calls to static methods from the class name (e.g., using A::foo()), consider using this notation when applicable.

Summary: AUTOSAR model build fails for lookup table with unmapped breakpoints
Description: Code generation for an AUTOSAR model might fail when the following conditions are met: The model contains a lookup table block with Data specification ?set to?Table and breakpoints . At least one of the following conditions is true: There is a mismatch between the input (operating point) data type and the breakpoint data type. The breakpoints are not mapped to an AUTOSAR parameter.   Simulink reports the following error: Unrecognized method, property, or field 'Identifier' for class 'coder.descriptor.StructExpression'

Summary: Assertion generated during HDL code generation for model using complex matrices with MATLAB Function block
Description: When generating HDL code from a MATLAB Function block with complex matrices, an assertion might be generated with the following message: In getStructType: Assertion failed: handleArrays() && MatrixType::isa(aType)

Summary: The model advisor check Check safety-related optimization settings for data initialization?displays incorrect recommended action when?Code interface packaging is set to C++
Description: The Model advisor check Check safety-related optimization settings for data initialization ?(ID: mathworks.hism.hisl_0052 ) recommends not to select the settings Remove root level I/O zero initialization ?and Remove internal data zero initialization ?for safety purpose. However, Embedded Coder by default selects these two settings for code generation when the parameter?Code interface packaging  set to?C++  type. So there is a conflict between Model Advisor recommendation and Embedded Coder validation when Code interface packaging  set to C++  type.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Stateflow charts with nonzero initial values for outputs and containing input events might generate incorrect HDL code
Description: The HDL code generated from a Stateflow chart might be incorrect if both these conditions are true: The chart contains input events ?The initial values specified for the outputs of the chart are not equal to zero

Summary: Instances deleted in Signal Labeler Fast Navigation mode arenot removed from plots in the main app
Description: When user enters the Fast Navigation mode of Signal Labeler , theexisting labels in the main app are imported into the mode. Theselabels could have been plotted before entering the mode. If the labels frommultiple members are deleted and the mode is closed, then labels from only the firstmember are unplotted in the main app. The labels are correctly deleted from theLabeled Signal Set Browser . This creates an incorrect state where labels plotted are not shown in the Labeled Signal Set Browser .
Workaround: If the member for the incorrectly plotted labels is unplotted and plotted back again, the labels are unplotted and plotted correctly. Labeled signal sets are exported correctly from the app.

Summary: Draw Labels?button in Signal Labeler toolstrip changes to 'off' state when user fits to view or zooms out
Description: When Draw Labels ?mode is active, the Draw Labels ?button in the Signal Labeler  toolstrip shows as 'on'. However, if the user fits to view or zooms out using either the toolstrip, a context menu, or keyboard shortcuts, the state of the Draw Labels  button changes to 'off' . The cursor icon still shows up as crosshairs, indicating that Draw Labels  mode is active.
Workaround: Although, the 'Draw Labels' button in incorrect state, the drawing is still active.If a new label is drawn, the button goes back to the correct state i.e. 'on'.?If the button is clicked, it goes to 'on' state and labels can be drawn.?

Summary: JMAAB check?Use of named Stateflow parameters and constants?incorrectly flags numeric literal 1 used in increment or decrement statement
Description: The Model Advisor check?Use of named Stateflow parameters and constants ? (ID:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0702 ) incorrectly flags?numeric literal 1 when it is explicitly type casted in increment or decrement statement.

Summary: JMAAB check Condition actions and transition actions in Stateflow incorrectly flags condition actions with C-style comments
Description: The Model Advisor check Condition actions and transition actions in Stateflow  (ID:mathworks.jmaab.jc_0753 ) incorrectly flags condition action when condition action is preceded by C-style comment.

Summary: Multicasting issues in UDP Send and Receive blocks
Description: Using the Simulink Real-Time UDP Send or Receive blocks for multicasting does not work without specifying a default gateway in the target computer routing table. Below are the multicasting issues that may occur on both development computer and target computer:Issues on Target Computer for UDP Send block:  If no default gateway is setup on the target computer, datagrams are not sent to a multicast address.?  Issues on Target Computer for UDP Receive block:  ?If no default gateway is set on the target computer, a UDP receive block with multicasting enabled and local address set to any IP address causes a bind error while loading the real-time application on the target computer. ?If local IP address on the UDP receive block mask is set to a local interface address, no multicast data is received even after setting up the default gateway on target computer.  Issues on development computer for UDP Send block:  While sending to a multicast address, the default gateway setup in the routing table on the development computer is used. There is no way to specify an interface from which to send the multicast messages.  Issues on development computer for UDP Receive block: ? No multicast messages are received on the development computer by either specifying the local IP address as an interface address or 0.0.0.0 (INADDR_ANY) on the UDP receive block mask.?
Workaround: As a partial workaround, allow multicast send and receive on the target computer through a single interface by setting a default gateway.To set the default gateway to 10.10.10.15, type this command in a terminal on the target computer:>> route add default 10.10.10.15

Summary: uitable ColumnWidth 'auto' value does not stretch to fill all available space when uitable has few columns
Description: When uitable ColumnWidth  is 'auto' , the table does not stretch to fit available space when there are few columns. A white unused area might appear on the right side of the table.
Workaround: For some scenarios, the 'auto' configuration of ColumnWidth can be replaced with?'fit'?or?'1x':'fit': show column at exact width to show content. This is best to use to replace 'auto' when the content or column name is wide.'1x': show column as a proportion of the available space in the table. This is best to replace 'auto' when the content would have even spacing with other auto columns.

Summary: When using APIs to create views, systemcomposer.arch.Component workspace objects that are part of the view become invalidated
Description: When creating views via System Composer APIs, any existing objects that have been cached in variables that point to components in the composition can become invalidated, if they are part of the view. When this happens, you can no longer use the existing objects to access properties or methods on the components.
Workaround: To work around this issue when an error occurs, fetch the component objects from the model via the APIs before proceeding.

Summary: Variant Subsystem may generate unconditional global data corresponding to the global data store memory
Description: Variant Subsystem may generate unconditional global data corresponding to the global data store memory?when all the below conditions are met:Variant Subsystem is configured to generate pre-processor conditionals with ert.tlc Variant choices have data store read/writers with Simulink.Signals and corresponds to global data store memory
Workaround: Convert global data store memory to local data store memory by using the Data Store Memory block

Summary: Loading certain apps in App Designer results in an axes that does not appear properly
Description: When certain apps are loaded in App Designer, some UIAxes  components will not appear in the app. This occurs in apps where the border of a UIAxes  component is exactly aligned with the border of its container.
Workaround: Ensure that the UIAxes components in your App Designer apps are well within the bounds of their container.? Specifically, when the UIAxes?does not appear, select the loading UIAxes and use the arrow keys on your keyboard to move the UIAxes further within the container.? Alternatively, use the mouse to drag the UIAxes?further within the container. This will cause the?UIAxes to appear in your app.

Summary: slvnvmakeharness and sldvmakeharness error out and pauses the simulation when the Bus element port is of inherit:auto data type
Description: Simulink Design Verifier requires every Bus element port to have a bus data type associated with it. Functions slvnvmakeharness  and sldvmakeharness? errors out on models and pauses the simulation when the model has an In Bus element  port with Inherit: auto data type. It is expected that the model errors out in the above scenario, however the functions slvnvmakeharness  and sldvmakeharness? pause the simulation which is an unexpected behavior.?

Summary: Code generation might crash when generating code for functions that have two consecutive parfor loops
Description: In certain situations, the code generator might crash when generating code for two consecutive parfor  loops if all  of these conditions are true: The two?loops have the same iteration space. The two loops contain different indexing expressions into the same variable. The loops contain no reduction assignments. See https://www.mathworks.com/help/coder/ug/reduction-assignments-in-parfor-loops.html .  This is an example of a sequence of parfor  loops that satisfies these conditions and might be affected by this issue: parfor p1 = int32(2):int32(17)    myVar(p1,5) = 59;endparfor p1 = int32(2):int32(17)    myVar(p1,1) = 42;end
Workaround: To avoid this issue, do any of the following:Avoid having two consecutive?parfor?loops in your MATLAB function. If your code contains two consecutive?parfor?loops, change one of them?to a serial for loop. Alternatively, place one of the consecutive parfor loops in a separate function that contains a coder.inline('never') statement.In either of the?parfor loops, rename the variable that is indexed into differently. For example, in the body of the second parfor loop in the preceding code snippet, rename?myVar?to myVar_1.Change the iteration space of one of the loops such that the two loops have different iteration counts.

Summary: The?example model?for field-orientated control with sensorless position decoder?fails to spin the motor and shows oscillation in estimated values
Description: The models? mcb_pmsm_foc_sensorless_f28379d.slx ? and? mcb_pmsm_foc_sensorless_f28069MLaunchPad.slx , which are part of the example Sensorless Field-Oriented Control of PMSM , shows the following behavior: The motor sporadically fails to spin with some driver boards due to ADC offset tolerance limit for the current sensor.  These models may display some oscillation in the speed, torque, and flux of the motor due to DC component in the flux observer inputs.  The motor fails to spin with flux observer at 10KHz switching frequency due to DC component in the flux observer inputs.

Summary: Unexpected error in Battery (Table-Based) block when enabling temperature-dependent fading with?Extrapolation method for all tables?parameter?set to 'nearest' or 'error'
Description: In the Battery (Table-Based) block, if you set the Extrapolation method for all tables  parameter to either 'nearest' or 'error' and the Fade characteristics defined by ?parameter to 'Lookup tables (temperature dependent)', MATLAB throws this error when you run the model:? Function, tablelookup, is wrong. Please check 1) whether input data points have correct sizes; 2) query values are scalar; 3) query values and table data have the commensurate units; 4) constants or compile time parameters are passed to interpolation and extrapolation argument.

Summary: In a model configured for concurrent execution, S-function blocks inside subsystems might cause a crash if the blocks are commented out or commented through
Description: In a model configured for concurrent execution, S-function blocks inside subsystems might cause a crash when you update diagram if the blocks are commented out or commented through.
Workaround: In a model configured for concurrent execution, remove the S-function blocks, which are commented out or commented through, from subsystems.

Summary: Whenviewing app conflicts in the Comparison Tool, saving or accepting the merged .mlapp file errors out
Description: Whenviewing conflicts for a .mlapp  file from current folder browser in the Comparison Tool, saving oraccepting the merged .mlapp  file fails. The following error is shown: Classes are not supported in the Live Editor.?Save the file as aplain text code file (.m).

Summary: Simulink compiler execution will crash due to reregistering existing dynamic enum definitions.
Description: If a Simulink model uses dynamic?enum data types, ?reregistering the same dynamic enum definition will cause the deployed application to crash.

Summary: Add-On Manager does not show?available support package updates in the Updates?tab
Description: When checking for updates in the?Add-On Manager, the Updates  tab does not show any available support package updates even when the matlabshared.supportpkg.checkForUpdate   ?command indicates that there are available updates. To open the Add-On Manager Updates ?tab, go to the?Home ?tab and click?Help > Check for Updates .??

Summary: MATLAB crashes when capturing baseline to Excel file if the file already exists and is open
Description: Clicking the Capture  button in the Baseline Criteria  section for a test case created from an external Microsoft Excel file causes MATLAB to crash if the file already exists and is open in the Microsoft Excel application.

Summary: HDL Coder Toolstrip can cause an internal error to be thrown if DUT subsystem name contains a new line
Description: HDL Coder Toolstrip can cause an internal error to be thrown if the DUT subsystem name in Simulink contains a new line. The error appears as follows.? Error: Character vector is not terminated properly.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Gain block with fixed point inputs? and Multiplication?block parameter set to?Matrix(K*u) (u vector)?generates incorrect HDL code.
Description: The generated HDL code for a Simulink model is incorrect under following conditions The model contains a Gain block with??Multiplication ?block parameter is set to?Matrix(K*u) (u vector)  The inputs are driven by signals configured with fixed-point type and?? Output data type on the Gain block is set to custom fixed point type (other than 'Inherit: Inherit via internal rule') Ex: numerictype(0, 16, 8)?

Summary: Executing MEX function out-of-process could cause running out of memory
Description: A MEX function with a considerable memory footprint, if executed repeatedly within a short period of time, could cause the host computer to run out of memory and crash.

Summary: Error?Unable to launch the MATLABWindow application on?Red Hat based distributions
Description: This issue is applicable to the following Linux distributions:  Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.2 and later CentOS 8.2 and later Fedora 32 and later  The following error can be observed while installing MATLAB or while running certain features in MATLAB (such as Simulink Start Page, Help Browser, Live Editor, UIFigure, App Designer, Simulink Data Inspector, etc.): terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::runtime_error'??  ????? what():? Unable to launch the MATLABWindow application ??? Aborted    You might also see: MATLABWindow application failed to launch. Unable to launch the MATLABWindow application.  Or if you enter this command in MATLAB: system(fullfile(matlabroot, '/bin/glnxa64/MATLABWindow'))   MATLAB displays: ./MATLABWindow: symbol lookup error: /lib64/libk5crypto.so.3: undefined symbol: EVP_KDF_ctrl, version OPENSSL_1_1_1b
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Stateflow fails to error for a?Stateflow chart with multiple states that have the same name and use the in operator
Description: Stateflow might not throw an error when a chart contains multiple states with the same name and uses the in   operator on one of those states without using qualified dot notation. The chart might produce incorrect results.?

Summary: MATLAB sporadically crashes when closing a figure immediately after opening it
Description: MATLAB sporadically crashes when a new figure is programmatically created or the existing figure is made current and immediately closed.

Summary: Performance problems with experiments?when showing training plot
Description: Experiment Manager training is slower when showing the training plot. The training time of a trial can be up to 20 times longer depending on the trials. For example, using smaller networks and small numbers of images with large numbers of iterations can result in a large slow down.
Workaround: To work around this issue, turn off the training plot when running the experiment.

Summary: An App Designer app's StartupFcn does not execute when the app is launched from an uninterruptible callback of another app
Description: When launching a child app in the component callback of a parent app, the child app's StartupFcn  does not execute if the component belonging to the callback has its Interruptible  property set to 'off' .
Workaround: Move the child app's StartupFcn logic to a public function, such as a function called initialize. In the callback of the parent app, instantiate the child app and call the?initialize function of the child app. For example:?a = childApp;a.initialize();

Summary: Assertion occurs when Simulink.Bus object containing 64-bit sized element is used in Rapid Accelerator or Accelerator mode
Description: When you use Rapid Accelerator or Accelerator mode to build the model containing Simulink.Bus  object whose elements have one of the 64-bit sized data types might cause the following assertion:CGIR assertion 'In restrict: Assertion failed: st->numFields() == r.getNumberOfFields()' failed in 'dom_core/analysis/range/RangeValueFactory.cpp

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generating code from MATLAB code that contains a switch statement with an integral input outside of int32 range might produce incorrect results
Description: The generated C/C++ code might be incorrect for MATLAB code that contains a switch statement whose input is an integral value that is outside of the range of the 32-bit integer?type. The issue can occur for MATLAB Coder and for MATLAB blocks in Simulink.? The issue occurs only if the value of the switch statement input is not a literal constant, but proves to be constant due to compiler optimizations that simplify the program, so that the only possible input value is an integral value that is outside of the?int32  range. The generated code might exclude the switch statement, and instead contain just the code for one of the switch branches which might not necessarily be the one that would be selected during MATLAB execution.? For example, in the below MATLAB code, consider inUint  to be a variable of type uint8 , with a value that is not known during code generation (for example, a run-time input). Since inUint  is of an unsigned type, its value is always equal to or greater than zero, so the expected behavior is to execute the otherwise  branch. However, code generation might produce the code of the single(0)  branch, and thus deliver an incorrect value of y . ? ? if(inUint >= 0)  ? ? ? ? x = single(-922337203685477580);  ? ? else  ? ? ? ? x = single(0);  ? ? end    ? ? y = 0;  ? ? switch x  ? ? ? case single(0)  ? ? ? otherwise  ? ? ? ? y = 10;  ? ? end

Summary: TLC error when code is generated for an AUTOSAR model that contains a calibration parameter within a private scoped Simulink Function
Description: If an AUTOSAR model contains a calibration parameter within a private scoped Simulink Function, code generation might fail with a TLC error similar to the following: Error: File: C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\autosarsup.tlc Line: 540 Column: 34Unable to find CallSites within the System scopeMain program:==> [00] C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\autosarsup.tlc:SLibAutosarFcnAddRunnableAccessInfoViaSystem(540)    [01] C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\autosarsup.tlc:SLibAutosarFcnAddRunnableAccessInfoViaSystem(554)    [02] C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\autosarsup.tlc:FcnAddAccessInfoForAutosarCalibrationHelper(602)    [03] C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:SLibCGIR_AUTOSAR_TrackCalPrm(2325)    [04] C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:FcnGenRateGroupedCodeFromModule(5765)    [05] C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:FcnGenerateCodeFromModule(5647)    [06] C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:SLibFcnGenBodySysFcn(292)    [07] C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:SLibFcnGenBodyFcnCache(509)    [08] C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:SLibFcnGenBodySysFcnEntry(667)    [09] C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\commonbodlib.tlc:FcnGenBodySysCache(1790)    [10] C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\commonbodlib.tlc:SLibGenBodyCache(6288)    [11] C:\MATLAB\R2020a\rtw\c\tlc\mw\formatwide.tlc:(70)

Summary: Incorrect MISRA-C:2012 9.1 violation when different cells of the same array are given to two parameters of a function
Description: When a local variable is initialized through function calls but the function takes the variable more than once as parameter, the Polyspace checker for rule MISRA-C:2012 9.1 incorrectly considers the variable as noninitialized. The issue typically shows up on array initializations when the initialization function takes different elements of the array as parameters in a given call. For example: void function(uint8 * hi_data, uint8 * lo_data);int main(void){   uint8 array[4];   uni32 acc = 0;   function(&array[0], &array[1]);   function(&array[2], &array[3]);   for (int i=0; i<4; i++)  {       acc = acc + array[i]; // incorrect MISRA-C:2012 9.1 violation   }}
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder, Polyspace Bug Finder Server

Summary: Incorrect diagnostic error for a model with reference configuration set
Description: Incorrect diagnostic error for a model with reference configuration when all the below conditions are true:The referenced configuration does not contain Design Verifier component. SLDV toolstrip when opened prompts requesting to attach Design Verifier component to the configuration set.  The Simulink Design Verifier shows the error message when the user selects No  to the above mentioned prompt.Error message(snippet):? Unrecognized function or variable 'DVMode'.

Summary: Error when trying to enter a numeric DNG ID into the Location field of Outgoing Links dialog
Description: When you create a link to an IBM DOORS Next (DNG) requirement in the Simulink Requirements Outgoing Links dialog, you can select DNG Requirement  from the Document Type ?drop down menu, and then click Browse ?to select the DNG project for linking. When you input the numeric DNG ID into the Location ?field, an error might occur and an error dialog box might appear.? The error dialog might say "Dot indexing not supported" or "Index out of bounds".

Summary: Simulation using the MinGW compiler might cause linker error for MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks that create a?monteCarloLocalization?System object
Description: Simulation using the MinGW compiler might cause linker error if the model contains a?MATLAB Function block or MATLAB System block that creates a?monteCarloLocalization ?System object. Summary: MATLAB hangs when you repeatedly run a script that displays a large number of different data types
Description: When you run a large Live Script successive times, MATLAB might hang and take a very long time to recover. Stepping through the code to debug or closing the MATLAB session might also cause MATLAB to hang.

Summary: Code inspection fails when multiple signals connect to a Mux or a Bus Creator block
Description: Simulink Code Inspector fails to inspect the code generated from a model that meets all of these conditions:The model contains multiple signals that connect to a Mux or a Bus Creator block The output signal of the Mux or Bus Creator block connects to a Source block

Summary: Names of logged signals are not propagated during SIL and PIL simulations
Description: You can configure logged signals to display propagated signal labels. For software-in-the-loop (SIL) and processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulations, the propagated names are not displayed in the Simulation Data Inspector or the exported variable in the workspace (logsout ). The signal names have the format <block name >:<port index >.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect result might be generated for Discrete State-Space block in accelerator and rapid-accelerator modes when adjusting the number of non-zero elements of coefficient matrices between runs
Description: The Discrete State-Space block might produce incorrect results when the following conditions are true: The model is simulated with accelerator or rapid accelerator mode. The pattern of the matrix coefficient A, B, C, or D is adjusted between simulation runs. The total number of non-zero elements of each coefficient matrix remains the same.
Workaround: To change a coefficient matrix between accelerator or rapid accelerator simulation runs, take the following steps (shown here specifically for changing A from [1 0 0; 0 2 0; 0 0 3] to [0 0 2; 3 0 0; 0 1 0]):Simulate the model in accelerator or rapid accelerator mode with coefficient matrix A set as? [1 0 0; 0 2 0; 0 0 3].Stop simulation. Change A to?[1 0 2; 3 0 0; 0 1 0] and?apply the change. Notice that the number of non-zero elements has changed.Simulate the model in accelerator or rapid accelerator mode.?Stop simulation. Change A to?[0 0 2; 3 0 0; 0 1 0]?and?apply the change. Notice that the number of non-zero elements has changed from four to three.Simulate the model in accelerator or rapid accelerator mode.?

Summary: Model configuration parameters dialog cannot be opened if the?Generate GPU Code parameter is set and Microsoft Visual Studio is not installed
Description: When the Generate GPU Code   parameter of a Simulink model is set, the software tries to automatically locate a supported toolchain on the development computer. On the Windows platform, when the Visual Studio application is not installed, an incorrect error is generated causing the Model configuration parameters dialog box to close abruptly.

Summary: Incorrect positions of some objects in exported OpenDRIVE scenes
Description: The exported position of some objects to OpenDRIVE might be incorrect. This can occur when objects are positioned near a road with changing elevation. Steeper elevation changes would cause a greater offset in the position.

Summary: Incorrect AUTOSAR-C++14 Rule A12-6-1 violation when using member default initializer
Description: AUTOSAR-C++14 Rule A12-6-1 states that all class data members that are initialized by the constructor shall be initialized using member initializers.Examples from the standards show only initialization through constructors:     class A {    public:         A(std::string s) : str{s} {} // Compliant    private:        std::string str;    }; However, Polyspace considered default member initialization as non-compliant:     class A {     public:         A(std::string s) {} // Non-compliant in R2020a    private:         std::string str = "default_val";    };

Summary: Accelerated simulation using code generation fails when mex default compiler is MinGW or Intel
Description: If your model simulation uses code generation and your default  mex ?compiler is a MinGW or Intel compiler, you might observe a compilation error.  With a MinGW compiler, you might see an error like the following: "F:\win64\MinGW\bin/gcc" -c -fwrapv -m64 -O0 -DCLASSIC_INTERFACE=1 -DALLOCATIONFCN=0 -DONESTEPFCN=0 -DTERMFCN=1 -DMULTI_INSTANCE_CODE=0 -DINTEGER_CODE=0? -DTID01EQ=1 -DMODEL=mVarDimsETVSWithResetSS -DNUMST=2 -DNCSTATES=0 -DHAVESTDIO? @mVarDimsETVSWithResetSS_comp.rsp -o "rt_size_check.obj" "C:/Users/AppData/Local/Temp/slprj/accel/mVarDimsETVSWithResetSS/rt_size_check.c"  In file included from E:/matlab/simulink/include/simstruc.h:64:0,  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?from C:/Users/AppData/Local/Temp/slprj/accel/mVarDimsETVSWithResetSS/rt_size_check.h:5,  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?from C:/Users/AppData/Local/Temp/slprj/accel/mVarDimsETVSWithResetSS/rt_size_check.c:2:  E:/matlab/simulink/include/simstruc_compcond.h:244:3: error: #error Unrecognized use.  ?# error Unrecognized use.  ? ?^~~~~  E:/matlab/simulink/include/simstruc_compcond.h:316:3: error: #error Must define one of RT, NRT, MATLAB_MEX_FILE, SL_INTERNAL, or FIPXT_SHARED_MODULE  ?# error Must define one of RT, NRT, MATLAB_MEX_FILE, SL_INTERNAL, or FIPXT_SHARED_MODULE  ? ?^~~~~?   With an Intel compiler, you might see an error like the following:? icl  -c -nologo -GS -W4 -DWIN32 -D_MT -MT /W3 /EHs /nologo /D_CRT_SECURE_NO_DEPRECATE /D_SCL_SECURE_NO_DEPRECATE /D_SECURE_SCL=0 /fp:precise  /c /Od /DCLASSIC_INTERFACE=1 /DALLOCATIONFCN=0 /DONESTEPFCN=0 /DTERMFCN=1 /DMULTI_INSTANCE_CODE=0 /DINTEGER_CODE=0  /DTID01EQ=1 /DMODEL=mVarDimsETVSWithResetSS /DNUMST=2 /DNCSTATES=0 /DHAVESTDIO @mVarDimsETVSWithResetSS_comp.rsp /Fo"rt_size_check.obj" C:\TEMP\4484~1\TPB544~1\slprj\accel\mVarDimsETVSWithResetSS\rt_size_check.c                           : rt_size_check.c                           : E:\matlab\simulink\include\simstruc_compcond.h(244): catastrophic error: #error directive: Unrecognized use. ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?:   # error Unrecognized use.   ? In either case, the source file can be? rt_size_check.c  or another file.
Workaround: Switch to a Microsoft compiler by using the mex -setup command.

Summary: Cannot create a service server for a service rosapi/GetParam
Description: When creating messages and services which contain the "C++" keyword, the following error is returned: Unable to create a service server.

Summary: Simulink may crash when calling a scoped Simulink function inside a referenced model from a MATLAB System block using the model name instead of the block name
Description: When a MATLAB System block (associated with a System object) calls a scoped Simulink function (FunctionName() ) inside a referenced model (Model_B.slx ) with a block name (BlockName ), the System object must call the scoped Simulink function using the format BlockName.FunctionName() . If the System object calls the scoped Simulink function using Model_B.FunctionName() , MATLAB might crash during model execution.?

Summary: Referenced model with constant rate inside a Simulink function inside a referenced export-function model executes multiple times at single time step during simulation
Description: When all of following conditions are met, during model simulation a referenced model that is inside another referenced model may incorrectly execute multiple times during a single time step: * The top model contains a Model block which references an export-function model.? * The top model contains a Function Caller block which calls a Simulink function that is defined in the referenced export-function model. * The referenced export-function model contains, in addition to the Simulink function, one or more function-call subsystems that are driven by root-level function-call Inport blocks.? ? * At least one of such function-call subsystems has constant (Inf ) sample time and contains an Initialize Function, Reset Function, or Terminate Function block. * The Simulink function that is inside the referenced export-function model contains a Model block that references a second referenced model.? * The second referenced model contains one or more blocks that have constant (Inf ) sample time.?
Workaround: Do either of the following:* Set the sample time of the blocks inside the second referenced model to a value other than Inf.* Remove the Initialize Function, Reset Function, or Terminate Function block from the function-call subsystem in the referenced export-function model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: dsp.Channelizer and Channelizer Block will generate incorrect channel data if the 'OversamplingRatio' property is greater than 2
Description: dsp.Channelizer and Channelizer Block will generate incorrect channel data if the 'OversamplingRatio' property is greater than 2.

Summary: When calling multiple packages that are created by MATLAB Compiler SDK in Python or C#, MATLAB Runtime crashes while terminating the packages.
Description: When calling multiple packages that are created by MATLAB Compiler SDK in Python or C#, MATLAB Runtime crashes while terminating the packages.  For example, ?if we have MATLAB functions that call Simulink Compiler's sim command ,? such as myFunc1 and myFunc2, [myFunc1. m]function out = myFunc1()? ??  model = 'model1'; ? ? ?simInp = Simulink.SimulationInput(model);   ? ? ?simInp = simulink.compiler.configureForDeployment(simInp);   ? ? ?out = sim(simInp);   end [myFunc2. m]function out = myFunc2()? ?? model = 'model2'; ? ? ?simInp = Simulink.SimulationInput(model);   ? ? ?simInp = simulink.compiler.configureForDeployment(simInp);   ? ? ?out = sim(simInp);   end after compiling these m files into separate Python packages?myFunc1 and?myFunc2, ?we can call multiple packages in Python. import myFunc1import myFunc2obj1 = myFunc1. initialize()obj1. myFunc1()obj2 = myFunc2. initialize()obj2. myFunc2() MATLAB Runtime crashes while terminating, and?end with "Abnormal termination:std::terminate()" error.?The issue is caused by each package unloading sl_proxy_deployed?microservice module and the sl_proxy_deployed?module doesn't handle multiple unload properly.
Workaround: Users can package multiple functions into one module to avoid crash during module termination.For example,?if we have MATLAB functions myFunc1 and myFunc2,[myFunc1.m]function out = myFunc1()? ?? model = 'model1';? ? ?simInp = Simulink.SimulationInput(model);? ? ?simInp = simulink.compiler.configureForDeployment(simInp);? ? ?out = sim(simInp);end[myFunc2.m]function out = myFunc2()? ?? model = 'model2';? ? ?simInp = Simulink.SimulationInput(model);? ? ?simInp = simulink.compiler.configureForDeployment(simInp);? ? ?out = sim(simInp);endWe can compile both m files into a single Python package myFunc12,?we can call both functions in the package in Python.import myFunc12obj = myFunc12.initialize()obj.myFunc1()obj2.myFunc2()The package will run and terminate successfully.

Summary: Analysis failure with Polyspace Server products when using the -batch option
Description: When a Polyspace analysis is sent to a remote server, in some circumstances, the analysis options can be altered.? This can lead to errors in the remote analysis, while the same analysis runs to completion when performed locally.

Summary: Compatibility check fails when signal name defined at an outport of referenced model is referred by a downstream block
Description: If a signal name defined at an outport of a referenced model is referred by some block downstream of that model reference block, Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails with following error message:Simulink Design Verifier failed to initialize: Checking compatibility failed
Workaround: Remove signal name defined at outport of the referenced model and instead assign it to the signal driving that outport.

Summary: Connections inside a Subsystem Reference block appears broken when a model is loaded
Description: In certain situations, when you load a model that contains a Subsystem Reference block, the blocks inside the Subsystem Reference block might not get rendered correctly. Also, the connections inside the Subsystem Reference block might be broken. This issue might happen if you try loading a model with a Subsystem Reference block immediately after performing?any  of these actions: Copy and paste to the Subsystem Reference block in which the paste operation fails and throws an error? Converting a Subsystem block to a Subsystem Reference block in which the conversion fails and throws an error
Fixed In: R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Calculate Best-Precision Scaling button in Sqrt block dialog does not work
Description: The Sqrt block Calculate Best-Precision Scaling button returns an error when the following is true: * The Output  parameter is set to one of the fixed-point data types (fixedt(1,16,0)  or fixdt(1,15,2^0,0) ).* In the Data Type Assistant, the Scaling  parameter is set to Binary point  or Slope and bias . These settings enable the Calculate Best-Precision Scaling  button. Clicking this button returns the error Invalid scaling tag: Out  instead of calculating the scaling factor.
Workaround: Use Product block as a workaround:?Add a Product block to the model, next to the Sqrt block.Copy the Output, Minimum, and Maximum parameter values from the Sqrt block to the corresponding parameters of the Product block.Open the Product block dialog box and navigate to the Signal Attributes tab.To expand the Data Type Assistant, click the >> button of the Output data type parameter.Click Calculate Best-Precision Scaling.Copy the calculated output data type expression back to the Sqrt block.Remove the Product block.?

Summary: Simulink Requirements link is not copied from library block to referenced instance of library block
Description: When you add a masked library block or a?Subsystem?block from a Library to a Simulink model, a referenced instance of the library block is created. If the library block contains a link to a requirement in Simulink Requirements, the referenced instance of the library block also contains the requirements link. You can edit the referenced instance of the library block by disabling the link to the library block. Then, you can re-establish the library link by pushing the changes back to the library block. However, the requirements link might not be copied into the referenced instance of the library block in some cases. This can happen when you are working with a referenced instance of a library block that is nested inside of another referenced instance of a library block. In this case, if you click Disable this link ?from the warning message on the Simulink canvas, then the requirements link will not be copied into the referenced instance of the library block. Instead, if you click Disable Links ?in the Subsystem ?tab, or right-click the subsystem and select Library Links  > Disable Link , then the requirements link is copied into the referenced instance of the library block as expected.
Workaround: If you click?Disable Links?in the?Subsystem?tab, or right-click the subsystem and select?Library Links >?Disable Link, then the requirements link is copied as expected.
Products: Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Coverage, Simulink Check

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible when a Constant block in a referenced model drives conditionally executed subsystem output
Description: Code generation might produce incorrect results when all of these conditions apply:The Underspecified initialization detection  configuration parameter is set to simplified ?for the model. The model contains a Model block inside a conditionally executed subsystem, such as an enabled, triggered, or function-call subsystem. The output of the Model block connects directly to an Outport block in the conditionally executed subsystem. The output of the Model block is driven by a Constant block connected to a root Outport block in the referenced model. The Sample time  of the Constant block is set to inf .
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the Model block output port and the Outport block in the conditionally executed subsystem.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur if reusable subchart contains exported graphical function with return value
Description: Incorrect code might occur for a model that meets the following conditions:The Stateflow chart contains a graphical function from custom Simulink library. This exported graphical function has output data. This graphical function is called from different subcharts present within the same model.
Workaround: Set the graphical function's Function Inline Option?property to Inline.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur when comparing integer and result of bitand/bitor if CastingMode set to 'Standards'
Description: Incorrect code might be generated from MATLAB code in below cases when the CastingMode  is set to 'Standards'  : i.
