Signed integer of negative value compared with unsigned integer which is a result of bitand/bitor expression using any of >  (greater than), <  (less than), >=  (greater than or equal to), <=  (less than or equal to) operators.  ii. Any of the operands in the above case (i) are casted to double and compared.    Sample code i:  function [out1] = func_swa_1(in1, u16)        out1 = in1 < (bitand(u16, uint16(49286))); % int32(negative) < uint16(result of bitand/bitor)  end  Sample code ii:   function [out1] = func_swa_2(in1, u16)       out1 = double(in1) > bitor(u16, uint16(49286);  % int32(negative) casted to double < uint16 (result of bitand/bitor)     end

Summary: Security Issue: MATLAB Compiler may include your SMTP server login information when packaging applications
Description: If you send email from within MATLAB using the sendmail  function after setting up your SMTP server information using the setpref  function, MATLAB Compiler includes your SMTP server username and password when packaging applications.
Workaround: To prevent this from happening, replace your SMTP server username and password in your MATLAB preferences with blank values. You can do this by typing the following commands at the MATLAB command prompt:setpref('Internet','SMTP_Username','');setpref('Internet','SMTP_Password','');

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for certain sparse assignment operations might produce incorrect answers
Description: Code generated for the statement?S(:,X) = R ?in your MATLAB code might produce?incorrect answers if all ?of these conditions are true: ? S is a sparse row vector R is a row vector (either sparse or full) X is a valid column index for S

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Top model may not initialize global variable associated with signal originating in referenced model
Description: In the configuration below: A signal "S" inside a Model block (referenced model A) is configured as Simulink.Signal  object with Owner  property set to A . Upstream of "S", a Constant block inside another Model block (referenced model B) is the originating signal of "S".  The top model (parent of A and B) may not initialize the global variable associated with the signal "S" to its initial value (determined by the constant block in model B) inside its model_initialize  method.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: err output port of NR Polar Decoder gives wrong answer
Description: Simulating NR Polar Decoder block in accelerator or rapid accelerator modes produces a wrong answer for the?err  output port.

Summary: A large number of global data shows up for some Schedule Editor models in the Model Comparison Tool
Description: Global data property for Schedule Editor models shows up in the Model Comparison Tool, which is not useful information and causes confusion.

Summary: Code replacements for MATLAB FFT functions do not support inherited Code Replacement Library (CRL) tables
Description: Inherited Code Replacement Library (CRL) tables are not supported for replacing the MATLAB fast Fourier transform (FFT) functions by CMSIS/NE10 libraries. This behavior can occur if all  these conditions are true: During code generation, you use the MATLAB FFT functions (fft, ifft, fft2, ifft2, fftn, ifftn) and generate code by using the Code Replacement Library.  For the code replacement library configuration parameter, you select an inherited CRL table whose base CRL table is any of the following: ARM Cortex-A ARM Cortex-M ARM Cortex-M (optimized) ARM Cortex-M (CMSIS)   This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?

Summary: The JMAAB checks for jc_0025 and jc_0026 flag bus elements of?Boolean data type for missing mix/max values
Description: The Model Advisor checks Design min/max specification of input interfaces  (ID:mathworks.jmaab.jc_0025) and Design min/max specification of output interfaces  (ID:mathworks.jmaab.jc_0026) might flag bus elements of Boolean data type for missing min/max values.

Summary: Incorrect?AUTOSAR-C++14 Rule M5-0-15 or MISRA-C++ Rule 5-0-15 violation on std container's iterators
Description: MISRA-C++ Rule 5-0-15 (or AUTOSAR C++14 Rule M5-0-15) explicitly states the following exception:  ? ?The increment/decrement operators may be used on iterators implemented by pointers to an array. Because std::array<T,N>::iterator  is effectively implemented by a pointer to an array, the exception applies to this iterator.? However, the Polyspace checker for this rule incorrectly raises a violation when the iterator is incremented. For instance: std::array<int, 3> v;for (auto it = v.begin(); it != v.end(); ++it) { // Incorrect 5-0-15 violation    // ...} The issue also happens for other containers implementing iterators in the same way.

Summary: Pasting large amounts of text into a Stateflow object is very slow with syntax highlighting enabled
Description: Simulink becomes visibly slow when more than a thousand characters of text are added to a Stateflow state or transition if you have syntax highlighting enabled. When you paste an order of magnitude more text than this, the editor stops responding as it completes the highlighting operation.?
Workaround: Use MATLAB Function blocks wherever possible to capture large amounts of code instead of putting it directly on a state or transition label.

Summary: Using the?memcpy function in a Stateflow chart throws assertion failure errors during simulation
Description: When you run a model that uses memcpy ?as a custom code C function in a Stateflow chart and the "Import Custom Code" configuration parameter is enabled, Simulink throws an error similar to: failed assertion in b:\matlab\toolbox\shared\cgxe\src\cgxe\engine\jitengineimpl.cpp at line 883:?In sanityCheckFunctionCalls: VM Internal Error: Found a function 'memcpy' with an unknown input type

Summary: MATLAB might crash if code generation for multi-line anonymous function is terminated by pressing Cancel or Ctrl + C
Description: You might encounter sporadic MATLAB crash during code generation if: Your MATLAB code contains multi-line anonymous functions, and You attempt to terminate code generation by clicking the Cancel  button in the Generate Code  step of the MATLAB Coder app or by pressing Ctrl? +?C .  The crash stack trace might contain either or both of these snippets near the top:  EML::FcnInfo::get_anon_disp_name  EML::LocationToNode::Impl::setSharedDataInLocations    This issue affects MATLAB Coder and MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink.?

Summary: MATLAB web browser might not work behind a proxy server
Description: The MATLAB web browser might fail to load external web pages when a proxy server is being used. For example, the following command might fail to load the mathworks.com page when a proxy server is in use. web('http://mathworks.com')

Summary: MATLAB might crash when opening AUTOSAR model in which blocks are linked to a library
Description: MATLAB might crash when opening an AUTOSAR model if both of these conditions exist in the model: Simulink states, signals, or data stores are mapped to AUTOSAR variables. The mapped states, signals, or data stores are associated with blocks that are linked to a library.

Summary: Physical signals at the same level as the DUT subsystem might generate errors when creating HDL test bench
Description: When physical signals or Simscape lines are present at the same level as the DUT subsystem, it might generate errors when creating the HDL test bench.
Workaround: Select all Simscape blocks and physical signal lines at the DUT level and place them inside a separate Subsystem.

Summary: Example model that implements six-step commutation to control BLDC generates unexpected spikes in motor phase currents
Description: The model?mcb_bldc_sixstep_f28379d.slx , mentioned in the example "Six-Step Commutation of BLDC Motor Using Sensor Feedback", generates? unexpected spikes in motor phase currents because of the current sampling that occurs at PWM transition.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash during Simulink simulation if you launch Simulation Data Inspector or Logic Analyzer for the first time.
Description: ?MATLAB might crash if you perform these steps:  Enable logging for matrix signal in the model.? Run the simulation. While the simulation is running, launch SDI or Logic Analyzer for the first time.
Workaround: Before starting a simulation with matrix signals, launch SDI or the Logic Analyzer.

Summary: Using the Fixed-Point Converter app to convert MATLAB code that already contains fixed-point data types throws an error
Description: Using the Fixed-Point Converter app to convert MATLAB code that already contains fixed-point data types may cause an Unhandled exception ?error. For example, the following MATLAB?code contains fixed-point data type and uses properties of fixed-point data type: if isfi(u)? S = u. Signed;? y = fi(u, S, 20,10);else??y = u;end When this code is converted to fixed point using Fixed-Point Converter app, an?Unhandled exception ?error is?thrown.?
Workaround: Avoid using properties of fixed point types in the code being converted using the Fixed Point Tool.

Summary: Scoping coverage to requirements-based tests causes 0% coverage for subsystem test harnesses
Description: Scoping model coverage results to requirements-based tests in the Simulink Test Manager incorrectly causes the aggregated coverage results to show as 0% achieved when the component-under-test is a subsystem test harness. The scoped coverage results are reported as if no requirements were linked to elements in the component-under-test.

Summary: Running MATLAB on Windows platforms using matlab -batch might?sporadically produce a warning message
Description: On Windows platforms, when you start MATLAB R2020b noninteractively?to run scripts, functions, or statements (using the?matlab ?command with the?-batch ?option), this warning message might appear in the command prompt or the console output log: IMAGE_REL_AMD64_ADDR32NB relocation requires anordered section layout.  The message appears sporadically and originates from a third-party library that is used by the MATLAB execution engine. You can ignore the warning because it does not affect the execution of your code.

Summary: Unhelpful error message when delay balancing is unsuccesful for a multirate model with resource sharing
Description: For a multi-rate model with sharing optimization enabled, the code generator might not be able to share resources and generate the error: Delay balancing unsuccessful because Clock-rate pipelining was applied in a feedback loop and introduced a sample step delay.?Consider increasing the Oversampling factor.  when the following conditions are met: The?SharingFactor? ?block property is specified for a component operating at a slower rate. The specified SharingFactor ?greater than or equal to the factor (rate_slow / base_rate_of_model).  In this case, the code generator cannot share these resources at a faster base rate and therefore generates an error.  For example, in the attached model sharingfailed.slx , the base rate is 1 , and a SharingFactor  of 3  is specified to share the adders inside the sharingfailed/DUT/Enabled Synchronous Subsystem . Since these components are operating at a slower rate and the SharingFactor  of 3 is equal to the factor (rate_slow/base_rate_of_model) = (3/1), sharing optimization is unsuccessful.? ? ? ?
Workaround: To avoid this error, use either of these methods:Increase oversampling factorDecrease the sharing factor?Increase the slow rate sample time?

Summary: Generating tests based on existing coverage data may fail to complete normally for models with Logical Operator blocks having unsatisfiable objectives
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may fail to complete normally when all of the following conditions are met: 1) Test Generation performed based on existing coverage data. 2) At least one logical operator block has all of the following properties:?? a) At least one objective covered in the existing coverage data?? b) At least one Unsatisfiable objective?? c) No vector inputs

Summary: Simulink tests using MATLAB Unit Test framework might fail if signals are logged from referenced models and iterations run in fast restart mode
Description: Simulink tests run in series or parallel using the MATLAB test framework might fail when both of these conditions are true: Signals from referenced models are logged as Simulation Output Tests contains iterations that are run in fast restart mode  The failure diagnostic message shown to the users is similar to:   Verification failed in DemoTests > New Test Suite 1/New Test Case 1(ScriptedIteration=Scripted_Iteration1).  ? ? ---------------------  ? ? Framework Diagnostic:  ? ? ---------------------  ? ? Errors running test case  ? ? --> Errors:  ? ? ? ? ? Invalid Simulink object name: mReferencedModel/Bias.  ? ? ? ? ? The block diagram 'mReferencedModel' is not loaded.  ?

Summary: Incorrect MISRA-C:2012 5.4 violation for undefined macro
Description: If a macro is defined twice with different values, and is undefined between these definitions, then a MISRA-C:2012 5.4 violation incorrectly occurs. This issue happens only if the macro identifier name has more than 31 characters in C90, or more than 63 characters in C99. This example shows a possible scenario in C90: #define MY_MACRO_HAS_DIFFERENT_VALUE_UNDEF 1#undef  MY_MACRO_HAS_DIFFERENT_VALUE_UNDEF#define MY_MACRO_HAS_DIFFERENT_VALUE_UNDEF 2 MY_MACRO_HAS_DIFFERENT_VALUE_UNDEF  is 34 characters long (> 31) and defined twice with different values (1 and 2). This causes a MISRA-C:2012 5.4 violation but the violation should not occur because the macro is undefined in between the two definitions.
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder, Polyspace Bug Finder Server

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Distributed Pipelining adds invalid registers when a model contains non-constant source blocks
Description: When applying Distributed Pipelining to a design with non-constant source blocks, such as a counter, invalid registers can be moved to the output of the block. The invalid registers break functional equivalence to the original model.  Using optimizations like Sharing or Streaming in conjunction with Clock Rate Pipelining will also introduce counters in the design which could be susceptible to this issue.  Checking simulation of the Validation Model can expose whether or not a design experiences this bug.

Summary: Error when launching the Bus Editor from a data dictionary in the Model Explorer
Description: Launching the Bus Editor from a data dictionary in the Model Explorer causes an error when all of these conditions apply: A top-level?Simulink.Bus ?object in the data dictionary specifies Bus  objects from the base workspace as elements.  The nested? Bus ?objects?are present in the base workspace.   The error is: The specified key is not present in this container.
Workaround: In the?Model Hierarchy?pane of the Model Explorer, right-click the data dictionary, then click?Import from Base Workspace.?Select a?Bus?object in the data dictionary, then click the Launch Bus Editor button.If you change the imported Bus?objects, you can export the changes to the base workspace by using one of these options:Copy and paste the modified?Bus?objects from the data dictionary to the base workspace in the Model Explorer.Export the modified?Bus?objects to a .mat or .m file by right-clicking them and selecting Export Selected, then load the file into the base workspace.

Summary: The input function might block callback processing in compiled applications and libraries on Windows
Description: Compiled libraries and applications that call input  might not process graphics or timer callbacks until user input is received. This issue is encountered only  on Windows.?

Summary: MATLAB crash during code generation if?VariantConditionMismatch?parameter is set to warning or error
Description: Model with variant block and if VariantConditionMismatch  parameter is set to warning or error then it may crash during code generation when all the below conditions are met:   Model block with one output block  Reference block connected to bus creator block?    Snippet from the stack trace:??  [? 0] 0x00007fb4d5875603 D:/ProgramFiles/MATLAB/R2020b/bin/glnxa64/builtins/sl_main/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwsl_compile.so+13944323  [? 1] 0x00007fb4d58759a3?D:/ProgramFiles/MATLAB/R2020b/bin/glnxa64/builtins/sl_main/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwsl_compile.so+13945251  [? 2] 0x00007fb4d58782cf?D:/ProgramFiles/MATLAB/R2020b/bin/glnxa64/builtins/sl_main/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwsl_compile.so+13955791  [? 3] 0x00007fb4d51192c6?D:/ProgramFiles/MATLAB/R2020b/bin/glnxa64/builtins/sl_main/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwsl_compile.so+06226630  [? 4] 0x00007fb4d51681da?D:/ProgramFiles/MATLAB/R2020b/bin/glnxa64/builtins/sl_main/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwsl_compile.so+06549978  [? 5] 0x00007fb4d51273e6?D:/ProgramFiles/MATLAB/R2020b/bin/glnxa64/builtins/sl_main/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwsl_compile.so+06284262 _Z17SLCompDiagramImplP8SLRootBD7SimTypeP30SlModelCreationAndCompileHooks12SS_SimStatusRK12CompileFlags+00011190

Summary: Code replacement report might not reflect replacements of MATLAB FFT functions by CMSIS/NE10 libraries
Description: The generated code replacement report might not reflect replacements of MATLAB FFT functions that occur in your code or model. This behavior can occur if all  these conditions are true: During code generation, you replace MATLAB FFT functions (fft ,?ifft , fft2 , ifft2 , fftn , ifftn ) by code replacement library (CRL) functions? You select any of these options for the code replacement library configuration parameter: ARM Cortex-A ARM Cortex-M ARM Cortex-M (optimized) ARM Cortex-M (CMSIS)   This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.

Summary: Simulink may crash when fixed-point logging is enabled and dataflow execution domains contain LMS blocks
Description: Simulink may crash when you simulate a model that meets all of the following conditions: Dataflow execution domains contain LMS blocks The fixed-point logging parameter MinMaxOverflowLogging  is set to MinMaxAndOverflow
Workaround: Disable the dataflow execution domain that contains the LMS block or disable fixed-point logging by setting the logging parameter MinMaxOverflowLogging to UseLocalSettings.

Summary: Analysis with coverage filter errors out when reference configuration set does not have Design Verifier component
Description: Simulink Design Verifier analysis errors out when all below mentioned conditions are met: The model is linked with a reference configuration set. Design Verifier component is missing in the reference configuration set. The Coverage filter CovFilter   option is set as on .  The analysis is called from the command-line. Please note, if the analysis is called from UI, the error would not be observed.  Error message: Error using sldvextract (line 87)  Configuration components cannot be attached to a Simulink.ConfigSetRef  Error in Sldv.Translator/extractSubsystem  Error in Sldv.Translator/initialize  Error in Sldv.Translator/translate  Error in runAnalysisScript (line 31)  status = sldvrun(subsystemList{1}, sldvOption) - Show complete stack trace

Summary: Unexpected errors might show up in the diagnostic viewer when MATLAB Function blocks are used inside Subsystem Reference blocks
Description: When you call a separate MATLAB script from a MATLAB Function block inside Subsystem Reference blocks, Simulink might throw an error similar to: Error in parameter number 3.
Workaround: If you encounter this error, update the model and run again.?

Summary: MATLAB stops responding after updating previously imported requirements in Requirements Editor
Description: When you have apreviously imported requirement set loaded in Requirements Editor,?and ?you use the Update  button?under Requirement Interchange  pane pull-in changes from the original external document,?MATLAB may become unresponsive for a long time after the update is finished. Depending on the number of changes received in the update, the unresponsiveness might continue for over an hour. If MATLAB eventually starts responding, any action that requires a refresh of the Import node property pane might lead to another prolong period of unresponsiveness. Alternatively, if the update is performed using the command-line API, MATLAB stops responding when opening the updated?.slreqx  file in the Requirements Editor, for the same reason.?

Summary: Simulation of model that includes S-Function block generated from function-call subsystem modeling pattern can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash under these conditions:? You create a model that includes a function-call subsystem that connects directly to a root Outport block. The signal line that connects the subsystem and Outport block branches to a block that has an Update  function (block needs to be updated at each major time step). The Unit Delay and Memory blocks are examples of blocks that have an Update  function. The branch can be inside or outside of the subsystem. You generate an S-Function block for the subsystem, as explained in?Accelerate Simulation, Reuse Code, or Protect Intellectual Property by Using S-Function Target . You add the generated S-Function block to a model. You simulate the model that includes the generated S-Function block.
Workaround: In the model that includes the function-call subsystem, insert a Signal Conversion block in one of these locations:Between the subsystem block and the branch.Between the branch and the root Outport block.Between the branch and the block that has the Update function.Set these Signal Conversion block parameters:Set Output to Signal copySelect?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization.Generate the S-Function block for the subsystem.Add the generated S-Function block to a model.Simulate the model that includes the S-Function block.

Summary: ReqIF ID values might change between revisions when exporting to ReqIF
Description: When you make changes to a requirement set in Simulink Requirements that has already been exported to a ReqIF file and then export it again, some existing ReqIF ID values might change. For example, in the first export, the ReqIF?replicationID  might have some value "a", and, when changes are made to the requirement set and exported a second time, the  replicationID ?might change to some value "b".  This might happen for requirement sets that are created in or imported to Simulink Requirements.

Summary: Using the Run on Target button for some models generates an error executing 'SetParam' command
Description: For some model constructs, using the Run on Target  button generates the external mode error: Executing the 'SetParam' command produced the following error:Invalid Simulink Object name.   An example model construct that may demonstrate this issue is a Switch block with two or more inputs from Bus blocks. When this issue occurs, some parameters associated with hidden or invalid blocks are downloaded to the real-time application. The real-time application may run briefly then error. If run repeatedly, this issue may cause MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Embedded Coder Model Advisor Check errors out for bus signals that are switched by a Switch block
Description: When code is generated for a model that contains a Switch block that switches bus signals defined by a workspace bus object, and the?Embedded Coder Model Advisor Checks are enabled, ?the Check ID:  mathworks. codegen. BlockSpecificQuestionableFxptOperations  (Identify blocks that generate expensive fixed-point and saturation code) errors out with the message "Unrecognized data type name ".
Workaround: Use a Bus Creator block instead of a workspace bus object, or use a Multiport Switch block instead of a Switch block.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when using Simulink buses in a MATLAB Function block
Description: If you use Simulink buses in a MATLAB Function block, MATLAB might crash and produce this message: JITRuntimeInstanceInfo::setIdcInfo(SF::CDR_InstanceDependentCompiledInfo*) ..../matlab/toolbox/stateflow/stateflow/sf_sfun.mexa64

Summary: Subsystem file with assocated test harness errors during export and comparision
Description: A subsystem file associated with a test harness errors when you either export the subsystem file to a older release, or compare the subsystem file to another slx  or mdl  file. The error message is of the form: Invalid name <subsystem file name> specified. Specify a saved subsystem file.?
Workaround: Remove the test harness from the subsystem file and then export or compare them separately.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code and MATLAB might produce different results when accessing a property from a cell array containing objects
Description: Generated code and MATLAB might produce different answers if?all  the following conditions are true: A MATLAB function accepts as input a cell array containing objects The function accesses a property of an element of this cell array  For example, this issue occurs when a MATLAB function contains an access of the form x{n}.prop  where?x  is an input parameter to the function and x  is a cell array containing objects. For example, suppose that you defined a MATLAB class MyClass  that has a property prop . The following MATLAB code is affected by this issue: function out = fooSimplified(n)a = MyClass;a.prop = 42; b = MyClass;% You do not define b.prop collection = {a, b};out = blah(collection, n);end function out = blah(collection, n)% collection{2}.prop is not definedout = collection{n}.prop;end This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB Functions in Stateflow. ?
Workaround: This issue occurs only if the property being accessed is not defined. To avoid this problem, pre-initialize the cell array value before accessing it.

Summary: Opening a referenced model that contains a block connected to bus ports and linked to a library might crash Simulink
Description: Opening a model referenced by a System Composer component might crash Simulink when all of these conditions apply:? The referenced model contains a block linked to a library. The linked block connects to bus ports. The referenced model is opened in the context of the System Composer component that references it.
Workaround: Open the referenced model in a separate window as a top model before opening it in the context of the System Composer component that references it.If you receive a warning about outdated profiles when you open the architecture model, also perform these steps before opening the referenced model.On the Modeling?tab of the architecture model, click?Import > Manage.In the dialog box, select the out-of-date profile, then click Remove.In the MATLAB Command Window, programmatically remove the profile. For example, after you define the referenced model (refmodel?and outdated profile (outdatedprofile),?enter:archmodel = get_param(refmodel, 'SystemComposer');archmodel.removeProfile(outdatedprofile);?Save the architecture model.Close and reopen the architecture model.

Summary: ModelCoveragePlugin used with Project errors or returns incorrect coverage metrics when running tests using the MATLAB Unit TestRunner
Description: If you are using sltest.plugins.ModelCoveragePlugin ?with a Project where your model is added onto the path only when the model loads, the ModelCoveragePlugin  either issues an error or does not provide the right coverage metrics. The error or incorrect coverage metrics occurs only when you are using?matlab.unittest.TestRunner ?to run the tests.  The ModelCoveragePlugin expects that the model continues to stay on path even after the project is closed, so it is unable to return the right coverage information, which can lead to an error.
Workaround: Before running the tests using matlab.unittest.TestRunner and using ModelCoveragePlugin, load the project by using matlab.project.loadProject or matlab.project.openProject either manually or in the appropriate script.?

Summary: Reading selected channels from an MDF-file returns a timetable with incorrect property values
Description: When reading MDF data from a number of selected channels in a channel group, the VariableDescriptions  and VariableUnits  properties of the returned timetable might contain incorrect values.

Summary: MATLAB occasionally crashes when opening a library block attached to a Bus Selector block that contains out-of-date bus information
Description: MATLAB might crash when navigating through library blocks on the canvas. If the library block is connected to a Bus Selector block that outputs a non-virtual bus and the following is true:? The Simulink.BusObject contains bus information that does not match the signals selected in the Bus Selector block. The Bus Selector block is inside a library subsystem.  MATLAB crashes with a stack trace similar to: [? 0] 0x00007fff69b47579? ? ? ? ? ? C:\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\sl_types.dll+00357753 SLTypeSys::ImmediateElementAccess::getUnitId+00000041  [? 1] 0x00007fff69b41747? ? ? ? ? ? C:\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\sl_types.dll+00333639 DtGetDataTypeElementUnitId+00000039  [? 2] 0x00007fff74c4bf28? ? ? ?C:\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+04308776 DiscreteBlockExecWrapper::getAdaptorTerminate+00019768  [? 3] 0x00007fff7510f156? ? ? ?C:\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+09302358 SetupToQueryCompiledInfo+00146870  [? 4] 0x00007fff757a668d? ? ? ?C:\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+16213645 LineTracer::getNumPortsForConnTokenBlock+00000045  [? 5] 0x00007fff757a8b15? ? ? ?C:\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+16222997 LineTracer::walkAcrossBlockToNextLine+00000021
Workaround: To update the bus signal information, resave the library model containing the Bus Selector block.

Summary: MATLAB unexpectedly terminates during accelerator mode simulation of a model containing blocks from a specific support package
Description: MATLAB unexpectedly terminates during simulation w hen simulating a model under the following conditions: Using accelerator mode for simulation. The model is configured for any of the following support packages: Communications Toolbox Support Package for Xilinx Zynq-Based Radio  HDL Coder Support Package for Intel SoC Devices  SoC Blockset Support Package for Intel Devices  SoC Blockset Support Package for Xilinx Devices  Embedded Coder Support Package for Xilinx Zynq Platform  Deep Learning HDL Toolbox Support Package for Intel FPGA and SoC Devices  Deep Learning HDL Toolbox Support Package for Xilinx FPGA and SoC Devices  Vision HDL Toolbox Support Package for Xilinx Zynq-Based Hardware  SoC Blockset Support Package for Embedded Linux Devices  Communications Toolbox Support Package for Analog Devices ADALM-Pluto Radio  HDL Coder Support Package for Xilinx Zynq Platform  Communications Toolbox Support Package for USRP Embedded Series Radio    MATLAB might unexpectedly terminate without any diagnostic or error message.
Workaround: Use any one of these workarounds:Use normal mode instead of accelerator mode for simulation.Delete the slprj folder in between simulations.Force Simulink to use Classic Accelerator Mode instead of Just-In-Time Accelerator by using set_param(0, 'GlobalUseClassicAccelMode', 'on').

Summary: Error with exporting the test cases generated by Simulink Design Verifier to Simulink Test to a Signal Builder harness
Description: Exporting the test cases generated by Simulink Design Verifier to a Signal Builder harness throws an error if the model contains an inport with array bus type.The error message shown is : Brace indexing is not supported for variables of this type.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect variant condition propagation with referenced protected model
Description: When a model containing Variant Source/Sink is protected, only the active variant configuration is expected to be retained. However, when we refer a protected model via a model block in a top model you can find that variant condition propagated out through the interface of the protected model.This is an incorrect behaviour because a variant configuration in a protected model cannot be modified. This issue can happen when all the below conditions are met. You are protecting a model containing variant blocks that can propagate variant conditions.? You have another model in which you are referring to this protected model via a model block.  In such scenarios, when the top model is simulated, an error message is displayed to define the variant control of variant blocks in the protected model. If you define the variant control value, you can see that value of the variant control parameter affecting the active/inactive signals coming out of protected model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Specify output when source is unconnected may be ignored for value mapped to name "ground"
Description: Variant Subsystem with?Specify output when source is unconnected ?ignores the user specified value and uses "0" when the following conditions are met: Specify output when source is unconnected? is selected on the Outport block inside Variant Subsystem Name of the variable used to specify the value when the source is unconnected is "ground", which is defined in the workspace

Summary: Rapid accelerator build with MinGW compiler fails when model reference hierarchy contains protected models
Description: If the MinGW compiler is the default MEX compiler, generating a protected model does not produce the model_capi_host.obj  file. If a top model? with tunable struct  parameters references the protected model, a rapid accelerator simulation of the top model produces an error during the building of host-based C API.

Summary: Running R2015a test cases with mapped inputs in R2020a might fail in Test Manager
Description: If you map inputs to model elements, running a test case in R2015a might fail when run from R2020a using the Run Tests in Multiple Releases workflow. Simulink Test throws the following error: Undefined function 'join' for input arguments of type 'cell'

Summary: Analysis may terminate with an?Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier back end error message
Description: Analysis might terminate without producing the results. When you run the analysis with a user interface, it displays the following error message: Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier back end . When you run the analysis in the?Test Generation ?mode, the error message displayed at the MATLAB Command Window is: Test generation produced errors?  Stack trace observed from R2018a: src/tempo/instance.c:807 tempo_error=tempo_err_usr    src/tempo/tempo_saturate.c:260 tempo_error=tempo_err_usr    src/data_graph/rat_prop_bt.c:702 err=data_graph_err_internal    src/data_graph/graph_rat_prop-new.c:110 err=data_graph_err_internal   When this issue is hit, the stack trace will contain rat_prop_bt.c  with a number between 690 and 720

Summary: Polyspace remote analysis with MATLAB Parallel Server fails with error: Unable to create package polyspace.zip
Description: If you send an analysis job consisting of more than 1000 C files to a MATLAB Parallel Server cluster, the analysis can fail with the error message: Unable to create package polyspace.zip  The error typically happens when the files are packaged before  submission to the cluster, but in some cases, the error can also happen post-analysis when downloading results from the MATLAB Job scheduler (the default scheduler packaged with MATLAB Parallel Server).
Workaround: Reduce the size of the analysis job. For instance, you can analyze individual components of a project instead of the entire project or use automatic modularization strategies provided with Polyspace if possible. For details, see?Reduce Memory Usage and Time Taken by Polyspace Analysis.
Products: Polyspace Code Prover, Polyspace Code Prover Server

Summary: Simulation range checking might not throw an error for a value that cannot be represented as a double
Description: When the value of a Stateflow data cannot be encoded as a double type without loss of precision, the Simulation range checking  diagnostic might fail to throw an out of range error or warning. For example, when the maximum value for a Stateflow data is set to 2^53 and the actual data's value is set to 2^53 + 1, 2^53 + 1 is rounded down to 2^53 as a double type. Simulation range checking  will deem the actual value of the data is equal to its maximum and fail to throw an error.

Summary: Code compilation failure due to missing AUTOSAR?Rte_Read?access function in generated code
Description: Code generation for a root inport in an AUTOSAR model fails to emit an Rte_Read  access function if all of the following conditions are true: The root inport is read in an initialize subsystem. The root inport branches in the initialize subsystem. The AUTOSAR data access mode for the root inport is ExplicitReceive .?  Code compilation fails due to the missing access function.

Summary: Test execution compiles model multiple times
Description: If a test case has iterations and parameter overrides, then executing the test case compiles the model multiple times for each iteration.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulation mismatches with may be observed when the Initial trigger signal state? of trigger port is not set to?Compatibility (no trigger on first evaluation)
Description: Trigger port is evaluated in the first cycle if, Trigger type ?is rising  and Initial trigger signal state ?is zero/negative  and the trigger port is driven by high value, Trigger type ?is rising  and Initial trigger signal state  is zero/positive  and value driven to trigger port in the first cycle is high.   HDL code generated with the above options may be incorrect and cause simulation mismatches with Simulator tools.

Summary: Error when calling TLC library function LibBlockInputSignalAllowScalarExpandedExpr
Description: When LibBlockInputSignalAllowScalarExpandedExpr  is invoked from a custom TLC file, Simulink code generation will fail with the following error message:Argument 4 to 'Block. SetInputAllowScalarExpandedExpr' must be a 'boolean'

Summary: Changed ports in an observer model do not highlight correctly in Manager Observer dialog box
Description: If you create a new observer model with a given port configuration, save the model, and then change the port wiring before closing the observer model, the port links do not appear to be updated in the Manage Observer dialog box. Although the observer connections are updated and appear correctly in the model, both the old and new connections are highlighted in the Manage Observer dialog box.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Delay introduced in locally upsampled regions leads to mismatch in validation model
Description: For models that have manual locally upsampled regions, delay balancing might not properly manage delay and/or phasing if latency is introduced. Delay can be introduced from various optimizations like pipeline insertion, adaptive pipelining, etc. Delay balancing can erroneously determine the resulting delay introduced in the upsampled region when propagating across a downsample operation (i.e. Rate Transition as Zero-Order Hold). This results in a numeric mismatch in the generated HDL code and assertions when simulating the validation model.

Summary: nufft and nufftn can return incorrect results for sufficiently large input sizes when both the sample and query points are nonuniformly spaced
Description: For input data with at least 32 sample points, and where both the sample points and ?query points are nonuniformly spaced, nufft  and nufftn  can return incorrect results in the following cases: For nufft , the issue is present only for complex-valued input data.  For nufftn , the issue is present either for complex-valued input data or when both the sample and query points are specified using cell arrays.

Summary: Property Inspector is not correctly updated after link is deleted
Description: When you have the Requirements Browser open in a Simulink model,?if you select a requirement or a link, the P roperty Inspector shows the information for the previously selected item instead of the currently selected item. This situation occurs for either of these cases:? When you select a requirement and, in the Property Inspector, click?Links > ?Show in Links View , the Requirements Browser sets the?View ?to Links ?and highlights the link. The Property Inspector still shows the information for the previously selected requirement. When you select a link and, in the Property Inspector, click?Properties > ?Source ?or?Properties >? Destination ?that corresponds to a requirement, the Requirements Browser sets the View ?to Requirements  and highlights the requirement. The Property Inspector still shows the information for the previously selected link.  During these cases, when some information is changed, the Property Inspector displays stale information. For example, if a link is deleted, the Property Inspector still displays the stale link.
Workaround: Click Refresh.orSelect another requirement or link item, and then select the original item.

Summary: Cross compilation failures related to certain Navigation Toolbox and Robotics System Toolbox functions during codegen
Description: Navigation Toolbox and Robotics System Toolbox functions that use the internal nonlinear program solver (including matchScans, lidarSLAM, optimizePoseGraph, inverseKinematics, etc.) under the hood may encounter issues during code generation when the target platform is different from the MATLAB host platform (i.e. during cross-compilation). Specifically, the generated code may contain platform-dependent timing function calls that will not work on the target platform.
Workaround: Install MATLAB on the target platform or in a machine that has the same operating system as the target platform, then rerun the codegen script.
Products: Robotics System Toolbox, Navigation Toolbox, ROS Toolbox

Summary: Simulation 3D Vision Detection Generator block displays rapidly flashing dialog boxes
Description: Interacting with the Simulation 3D Vision Detection Generator block causes a dialog box to open and close in rapid succession several times, though the block remains fully functional.

Summary: Sampling internal nodes of PLL model makes the model?unstable and runs extremely slow
Description: If you sample one of the internal nodes of a PLL model, for example, the loop filter output, using a Zero-Order Hold block, the PLL model becomes unstable. The model slows down considerably. This behavior is not dependent on the sample time chosen on the Zero-Order Hold block.

Summary: Event bindings changed in reference models are not reflected in top models.
Description: When event bindings in a top model are overridden by new or updated bindings in reference models, the top model compilation might fail to reflect the new reference model bindings, or MATLAB might crash.
Workaround: The binding can be updated by removing and re-creating the affected aperiodic partition:Set the "Schedule As" parameter to "Sample time" for the aperiodic partition subsystem in the reference modelUpdate diagram for the top modelRestore the "Schedule As" parameter to "Aperiodic Partition" for the subsystem affected in step 1.Update diagram for the top modelConfigure any bindings to events.Note that this procedure will also remove all other scheduling information for this partition.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when data type of Simulink.LookupTable table data values differ from specified data type
Description: MATLAB might crash when the table data (Table ) contained in a Simulink.LookupTable  object has a Value  with a data type different from the type specified by the?Data type  property of Table . The crash might occur when these conditions exist:The model has Model blocks with hierarchy depth of at least 3.? The model uses data dictionaries.? The Breakpoints specification  property of the Simulink.LookupTable  specification is set to 'Reference '.?
Workaround: For the Table property of the Simulink.LookupTable object, leave the data type of Value unspecified or set the data type to the type specified in Data type.

Summary: Merge block is incorrectly linearized to zero when in a specific configset setting and directly driven by concatenate block
Description: When a Merge block is directly driven by a Concatenate block, and model diagnostics setting?Detect multiple driving blocks executing at the same time steps  is set to none ,? the Merge block will be incorrectly linearized to 0.?
Workaround: Option 1: Through UI?Set Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Data Validity > Advanced >?Detect multiple driving blocks executing at the same time steps to Warning or Error.?Option 2: Through command line API:set_param(modelName, 'MergeDetectMultiDrivingBlocksExec', 'Warning'); or?set_param(modelName, 'MergeDetectMultiDrivingBlocksExec', 'Error')Neither option will work if the merge block is in a model reference .?

Summary: SoC Builder tool encounters synthesis error for Intel boards on Windows platform
Description: SoC Builder tool encounters a synthesis error: 'tee' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file  for Intel boards on Windows platform. This error is due to the non-availability of tee.exe file in the Windows folder.
Workaround: 1. Download the tee.exe file for Windows from this link:?http://ss64.net/westlake/nt/tee.zip2. Unzip and copy the EXE file to the C:\Windows folder.?3. Add the folder path to the System variables pane as Windows environment variables.Right-click the Computer icon and choose Properties, or in Windows Control Panel, choose System. Choose Advanced system settings. On the Advanced tab, click Environment Variables. In the System variables pane, select the Path variable and click Edit. In the Edit environment variable?pane, click New?to add a new folder path.3. Run the SoC Builder tool.

Summary: Type mismatch error when converting a division operation between non-scalar data using the Fixed-Point Converter app
Description: When converting a division operation between non-scalar data using the Fixed-Point Converter app, a Type mismatch  error may be thrown. For example:? function y = fcn(n, w)?  if n == 3  ? ? den = w(1:4, 1:4);  ? ? num = w(:, 2:end);  else  ? ? den = 1;  ? ? num = w(1:2,1);  end  y = num / den;  end?  The size of variables num  and den  are not fixed when the above code is compiled. This leads to a type mismatch error during fixed point conversion.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might produce different answers when indexing is done by using half datatype and the colon operator.?
Description: Generated code might produce different answers when indexing is done by using half  type and the colon operator. When half  type values are used for indexing operations with colon and the indexing value is greater than the flintmax  value for half  type, which is 2048, then the output of the MATLAB and generated C/C++ code might differ due to their rounding behaviors.? This error can occur in the following code snippet: out = in(half(1):half(2500))?  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks, or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.

Summary: Fixed-Point Converter app assigns unnecessarily large fraction length for some exponent expressions
Description: The Fixed-Point Converter  app assigns an unnecessarily large fraction length for some exponent expressions when a constant exponent value is stored in a local variable. For example: This code stores a constant exponent value in the local variable S :? ? ?S = 5;? ? ?out = in+2^S;  This code uses a structure containing a constant exponent value:? ? ?S = coder.load('expvalue.mat'); % Loads a structure with exponent ? ? ?out = in+2^S.exponent;
Workaround: To work around the issue, you can either:Use the constant value directly as the exponent. For example: ? ? ?out = in+2^5;Use an input variable to define the exponent. For example:? ? ?function out = fcn(in, S)? ? ? ? out = in+2^S;? ? ?end

Summary: MATLAB crashes when dragging ports on?an Enabled Synchronous Subsystem block
Description: Dragging a port on an Enabled Synchronous Subsystem block with a mouse causes MATLAB to crash.Snippet from the MATLAB crash:  [??0] 0x00007ff9a135e410???C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2020a\ bin\ win64\ mf0. dll+00451600 mf::zero::ModelElement::Impl::isProxy+00000000  [??1] 0x00007ff9a11fac01 C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2020a\ bin\ win64\ mm_interface. dll+00568321 sl::mfzero::interfaceModel::Port::Impl::setIndexOne+00000049  [??2] 0x00007ff9866ef822 C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2020a\ bin\ win64\ sl_lang_blocks. dll+07796770 PortInterface::CopyHandler::willUseCopyInfoForAdd+00088242  [??3] 0x00007ff98671a71c C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2020a\ bin\ win64\ sl_lang_blocks. dll+07972636 sl::interfaceModel::modify::transferMastershipTo+00142156  [??4] 0x00007ff9866f78bf C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2020a\ bin\ win64\ sl_lang_blocks. dll+07829695 sl::interfaceModel::modify::renumberPort+00000063  [??5] 0x00007ff987f34e92 C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2020a\ bin\ win64\ libmwsimulink. dll+11554450 SLGlue::renumberPort+00000082  [??6] 0x00007ff9775dec13 C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2020a\ bin\ win64\ sl_editor. dll+08186899 Simulink::FlexiblePortPlacement::doesSimulinkSupportFlexiblePortPlacement+00206307  [??7] 0x00007ff9775c11ad C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2020a\ bin\ win64\ sl_editor. dll+08065453 Simulink::FlexiblePortPlacement::doesSimulinkSupportFlexiblePortPlacement+00084861

Summary: Export-function error when creating a protected model from some models with a scoped Simulink function
Description: Simulink incorrectly throws an error when you create a protected model from a model that meets all of the following criteria: The Total number of instances allowed per top model  parameter?is set to?Multiple ?in Model Settings > Model Referencing ; The model is not configured to be an export-function model;?and The model contains a scoped Simulink function placed in a non-root hierarchy level.  An error message like the following is displayed when saving a protected version of the model: Model 'myModel' containing Simulink function 'myFcn' must follow export-function rules.

Summary: MATLAB might crash during floating-point target code generation if both?ClockRatePipelineOutputPorts?and?ScalarizePorts?are set to 'on'
Description: MATLAB might crash while generating HDL Code from a subsystem if all  these conditions are satisfied:? NativeFloatingPoint  or target-code generation is selected ClockRatePipelineOutputPorts  is set to 'on'  ScalarizePorts  is set to 'on'

Summary: Assertion generated during HDL code generation with Optimization Report turned on
Description: When generating HDL code from a Simulink model with complex types and the Optimization Report is enabled, an assertion might be generated: failed assertion in util_hdl/HDLTypeUtils.cpp at line? 103 : ? In TypeProps: Found unhandled type:? 'unknown type'  There is certain criteria required in order for this to be hit: Optimization Report is enabled Distributed Pipelining is enabled Resource Report is enabled Simulink model contains a MATLAB Function block or Stateflow chart Model contains complex types

Summary: When running simulation for a Simulink model containing a deep learning network in either a MATLAB function block or a Deep Learning block, if the user does not have the correct support package, the thrown error is incorrect.
Description: Performing simulation or code generation for a deep learning model without the requisite MATLAB Coder Interface or GPU Coder Interface for Deep Learning Libraries support package returns an incorrect error.? In such situations, you may observe the following error messages in the Diagnostic Viewer: Simulink does not have enough information to determine output sizes for this block. If you think the errors below are inaccurate, try specifying types for the block inputs and/or sizes for the block outputs.  Component:MATLAB Function | Category:Coder error  Undefined function 'coder.loadMatObj' for input arguments of type 'char'.    Function 'MATLAB Function' (#25.56.96), line 3, column 13:  "coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('alexnet')"  Launch diagnostic report.

Summary: Missing cast may cause warning when building generated code
Description: When casting a multi-dimensional array to single dimension pointer variable, MATLAB Coder drops an essential cast which may cause warning(s) when building the generated code. The following code snippet generated by MATLAB Coder shows an example of this issue: ? ? int multi_dim_array[2][4];? ??  ? ? int (*single_dim_pointer)[8];? ??  ? ? single_dim_pointer = &multi_dim_array[0][0];  Compiler issues warning message "assignment to ˇ®int (*)[8]ˇŻ from incompatible pointer type ˇ®int *ˇŻ "? when building this code.  To avoid this warning, MATLAB Coder needs to insert a cast to the right-hand side of the assignment: ? ? single_dim_pointer = (int (*)[8]) &multi_dim_array;

Summary: MATLAB crashes when using Teledyne DALSA hardware with the dalsa adaptor
Description: When using the dalsa ?adaptor with videoinput  or imaqtool  to connect to?Teledyne DALSA cameras and framegrabbers, MATLAB might crash and return the following crash log: Stack Trace (from fault):[  0] 0x00007ffddd3e8470 matlab\toolbox\imaq\imaq\imaqmex.mexw64+00099440[  1] 0x00007ffddd432a5e matlab\toolbox\imaq\imaq\imaqmex.mexw64+00404062 mwboost::serialization::singleton_module::get_lock+00000382[  2] 0x00007ffddd437948 matlab\toolbox\imaq\imaq\imaqmex.mexw64+00424264 mexFunction+00001384[  3] 0x00007ffdbafaa289 matlab\bin\win64\libmex.dll+00565897 mexfile::Inspector::needs_upgrade+00015977
Workaround: For the following MATLAB releases, a fix is provided as an update to the?Image Acquisition Toolbox Support Package for Teledyne DALSA Sapera Hardware.To install the updated version, start Add-On Explorer:On the MATLAB Home tab, in the Resources section, click Add-Ons > Get Hardware Support Packages , orExecute supportPackageInstaller command in MATLAB command window.MATLAB R2020bOpen Support Package Installer and update the 'Teledyne DALSA Sapera Hardware' support package to version 20.2.1 by uninstalling the support package and reinstalling it.MATLAB R2020aOpen Support Package Installer and update the 'Teledyne DALSA Sapera Hardware' support package to version 20.1.1 by uninstalling the support package and reinstalling it.?

Summary: ros2genmsg function returns error: Could NOT find PythonInterp
Description: If you have a CMake version less than 3.13.0, you might see the following error when trying to build an ROS2 custom message with the?ros2genmsg  function: CMake Error at C:/Program Files/CMake/share/cmake-3.10/Modules/FindPackageHandleStandardArgs.cmake:137 (message):Could NOT find PythonInterp: Found unsuitable version "1.4", but required is at least "3" (found C:/Users//AppData/Local/Microsoft/WindowsApps/python3.exe)

Summary: Matrix multiplication in Simulink causes long code generation times for large matrices
Description: When generating code for large matrices, HDL Coder can take a long time and use lots of memory during code generation. Depending on the matrix sizes and types, code generation can take upwards of 10's of hours.
Workaround: There are a few settings that can be tweaked to help speed up code generation.Turn off model generation layout.>> hdlset_param(bdroot, 'AutoPlace', 'off')Disable model generation. (not recommended if using HDL test bench or Cosimulation model)>> hdlset_param(bdroot, 'GenerateModel', 'off')Change Dot Product Strategy to Serial Multiply-Accumulate. This will also only use 1 DSP per dot product operation in the generated code.Select the matrix multiply block in the Simulink model and run the following command.>> hdlset_param(gcb, 'DotProductStrategy', 'Serial Multiply-Accumulate')

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unit Delay block with non-virtual bus as input generates incorrect HDL code
Description: Generating HDL code from a Unit Delay block with a non-virtual bus as input and a non-zero initial condition results in a simulation mismatch.
Workaround: Consider using Unit Delay or Integer Delay with zero initial value for code generation.

Summary: Shared library conflicts cause standalone applications to fail in some Windows based deployment environments
Description: Shared library conflicts cause standalone applications to fail in some Windows based deployment environments. When a standalone application created by MATLAB Compiler is running on a Windows system, the default Windows library search order is followed. This causes conflict in some deployment environments where DLLs shipped with MATLAB Runtime take lower precedence over the same DLLs present in other directories such as the Windows System  directory. As a result, standalone applications fail in certain environments. The conflict manifests itself as follows: The standalone application might report DLL library related error message. The standalone application runs fine on most machines but only fails on certain machines. The standalone application can run successfully when copied to <MATLAB Runtime Root>\bin\win64  and be launched from there in an environment where it fails.

Summary: Generated Verilog code for Moore charts due to mix of blocking (=) and non-blocking (<=) assignments in the same process
Description: When generating Verilog code from a Moore  chart using HDL Coder, the generated code contains a mix of blocking (=)?and non-blocking (<=) assignment statements?in the same always process block. This can result in a STARC violation as the STARC coding style recommends that an always process block contains either blocking or nonblocking assignments.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Undefined simulation and code generation behavior might occur when signal that drives two Outport blocks resolves to a Simulink.Signal object
Description: Undefined simulation and code generation behavior might occur for the following modeling pattern:  A signal resolves to a  Simulink.Signal  object with a non-Auto storage class and a non-empty initial value.?  The signal branches and feeds into two or more Outport blocks. This bug can still occur if one or more virtual blocks are present between the driving signal and any of the Outport blocks.  No error is reported for this modeling pattern during model compilation.
Workaround: The issue might be avoided by inserting non-virtual blocks between the driving signal and each of the destination Outport blocks.

Summary: MATLAB stops responding during model simulation if protected model includes FMU Import block
Description: Simulating a protected model might cause MATLAB to stop responding if the protected model is exported from a Simulink model that contains an? FMU Import block.? Examplary Stack Trace (from fault): [? 0] 0x00007ff90e9ca831 C:\Work\MATLAB\fmudemo_vdp_msp.mexw64+00043057 mexfilerequiredapiversion+00009265  [? 1] 0x00007ff90e9c8a7d C:\Work\Documents\MATLAB\fmudemo_vdp_msp.mexw64+00035453 mexfilerequiredapiversion+00001661  [? 2] 0x00007ff90e9c7288 C:\Work\Documents\MATLAB\fmudemo_vdp_msp.mexw64+00029320 mexFunction+00025224  [? 3] 0x00007ff86de5e541 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\sl_lang_blocks.dll+06612289 SetSFcnExportsFunctionCalls+00030865  [? 4] 0x00007ff86de71c33 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\sl_lang_blocks.dll+06691891 CallCMexSFcnSetupRuntimeResources+00000083  [? 5] 0x00007ff86ff2937d C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+15045501 slSetStateflowChartAccessesModelInfo+00019869  [? 6] 0x00007ff86fda3bdd C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+13450205 BlockSetLocation+00000477  [? 7] 0x00007ff86fc96d85 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+12348805 Simulink::ProtectedLibrary::protectLibrary+00322885  [? 8] 0x00007ff86ec993a9 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\sl_lang_exec_blocks.dll+00168873 ModelRefExecBlock::SetupRuntimeResources+00000089  ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink produces incorrect result from atomic subsystem that outputs same signal multiple times with different initial values
Description: A Simulink model might produce a silent wrong answer when all the following conditions are true: The model has an atomic subsystem with multiple output ports. Two or more of the output ports have the same source but different initial value settings.? A Bus Creator block groups the signals from the two output ports into a virtual bus. Then, a Bus Selector block selects the two signals and feeds them to different destinations.
Workaround: Add a Signal Conversion block before one of the Outport blocks in the atomic subsystem.

Summary: Performance regression caused during code generation for models with large data set
Description: You will encounter slow code generation under all these conditions:?Model contains data that use ExportToFile  storage class. Data size is large.

Summary: Subsystem Reference block's port properties are lost on reloading the model or if the contents of the Subysystem Reference block are refreshed
Description: Port properties (such as tag, description, signal info etc.) specified for a Subsystem Reference block are lost if you either reload the model or refresh the Subsystem Reference block's contents. A refresh happens if you modify and save the contents of the block. These properties are also lost for a Subsystem block when it is converted to a Subsystem Reference block.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Assertion generated when bus input with input port parameter Latch input by delaying outside signal selected is input to a Triggered Subsystem
Description: The?Latch input by delaying outside signal ?block parameter on the input port is not supported for HDL code generation when input to a Triggered Subsystem input port. For scalar, vector, and complex signals, an error message is generated that indicates the?Latch input by delaying outside signal  parameter is not supported. However, for bus signals, when this parameter is selected on the input port, the following cryptic error is generated: Assertion failed: dom_pir_core/pirsignal.cpp :501: Error receiver is already connected to signal
Workaround: Clear the?Latch input by delaying outside signal check box on Triggered Subsystem input ports as it is not supported for HDL code generation.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when?linearizing Interpolation Using Prelookup block with lookup table object
Description: MATLAB might crash when linearizing the Interpolation Using Prelookup  block when the following conditions are true: ?The TableSpecification  parameter is set to Lookup table object  ?The algorithm used for linearization is set to blockbyblock.  ?For example via MATLAB command: linOption = linearizeOptions('LinearizationAlgorithm','blockbyblock');   ?A linear approximation of an?Interpolation Using Prelookup? block in model?mdl ?is obtained. For example by executing MATLAB command: linearize   (mdl  ,blockpath ,linOption)

Summary: Some model hierarchies might throw an incorrect error when simulated
Description: Simulating a model hierarchy returns an incorrect error when all of the following conditions apply: The model hierarchy has at least three levels of referenced models. Referenced models have the configuration setting Propagate all signal labels out of the model ?selected. In a mid-level model, an output signal of a Model block has a label. The output signal passes through a Goto block and a From block then connects to an output port.  The error is: Computed interface information must match saved interface information for referenced model '<model name>'.?  ?Update diagram and resave the model '<model name>'.   For model hierarchies that meet the conditions, updating the diagram and saving the model does not fix the error.
Workaround: Identify any labeled output signals of a Model block that pass through a Goto block and a From block then connect to an output port.For each of these signals, right-click the signal line that connects to the output port, then select Properties. In the dialog box, select Show propagated signals.

Summary: Starting a standalone application on macOS systems by double-clicking does not generate error messages
Description: When you start a standalone application created using MATLAB Compiler on macOS systems by double-clicking the app, any potential errors are not displayed in pop-up dialog boxes.

Summary: Model Advisor checks do not report Reserved Keywords
Description: Model Advisor checks checking for Reserved  Keywords do not report C Reserved  Keywords,?C++ Reserved  Keywords, Reserved  Keywords for Code Generation but reports only MATLAB  keywords.

Summary: Code generation time increases significantly for complex designs with reporting features
Description: For large complex designs, HDL code generation time will experience an overhead when reporting features such as traceability and model web view are enabled on the Simulink model. These features consume almost 50% of the total code generation time.
Workaround: Turn off Generate traceability report?and Generate model Web view?features to improve the HDL code generation time.

Summary: The pcdownsample function causes build errors during code generation
Description: The pcdownsample  function causes build errors when performing code generation for MATLAB Coder supported hardware interfaces.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Conditionally executed subsystem blocks that propagate variable-size signals, when enabled might return incorrect numerical results
Description: Normal, accelerator and rapid accelerator simulation might cause incorrect output values for models that meet all these conditions: The model contains a conditionally executed subsystem block. The model propagates variable-size signals into the conditionally executed subsystem block  The conditionally executed subsystem block Propagate sizes of variable-size signals  is set to Only when execution is resumed  for an Action Port.  Only when enabling  for Enable and Trigger or Function-Call Port.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Calling a function with the same input types from within and outside a parfor, with a low stack size limit, might result in a mismatch between MATLAB and generated MEX code outputs
Description: When using a function with the same input types from within and outside a parfor, ?you might see answer mismatches between MATLAB and generated MEX code when all of the following conditions are true: The code configuration parameter StackUsageMax  is enabled. The code includes a stack allocated local variable which exceeds the StackUsageMax  parameter size. The same function call from within and outside a parfor  loop with the same input types.
Workaround: Convert the parfor loop containing the shared function call to a for?loop.

Summary: Code Generation Advisor does not run on a model that has a Polyspace options object in its configset
Description: You cannot run Code Generation Advisor on a model if a Polyspace options object is attached to its configset.? The process exits with this message: Abnormal exit: Dot indexing is not supported for variables of this type.

Summary: When starting a MATLAB process on Windows via certain external interfaces, the MATLAB process may stall or hang.
Description: When using MATLAB C++, Java, or Python engine APIs to start a MATLAB process, the process might stall or hang. The stall or hang might appear as a timeout while trying to find graphics devices during MATLAB graphics initialization.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: Polyspace analysis stops with error: declaration is incompatible with "void OSEK_polyspace_ActivateTask(OSEK_polyspace_task_type)"
Description: When you analyze your C application using the option?-autosar-multitasking , Polyspace might stop during the compilation phase with an error message similar to the following: Verifying MyFile.c (Y/XX)pathto\Os.h, line xxxx: error: identifier "OSEK_task_polyspace_TaskType" is undefined|  StatusType OSEK_polyspace_ActivateTask(OSEK_task_polyspace_TaskType TaskID);|                                         ^
Workaround: Try configuring your Polyspace multitasking analysis manually. See?Configuring Polyspace Multitasking Analysis Manually.
Products: Polyspace Code Prover Server, Polyspace Code Prover

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Native floating-point with hierarchical clock-rate pipelining might cause validation model mismatch
Description: The clock-rate pipelining optimization has the ability to work across hierarchical boundaries. For more information, see?Clock-Rate Pipelining and Hierarchy Flattening .? When this optimization is combined with floating-point libraries, the result of delay balancing on the design might be incorrect.?
Workaround: Hierarchical clock-rate pipelining is not applied if no hierarchy exists. For the parts of the design that are affected by this issue, apply FlattenHierarchy on the subsystem to avoid the mismatch. To perform this action, run this command:hdlset_param(<path_to_subsystem>, 'FlattenHierarchy', 'on')?To disable hierarchical clock-rate pipelining for the entire model, run the following command. Doing so might cause clock-rate pipelining results to be less optimal.hdlset_param(<model_name>, 'CRPWithoutFlattening', 'off')

Summary: Bus type with string elements cannot be exported
Description: A Simulink bus type can be exported to a custom header file in code generation when its DataScope   property is set to Exported . However, if the bus type has a string element, the bus type cannot be exported. Instead, an error like the following displays: "Error occurred when generating shared type: Exported type, ?BusObject, ?depends on a non-exported type, ?SLStringType. ?Either set DataScope of BusObject to 'Auto' or set DataScope of SLStringType to 'Exported'. "  Neither of the suggestions in the message works.?

Summary: MATLAB prints message about "canberra-gtk-module" and/or "pk-gtk-module" on startup
Description: On some Linux distributions, a message appears in the terminal window when starting MATLAB:? Gtk-Message: <timestamp> : Failed to load module "canberra-gtk-module"  and/or  Gtk-Message: <timestamp> : Failed to load module "pk-gtk-module"  ?This message is informational and has no effect on MATLAB.
Workaround: You can suppress this message by loading a missing Linux package to provide libcanberra-gtk-module.so and/or libpk-gtk-module.so.For the canberra-gtk-module error, open a terminal with root privileges and execute one of the following commands:Red Hat distributions (including CentOS and Fedora)yum install libcanberra-gtk2Debian-based distributions (including Ubuntu):apt-get install libcanberra-gtk-moduleFor the pk-gtk-module error, open a terminal with root privileges and execute one of the following commands:Red Hat distributions (including CentOS and Fedora)yum install PackageKit-gtk3-moduleDebian-based distributions (including Ubuntu):apt-get install packagekit-gtk3-module

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model arguments with a non-Auto storage class might lose tunability in referenced models in accelerator mode
Description: Simulink does not honor changes to the value of model arguments, during both rebuilds and simulation, when all of the following conditions are true: The model argument has a non-Auto  storage class. The model argument is not pushed all the way up to the top model in the hierarchy. The referenced models?providing values for the model arguments use a simulation mode other than Normal .

Summary: Ellipsis line continuation inside a matrix or cell array is not treated as a space after plus or minus
Description: In matrices and cell arrays, MATLAB does not properly interpret an ellipsis as a space character when using plus and minus. For example: >> [1 + 2] ans =     3 >> [1 + ...2]ans =     3   >> [1 +...2] ans =     1     2 Note that the ellipsis here is causing the last matrix to be interpreted as [1, +2]  rather than [1 + 2] . This bug occurs when both these conditions exist:  Using a matrix [ ]  or cell array { } . The +  or -  operator is followed immediately by an ellipsis (... ) without whitespace.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated lookup table code using coder.approximation produces less accurate results in some cases
Description: When using coder.approximation  during fixed-point conversion of MATLAB code, generated lookup table code produces less accurate results for generated lookup tables that use 1D interpolation. Similar inaccuracy exists in the Fixed-Point Converter and MATLAB Coder apps when you use lookup table replacements in Function Replacements  tab during conversion of MATLAB code to fixed point. In the Fixed-Point Converter and MATLAB Coder apps, the "Flat" interpolation method is not affected. Other interpolation methods are affected.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when using a DBC-file that contains inconsistencies
Description: When trying to open a DBC-file that contains inconsistencies, MATLAB might crash without generating any crash log. The impacted workflows include:? Calling the function canDatabase  with such a DBC-file. Specifying such a DBC-file to use with the following blocks: CAN Pack, CAN Unpack, CAN FD Pack, CAN FD Unpack, J1939 Network Configuration.
Workaround: If accessing a DBC-file causes MATLAB to crash, you can use Vector CANdb++ to perform a consistency check for the file in question. Fixing the reported inconsistencies should prevent the crash.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b
